chapters.title: Chapter 1797 Heavenly Beast Sect(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1797 Heavenly Beast Sect(3)

“While we travel to our destination, allow me to tell you about our Heavenly Beast Sect’s history—our foundation,” said Hong Ling.

“The Heavenly Beast Sect is one of the most ancient sects within the Sixth Heaven, dating all the way back to the Immortal Monarch’s Era. We were founded towards the end of the era, shortly after the Divine Heavens was split.”

“Our founder, who had formed a contract with three Divine Beasts, was titled Savage Beast King.”

“Three? That’s impressive,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“Yet it pales in comparison to your master, who has contracted a Phoenix with a Divine-grade bloodline.”

Feng Yuxiang lifted her head slightly in a prideful manner and said, “My master is unique. You shouldn’t compare him with anyone because it would only make the other party look bad.”

“What’s your relationship with your master like?” Lan Yingying suddenly asked.

“I think it’s a pretty ordinary relationship. I met him six hundred years ago when he climbed the Crimson Mountain just to meet me. I admired his dedication, and he was quite talented, so I agreed to form a contract with him for a decade to prove himself—that he was worthy of becoming my partner. And as you can see, I am still here even after six hundred years.”

“What do you typically do as his partner?” Lan Yingying then asked. Searᴄh the nôvel_Fire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Huh? We can absorb our master’s cultivation? This is the first time I’ve heard about this.” Lan Yingying’s eyes widened.

“Whenever he’s busy managing the sect, I spend most of my time cultivating. When he has to leave the sect, I will follow him as his guard. When he gets into a fight, I become his weapon. Although he can fight, most beast tamers are typically weaker than their beasts.”

“Huh? Why are they usually weaker than their partners?” Lan Yingying asked, her eyebrows raised in a puzzled manner. “Wouldn’t it make more sense if the master was stronger than the follower? After all, who’d want a weak master?”

“That may be how you humans—people who are not beast tamers—see it,” Hong Ling began with a knowing smile, “but most beasts enter a contract with the intent of becoming stronger. If the master fails to provide their contracted beast with enough resources or opportunities to grow, the beast will leave them. Of course, this only applies to consensual contracts, where both parties willingly agree to the partnership.”

“As contracted beasts, we can also absorb some of our master’s cultivation,” Hong Ling continued. “This is one of the main things most contracted beasts demand from their masters. It’s a mutually beneficial exchange—while the master gains a powerful ally, the beast can grow stronger by sharing in their master’s progress.”

“Huh? We can absorb our master’s cultivation? This is the first time I’ve heard about this.” Lan Yingying’s eyes widened.

“What? You didn’t know this entire time? I assumed you were just being humble,” Feng Yuxiang looked at her with a surprised face. “Ah, if you’re wondering why I never asked the Young Master to share his cultivation with me, it’s because my cultivation is already much higher than his—and cultivation isn’t the reason I formed a contract with him.”

“I really didn’t know. Though, even with that knowledge, I have no intention of asking Yu—the Young Master for his cultivation.” Lan Yingying almost subconsciously called Yuan by his name.

“Wait… You’re a magical beast, as well? I couldn’t tell at all!” Hong Ling stared at Lan Yingying with a shocked face.

Even in their human form, most beasts typically cannot completely mask their beastly scent or identity as a beast, especially from other beasts.

Hong Ling immediately recognized Feng Yuxiang and Xi Meili as Divine Beasts the moment their distinct scent reached her. However, when it came to Lan Yingying, her scent and demeanor gave no such indication, making her appear essentially human in Hong Ling’s eyes. “Now that you mentioned it… I never realized this.” Feng Yuxiang muttered after hearing Hong Linge’rs words.

“It likely has something to do with my bloodline, but I am not certain,” said Lan Yingying.

“Are you also a Divine Beast?” Hong Ling looked at Lan Yingying in a new light.

“Yes.”

“Amazing! To have contracted three Divine Beasts! It’s just like our founder!”

“Actually, I am not in a contract with Xiao Yang,” Xi Meili suddenly said with an awkward smile on her face. “I’m just traveling with them.”

“You might as well form a contract with the Young Master, Meili. I know you want to.” Feng Yuxiang looked at her with a smug-looking smile on her face.

Xi Meili showed a bittersweet smile and said, “I do, but I also don’t want to at the same time. If I form a contract, our relationship would be akin to master and servant. I don’t want that. I want to stand beside him as someone more significant.”

Feng Yuxiang raised an eyebrow and commented, “You mean like his dao companion?”

Xi Meili’s face immediately flushed with redness.

“While it’s true that our contract defines us as master and servant, what truly matters is how you and your master perceive your relationship,” Hong Ling explained with a warm smile. “For example, my master and I are like best friends. I don’t feel like a servant at all.”

Her words carried a sense of pride and affection, emphasizing the importance of mutual respect and understanding in such bonds, even when the terms of the contract suggested otherwise.

“She’s right, you know? Also, the Young Master isn’t that kind of person. I’ve been with him for years now, and he’s never once treated me like a servant,” Feng Yuxiang added.

“I know he isn’t that kind of person, and I am aware that this is a mindset issue on my part. Once I can get over it, I’ll ask him for a contract,” Xi Meili said with an eager smile.

“If you don’t mind me asking, what kind of person is your master? What made you want to follow him?” Hong Ling asked a moment later.

“I’m glad you asked!” Feng Yuxiang’s face lit up with excitement after hearing this question.

chapters.title: Chapter 1798 Heavenly Beast Sect(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1798 Heavenly Beast Sect(4)

“My Young Master is not only the most talented individual in the Nine Heavens, but he is also the kindest, most handsome, coolest, unrivaled—” Feng Yuxiang began complimenting Yuan nonstop after Hong Ling asked about him.

If Hong Ling wasn’t aware of her relationship with Yuan in the beginning, she would’ve mistaken her as some obsessive crazy lady.

“Y-You’re really passionate about your master, huh?” Hong Ling said with a stiff smile.

If she hadn’t interrupted Feng Yuxiang, heaven knows how long she would’ve kept on mumbling.

“Of course, I am! The Young Master not only changed my life—he practically saved it!” “What about you, Senior Lan?” Hong Ling turned to look at Lan Yingying next.

“You can just call me by my name. I am actually much younger than you.”

“No, I cannot do that. Even if you were just born yesterday, you have seniority over me as a Divine Beast, as I am only an ordinary magical beast.”

“If that’s what you want to do, I won’t say anything else. Anyway, I followed the Young Master for an entirely different reason. Although he did save my—and my family’s life— that wasn’t the reason I wanted to follow him. Also, while I did have my own desires to follow him, it was mostly my grandparents’ advice and persuasion that made me actually do it.”

“As I continued following him, my desire to stay together with him grew stronger. Now, I don’t want to be separated from him at all. Most importantly, I am going to be giving birth to his child in the future.”

“What?! You’re bearing your master’s child?!” Hong Ling cried out loud, nearly falling off the flying treasure from shock.

She opened her mouth again, but no words came out. She was going to question Lan Yingying’s reasoning for bearing a human’s child, but she refrained herself, realizing it would be disrespectful.

“Hmph! Once I fully regain my strength and is worth more to the Young Master, I will also ask to bear his child!” Feng Yuxiang declared with a determined look on her face.

“What? But you’re of royal bloodline! Are you even allowed to conceive with a human? Won’t that significantly degrade your bloodline?” Hong Ling couldn’t stay silent after hearing Feng Yuxiang’s outrageous declaration.

In their world, royal bloodlines are considered sacred and are strictly protected and regulated by the royal families of their respective races. Degrading or defiling a royal bloodline is regarded as an unforgivable act of blasphemy, often punishable by death. “I don’t care about that. It’s not like I am associated with the royal families. I will conceive with whoever I want, regardless of what anyone says. If anyone dares to try and stop me, I will simply kill them all.” Feng Yuxiang sneered.

Hong Ling shuddered in fear when she sensed Feng Yuxiang’s killing intent from her narrowed eyes. seaʀᴄh thё ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

It suddenly became dead silent, and it stayed like that for the remainder of the ride. Fortunately, they were already almost at their destination by then, so it was only a few minutes of silence.

“Okay, we have arrived at our first destination—our Heavenly Beast Sect’s magical beast breeding ground,” Hong Ling said, gesturing toward the massive expanse of land before them. “This is where the sect breeds and nurtures magical beasts for its disciples. While it’s not a requirement to have a contracted beast to join the sect, all disciples are expected to form a contract with a beast within ten years of enrollment.”

The area resembled sprawling farms, each section dedicated to breeding and raising various types of magical beasts. The air was filled with the sounds of creatures, and the energy in the atmosphere was vibrant.

“Our disciples are encouraged to explore the world and form a contract with a wild magical beast. If they are unable to do that, they can try to form a contract with a beast here. We have over a thousand different species of magical beast, including some rare ones like the Obsidian Bull, Jade Rabbit, and the Red Horned Wasp.”

“However,” Hong Ling continued, “these magical beasts are not free, nor are they available to every disciple. Only those who demonstrate promise and earn enough points are allowed to form a contract with the beasts here.”

“Points can be earned by completing sect missions or through accomplishments that enhance the sect’s reputation. It’s a way to ensure that only the most dedicated and capable disciples gain access to these valuable resources.”

“As for the beasts, we either capture them directly from the wild or purchase beast eggs from the market. Capturing wild beasts isn’t too difficult, but the real challenge lies in training them to be obedient.”

She paused for a moment before continuing, “As for beast eggs, they’re a different story. They’re incredibly rare and not always available for purchase, even if you have the resources.”

“This place took countless resources and an unfathomable amount of time and effort to become what it is today,” Hong Ling said, her voice brimming with pride.

“Can we take a look around this place?” Feng Yuxiang asked, her tone curious.

Hong Ling immediately offered an apologetic smile and said, “About that… Normally, we’d allow it, but because of your bloodlines, I’m afraid your scent would terrify the beasts. It could cause a panic, and that would be disastrous.”

“Oh… What a pity,” Feng Yuxiang said with a soft sigh, shaking her head in clear disappointment.

Despite her reaction, there was no trace of blame in her expression toward Hong Ling. She understood all too well the impact of their powerful bloodlines as a phoenix and a dragon. Such a presence would undoubtedly terrify the magical beasts in the breeding grounds, especially since these creatures were likely more mentally fragile than their wild counterparts.

“It’s understandable,” she added softly, her tone accepting.

“Thank you for your understanding,” Hong Ling said with a grateful smile. “Let’s head to our next destination. It’s where our disciples, who have been approved to form a contract with the beasts here, perform their ritual. As a matter of fact, we happen to have someone preparing to perform the ritual soon.”

They sped off to their next destination shortly later.

chapters.title: Chapter 1799 Heavenly Beast Sect(5)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1799 Heavenly Beast Sect(5)

After traveling for half an hour, they arrived at their next destination.

Hong Ling made the flying treasure descend before saying to the others, “You can follow me.”

Lan Yingying and the other two followed Hong Ling into the large square building that only had a single floor before them.

“Greetings, Senior Hong.”

The guards standing by the entrance greeted with lowered heads as they opened the door for them.

“Good work,” she said to them.

The interior of the building was stark and empty, its design emphasizing functionality over aesthetics. The walls were crafted from a durable metal commonly used in the creation of treasures, giving the space an aura of resilience and strength. Additionally, several intricate formations were embedded into the walls and floor, their faint glow indicating their activation.

“Hm? If it isn’t Senior Hong. What brought you here today?” A sect elder there noticed her and asked. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I’m here as a tour guide, and I want to let them watch the ritual. There’s one happening soon, correct?”

“Yes, and you’re just in time. We’re currently waiting for the magical beast to be brought in, which should only take a few more minutes,” said the sect elder.

Standing by the sect elder was a young male disciple, clearly eager to be there.

“It’s an honor to have my ritual witnessed by Senior Hong.” The disciple bowed.

Hong Ling acknowledged him with a silent nod before turning to Feng Yuxiang and the others.

“During the ritual, the disciple must fight the magical beast and earn its acknowledgment,” Hong Ling explained.

“Most magical beasts that lack the capability to speak or think like us must be defeated in battle to earn their acknowledgment,” she continued. “It’s a way of proving one’s strength and qualifications to the beast. Of course, there are rare cases where a magical beast willingly forms a contract without needing to be dominated, but such instances are exceedingly rare and almost unheard of.”

“Also, even if the disciple defeats the magical beast, it doesn’t guarantee the beast would acknowledge the disciple as its master. With that being said, we have a very high success rate of around 95 percent. If the magical beast rejects the disciple, we will simply find another one for him, and every disciple will be given three chances.”

About ten minutes later, a magical beast was led into the building.

It was an Earth-Shattering Ape, a formidable and rare magical beast renowned for its immense strength and robust physique. With a height of 4 meters, its muscular frame radiated power, and its deep, glowing eyes scanned the room with a mix of curiosity and wariness.

Feng Yuxiang and the others had to move to a separate area before the Earth-Shattering Ape arrived because of their bloodline, and they were currently watching everything behind a glass wall in a sealed spectator’s room, ensuring that the ritual couldn’t be disturbed in any manner.

“The ritual will begin once I remove its shackles,” the sect elder said to the disciple, who nodded with a nervous smile on his face.

A few moments later, the shackles were removed from the Earth-Shattering Ape. Yet, it didn’t immediately move and turned to look at the sect elder.

Seeing this, the sect elder turned to the disciple and pointed at him. “Is he worthy to be your master? Go find out,” she said firmly.

As if it understood her words, the Earth-Shattering Ape nodded slightly before locking its gaze on the disciple. Without hesitation, the beast leaped toward him, its robust frame cutting through the air with incredible force.

The ritual had officially begun.

The disciple immediately poured every ounce of his strength and focus into the battle, determined to prove himself worthy. He fought the Earth-Shattering Ape with the intensity of a life-or-death struggle, dodging its crushing blows and counterattacking with his own martial techniques. Despite the fierce exchange, the disciple carefully avoided striking the beast’s vitals to avoid killing it by accident.

The battle raged on for nearly three grueling hours, each moment filled with thunderous blows that would’ve destroyed the building if it weren’t for the reinforced walls and formations. The Earth-Shattering Ape, with its immense strength and durability, proved to be a challenging opponent for the disciple, a young genius with a cultivation at the peak of Spirit Enlightenment.

But as time wore on, the beast began to falter. Its once-commanding presence started to waver, and its silver fur became matted with sweat and stained with its own blood. Finally, with a resounding thud, the ape fell to its knees, its breathing ragged and heavy.

Despite its exhaustion and injuries, the beast’s eyes still glimmered with a sense of pride and acknowledgment, as though it had tested the disciple and found him worthy. The atmosphere in the room was thick with tension and awe as the disciple, equally battered but unyielding, stood before the beast, awaiting the final sign of submission.

After a moment of heavy silence, the Earth-Shattering Ape lowered its massive head, a clear sign of acknowledgment. The room seemed to hold its breath as the beast extended a finger to one of its wounds, gathering a drop of its own blood.

Seeing the Earth-Shattering Ape’s gesture, the disciple wasted no time. He immediately activated a unique technique, his hands glowing faintly as he infused the beast’s offered blood with his own.

A wave of energy surged through the room as their blood merged, forming a mystical bond that tethered their souls together. Symbols began to appear in the air around them, shimmering briefly before vanishing into their bodies, signifying the completion of the contract.

The Earth-Shattering Ape let out a low, rumbling growl—not of defiance but of acceptance and excitement. The disciple, now connected to the beast, knelt respectfully before it, silently thanking the powerful creature for its trust. The ritual was complete, their partnership officially forged.

“So that’s how magical beasts typically form a contract with humans… Mine was vastly different.” Lan Yingying muttered as she recalled the moment she formed a contract with Yuan.

chapters.title: Chapter 1800 Heavenly Beast Sect(6).
chapters.body:
Chapter 1800 Heavenly Beast Sect(6).

“Different? How?” Hong Ling inquired after hearing Lan Yingying’s mumble, her face filled with curiosity.

“I consumed some of my master’s blood and initiated the contract myself,” she explained.

“That’s what I did, too.” Feng Yuxiang added.

“I see… Well, there are several methods to form a contract, and Divine Beasts are inherently different, so it’s not that weird your method was different,” said Hong Ling. “As for our Heavenly Beast Sect, this is how we teach our disciples.”

“Also, we’re not done yet.”

Hong Ling shifted her attention back to the Earth-Shattering Ape and the disciple, who were silently staring at each other, almost as if they were communicating through voice transmission.

Several minutes later, the disciple’s aura suddenly weakened, and his cultivation, which was at the peak of Spirit Enlightenment, suddenly dropped to the seventh level of Spirit Enlightenment.

Meanwhile, the Earth-Shattering Ape, which was at the fifth level of Spirit Enlightenment, suddenly soared to the peak of Spirit Enlightenment.

“While the master only lost two levels, the servant’s cultivation increased by four whole levels. This is why magical beasts willingly form contracts with humans.” Hong Ling said.

“Can the servant forcefully take the master’s cultivation?” Lan Yingying asked.

“Yes, it’s possible, and it has happened before, albeit rarely.”

“What typically leads to such an outcome?” Xi Meili asked next.

“In my experience, such incidents only occur when the beast is severely neglected or abused by its master. After all, forcefully taking their master’s cultivation significantly reduces the beast’s own longevity, so it doesn’t truly benefit much from doing so.”

“What happens to the master?” Xi Meili asked.

“Most die shortly after having all of their cultivation forcefully removed. Some have managed to live a few more years, but I don’t know anyone who has managed to live a normal life afterward.”

With a heavy sigh, she spoke, “Yes, it’s also possible. However, it rarely happens because when a human takes our cultivation—even if it’s only slightly—our longevity will decrease.”

Lan Yingying then asked, “If the beast can take their master’s cultivation… can the master do the same to their beast?”

Hong Ling fell silent after hearing such a question.

With a heavy sigh, she spoke, “Yes, it’s also possible. However, it rarely happens because when a human takes our cultivation—even if it’s only slightly—our longevity will decrease.”

“In cases where a human forcefully takes their beast’s cultivation,” Hong Ling continued, her tone turning somber, “the human doesn’t lose their longevity. However, over time, they start losing their sanity, and if they do it too many times, they eventually become beast-like themselves.”

She paused for a moment before adding, “That said, humans can get away with doing it a few times without immediate consequences. It’s not a fair system, but that’s just the way it works.”

“Has it happened in the Heavenly Beast Sect before?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly asked, her tone curious yet serious.

“Which…?” Hong Ling inquired hesitantly.

“Either,” she clarified.

Hong Ling sighed deeply, her expression tinged with reluctance. “I probably shouldn’t say this, but there’s no point in hiding it since it’s already public knowledge,” she admitted. “Yes, we have had such incidents before—both ways.” Searᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“However, these incidents occurred during the sect’s earlier days, when we didn’t know much and weren’t teaching our disciples properly. We haven’t had such incidents in centuries.”

“Anyway, let’s talk about something less depressing. Once a disciple successfully forms a contract, they will have to spend the next year building a bond with their beast. Let me show you.”

They left the building shortly after, and Hong Ling led them to their next destination on the flying treasure.

Sometime later, the group arrived at a vast expanse that closely resembled the untamed wilderness. Rolling hills stretched into the distance, dotted with dense patches of forest and rocky terrain. The air was filled with the sounds of nature—birds chirping, leaves rustling, and the occasional roar of a magical beast echoing in the distance.

“Disciples who have recently formed a contract with their beasts will spend a month here. During that time, they’ll do whatever is necessary to strengthen their bond with their partner—whether it’s casually strolling together to build trust or engaging in battles with other magical beasts to grow stronger together,” Hong Ling explained.

“While the goal is to strengthen their bond, there’s always a chance the opposite might occur. For instance, the disciple might come to realize that the beast is incompatible with them, or they may notice issues that weren’t immediately apparent at first.”

“This actually happens quite often. When they do, it’s up to the disciple and the sect to decide the next steps—whether it’s continuing the partnership or seeking a more suitable match. Building a bond isn’t just about strength; it’s about compatibility and mutual growth.”

“I must say, I’m very impressed with the Heavenly Beast Sect. I have been to several sects that focus on beast taming before, but none of them are as organized and dedicated as you guys,” Feng Yuxiang said.

Hong Ling’s face brightened at her complement. “Thank you very much! You have no idea how much that means to me—and the sect! This isn’t even half of our sect! Come, let me show you more!”

She eagerly led them to the next destination.

When they reached their next destination, a sprawling training ground divided into several distinct areas, Hong Ling gestured toward the open space and said, “This place is for sparring and combat training. Disciples and their beasts come here to learn to fight together and prepare for real battles.”

“If you’d like, we can let you experience what it feels like to fight a beast tamer and their partner.”

“Really? I’m interested.” Xi Meili was the first one to volunteer. In fact, she raised her hand almost immediately.

Hong Ling nodded before scanning the training ground, her gaze darting between the disciples and their beasts to find a suitable sparring partner for Xi Meili.

“I know you’re only a Divine Warrior, but the weakest magical beast currently available is at the seventh level of Divine Master. If you would like a weaker opponent, we can set that up for you.”

“No, a Divine Master is fine,” Xi Meili responded with a confident and excited smile on her face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1801 Heavenly Beast Sect(7)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1801 Heavenly Beast Sect(7)

“Very well, then I’ll set it up for you immediately. Please give me a few moments.” Hong Ling jumped off the flying treasure and descended into the area with the disciple and the seventh level Divine Master beast.

“You want us to spar with a Divine Beast—a dragon, to the boot?” The female disciple’s eyes widened after hearing Hong Ling’s suggestion.

“What do you think? Want to experience fighting a dragon?”

The disciple turned to her beast, a Purple Scaled Long Tail. This imposing creature had a sleek, armored body covered in shimmering purple scales that gleamed under the light. Its tail, exceeding six meters in length, swayed slowly behind it.

The beast’s piercing red eyes locked onto its master, exuding an air of intelligence and loyalty, yet carrying a hint of wild ferocity.

After a brief moment of silence, the Purple Scaled Long Tail suddenly let out a loud, resonant battle cry. The sound echoed across the training ground, drawing the attention of nearby disciples and their beasts. Its piercing red eyes gleamed with excitement, and its long tail lashed the ground rhythmically, creating small shockwaves with each impact.

Seeing the beast’s reaction, the disciple nodded at it with a smile before turning to face Hong Ling again.

“We’ll spar with the dragon,” she declared.

“Okay. I’ll be right back.”

Hong Ling returned to the flying treasure and announced the disciple’s decision to Xi Meili, who followed Hong Ling to the arena shortly after.

Lan Yingying and Feng Yuxiang remained on the flying treasure, which hovered above the arena, giving them a perfect view as spectators.

When Xi Meili landed gracefully before the disciple and the Purple Scaled Long Tail, the beast’s demeanor changed instantly. Despite its earlier confidence and excitement, its natural instincts betrayed it. The overwhelming presence of Xi Meili’s dragon aura caused the Purple Scaled Long Tail to tremble visibly, its tail curling slightly in a defensive posture.

Its piercing red eyes, once filled with ferocity, now reflected hesitation and fear as it instinctively lowered its head, acknowledging the dominance of the mighty bloodline before it. Even the disciple couldn’t help but feel the weight of Xi Meili’s presence, though she tried to maintain her composure.

“It’s a pleasure to meet you. I’m Xi Meili, a dragon.”

Once the disciple introduced herself, Hong Ling stepped forward and addressed both parties with a clear, authoritative tone.

“The match will end once Senior Xi or the Purple Scaled Long Tail is deemed incapable of continuing to fight or if one side chooses to surrender.”

“Whenever you are ready.” Xi Meili beckoned them.

In response, the disciple jumped back, distancing herself from the fight. Meanwhile, the Purple Scaled Long Tail stepped forward, standing between Xi Meili and its master.

Xi Meili remained still, clearly letting the disciple make the first move. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Realizing this, the disciple commanded the beast through their master-servant connection, which allowed them to communicate with their mind, similar to voice transmission. But unlike voice transmission, their conversations could not be intercepted, making it impossible for one to eavesdrop.

Upon receiving its master’s command, the Purple Scaled Long Tail immediately sprang into action. Its long, powerful tail whipped forward with precision like a mighty spear. The beast jabbed repeatedly at Xi Meili from a distance, skillfully utilizing the tail’s impressive length to keep her at bay while maintaining a safe range.

Xi Meili casually dodged the Purple Scaled Long Tail’s relentless strikes, her movements minimal yet precise, as though she were merely toying with her opponent.

Noticing her beast’s attacks were ineffective, the disciple’s eyes narrowed. Unexpectedly, she began to draw array symbols in the air. Within moments, the sky above them filled with glowing Level 2 array symbols, forming an intricate formation.

The disciple wasted no time and activated the array, directing it toward Xi Meili’s feet. Golden chains emerged from the ground, their shimmering surfaces radiating power as they wrapped tightly around Xi Meili’s ankles.

Seeing the opportunity, the Purple Scaled Long Tail intensified its assault, its tail jabbing with greater speed and ferocity, aiming to exploit Xi Meili’s seemingly restrained state.

However, Xi Meili’s calm expression didn’t waver. With a single, decisive step, she shattered the golden chains as if they were nothing but fragile glass, the broken fragments dissolving into the air.

The disciple’s eyes widened briefly in surprise at how effortlessly Xi Meili had destroyed her Level 2 formation. However, she quickly composed herself, her hands moving deftly through the air as she began creating a new set of array symbols.

This time, instead of targeting Xi Meili directly, the disciple focused on supporting her beast. The formations glowed brightly as they activated, enveloping the Purple Scaled Long Tail in a radiant light.

The beast’s movements grew noticeably sharper, its strikes faster, and its aura more oppressive. Its already formidable strength seemed to double, and its speed became a blur as it resumed its assault on Xi Meili with renewed vigor.

Once she had seen enough, Xi Meili began her offense.

“Dragon transformation—25 percent.” The moment Xi Meili activated her transformation, an overwhelming surge of energy erupted from her body. Her aura skyrocketed, blazing like a beacon as it soared toward the heavens. The sheer intensity of her presence rippled through the air, sending waves of pressure cascading across the arena.

The Purple Scaled Long Tail froze mid-attack, its red eyes widening in fear as its instincts screamed at it to submit or flee.

The disciple stumbled backward, beads of sweat forming on her forehead as she struggled to keep her composure.

“This… this is the power of a dragon’s bloodline,” she muttered, her voice tinged with awe.

“Here I come!” Xi Meili declared with a confident and thunderous voice that echoed throughout the arena.

With astonishing speed, she pounced toward the Purple Scaled Long Tail, her arm pulled back in preparation for a devastating strike. Beautiful golden scales shimmered across her arm, reflecting the light like molten gold, adding to the spectacle of her overwhelming presence.

The Purple Scaled Long Tail’s red eyes widened in panic as it lashed out its tail in a desperate attempt to fend off Xi Meili. However, with a swift mid-air spin, she narrowly dodged the attack.

Before the beast could retract its tail, Xi Meili’s other hand shot out, firmly grabbing hold of it. Using the momentum of the tail’s motion, she propelled herself forward like a missile, closing the gap between her and the beast in an instant.

Xi Meili’s fist connected with the Purple Scaled Long Tail’s back with a thunderous crack that echoed across the arena. The sheer force of the impact sent shockwaves rippling through the air, and the ground beneath the beast shattered from the intensity.

The Purple Scaled Long Tail let out a pained cry as its massive body slammed into the ground, creating a small crater where it landed. Dust and debris rose into the air, obscuring the scene momentarily, but the beast’s anguished roar made it clear how devastating the blow had been.

The disciple, frozen in place, could only watch in shock and disbelief. The sight of her beast, a creature known for its durability and strength, crumpling under Xi Meili’s single strike, left her utterly speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 1802 Heavenly Beast Sect(8)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1802 Heavenly Beast Sect(8)

After delivering her powerful strike, Xi Meili gracefully leaped backward, creating some distance between herself and the Purple Scaled Long Tail. Although her attack had undoubtedly dealt a significant blow, Xi Meili knew it wasn’t enough to defeat the beast.

As the dust and debris settled, the Purple Scaled Long Tail came into view, its massive body trembling as it struggled to rise. Its purple scales glistened with sweat and blood, and a large, jagged crack marred its back.

Despite its injuries, the beast let out a low, guttural growl, its red eyes burning with determination. It planted its claws firmly into the ground, lifting itself inch by inch, refusing to yield despite the pain wracking its body.

Hearing the Purple Scaled Long Tail’s determined cry, the disciple clenched her fists tightly and quickly snapped out of her daze.

“It’s not over yet!” she shouted.

Without hesitation, the disciple began gathering her spiritual energy, the air around her rippling with power. Her hands glowed faintly as she focused her energy, directing it toward her beast. Threads of spiritual energy, vibrant and pulsing with life, flowed from her body to the Purple Scaled Long Tail, wrapping around it like shimmering ribbons.

The beast roared as it absorbed the energy, its cracked scales starting to shimmer faintly with a new vitality. Its injuries didn’t fully heal, but its movements grew steadier, and its aura surged as it stood tall once more, defiance burning in its crimson eyes.

“No, this isn’t enough!” The disciple gritted her teeth and continued to channel almost all of her spiritual energy into the beast, causing its aura to soar even further, surpassing its original strength.

However, after giving most of her spiritual energy to her beast, the disciple fell to her knees from exhaustion.

“Oh? You can do something like that? How interesting,” Xi Meili muttered in a somewhat surprised tone. This was her first time fighting a beast tamer, so she was still unfamiliar with their methods.

Despite her exhaustion, the disciple pushed herself to continue as she instructed the Purple Scaled Long Tail for their next move. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The beast let out a thunderous roar before leaping high into the sky. As it reached the peak of its jump, its crimson eyes locked onto Xi Meili.

Without hesitation, the Purple Scaled Long Tail opened its massive jaws, releasing a barrage of shimmering projectiles. Each projectile radiated with a faint, fiery glow, crackling with explosive energy as they rained down toward Xi Meili in rapid succession.

As the barrage of projectiles descended upon her, Xi Meili remained unfazed, standing her ground with unwavering confidence. She didn’t even attempt to dodge the attack, allowing the fiery projectiles to collide directly with her body.

Each impact exploded with a resounding boom, sending shockwaves rippling through the arena. However, to Xi Meili, the force of the attacks felt no more harmful than mere ant bites. Her golden-scaled body easily absorbed the energy from the blasts, leaving her completely unscathed.

The disciple’s eyes widened in disbelief as she watched Xi Meili stand unshaken amidst the barrage. “Impossible… those projectiles have enough power to shatter mountains!”

Once the barrage ended, Xi Meili dusted off her golden scales with a casual motion, her expression calm yet filled with an air of dominance.

“Is that all?” Xi Meili asked, her calm yet sharp voice cutting through the tense atmosphere like a blade. Her golden gaze locked onto the Purple Scaled Long Tail as it landed heavily on the ground.

The beast growled lowly, its pride wounded by her words.

“Then I should end this soon.”

“Dragon transformation—50 percent.”

Xi Meili’s aura soared even higher, and her power doubled within the blink of an eye.

The Purple Scaled Long Tail trembled uncontrollably, caught between awe and fear as Xi Meili’s oppressive presence bore down on it like a towering mountain. Yet, it wasn’t alone in its fear. The presence Xi Meili exuded extended far beyond their arena, affecting even the other magical beasts in the vicinity. Beasts with far higher cultivation than the Purple Scaled Long Tail were visibly shaken, their heads lowered, and their tails tucked in submission.

“Let me show you a real breath attack!” Xi Meili declared, her voice resonating with power as she swiftly gathered her spiritual energy.

The golden scales on her body began to glow brilliantly, radiating an overwhelming heat that caused the air around her to blur. A swirling orb of light began forming before her open mouth, small but intense, as if it were a condensed star ready to unleash its fury.

The Purple Scaled Long Tail froze, its instincts screaming at it to escape, but it was too afraid to move, keeping it rooted in place. The disciple’s eyes widened in shock, her voice caught in her throat as she watched the scene unfold.

In a flash, the small orb transformed into a dazzling beam of golden light, roaring through the arena with unstoppable force. The ground beneath its trajectory cracked and burned, leaving a deep scar in its wake as it flew directly at the Purple Scaled Long Tail.

The beast tried to react, raising its tail as a shield. Upon contact, the golden light exploded, creating a shockwave that reverberated throughout the arena, sending dust and debris flying everywhere.

When the dust settled, the Purple Scaled Long Tail was sprawled on the ground, its tail scorched and its body motionless, overwhelmed by Xi Meili’s devastating attack.

“Chang Wei!” the disciple screamed in a terrified voice, her knees hitting the ground as she rushed to her partner’s side.

The beast, despite its battered and motionless appearance, let out a faint growl, signaling that it was still alive.

Xi Meili then spoke, “Relax, I held back as much as I could. The damage will heal in a few days with some recovery pills.”

Her attack had been controlled, meant to incapacitate without causing permanent or grievous harm. Otherwise, the beast would’ve been erased from existence.

The disciple looked at Xi Meili with a thankful expression.

“T-Thank you!”

Xi Meili shook her head before clasping her hands together and bowing to them.

“I should be thanking you for the valuable experience. Thank you.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1803 Heavenly Beast Sect(9)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1803 Heavenly Beast Sect(9)

“That was a great match.”

Shortly after the match ended, Hong Ling entered the stage and clapped with a satisfied smile.

“Disciple Dong, your strategy wasn’t bad, and you used your beast’s advantage correctly. Unfortunately, your opponent was too overwhelming, so don’t kick yourself over this defeat.”

“I won’t.” The disciple nodded.

“Here, take this pill to recover your spiritual energy. The other pill is for your partner.” Hong Ling handed two pills to the disciple.

“Thank you, Senior Hong.” The disciple accepted the pill and consumed one, feeding the other to her beast.

The pill was incredibly effective, and within moments, the beast could stand again.

“Senior Xi, I apologize for underestimating you,” Hong Ling said a moment later, bowing deeply to Xi Meili with an expression of both awe and humility. “With your prowess, you could probably fight two, maybe even three realms above your own cultivation. I’ve always known dragons were powerful, but to witness such overwhelming strength firsthand is truly humbling.”

Xi Meili’s golden gaze softened slightly as she looked at Hong Ling. “There’s no need to apologize,” she said calmly, her aura now subdued. “As a matter of fact, I also underestimated my opponent. Are all beast tamers also Array Masters?”

Hong Ling shook her head, “No, but many try to become Array Masters because that would allow them to support their partners more efficiently from the backlines. Meanwhile, some beast tamers like to fight alongside their partners. It all depends on the individual’s style.”

“What about that power-sharing trick at the end? Can all beast tamers do that?” Xi Meili continued to ask. Sёarᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Yes, all beast tamers have the capability to share their spiritual energy with their contracted beast, temporarily boosting the beast’s fighting prowess. However, it comes at a cost—it exhausts the master, leaving them far more vulnerable in battle, so it’s only used during desperate life-or-death situations or official matches like the one you just had, where two beast tamers fight through their beast only.”

Sometime later, Xi Meili and Hong Ling returned to the flying treasure, and Hong Ling resumed their tour of the Heavenly Beast Sect.

Over the next several days, Hong Ling showcased almost every aspect of the sect’s operations, culture, and teachings.

Hong Ling also shared various methods that the sect employed to strengthen the connection between tamers and their beasts. These included spiritual bonding exercises, shared cultivation sessions, and even rituals that involved synchronizing their spiritual energy flows.

By the end of their tour, Xi Meili and Lan Yingying had developed a good understanding of the intricacies of beast tamers and their own roles.

“And that marks the end of our tour. Although short, it was an honor to be your guide. I hope I have managed to satisfy your curiosity and taught you something new, but if you still have questions, don’t hesitate to ask them!” Hong Ling said to them as they returned to their living quarters.

“Thank you for the tour. I have learned far more than I anticipated,” Lan Yingying said. “Looking back at my own position and experience, I don’t think I could even be considered a proper contracted beast.”

Hearing this, Hong Ling dismissively shook her hand, “Actually, you shouldn’t apply our logic to your own. At the end of the day, we mainly deal with magical beasts. As a Divine Beast, your experience and methods should be different. Moreover, there are things that you, as a Divine Beast, can do with your master that normal magical beasts aren’t capable of doing.”

“If you want to learn more about Divine Beasts as contracted beasts, you should consider visiting the Divine Beast Sanctuary in the upper heaven. They’re a small but elite group of beast tamers, and every single one of them is contracted with a Divine Beast.”

“Divine Beast Sanctuary?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly muttered, raising an eyebrow. A faint smirk tugged at her lips as she continued, “Ah, I remember them. They’re a bunch of annoying bastards who kept pestering me to join them in the past. As for the Divine Beasts there—they’re even worse. Most of them have inferior bloodlines, yet they all act high and mighty, as if they stand at the peak of their bloodline.”

Hong Ling could only manage a stiff smile in response to Feng Yuxiang’s bold words. For her, the Divine Beast Sanctuary was an almost mythical place, one far beyond her reach or comprehension.

“You’re really something, Senior Feng,” she finally said, trying to mask her envy. “For someone like me, the Divine Beast Sanctuary is a place of legends. The thought of rejecting their invitations is… unfathomable.”

Once they finally returned to their guest house, Hong Ling said, “I’ll take my leave now, but if you wish to contact me for anything, you can use this communication jade slip to contact me.”

She handed Feng Yuxiang her communication jade slip.

“Okay. Once again, thanks for the tour.” Feng Yuxiang accepted the jade slip with a smile.

After Hong Ling left, they entered the building.

“Welcome back.” Yuan, who was cultivating in the living room, said to them.

“Young Master! I missed you!” Feng Yuxiang had the desire to leap onto him after being separated for a few days.

“How was your tour around the Heavenly Beast Sect?” he asked.

“We learned a lot about beast tamers,” Lan Yingying said. “There was so much I didn’t know about my role as your contracted beast that I feel ashamed.”

Yuan chuckled and replied, “There’s no need to worry about your ‘role.’ Contracted beast or not, you’re a precious companion of mine. Just keep being yourself.”

“I knew you’d say something like that, but I want to support you properly,” Lan Yingying sighed. “Despite following you for years, I haven’t really done anything befitting of my role.”

Not wanting to dismiss her feelings, Yuan asked gently, “Then what do you want to do? Let me hear it.”

Lan Yingying wore a perplexed expression on her face, and she responded with a defeated sigh, “Honestly, I don’t know. You’re so much stronger than me—strong enough to overwhelm opponents that could easily kill me. I don’t know how I can fulfill my role as your contracted beast properly.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1804 Heavenly Beast Sect(10)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1804 Heavenly Beast Sect(10)

“I understand your feelings, Yingying, but being a contracted beast is more than just about fighting,” Yuan spoke after hearing Lan Yingying out.

“When I was the Immortal Monarch, the Nine Divine Supremes barely stood in the frontlines, and I fought most of my enemies on my own, but that didn’t mean they were useless or didn’t fulfill their role. Everyone has their own role.”

“For example, the Shadow Dweller rarely fought but specialized in information gathering. The Celestial Turtle spent most of its time serving as my flying treasure and living quarters as we traveled the starry sky. The Divine Yin Yang Crane, the laziest of all, spent most of its time sleeping.”

“Instead of focusing on how you can assist me in battle, you should try to figure out the kind of role you want to play as my contracted beast. Even if you don’t have any specific role, I will never see you as useless or a burden.”

“My own role…” Lan Yingying muttered with a contemplating expression on her face.

“That’s right,” Feng Yuxiang suddenly joined the conversation. “In my case, I see myself as our group’s finance expert and wallet. If you ever need money, you can always come to me.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow at her words and asked, “Our wallet? Since when?”

“I decided that just now,” she proudly announced. “Doing business was just a hobby—something to pass the time while I figured out how to remove my curse, and I have more money than I ever need, so I might as well put it to good use.” Searᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Although Feng Yuxiang certainly had enough wealth to serve as the group’s financier, Yuan wasn’t lacking either, with piles of treasures and precious materials at his disposal.

“Although I don’t know how I feel about using you as a wallet, there is something I would like you to do for me using your expertise as a business expert,” Yuan said a moment later. “Can you help me sell all of these treasures and materials that I have been collecting?”

He showed all of the treasures he’d collected over time, from treasures he looted from his enemies to materials he found in the Primal Expanse.

“Wow, there’s enough to open a store…” Feng Yuxiang swallowed nervously after seeing the massive pile of treasures before her.

“Are you in a hurry to sell them?” she then asked.

“No, you can take your time selling them. If it’s too much, you can just sell the most valuable treasures,” he said.

“No! This is something you entrusted me to do! I will sell every single treasure here, even if some of them are only worth less than a single spirit stone!” she declared.

“Thank you.”

“Since we still have plenty of time before the Hidden Domain is accessible, I’ll start selling the treasures now.” Feng Yuxiang said.

“How do you intend on doing that?” Xi Meili asked out of curiosity.

“I can easily hand off most of these treasures to auction houses and sell them that way, but that would be too easy, and we won’t be getting the full value of these treasures. In that case, I’ll open a store and sell them myself.”

“Opening a store? You make it sound so easy…” Xi Meili said.

“That’s because it’s easy. Do you want to come and experience it yourself? I can use a helper.”

“A helper, huh? Sure.” Xi Meili nodded after pondering for a moment.

“What about you?” Feng Yuxiang looked at Lan Yingying.

“I’ll stay in the Heavenly Beast Sect to study more about beast tamers,” she said.

“Just to be safe, I’ll have Yin or Yang follow you,” Yuan said a moment later.

Feng Yuxiang smiled and said, “You don’t need to worry too much, Young Master. We’re not going out to play. Most marketplaces have strict rules and are well-guarded.”

A few days later, once all preparations were done, Feng Yuxiang and Xi Meili set out for their business endeavor, leaving the Heavenly Beast Sect.

“Yin, are you here?”

“I am here,” Like a ghost, Yin responded to his call through voice transmission.

“Can you follow Feng Feng and Meili and watch over them? If anything happens, contact me immediately.”

“As you command.”

After leaving the Heavenly Beast Sect, Feng Yuxiang and Xi Meili traveled to the nearest business city to open a store. Thanks to Feng Yuxiang’s experience, the process went incredibly smoothly, and within just three days, they had their store up and running.

“How can you tell a treasure’s worth? I can’t tell at all.” Xi Meili said as she helped Feng Yuxiang place the treasures inside their display case.

“Experience, experience, and experience. I have been selling treasures for centuries, after all.”

“What made you choose this kind of work?” Xi Meili then asked.

“I honestly don’t know. It was the first thing that came to my mind when I thought about what I wanted to do. I didn’t question it, either.”

Sometime later, their store had its first customer.

“W-what kind of material is this?! Where did you acquire it?!” The customer was shocked after seeing an unknown material that Yuan had acquired from the Primal Expanse.

Feng Yuxiang calmly smiled and said, “I’ll tell you if you purchase a treasure.”

The customer swallowed nervously.

“Any treasure?”

“Yes.”

After some contemplating, the customer purchased one of the cheaper treasures in the store, paying several hundred spirit stones just to learn more about the material.

“This material was acquired from a Hidden Domain,” Feng Yuxiang said.

“A Hidden Domain! Is it still available?!”

“I’m sorry, but I cannot say more. Moreover, I am not the owner of this material, and I am merely helping the owner sell it.”

“How much for this material?”

“This is a one-of-a-kind material, so I cannot sell it immediately. However, you’re more than welcome to leave an offer for the owner.”

“In that case, I will be back later with some experts,” said the customer.

“As you wish.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1805 One-Of-A-Kind Material
chapters.body:
Chapter 1805 One-Of-A-Kind Material

Once the customer left, Xi Meili asked, “Should we really be selling this material when we don’t know its worth?”

Feng Yuxiang showed a cunning smile after hearing this question before responding, “Of course not. This is just there to attract customers. That said, if the offer is enticing enough, I will consider selling it for real.”

“That’s… genius!” Xi Meili couldn’t help but look at Feng Yuxiang in a new light.

A few hours later, the customer returned with several experts, most of them being blacksmiths.

“Heavens! What a mythical material! I have never seen anything like this before!”

“I can sense a very chaotic but powerful energy residing within the material! This can easily become the core material of a Celestial-grade treasure!”

“Madam, may we hold the material and inspect it in more detail?” one of the blacksmiths asked.

Feng Yuxiang shook her head and said, “I’m sorry, but by the owner’s orders, I am not allowed to do so.”

“Then, can you tell us the identity of the owner? We’d like to speak with him!”

“The owner of the material wanted me to sell it for them for a reason. They do not wish for their identity to be disclosed.”

The blacksmiths quickly gave up after hearing this, and they began debating about their offer for the material.

“As the core material for a Celestial-grade treasure, this material is worth at least a billion spirit stones. Its rarity as a one-of-a-kind material increases its value. However, since its properties are unknown, it’s extremely hard to put a price on it.”

“Moreover, we cannot examine the treasure, so we won’t know its true value until we get our hands on it. This is basically one huge gamble.”

After pondering for many minutes, the customer eventually gave Feng Yuxiang an offer, “I’d like to offer three billion spirit stones.”

Feng Yuxiang nodded and proceeded to place a sign above the display case that read, “Current offer—3 billion spirit stones.”

“If someone outbids you, I will change the price. If nobody outbids you in a month, I will contact the owner to see if he’d like to take your offer.”

The customer nodded, “I’ll come back every few days to check on the offer.”

The customer left shortly after, but the blacksmiths lingered in the store to examine the material a little longer.

Soon, news of a one-of-a-kind material being sold spread throughout the city before quickly spreading to the rest of the Sixth Heaven.

Blacksmiths and collectors swiftly gathered at Feng Yuxiang’s store when they heard about it.

“Wow! What a beautiful and mysterious treasure! I cannot imagine what kind of environment could breed this kind of material!”

“I really want to touch it…”

Dozens of people crowded around the display case, their eyes sparkling with excitement, much like kids at an amusement park. Meanwhile, the highest offer for the material had reached an eye-watering 7 billion spirit stones.

Eventually, people from the upper heavens started showing up at the store. Searᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I am from the Sacred Furnace, and I would like to speak with the owner of this material!” A middle-aged man approached Feng Yuxiang and spoke in a demanding tone.

“The owner does not wish to have their identity disclosed,” Feng Yuxiang declined for the 10th time that day.

“Do you know who we—the Sacred Furnace—are?!” The middle-aged man did not give up.

“I don’t know, nor do I care who you are. I have a reputation to uphold, and I am not going to destroy it for you.” Feng Yuxiang calmly responded.

“I am from the upper heavens! You’re just a mere store manager!”

Feng Yuxiang sneered in disdain, “So what if you’re from the upper heavens? Do you think you can get whatever you want if you come from there? I also happen to be from the upper heavens, and I demand you leave my store before I have someone escort you out.”

The middle-aged man trembled in anger, his face flushed with embarrassment after being called out.

“You think you can force me to leave? Let me see who dares to escort me out!” The man released some pressure from his cultivation.

However, that pressure was quickly canceled out by someone else.

“I see the Sacred Furnace is being as unsightly as usual,” another voice resounded.

“Which bastard dares to mock us?!”

The middle-aged man turned around to see an old man approaching him.

“Y-you’re from the Steel Sovereigns!” The middle-aged man’s eyes widened after recognizing the old man’s uniform.

The Steel Sovereigns was a top faction created in the Ninth Heaven with many Divine Blacksmiths amongst their ranks. In terms of reputation and prestige, they were above the Sacred Furnace.

“How can you call yourself a blacksmith when you act so rudely before such a divine material? Get out of here before I drag you out myself,” the old man continued with a cold expression.

The man from Divine Furnace knew he couldn’t offend the old man, so he could only swallow this humiliation and leave the store.

Once peace returned to the store, the old man acted as if nothing had happened and approached the display case with the material, his eyes filled with awe and interest.

“Only seven billion spirit stones? What a joke! Inexperienced brats!” the old man scoffed, his voice booming with confidence. “Fifty billion—I will offer fifty billion for this material!”

Gasps rippled through the crowd, their astonishment palpable at the staggering leap in price.

Yet, Feng Yuxiang remained unfazed by the offer. With a serene smile, she quietly removed the old sign and replaced it with the updated offer.

The old man lingered in the store for a few more minutes, his gaze fixed on the material as though memorizing its every detail before finally departing.

In the days that followed, similar scenes unfolded repeatedly. One by one, the treasures Yuan had entrusted to Feng Yuxiang found eager buyers until only a handful of the priciest items and the one-of-a-kind material remained.

chapters.title: Chapter 1806 One-Of-A-Kind Material(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1806 One-Of-A-Kind Material(2)

Nearly a month had passed since Feng Yuxiang had opened her store. During this time, tens of thousands of experts, including many from the upper heavens, had visited to marvel at the treasures on display. Yet, only a select few dared to make an offer for the one-of-a-kind material. The reason was simple: the price had soared to a staggering 75 billion spirit stones.

This astronomical sum could easily buy an entire Celestial-grade treasure—or even two—making it seem almost absurd for a single piece of material. However, its unparalleled rarity and enigmatic origins made it a prized target for collectors, who were willing to pay a premium for the chance to own it.

When Yuan heard about the offers, he was elated. In his eyes, the material wasn’t worth more than ten billion spirit stones at most. Yet, to his astonishment, it had fetched over seven times that amount.

In the end, the material was sold for a staggering 81 billion spirit stones, and the one who won the bid was the old man from Steel Sovereigns.

“Congratulations, the owner of the material has agreed to sell it to you,” Feng Yuxiang announced with a graceful smile after receiving Yuan’s approval.

The old man’s face lit up with elation, knowing he had secured the prized material. Yet, beneath his joy, a twinge of pain lingered in his heart as he handed over the spirit stones. He was fully aware that he had overpaid, but to him, the material’s rarity and potential were worth the sacrifice. With a deep breath, he accepted the bittersweet victory.

Feng Yuxiang handed the material to the old man after confirming the payment.

“Oh! Finally! I’ve been dreaming of this moment since I first laid eyes on it!” the old man muttered, his voice quivering with emotion. His hands trembled as he held the material, running his fingers over its surface, savoring the texture as if trying to etch every sensation into memory. seaʀᴄh thё ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I’m glad to see you’re pleased with your purchase,” Feng Yuxiang said with a warm smile. Then, her voice took on a more commanding tone as she added, “Now, I have another announcement…”

Her words immediately captured the attention of everyone in the store. The shop was packed tighter than ever, bustling with onlookers eager to witness the winner of the auction. Many hoped for just a closer glimpse of the material, and a few even harbored dreams of touching it. The crowd fell silent, their anticipation palpable as they waited to hear what Feng Yuxiang would reveal next.

Then, to the utter shock and disbelief of everyone present, Feng Yuxiang calmly reached into her spatial ring and retrieved another piece of material, its mysterious aura captivating the room instantly.

“The owner of the previous material,” she began with a cunning smile, “has decided to sell yet another one-of-a-kind treasure!”

“What?! There’s another one?!”

The crowd erupted into a frenzy, their astonishment visible. Murmurs of disbelief rippled through the store as countless pairs of eyes locked onto the new material.

The old man who’d just purchased the last material stood in place with a look of bewilderment on his face.

“Another…one?” he mumbled.

Feng Yuxiang nodded and said, “Yes, this material was found alongside the last one. For this material, we’ll start the offer at 70 billion spirit stones.”

After putting the material into the display case, Feng Yuxiang stepped back to allow the crowd to examine it more closely.

“Although the material is entirely different, it has the same chaotic energy, so it was definitely found within the same place.”

“The energy it’s emitting is even stronger than the last one!”

After closely examining the new material, the old man from Steel Sovereign clenched his jaw, his expression a mixture of frustration and resolve. Without a word, he turned and walked out of the store, his steps heavy yet purposeful. It was clear to everyone watching that this wasn’t the last they’d see of him. With an entire month before the material was set to be sold, it was only a matter of time before he returned.

Over the next two weeks, the bidding war for the second material intensified, with offers steadily climbing. By the end of the third week, the price had reached an astonishing 80 billion spirit stones, nearly matching the record-breaking sum fetched by the previous material.

At the start of the final week, the old man from the Steel Sovereigns returned, this time accompanied by a strikingly handsome young man whose presence commanded attention.

The young man approached the material with a calm yet intense demeanor, his sharp eyes scrutinizing every inch of its mysterious surface. For three days, he examined it without pause, unmoving as if in a trance. Finally, on the fourth day, he broke his silence.

“I’ll offer 95 billion spirit stones for this material,” he declared, his voice steady and filled with conviction.

The moment those words left his lips, a wave of astonished gasps swept through the store.

“Heavens! Nearly 100 billion spirit stones for a single material!”

“If this material were sold in the Ninth Heaven, it’d probably fetch for far more.”

Most of the astonished reactions came from individuals of the Sixth Heaven, for whom such an astronomical price was nearly unfathomable. On the other hand, those from the upper heavens remained relatively composed. They had witnessed treasures sold for even greater sums in their realms and regarded the 95 billion spirit stone offer as impressive but not unprecedented.

“Who is that young man, anyway?”

“He’s the Steel Sovereigns’ leader’s son. He’s probably buying that material for his father, who is currently collecting rare treasures for his life-long masterpiece.”

In the end, the young man won the material for 95 billion spirit stones.

After selling the material, Feng Yuxiang closed the shop and returned to the Heavenly Beast Sect with Xi Meili.

“Young Master, guess how much money we’ve earned!” Feng Yuxiang approached him with a prideful smile.

“200 billion spirit stones?” he guessed.

“Close! We earned a grand total of 207 billion spirit stones!” she declared.

chapters.title: Chapter 1807 Spirit Jade
chapters.body:
Chapter 1807 Spirit Jade

“207 billion spirit stones, huh? And to think I was sweating over a few thousand gold coins just a few years ago,” Yuan chuckled after hearing the full amount of their sales. “Most of the earnings came from the material you acquired from the Primal Expanse. Do you intend on selling more of them?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“No-at least not at this moment. I’ll think about it once we need money again.”

“Any news on the Hidden Domain’s opening?” Xi Meili asked a moment later.

“The Sect Leader said that he’d be making an announcement about it soon-probably within this week.”

“Finally. I cannot wait to experience a Hidden Domain for the first time,” Xi Meili said with an eager smile.

Feng Yuxiang looked at her and said, “You know, the Ancient Dragon City is also considered a Hidden Domain.”

“What? Really?” Xi Meili’s eyes widened at this information. This was her first time hearing about it.

“Probably. While it might not be the exact same, it’s similar. After all, it’s not a place meant to be discovered, and it was hidden for a reason. Otherwise, you’d be receiving new visitors every day.”

“That makes sense…” Xi Meili muttered. “By the way, where’s Yingying?”

“She’s at the usual place-taking lectures from the Heavenly Beast Sect,” Yuan said.

The desire to understand the bond between humans and contracted beasts had driven Lan Yingying to spend the past two months at the Heavenly Beast Sect as a guest disciple. Just like an ordinary disciple, she attended lectures by the sect elders, joined training exercises, and even sparred with the disciples.

“Is she doing this alone?” Xi Meili asked.

“At first, yes,” Yuan replied, “but for the past month, I’ve been accompanying her. She is my contracted beast, after all, and it’s only natural for me to stand by her side and support her. As a matter of fact, we have a sparring match with one of the sect elders scheduled in a few hours.”

“Oh? You’re going to spar alongside Yingying? Now this is something I must watch!” Feng Yuxiang commented.

A few hours later, Lan Yingying returned and said, “Yuan, are you ready for the match? It begins in two hours.”

“Yes, I am ready whenever you are.” Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Okay”

Lan Yingying noticed Feng Yuxiang and Xi Meili had returned and asked them a moment later, “Welcome back, you two. How is the store?”

“We sold pretty much everything and already closed the store down. The Hidden Domain will be opening soon, anyway,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“We earned 207 billion spirit stones!” Xi Meili proudly declared.

“207 billion?” Lan Yingying’s eyes widened at this astronomical number.

“It may sound like a lot, but it’s not much-at least compared to Spirit Jade, which is considered true cultivator currency in the upper heavens,” Feng Yuxiang said.

“Speaking of Spirit Jade, how much do you think this is worth?” Yuan retrieved the Spirit Jade given to him by Xu Jiaqi when he first started Cultivation Online.

Feng Yuxiang’s eyes widened with shock after seeing it, and her heart almost jumped out of her chest.

“W-where in heaven’s name did you acquire such a Spirit Jade?!”

“It was given to me by Xu Jiaqi.”

“That Celestial Overlord? Why would she give you such a treasure?”

This was Feng Yuxiang’s first glimpse of the Spirit Jade, as Xiao Hua had been carefully safeguarding it, knowing it was not something to be revealed lightly.

“She wanted me to join the Celestial Overlords, and she gave me this in exchange.” “So she basically purchased you?” Feng Yuxiang showed a slight smile. “Still, I cannot believe she’d be so generous. This Spirit Jade is Ancient-grade and contains enough spiritual energy to instantly turn a mortal into an Immortal. As for its value… Honestly, even I don’t know. I have dealt with Spirit Jades before, but nothing of this quality and quantity. You can probably purchase the entire Lower Heavens with a third of this Spirit Jade.”

“Purchasing the Lower Heavens? Is that even possible?” Yuan asked with intrigue.

“Probably, but you’d have to speak with the Celestial Emperor, who technically owns the Nine Heavens.”

Yuan looked at the Spirit Jade in silence for a moment before speaking, “Honestly, it’d be a waste to use this as a currency. According to Xiao Hua, it can also be used to awaken or evolve bloodlines. Maybe I can use it for a similar purpose.”

Feng Yuxiang nodded in agreement. “Aside from being a form of currency, most immortal cultivators use Spirit Jade to enhance their Immortal Qi and Celestial Qi, much like how mortal cultivators rely on spirit stones to advance their cultivation. However, Spirit Jade is far more potent. Its unparalleled purity also makes it significantly easier to absorb-like swimming in clear water compared to wading through mud.”

Sometime later, Yuan and Lan Yingying departed to engage in their scheduled sparring match, with Feng Yuxiang and Xi Meili naturally accompanying them.

When they arrived at the arena, they were met by Hong Ling, who had been assigned as the referee for their match.

“It’s been a while, Senior Feng, Senior Xi,” Hong Ling greeted them with a respectful bow.

“Looks like we’ll be bothering you again,” Xi Meili smiled.

“It’s no bother at all. In fact, I was the one who requested to be here. I have always wondered what kind of master you contracted with. There’s no way I’m missing this

chance.”

Although Yuan had previously accompanied Lan Yingying to sparring sessions, this was the first time they would be fighting together in an actual match. “Hello, I am Elder Qian, and I will be your sparring partner today,” a middle-aged woman introduced herself moments later, her contracted beast standing calmly behind her.

After returning her greeting, Yuan turned his attention to the beast behind Elder Qian. It was an enormous bird with sleek, metallic black feathers that shimmered like armor, and its wings glinted with a razor-sharp edge, resembling finely honed swords.

“This is my beast, a Midnight. Armored Talon,” Elder Qian introduced her beast.

chapters.title: Chapter 1808 Fighting Like A Beast Tamer
chapters.body:
Chapter 1808 Fighting Like A Beast Tamer

“This is my partner, Yingying!” Yuan introduced Lan Yingying not as his beast but as his partner.

This little detail made Hong Ling smile.

Lan Yingying continued, “I am a Divine Beast-a Divine Serpent. Thank you for giving us this opportunity.”

Elder Qian shook her head and said, “No, I should be thanking you for this

opportunity. We have never fought a Divine Beast before. However, I am a little worried about your cultivation…”

Yuan was only at the first level of Spirit Enlightenment, and Lan Yingying’s cultivation wasn’t much better, being only at the ninth level of Spirit Enlightenment. Meanwhile, Elder Qian’s cultivation was at the peak of Divine Emperor, and her beast was at the third level of Divine Sovereign.

“Don’t worry, we’re stronger than we appear,” Yuan said reassuringly, but it didn’t clear any doubt at all.

‘No matter how talented you are, there’s no way you can overcome the difference of seven entire realms…’ Not wanting to appear rude, Elder Qian shook her head inwardly.

Sometime later, Yuan and Elder Qian distanced themselves from the arena.

“Are you going to fight in your human form?” Yuan asked Lan Yingying.

She nodded and said, “For now, at least. I want to see my limit in my human form. Also, can you allow me to fight alone for a bit?”

“Of course. Just let me know when you need assistance”

The match began shortly after, and Elder Qian allowed Lan Yingying to have the first

move.

“You can go first.” Elder Qian declared. Sёarᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Then I won’t be humble.”

Lan Yingying responded swiftly, preparing her first attack. As she gathered her spiritual energy, a striking transformation began-her flowing black hair shifted to a radiant white, and her azure eyes shimmered as they turned a vivid pink.

“Fiery Lotus!”

With a graceful motion, she unleashed one of the first martial techniques she had ever mastered. A dozen brilliant flaming lotuses bloomed into existence, their fiery petals swirling elegantly in the sky, radiating both beauty and destruction.

When Lan Yingying first learned this technique, she could barely summon two lotuses before exhausting her spiritual energy. Now, she could manage a dozen without breaking a sweat.

The flaming lotuses erupted a moment later, exploding like a cascade of fireworks and engulfing the entire arena in mesmerizing white flames.

Despite the gap in their cultivation levels, the Midnight Armored Talon could feel the intense heat emanating from the white flames. The searing energy pierced through its metallic armor, leaving it visibly unsettled.

However, that was the extent of the damage. Apart from a slight discomfort from the heat, the Midnight Armored Talon remained unharmed.

Once Lan Yingying used her free attack, Elder Qian wasted no time commanding her beast. The Midnight Armored Talon soared into the air. Once airborne, it began fanning its massive wings, sending razor-sharp wind blades hurtling toward Lan Yingying with incredible force.

Fearing the possibility of accidentally harming Lan Yingying, Elder Qian instructed the Midnight Armored Talon to hold back its full strength. However, when the dust settled and Lan Yingying’s figure emerged, Elder Qian was taken aback.

Lan Yingying stood there nearly unscathed, with only a few scratches on her arms. However, even those minor wounds vanished in the blink of an eye.

“I appreciate you holding back for me, but you really don’t need to.” Lan Yingying said as she made her next move.

“Sacred Fire!”

Lan Yingying manifested a massive ball of Sacred Fire, its intense heat distorting the air around it. With a swift motion, she molded it into the shape of a spear and hurled it toward the flying beast. The flaming spear tore through the sky like a blazing comet, leaving a trail of scorching heat in its wake as it surged toward its target.

The Midnight Armored Talon instinctively tried to evade the incoming spear. However, it quickly realized that the spear was no ordinary attack-it adjusted its trajectory mid-flight, seemingly locked onto the beast with an uncanny homing capability.

Realizing it could not evade the attack, the Midnight Armored Talon resolved to confront it head-on. Channeling its full cultivation, the beast unleashed a thunderous cry, its power so immense that it created visible ripples in the air. When the flaming spear met the force of the ripple, the two clashed violently for a split second before the flaming spear was extinguished.

‘So this is my limit in my human form, huh?” Lan Yingying sighed inwardly.

In the very next moment, Lan Yingying decided to reveal her true form. With a surge of energy, she transformed into a stunning white snake, her scales gleaming with a radiant purity that captivated all who watched.

“So this is a Divine Serpent!” Elder Qian exclaimed, her voice trembling with a mix of excitement and awe as she took in the majesty of Lan Yingying’s true form. Her eyes sparkled with reverence, unable to look away from the radiant white serpent before her.

After transforming, Lan Yingying’s aura surged dramatically, radiating an overwhelming pressure. Upon closer inspection, her white scales shimmered faintly with Sacred Fire, adding a divine and imposing brilliance to her presence.

In the next moment, Lan Yingying leaped into the air, soaring toward the Midnight Armored Talon with newfound ferocity. Her Sacred Flames, now amplified several times by her transformation, burned brighter and hotter than ever. However, despite the increase in power, the gap in their cultivation levels remained insurmountable, leaving her attacks unable to fully penetrate the beast’s defenses.

Before long, Lan Yingying’s pristine white body was marred by streaks of her own blood, her scales torn and glistening with large, sword-like wounds.

Yuan watched the entire fight with a calm expression on his face, seemingly unaffected by how badly Lan Yingying was losing.

“Are you ready for my assistance?” Yuan asked her through voice transmission.

After a brief pause, Lan Yingying nodded silently.

Yuan smiled in acknowledgment, and without hesitation, he began channeling his spiritual energy into her body. Her wounds closed almost instantly, and her aura intensified even more.

chapters.title: Chapter 1809 Immortal Monarch'S True Prowess
chapters.body:
Chapter 1809 Immortal Monarch'S True Prowess

“So you’re finally relying on your master…” Elder Qian smiled after seeing Yuan share his spiritual energy with Lan Yingying. “Unfortunately, it won’t help you defeat my Nightmare Armored Talon.”

At the end of the day, Yuan’s cultivation remained weaker than Lan Yingying’s, and even with his support, Lan Yingying lacked the extraordinary talent required to bridge the gap of seven major cultivation realms. After all, Yuan’s assistance was limited to sharing his spiritual energy, replenishing her depleted reserves, and healing her injuries. It provided much-needed sustenance, but it wasn’t enough to significantly enhance her overall strength or tip the scales in her favor.

Yuan agreed with Elder Qian, but he remained silent, allowing Lan Yingying to fight as she pleased.

With Yuan’s near-limitless spiritual energy sustaining her, Lan Yingying wielded her martial techniques without worrying about exhaustion, pushing them to their full potential and relentlessly unleashing them in rapid succession, essentially spamming her attacks.

Though her individual strikes lacked the power to inflict significant damage on their own, the overwhelming volume and ceaseless barrage left the Midnight Armored Talon visibly dumbfounded.

‘Heavens! Just where is she getting this much spiritual energy?! There’s no way her master has this much spiritual energy at the first level of Spirit Enlightenment!’ Elder Qian was shocked when she realized Lan Yingying was not exhausting her spiritual energy despite the spam.

She looked at Yuan, who was confidently and calmly sharing his spiritual energy with Lan Yingying, looking like he was just a spectator.

‘Let’s see how long you’ll be able to keep this up!’

Elder Qian commanded her beast to focus entirely on defense. With the vast disparity in their cultivation levels, the Midnight Armored Talon could afford to endure Lan Yingying’s relentless attacks for hours, sustaining only minimal damage at most.

For the next hour, Lan Yingying unleashed an endless barrage of attacks trying to break the Nightmare Armored Talon’s defenses, her Sacred Fire consuming the entire arena. The once orderly battleground was transformed into an infernal landscape, resembling the Nine Levels of Hell in the Eternal Phoenix Domain.

However, despite her endless assault, the Midnight Armored Talon’s defenses remained steadfast, showing no signs of yielding.
