Eventually, Lan Yingying recognized the futility of her efforts and ceased her attacks.

‘Looks like she’s finally out of spiritual energy… but heavens… what a monstrous amount of spiritual energy her master has!’ Elder Qian thought to herself, her back soaked in cold sweat as she marveled at the Yuan’s frightening amount of spiritual energy.

“I guess this is it,” Elder Qian said, a hint of admiration in her tone. “Although you weren’t able to defeat my Midnight Armored Talon, your potential is undeniable. I can’t begin to imagine how terrifyingly powerful you’ll be once your cultivation improves further.” She spoke with confidence, already declaring her victory.

However, Yuan responded in a slow but confident tone, “This is far from over, Elder Qian.”

Elder Qian frowned and said, “You should know when to give up. Even if you have unlimited spiritual energy, your beast lacks the prowess to break through my beast’s defenses. Also, we’ve only used half of our strength.”

Yuan did not respond to Elder Qian. Instead, he asked Lan Yingying, “What do you think?”

Lan Yingying lowered her head and sighed in a defeated tone, “Elder Qian is correct. No matter how much spiritual energy you share with me, I do not possess the same talent as you. I don’t have what it takes to defeat them in my current state.”

Yuan nodded and responded, “Under normal circumstances, you are correct. No matter how much spiritual energy I share with you, it won’t increase your talents or give you supernatural strength. However, have you forgotten who your master—I am?”

Lan Yingying raised an eyebrow at Yuan’s words, her expression reflecting a mix of confusion and curiosity, as though she struggled to fully grasp the meaning behind the statement.

Yuan chuckled and continued speaking to her through voice transmission, “I am not your ordinary Beast Tamer—I am the Immortal Monarch!”

“Let me show you what it means to be the Immortal Monarch’s contracted beast—the Immortal Monarch’s true prowess!”

After recovering the majority of the Immortal Monarch’s memories, Yuan also inherited the entirety of his beast-taming techniques.

“However,” Yuan continued, his tone calm but firm, “before I can use the Immortal Monarch’s powers on you, you must first accept the Immortal Monarch’s seal. It’s a unique bond, distinct from our existing contract, and it allows me to have complete control over your life. If I want you to die, I can do so with a thought. Also, the seal can only be removed with my death.”

“Even knowing that, are you willing to accept my seal, Yingying?”

Without hesitation, Lan Yingying nodded.

“I accept!” Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

After receiving Lan Yingying’s approval, Yuan began to draw a complex symbol in the air with precise, fluid motions. Though it bore a superficial resemblance to Array Symbols, its essence was entirely different. This symbol radiated an otherworldly energy, its power stemming directly from Yuan’s soul, pulsating with a profound and ancient aura.

“W-what are you doing?” Elder Qian muttered in a daze.

Meanwhile, Hong Ling. Feng Yuxiang, Xi Meili, and every beast in the Heavenly Beast Sect could sense the profound aura emanating from the symbol. The energy it radiated was so overwhelming and majestic that it stirred something primal within them, compelling a powerful and natural urge to bow in reverence, as if they were standing in the presence of a divine force.

<You have learned Immortal Monarch’s Supreme Seal>

Once Yuan completed the seal, he made a graceful gesture, sending it soaring toward Lan Yingying. The seal entered her body effortlessly, merging with her very being. Moments later, the words “Immortal Monarch” manifested on her skin, forming an elegant symbol that radiated a subtle yet profound aura.

<Lan Yingying has accepted your Supreme Seal>

<Due to the Supreme Seal, Lan Yingying has been granted the title Divine Supreme>

After accepting the Supreme Seal, Lan Yingying felt an unfamiliar sensation coursing through her body, as though something profound had awakened within her. However, the nature of this change eluded her for now.

chapters.title: Chapter 1810 Immortal Monarch’S Supreme Seal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1810 Immortal Monarch’S Supreme Seal

[Immortal Monarch’s Supreme Seal]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: Max]

[Description: Bestow a Supreme Seal onto a beast. When a beast accepts your seal, you will have complete control over their life, and in exchange, they will acquire a portion of your overall stats and talents. You can only have nine Supreme Seals active.]

<Lan Yingying’s status has been updated>

[Name: Lan Yingying]

[Servant Grade: Ancient] -> [Servant Grade: Mythic]

[Master: Yuan]

[Cultivation: Ninth Level Spirit Enlightenment]

[Legacy: None]

[Bloodline: Sacred Serpent Bloodline]

[Physique: World Eater Physique]

[Title: Divine Supreme]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Description: The bearer of this title will automatically gain a portion of all spiritual energy absorbed by their Master. The bearer of this title will acquire 25% of their Master’s overall talents and stats(effect increases as the servant’s bond with the master deepens).]

The Supreme Seal bestowed a unique blessing upon the Divine Supremes, enabling them to grow alongside Yuan. This bond ensured that, despite Yuan’s monstrous talents and rapid advancement, they would not lag too far behind, maintaining their relevance and strength as his companions.

However, this came at a cost—Yuan’s cultivation progress would be slowed down even further. Yet, he was fully aware of this drawback and willingly accepted it, prioritizing Lan Yingying’s growth.

Fortunately, the Supreme Seal had no negative impact on Yuan’s overall prowess. While the Divine Supremes inherited 25 percent of his strength and talents through the seal, this did not mean Yuan himself became 25 percent weaker. Instead, the seal functioned as a conduit, allowing them to draw upon his power directly.

“Yingying, go ahead and try fighting again,” Yuan said to her.

She nodded and turned to look at Elder Qian.

“Let’s continue the fight,” she said.

Elder Qian showed a stiff smile and responded, “I don’t know what just happened, but if you think it will help you defeat—”

In the midst of Elder Qian’s speech, the Supreme Seal on Lan Yingying suddenly activated without warning. Her aura surged dramatically, radiating an overwhelming presence that filled the arena. The intensity of her presence was so imposing that even the Midnight Armored Talon, previously unfazed, instinctively recoiled, feeling a genuine sense of threat for the first time.

“Fine,” Elder Qian said a moment later. “If you wish to continue, we can do that. However, this time, we won’t hold back anymore.”

The Midnight Armored Talon let out an eager, piercing cry, as if shaking off its earlier fear. Its aura erupted like a storm, surging to its peak as it fully unleashed its cultivation base, preparing to confront Lan Yingying with all its might.

Lan Yingying leaped gracefully into the air, her movements swift and fluid, before soaring toward the Midnight Armored Talon. The two clashed mid-sky, their battle unleashing shockwaves of energy as they exchanged ferocious blows at each other.

“Impossible! She’s actually fighting on par with my beast?!” Elder Qian exclaimed, her eyes wide with disbelief. Sёarch* The NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Despite the surge in her aura and presence, Lan Yingying’s cultivation level hadn’t changed; it remained at the peak of Spirit Enlightenment. Yet, she was somehow able to hold her ground against the Midnight Armored Talon, a beast operating at the full power of the seventh level of Divine Sovereign.

Moreover, Lan Yingying’s prowess seemed to intensify with each passing moment, her attacks becoming more powerful and deadly. The sight defied logic, leaving Elder Qian struggling to comprehend the reality unfolding before her.

‘What is this overwhelming power?’ Lan Yingying thought, feeling just as astonished as Elder Qian, if not even more so.

‘I see… this is Yuan’s power,’ Lan Yingying quickly realized. ‘And I can feel it growing stronger as my body adapts to it…’

As Yuan’s energy coursed through her entire being, it felt warm and reassuring, almost like she was being embraced by him, and she couldn’t help but feel a profound sense of unity with him.

<Your bond with Lan Yingying has deepened>

<The strength of Lan Yingying’s Supreme Seal has increased to 35%>

The moment the Supreme Seal strengthened, Lan Yingying’s aura surged to an even greater height, and to everyone’s astonishment, a pair of magnificent wings composed entirely of white flames emerged from her back, their ethereal glow illuminating the battlefield and exuding a divine presence that left onlookers breathless.

“Amazing…” Xi Meili muttered unconsciously, her eyes wide with wonder as she felt her breath stolen by the sheer beauty and power of Lan Yingying’s transformation.

After her transformation, Lan Yingying’s attacks grew exponentially more powerful. The Midnight Armored Talon, once an impenetrable fortress, began to falter under the relentless onslaught. Its formidable defenses, which had previously withstood everything she threw at it, could no longer hold against the might of her Sacred Fire. Eventually, Lan Yingying started to push the beast back.

Seeing her beast being overwhelmed, Elder Qian quickly began transferring her spiritual energy to the Midnight Armored Talon. While this replenished some of its depleted reserves, it was only a temporary measure. Against Yuan’s seemingly endless well of spiritual energy, Elder Qian’s efforts were no match. Her spiritual energy could only sustain the Midnight Armored Talon for an additional twenty minutes before she, too, began to run dry, leaving her beast vulnerable once more.

Once her spiritual energy had been exhausted, the Midnight Armored Talon could no longer defend against Lan Yingying’s Sacred Fire and became engulfed in it.

Seeing this, Elder Qian clenched her fists and shouted, “Enough! I admit defeat!”

Hearing her opponent surrender, Lan Yingying promptly extinguished her Sacred Fire and reverted to her human form.

With graceful steps, she approached the Midnight Armored Talon, now resting by Elder Qian’s side, its aura dim but steady.

Bowing deeply to both Elder Qian and her beast, Lan Yingying spoke with sincerity, “Thank you very much for the match. I will never forget this experience.”

Elder Qian showed a bittersweet smile, “I will also never forget this experience.”

Lan Yingying returned to Yuan’s side a moment later, her face glowing with a bright, triumphant smile. Without hesitation, she dropped to one knee and bowed deeply before him.

“Thank you for bestowing this power upon me,” she said through voice transmission, her voice filled with unwavering gratitude and devotion. “Allow me to swear my loyalty to you once again, my Lord.”

Yuan smiled and said, “You haven’t called me Lord in a while. It feels weird, so just keep calling me Yuan.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1811 Immortal Monarch'S Supreme Seal(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1811 Immortal Monarch'S Supreme Seal(2)

Shortly after the match ended, Hong Ling entered the arena and said to Lan Yingying, “Honestly, I didn’t expect you to win this match.”

“I wouldn’t have won without my Young Master’s assistance,” she said.

Hong Ling’s eyes shifted to Yuan, her gaze filled with interest, even showing a hint of envy and longing.

“I’m afraid to ask, but I won’t be able to sleep if I don’t. What kind of technique did you use at the end? Of course, you can refuse to answer.”

Yuan smiled faintly behind his mask and replied, “It’s a secret technique that has been passed down through generations in my family.”

“A family technique, huh? In that case, I won’t inquire further,” Hong Ling said with a faint hint of disappointment in her voice. Though curious, she knew her boundaries.

“Anyway, before you leave, the Sect Leader said that he will be announcing the Hidden Domain’s opening in three days,” she continued a moment later.

“Okay.”

Sometime later, Yuan and the others returned to their living quarters.

“Young Master! What happened during the match?! What did you do to Yingying?!” Feng Yuxiang questioned the moment they returned. “I have never seen or heard of anything like that before!”

“That was just some of the Immortal Monarch’s techniques. I gave Yingying the Immortal Monarch’s Supreme Seal, making her one of the Divine Supremes. In exchange for her loyalty, she will be able to use some of my strength as if it were her own.”

“What?! One of the Divine Supremes?! What about me?! I was your first servant—excluding Xiao Hua since she is human!” Feng Yuxiang was on the verge of tears after realizing Lan Yingying had become his first Divine Supreme despite appearing after her.

“I’ve only remembered this technique recently, so there’s no way I could’ve given it to you. Moreover, to accept the Supreme Seal is to offer your life to me. If I wish for your life, I could take it with a single thought. It’s not something I can casually hand out.”

“I accept! Even if I have to offer my soul to you, please grant me your Supreme Seal and make me one of your Divine Supremes, Young Master!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed passionately, her voice trembling with desperation.

Her tone was so fervent that it resembled that of a beggar pleading—no, perhaps even a beggar wouldn’t sound as utterly desperate as she did at that moment, her pride cast aside entirely.

Seeing how desperate Feng Yuxiang was, Yuan had no choice but to agree. Of course, he would’ve still given Feng Yuxiang the Supreme Seal even if she didn’t beg him to do so.

“Here.”

Yuan created another Supreme Seal and offered it to Feng Yuxiang, who accepted it with open arms.

“Ah~! So this is the Young Master’s power! It’s flowing throughout my body!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed, her voice quivering with delight. “With this seal, I will always feel connected to you, as if you are with me even when we are apart!”

She trembled with joy, savoring the profound sensation coursing through her.

Xi Meili swallowed nervously after seeing Feng Yuxiang’s reaction. She shifted her gaze to Yuan, seemingly contemplating whether she should also ask for one. However, after some pondering, she decided to wait a little longer.

<Feng Yuxiang’s status has been updated>

[Name: Feng Yuxiang] S~eaʀᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

[Servant Grade: Divine] -> [Servant Grade: Mythic]

[Master: Yuan]

[Cultivation: Ninth Level Divine Warrior]

[Legacy: None]

[Bloodline: Ancient Phoenix Bloodline] -> [Bloodline: Primordial Phoenix Bloodline]

[Physique: Inextinguisable Immortal Fire]

In the last two months, Feng Yuxiang’s cultivation increased even further, reaching the peak of Divine Warrior.

After accepting Yuan’s Supreme Seal, the words “Immortal Monarch” manifested near Feng Yuxiang’s chest in the form of a symbol directly over her heart. For Lan Yingying, once she reverted to her human form, the symbol appeared on her stomach, slightly below her belly button.

The markings were subtle, barely visible under normal circumstances. However, they’d quickly become prominent when they channeled the powers bestowed by the seal.

“What was that energy just now?”

Shortly after Feng Yuxiang accepted the Supreme Seal, Yingzi appeared out of thin air and asked.

Yuan explained the Supreme Seal to her.

“I see. How interesting.”

Yuan smiled and said, “I also see that you’ve successfully cultivated the cultivation technique.”

In two short months, Yingzi successfully cultivated Shadow Extraction and created a Dantian, which allowed her to cultivate spiritual energy.

“Yes, I managed to create my Dantian yesterday,” she nodded.

“What? Yesterday? But you’re already at the peak of Spirit Lord!” Xi Meili exclaimed in a shocked tone.

“Such ridiculous progress is even faster than the Young Master… Of course, she has an advantage since she started cultivating in the Sixth Heaven, where the spiritual energy is much more powerful…” Feng Yuxiang muttered with a dumbfounded look on her face.

“Cultivating spiritual energy is much easier than cultivating Chaotic Essence,” Yingzi said calmly. “It’s so much purer, which makes it significantly easier to absorb and refine. If cultivating was this easy in the Primal Expanse, I would’ve long reached Chaotic Immortal.”

To reach Chaotic Immortal was akin to achieving at least Golden Immortal in the Nine Heavens. As for Yingzi, she was currently around Immortal Ascension with her Chaotic Heart.

“I guess we won’t have to worry about your sustainability in the Nine Heavens anymore.” Yuan chuckled.

“By the way, Young Master, does the Supreme Seals negatively affect you in any way?” Feng Yuxiang asked a moment later.

“You’re asking that after you accepted it?” Yuan joked. “Yes, it does. Since I automatically share some of the spiritual energy I absorb with the Divine Supremes, I will naturally cultivate slower.”

“H-how much slower?” she swallowed nervously.

“I don’t know, but we can test it out.”

Yuan retrieved a Divine Sovereign-level monster core he’d acquired from the Vanishing Valley and casually tossed it into his mouth.

After consuming the monster core, Yuan could feel his cultivation increase slightly. Feng Yuxiang felt a refreshing sensation, but her cultivation did not change in the slightest.

As for Lan Yingying, her cultivation soared all the way to the fourth level of Divine Warrior in the blink of an eye.

chapters.title: Chapter 1812 Heading To The Hidden Domain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1812 Heading To The Hidden Domain

“Huh? My cultivation…” Lan Yingying looked at her palms with a bewildered expression on her face.

Yuan raised an eyebrow at the puzzling results. If Lan Yingying only received a portion of the spiritual energy he absorbed, there was no way her cultivation would’ve increased so much.

“If your cultivation increased while mine barely moved, there’s only one explanation… The Supreme Seal completely ignored the Celestial Emperor’s curse!” Yuan exclaimed after coming to this realization.

The Celestial Emperor’s curse on Yuan directly reduced the effectiveness of all spiritual energy he absorbed by a staggering 99 percent. If the curse had worked properly, Lan Yingying would’ve only absorbed a portion of that one percent. Yet, she had a breakthrough while already having a higher cultivation than Yuan—multiple breakthroughs at that.

Wanting to confirm this theory, Yuan consumed another monster core. This time, the monster core belonged to a magical beast at the level of Divine Ancestor.

After consuming the monster core, Yuan’s cultivation made a breakthrough, entering the second level of Spirit Enlightenment. Meanwhile, Lan Yingying’s cultivation soared all the way to the first level of Divine Master, surpassing Feng Yuxiang and Xi Meili, who were still Divine Warriors.

“Heavens! This speed of cultivation is outrageous! It’s heaven-defying! It’s unfair!” Xi Meili exclaimed, her voice trembling with a mix of awe and terror. Compared to what Lan Yingying had just experienced, the thousands of years that she had painstakingly spent cultivating now felt utterly insignificant—pointless, even.

“I guess that confirms my suspicions. The Supreme Seal somehow bypasses the Celestial Emperor’s curse,” Yuan muttered in a daze.

“Well, aren’t you lucky, Yingying—” Feng Yuxiang began, turning toward her with a playful tone, but her words caught in her throat mid-sentence.

“Yingying? Are you okay?” Feng Yuxiang asked, her expression shifting to one of concern as she noticed Yingying’s pale complexion and unsteady stance.

Lan Yingying nodded slightly, her movements slow and unsteady. “I suddenly feel a little nauseous,” she admitted, her voice soft. “I think it’s because my cultivation increased so much, so quickly. My body’s struggling to adjust.”

“Oh, yeah, that’s actually quite normal.” Feng Yuxiang said.

“Really? I’ve never experienced anything like that,” Yuan casually remarked.

Feng Yuxiang shook her head with a slight smile. “How can you compare yourself to us, Young Master? There’s a reason most people take the time to adapt to their new cultivation after a breakthrough. If we rush cultivation like a madman, it would actually harm us instead.”

“What about Yingzi?” Xi Meili asked.

“Her body is already adapted since this isn’t her first time reaching that level.”

“That makes sense…”

“Sorry, Yingying. This is my fault.” Yuan apologized to her.

“No, it’s fine. I just need some time to adapt.”

After saying that, Lan Yingying sat on the ground and began to circulate her Dantian to speed up the process.

“Even though I can increase their cultivation rapidly through the Supreme Seal, I shouldn’t be too extreme or hasty,” Yuan muttered softly, almost as if he were reminding himself.

Over the next three days, Lan Yingying spent all of her time adjusting to her new cultivation. Meanwhile, Yingzi continued to increase her cultivation, reaching the peak of Spirit King.

After three days had passed, Sect Leader Bao made his long-awaited announcement through the communication jade slip he gave to everyone with a slot.

“The time has come,” he declared in a clear voice. “In precisely fourteen days from now, the Hidden Domain to the Immortal Monastery will be open. If you have a slot, please gather at the Hidden Domain with your entry token before then. I will send the location of the Hidden Domain to everyone through the communication jade slip shortly after this announcement.”

The announcement was short but straight to the point.

“Fourteen days, huh?” Yuan mumbled.

Shortly after the announcement ended, Yuan received the location of the Hidden Domain. Sёarᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“It’s quite far from here, so we should start making our way there as soon as possible,” Yuan said after memorizing the location.

“Okay.”

Feng Yuxiang and Lan Yingying entered his Dantian while Yingzi returned to his shadow. When Tan Songyun emerged from her room a few hours later, Yuan asked, “Do you know where to go?”

She nodded, “Are we leaving now?”

“We can.”

“Before we leave, I should warn you to be careful.” Tan Songyun suddenly said.

“Careful? Of what?” Yuan asked.

“There are only 20,000 slots, nowhere near enough to satisfy everyone. There’s a good chance some people will attempt to steal our slots while we make our way to the Hidden Domain,” she explained.

“Oh, I can see that happening. Don’t worry. Nobody will be able to steal from us.”

“Hmph,” she coldly snorted, “You say that now, but no matter how talented you are, you’ll be helpless once you encounter someone who can use Immortal Qi.”

Yuan didn’t comment on that remark and merely smiled in silence.

Sometime later, Yuan, Tan Songyun, and Xi Meili departed from the Heavenly Beast Sect, setting off on their journey to the Hidden Domain. As they made their way, they noticed a steady stream of others also leaving the sect and heading in the same direction.

To minimize the risk of encountering thieves, Yuan’s group decided to travel with the larger crowd until they reached the nearest city equipped with a teleportation device.

Yuan hoped to remain with the group until they reached the Hidden Domain, but after using the teleportation device to reach the city closest to the Hidden Domain, the group began to disperse. It became clear that the cultivators were purposefully going their separate ways, likely to avoid competition or unnecessary trouble as they approached the Hidden Domain.

“Everyone must be wary of each other since we don’t know who has a slot,” Tan Songyun said after seeing the group scatter.

“What should we do now?” Xi Meili asked.

“There’s nothing we can do except head to the Hidden Domain ourselves.” Yuan shrugged.

“That said, we shouldn’t head there immediately. Let’s wait a few more days before we do so.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1813 The Hidden Domain'S Opening
chapters.body:
Chapter 1813 The Hidden Domain'S Opening

“A few days? What are you going to do in the meantime?” Tan Songyun asked.

Yuan smiled and said, “Look around you—at this city. There are plenty of things to do. I haven’t had a good meal in a while, so I’ll be spending my time in restaurants.”

Tan Songyun raised an eyebrow and remarked, “Food? Cultivators don’t need to eat, and that stuff will only dirty your body with impurities.”

Most cultivators avoided eating food or even drinking water for precisely this reason. The impurities in ordinary meals—even spiritual food—were considered detrimental to their refined bodies and cultivation progress. Instead, they sustained themselves by absorbing spiritual energy, whether through diligent cultivation or by utilizing spirit stones, which provided a pure and efficient source of nourishment.

“Can you even call yourself human if you stop eating food entirely? So what if it contains a little impurity? You can easily cleanse it,” Yuan shook his head.

Tan Songyun fell silent after hearing his response.

Sometime later, they went to a nearby restaurant and filled their table with food, with Yingzi joining them at their feast. As for Tan Songyun, she merely cultivated in silence, not touching any of the food before her.

After finishing their meal at one restaurant, they moved on to another, indulging in a variety of dishes. This pattern continued over the next few days, turning their journey into an impromptu food tour, much to the bewilderment of Tan Songyun.

‘How can they eat this much?’ Tan Songyun wondered inwardly, her body trembling slightly at the mere thought of the sheer amount of impurities they must have consumed over the past few days.

Unbeknownst to Tan Songyun, Yuan possessed an Untainted Body, a physique that automatically cleansed any impurities he consumed, rendering them completely harmless.

Xi Meili, being a Divine Beast, also had a body that naturally purified impurities, though not as efficiently or rapidly as Yuan’s Untainted Body.

As for Yingzi, who has a Chaotic Heart, the situation was entirely different. Impurities, rather than being harmful, actually benefited her cultivation.

With just ten days remaining before the Hidden Domain’s opening, Yuan and his group decided to conclude their leisurely detour and resume their journey. “If nothing happens, we should arrive at the Hidden Domain in six days,” Tan Songyun said as she rode her flying treasure with Xi Meili.

“By the way, what are you doing?” Tan Songyun continued as she shifted her gaze to Yuan, who was using an ordinary sword as a flying sword.

“Are you talking about my flying sword? I prefer traveling like this,” he said.

“I don’t get it. It’s too inefficient,” she said.

“It may be, but it feels good. There’s just something about soaring through the sky while standing on a sword.”

Tan Songyun merely shook her head inwardly at his weird preferences.

After traveling for a day straight without encountering anyone, Tan Songyun suddenly warned Yuan through voice transmission, “In case you didn’t notice, we’re being followed.”

“I am aware,” he calmly responded.

Yuan had sensed their presence the moment they appeared, roughly ten minutes ago. The individuals trailing them were skilled, maintaining a distance just far enough to avoid detection by most cultivators at the Divine Master level.

However, they had severely underestimated Yuan’s abilities, and Tan Songyun had deliberately concealed her true cultivation, suppressing it to appear as though she was only at the Divine Warrior realm, making their group seem like an easy target.

“They’re probably trying to feel us out—to see whether we have a slot or not. What do you want to do? Kill them?” Tan Songyun asked.

“Why bother? As long as they don’t act, we can ignore them.”

“Are you afraid of them? Judging by their presence, they probably come from the upper heavens.”

Yuan chuckled openly, his voice carrying a hint of amusement. “Afraid? Why would I be afraid of them? I just find it a hassle, that’s all.”

“You…” Tan Songyun’s eyes widened in surprise as she realized what Yuan had done. He had deliberately made their pursuers aware that he knew of their presence, a move so bold it left her momentarily speechless.

Sure enough, a few moments later, the people trailing them abandoned all attempts of stealth. Their presence became overbearing as they swiftly closed the distance and positioned themselves to block Yuan and his group’s path.

The group consisted of two individuals: a middle-aged woman and a young man, both radiating the aura of Immortals. Their cultivation levels were at the peak of Divine Ancestor.

“How can we help you?” Yuan asked, his tone calm and nonchalant, accompanied by a serene smile that showed no hint of concern despite the overwhelming pressure exuded by the duo.

“We’ll get straight to the point. Give us your slots to the Hidden Domain, and we’ll spare your lives,” said the woman.

Yuan raised an eyebrow and responded with a puzzled face, “Slots to the Hidden Domain? What are you talking about?”

“Do you think we are stupid? We know you’re heading to the Hidden Domain! This is your last chance!” The woman suddenly exuded some of her Immortal Qi.

Xi Meili clenched her jaw after sensing the Immortal Qi, something she was not able to withstand in her current state.

Seeing this, Tan Songyun used some of her own Immortal Qi to protect her.

“Oh? You’re also an Immortal?” The middle-aged woman was somewhat surprised by Tan Songyun’s ability to use Immortal Qi. “It’s no wonder why you’re so calm.” Sёarᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Did you descend to a lower heaven just to rob the people here? Do you even know what shame means?” Yuan asked.

“You think we want to do this?” The young man finally spoke. “All of the slots for the Hidden Domain are taken, but we have to enter it no matter what.”

“And what makes you think we don’t need to enter the Hidden Domain?”

“Hmph. I don’t have the time or patience to entertain you,” the young man scoffed, his tone dripping with arrogance. He extended his hand toward Yuan’s group, his gaze cold and commanding. “Hand over your tokens now, or we’ll take them by force.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1814 The Hidden Domain'S Opening(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1814 The Hidden Domain'S Opening(2)

“Sorry, but that’s not happening,” Yuan calmly replied, a sneer of disdain spreading across his face. “If you want our tokens, you’ll have to take them from our corpses. However, I don’t think you have the capabilities to make that happen.”

“Then you can die!” the young man roared, his patience snapping. Immortal Qi erupted from his body in a brilliant surge, the air around him trembling under the sheer force of his power as he prepared to strike.

Seeing the young man’s aggressive move, Tan Songyun instinctively prepared to counter. However, before she could act, the middle-aged woman beside him intercepted.

“You can deal with me!” she shouted, her voice echoing with confidence as she unleashed a powerful blast of Immortal Qi toward Tan Songyun and Xi Meili.

Tan Songyun was forced to go into the defense because of Xi Meili, who would not be able to endure the Immortal Qi as a mortal.

Meanwhile, the young man launched his assault on Yuan, his Immortal Qi surging forward like a tidal wave. The overwhelming force of his attack distorted the air around it, crackling with destructive energy as it barreled toward Yuan with tyrannical intensity.

In response, Yuan calmly summoned his incomplete Immortal Qi. With a swift motion, he sliced through the young man’s Immortal Qi as if it were nothing more than a thin veil.

“What?!” the young man exclaimed, his voice filled with shock and disbelief. He stared wide-eyed at Yuan. “W-what did you do just now?!”

“Sorry, but I don’t have time to entertain you.”

Yuan calmly retrieved Baneful Abyss, its dark blade gleaming ominously in his hand. Activating True Dragon Transformation, his aura erupted with overwhelming force, surging to an intensity that momentarily stunned the young man, freezing him in place with sheer pressure and astonishment.

Seizing the opportunity, Yuan moved with lightning speed, closing the distance in an instant. With the Baneful Abyss in hand, he launched a swift and precise strike aimed directly at the young man, the blade cutting through the air with a menacing hum as it bore down on its target.

The young man instinctively reacted to the incoming attack, his body moving just in time to avoid a fatal blow to his neck. However, his evasive maneuver was not perfect, and the Baneful Abyss grazed his cheek, leaving a shallow but stinging cut.

Afterward, Yuan calmly put away Baneful Abyss, an action that immediately puzzled his opponent.

“What do you think you’re—” the young man began to speak, but his words were abruptly cut off.

His face contorted in excruciating pain as the Baneful Abyss’ poison began coursing through his body, melting him from the inside. The young man’s screams echoed as he staggered, clutching his body in desperation.

Young Master?!” The middle-aged woman abruptly stopped her clash with Tan Songyun, her attention snapping toward the young man as his screams of agony filled the air.

“Did nobody teach you to never look away in the middle of a battle?” Tan Songyun remarked coldly, a sharp edge in her tone.

Without hesitation, she seized the opportunity created by the woman’s distraction, unleashing a decisive and fatal strike. The attack caught the woman off-guard, sealing her fate. As for the young man, he also perished shortly after.

Tan Songyun glanced at the young man’s corpse and swallowed nervously. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

‘What kind of poison is powerful enough to kill an Immortal in seconds?’ she wondered inwardly.

Shortly afterward, Yuan searched their bodies and acquired their spatial rings.

“Radiant Sky Hall—have you heard of them?” Yuan asked as he examined the medallion he had retrieved from their spatial ring.

“Radiant Sky Hall… They’re a prominent sect within the Seventh Heaven.” Tan Songyun said with a slight frown on her face.

“Let me see that medallion.”

Yuan handed the medallion to her, and she examined it more intently.

A moment later, Tan Songyun rubbed her eyes and sighed, “That young man you killed was the Radiant Sky Hall’s Prime Disciple. He’s essentially their Sect Leader’s direct disciple.”

“So what? Are you saying I shouldn’t have killed him?”

“That would’ve definitely been better… but since you already killed him, there’s nothing we can do.”

“Even if I killed him, there are no witnesses, so there’s no way for the Radiant Sky Hall to know that I killed him,” Yuan said.

“Actually, it’s quite common for sects and families in the Upper Heavens to place trackers on their most valuable members,” Tan Songyun revealed, her tone serious. “If anything happens to them, the sect or family would know immediately. The more advanced and expensive trackers can even identify and locate the individuals responsible for their deaths. Though, they are extremely rare and expensive, so I doubt the Radiant Sky Hall would use that for a mere disciple.”

“Even if that’s the case, what’s done has been done. Also, they were in the wrong for trying to rob us in the first place. Unless the Radiant Sky Hall is shameless enough to come after us, who are the victims, we should be fine.”

Tan Songyun nodded in agreement.

“Sorry for being in your way, and thank you for protecting me just now.” Xi Meili said to Tan Songyun as they resumed their journey.

“Don’t worry about it. Mortals are no match for Immortals, anyway.”

Tan Songyun looked at Yuan as she spoke, pondering about the incomplete Immortal Qi he used during his fight.

‘Although it wasn’t true Immortal Qi, it was very similar, and it even had the capability to fight true Immortal Qi. To use such a power when he’s only at Spirit Enlightenment, what a monster…’ she sighed inwardly.

‘If he’s already this powerful at Spirit Enlightenment, I cannot imagine how powerful he’ll become once he becomes an Immortal.’

After traveling for several hours without any interruptions, Yuan and his group had to stop again when another group of individuals blocked them.

“You lot must be going to the Hidden Domain! Hand over your tokens, and we’ll spare your lives!” The leader of this group demanded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1815 The Hidden Domain'S Opening(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1815 The Hidden Domain'S Opening(3)

This new group of thieves was significantly larger than the previous one, numbering over a hundred individuals. However, despite their overwhelming numbers, their cultivation levels ranged only from Divine Warrior to Divine Lord.

“Young Master, let me deal with them for you! I also want to use this opportunity to test out the Supreme Seal!” Feng Yuxiang suddenly declared with an eager voice. Searᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan nodded without hesitation. “Sure. I’ll leave them to you.”

A moment later, Feng Yuxiang emerged from Yuan’s Dantian in a blaze of fiery elegance, and without offering the cultivators so much as a word, she unleashed her flames upon them, her phoenix fire sweeping across the battlefield.

The flames consumed the group in an instant, reducing them to ashes before they had the chance to react.

Unable to contain her excitement, Feng Yuxiang laughed merrily. “Hahaha! So this is the Young Master’s power! It’s marvelous!” After the brief delay, Yuan and his group resumed their journey. Unfortunately, their troubles were far from over. Over the next several days, they faced numerous interruptions as more groups of opportunistic cultivators attempted to steal their tokens.

Meanwhile, Feng Yuxiang and Lan Yingying would take turns removing the thieves.

“Are these people just stopping everyone heading to the Hidden Domain?” Xi Meili sighed out loud after their 20th encounter with thieves.

“Seems like it.” Yuan shrugged.

Despite Yuan and his group’s repeated assertions that they weren’t headed to the Hidden Domain or didn’t possess any entry slots, none of the thieves believed them. In fact, it only made them more aggressive. It reached the point that they stopped trying to avoid a fight and would just deal with the thieves on the spot without any talking.

After seven days of arduous traveling, Yuan and his group finally reached the location of the Hidden Domain.

Nestled deep within the wilderness, the entrance was concealed behind a massive, roaring waterfall that cascaded down with breathtaking force. The sound of rushing water filled the air, and the misty spray created a mystical aura around the area.

The area around the Hidden Domain was bustling with activity, packed with cultivators of all levels. Most of them, however, did not possess an entry token. Despite the Heavenly Beast Sect’s best efforts to keep the Hidden Domain’s details a secret, the information had inevitably leaked to the public, drawing a massive crowd eager to try their luck or profit from the chaos.

“I’m willing to trade a Mythic-grade treasure for a single slot!”

“I’ll offer two Ancient-rank martial techniques for a slot!”

The area resembled a bustling open market, with people shouting their offers for an entry slot to the Hidden Domain.

Whenever a new face appeared, the entire crowd’s attention would inevitably shift, their eyes filled with suspicion, curiosity, or greed. The moment Yuan’s group arrived, countless gazes locked onto them, evaluating their strength, identity, and most importantly, whether they might be carrying entry tokens.

Yuan’s group paid no attention to the stares and headed directly to the entrance of the Hidden Domain—a shimmering portal hidden behind the massive waterfall and protected by several powerful formations.

The Sect Leader of the Heavenly Beast Sect was guarding the entrance with several other experts. Of course, Hong Ling was there too.

“Welcome to the Hidden Domain, esteemed guests!” Sect Leader Bao smiled upon seeing them.

“So we can only enter with an entry token?” Yuan asked.

“No, anyone can enter since this isn’t the only entrance you have to go through to enter the Immortal Monastery. That said, we cannot allow just anyone to enter. Currently, we’re only allowing those with a verified identity or trustworthy background to enter, as we don’t want any unnecessary troublemakers.”

“I see.”

“Would you like to enter now?”

“Yes.”

Sect Leader Bao opened the formation, allowing Yuan and his group to enter.

“I’ll show them around.” Hong Ling suddenly said.

“Okay.” Sect Leader Bao nodded.

“Follow me!”

Following Hong Ling, Yuan and his companions stepped through the portal.

On the other side, they found themselves in a sprawling, seemingly bustling city. The architecture was grand and vibrant, exuding an air of ancient sophistication. However, it quickly became apparent that something was amiss.

Despite the city’s appearance, none of the stores were open for business. The shops stood eerily empty, their interiors devoid of any goods or signs of activity. Not a single vendor or resident could be found, giving the city an unsettling, lifeless quality.

The only people present were fellow visitors to the Hidden Domain—cultivators who had entered through the portal, their curious gazes sweeping over the strange, deserted cityscape.

However, as Yuan’s eyes scanned the city, an unexpected feeling washed over him. It was a sense of nostalgia, almost as if he’d been to this place before.

“This is the City of Radiance…” Yuan subconsciously muttered as he recalled Tian Yang’s memories of visiting this city before.

“Huh? You know this place?” Hong Ling asked, her eyes widening in disbelief.

The Heavenly Beast Sect had spent years trying to uncover information about the city, yet their efforts yielded little to nothing.

He nodded calmly. “This city existed near the borders of the Immortal Monastery. It was a popular hangout spot for the disciples.”

“How do you know this?” Hong Ling inquired.

“My family has records of the Immortal Monastery.”

“I see…”

Yuan retrieved the Baneful Abyss and wore it around his waist so Ji Ran could see the scenery.

“T-this is! City of Radiance!” Ji Ran immediately recognized the place.

“Where is the Immortal Monastery?” Yuan asked a moment later.

Hong Ling pointed toward a majestic mountain in the distance, its peak shrouded in a thick fog.

“The entrance to the Immortal Monastery is at the top of that mountain. It’s not open yet, but do you want to go now? I was going to show you around the city first,” Hong Ling said.

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, let’s take a look around the city first.”

“Understood!” she acknowledged.

chapters.title: Chapter 1816 City Of Radiance
chapters.body:
Chapter 1816 City Of Radiance

“When we first discovered the Hidden Domain, we thought this city was all it had to offer,” Hong Ling explained as they walked through the streets of the City of Radiance. “Because of the size of this city, it took us several years to search through it thoroughly. Unfortunately, we didn’t find many treasures here.”

Her tone carried a hint of frustration, clearly recalling the disappointment of their fruitless efforts.

“While most Hidden Domains are treasure troves, offering priceless artifacts or cultivation techniques, that’s not always the case,” she continued. “Some Hidden Domains turn out to be completely barren, with nothing of value to offer. For a while, we thought this was one of those.”

As they continued their walk through the city, Yuan and his group noticed other visitors rifling through the empty stores like desperate bandits.

Some tore apart shelves and counters in search of hidden compartments, while others pried at walls and floors, hoping to uncover secret caches. The scene was chaotic, filled with muttered curses and the occasional exclamation of frustration as their efforts turned up nothing.

It was clear that, despite the city’s emptiness, the visitors weren’t ready to give up on the possibility of hidden treasures lurking within its abandoned structures.

“Are you okay with them tearing up this place like that?” Feng Yuxiang asked, raising an eyebrow as she glanced at the chaotic scene around them.

Hong Ling smiled faintly and replied, “As long as they don’t alter the structure of the city or cause any major damage, it’s fine. Besides, entering the Hidden Domain isn’t free. The Heavenly Beast Sect collects fees for access for those without a slot, so it’s not like we aren’t benefiting from their desperation.”

Hong Ling didn’t bother stating the obvious—that the Heavenly Beast Sect had already scoured every inch of this city with meticulous care. They had left no stone unturned, no hidden compartment unchecked.

As they ventured deeper into the city, Yuan’s sense of nostalgia grew increasingly profound. Every detail—the architecture, the layout of the streets, and even the faint traces of energy lingering in the air—stirred something deep within him.

‘That noodle store belonged to Granny Mai,’ Yuan thought, his gaze lingering on a small, dirty, and rundown store nestled between two much larger and more luxurious establishments.

A faint smile touched his lips as memories stirred within him. ‘She would always give me a free meal whenever I visited… even when I insisted on paying.’

A few blocks down, Yuan’s gaze landed on an old smithy, its structure dusty and abandoned.

‘Uncle Dong…’ Yuan thought, a wave of nostalgia washing over him. The memory of the reliable blacksmith surfaced clearly in his mind. Uncle Dong had always been there to help, fixing Tian Yang’s swords whenever they became too worn or damaged from rigorous use.

Eventually, Yuan and his group reached the outer area of the city, where the shops and bustling streets gave way to rows of residential buildings.

The houses varied in size and design, but most shared an elegant simplicity, showcasing the same ancient craftsmanship as the rest of the city. However, like the rest of the city, these homes were eerily empty, their windows dark and lifeless, as though the residents had vanished without a trace.

“This is the residential area. There’s not much to see here,” Hong Ling said, her tone casual as she began to turn, clearly intending to lead them elsewhere.

“Wait,” Yuan interjected, his voice calm but firm. “There’s a place I want to visit.”

“Huh? Sure,” Hong Ling replied, nodding despite the puzzled expression on her face. She followed Yuan, clearly curious about what he was looking for.

Without another word, Yuan stepped forward and began leading the group deeper into the residential area. His movements were confident, as if he knew exactly where he was going.

Seeing this, Hong Ling wondered inwardly, ‘This should be his first time here, but why does it feel like he’s very familiar with this place, almost as if he’d lived here for his entire life?’

After walking several blocks in silence, Yuan came to a halt in front of an unassuming building.

It was modest in design, blending seamlessly with the other residential structures around it. The building’s weathered exterior showed signs of age, but there was an undeniable warmth to its simplicity. Yuan stood still for a moment, gazing at it with a mix of nostalgia and melancholy, as though memories from long ago were flooding back to him.

“This is the place you want to visit?” Hong Ling asked.

He nodded and said, “I’m going to check it out.”

“Can we come with you?” Hong Ling continued.

“Sure.”

They stepped inside the building shortly after, immediately noticing how tight and compact the interior was.

The small space felt even more cramped with the entire group inside. There was little to no furnishing, only a few bare, dusty shelves and an empty table in the corner. A thick layer of dust coated every surface, and cobwebs hung in the corners.

Yuan didn’t say a word or take any specific action as he stood inside the building. He simply looked around, his gaze wandering slowly across the small, dusty space. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

There was a certain reverence in his expression, as though he were marveling at something unseen to the others. “Alright, I am done,” he said a few minutes later.

“Huh? That’s it? You made it seem like you were looking for something,” Hong Ling remarked with a dumbfounded expression on her face.

“It’s a misunderstanding,” he said, offering no explanation.

“Alright, let’s head to the Immortal Monastery now, shall we?” he continued.

“Sure…” Hong Ling nodded in a dazed manner.

“Young Master, what were you searching for?” Ji Ran asked curiously.

“I wanted to check if this city is real or just a replica of the City of Radiance,” he revealed.

“A replica…? This place looks real to me,” Ji Ran remarked.

“That’s what I thought at first, too. Unfortunately, that isn’t the case.”

“What…?” Ji Ran swallowed nervously after hearing this.

chapters.title: Chapter 1817 City Of Radiance(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1817 City Of Radiance(2)

“This City of Radiance is a replica? So it’s fake? For what purpose?” Ji Ran questioned, his brow furrowed in confusion as he tried to make sense of Yuan’s revelation.

“I don’t know,” Yuan replied thoughtfully. “But whoever made this place definitely invested an immense amount of energy and effort to ensure it was as realistic and detailed as possible.”

“What about the Immortal Monastery? Are you saying that it’s also fake?”

“Possibly, but we won’t know for certain until we see it.”

Although Yuan claimed he didn’t know why anyone would go to such lengths to create a replica of the City of Radiance, several possibilities crossed his mind.

One plausible explanation was that this Hidden Domain could be a trap—a meticulously crafted lure designed to draw people in for some unknown purpose. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Hidden Domains always attracted attention, drawing cultivators from far and wide with the promise of treasures and opportunities. It wasn’t unheard of for such places to be disguised traps, crafted to exploit the greed and curiosity of those who entered.

Another possibility was that the City of Radiance had been replicated simply to serve as a replacement for the original, which may have long since perished.

In this case, the replica might not hold any sinister purpose or hidden agenda. Instead, it could be nothing more than a decorative tribute—a way to preserve the memory of a once-glorious city. Perhaps it was created by someone who revered the original City of Radiance, ensuring that its legacy would not be forgotten, even if its inhabitants and purpose had vanished with time.

Sometime later, they arrived at the bottom of the mountain that led to the Immortal Monastery.

“The entrance to the Immortal Monastery is at the top,” said Hong Ling as she stepped onto the first step.

Feng Yuxiang raised an eyebrow at her actions and asked, “We’re walking? Why don’t we just fly up there?”

Hong Ling smiled at her words and calmly responded, “Go ahead and try—flying up there, I mean.”

Feng Yuxiang didn’t think much about it and gathered her spiritual energy. However, when she tried flying, she realized she couldn’t control her spiritual energy, almost as if something was disrupting it.

“What’s going on?” Feng Yuxiang muttered as she tried gathering her spiritual energy again, only for them to disperse due to a lack of control.

“Although this world has spiritual energy—and we can cultivate it—we cannot control it for some reason,” Hong Ling revealed. “We’re almost no different from mortals while we are inside the Hidden Domain.”

“Seriously?” Feng Yuxiang muttered.

With their ability to use spiritual energy sealed, they were forced to walk up the mountain, which contained tens of thousands of steps.

Fortunately, while they could not use their spiritual energy, their cultivation wasn’t affected, allowing them to climb the mountain without breaking any sweat. It was mostly a chore—an inconvenience than anything.

Upon reaching the peak of the mountain, they found themselves on a spacious flat surface, its layout eerily reminiscent of the peak of the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

In the distance, near the edge of the mountain, stood a massive open gate colored in gold. Above it, a grand sign displayed the words ‘Immortal Monastery’ in bold, majestic script, exuding an aura of authority and timelessness.

“I never thought I would see the Immortal Gates again…” Ji Ran commented in a teary voice as he stared at the gates.

“That’s the Immortal Monastery? Where’s the rest of the sect?” Xi Meili asked.

“That gate is also a teleporter. It’ll lead us to the Immortal Monastery once it opens,” Yuan said.

Hong Ling nodded and said, “Indeed, and only those with a token will be able to enter the Immortal Monastery. Everyone else will have to stay here.”

“There’s so much more than 20,000 people here,” Xi Meili remarked after scanning the area with her gaze.

Despite there only being 20,000 slots available, there were more than 50,000 people at the peak of the mountain. This did not include the thousands of people searching the city.

“Young Master, what do you think about the gate? It looks real to me,” Ji Ran asked him.

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the golden gates. After a moment of silence, he said, “It definitely looks real, but I have no real way of knowing whether it’s real or just another perfect replica.”

“The only reason I knew the City of Radiance is a replica is because something I left in my living quarters at the real one was missing. However, there’s nothing about the gate I can use to compare. If anything, you should be more familiar with it than me.”

Ji Ran sighed, “Unfortunately, I really can’t tell. I haven’t seen the Immortal Monastery since the Primordial Era, so my memories of it are not reliable at all. Honestly, even if my memory was perfect, I don’t think I’d be able to tell.”

“I guess we’ll only be certain once we go inside. It’s extremely risky, but there’s a reason why I am here,” Yuan said.

“Well then, you guys can do whatever you want until the Immortal Monastery opens. I’ll head back to my master now,” Hong Ling said a moment later.

“Alright. Thanks for the tour.” Feng Yuxiang said.

Once Hong Ling left, Feng Yuxiang returned to Yuan’s Dantian to rest.

“I’ll be back,” Tan Songyun suddenly said.

“You see your friends?” Yuan guessed.

“Something like that.”

Tan Songyun didn’t explain further and walked away. Yuan then watched as she approached two beautiful ladies in the crowd.

“Hello, Senior Sister Li, Senior Sister Zhang,” Tan Songyun greeted them.

“Oh? If it isn’t Junior Sister Tan. What are you doing here?” asked one of the ladies, who was wearing a bright red robe.

“I’m here to participate in the Hidden Domain. Are you also here as participants?” Tan Songyun asked, her tone polite and refined.

Her demeanor was a stark contrast to how she spoke with Yuan—graceful and poised, resembling the renowned Zither Goddess she had been before her heart was broken. Her words carried an air of elegance that drew the attention of those around her.

chapters.title: Chapter 1818 Immortal Monastery
chapters.body:
Chapter 1818 Immortal Monastery

“Yes. Although we weren’t that interested at first, we came here after hearing about the Immortal Monastery. Believe it or not, my ancestors used to be disciples of the Immortal Monastery—at least according to the records they left behind,” said the red-robed woman, who was surnamed Zhang.

“I decided to tag along with her since it sounded interesting,” said Fairy Li.

“What about you, Junior Sister Tan? Where have you been lately? It’s been years since we’ve heard from you,” Fairy Zhang inquired.

“I went to fulfill my Pact of Vengence,” she calmly said.

“Oh? You’ve finally found that bastard who broke your heart? Did you tear him limb from limb?” Fairy Li’s eyes flickered with excitement.

“I hope you cut off his manhood!” Fairy Zhang gestured a chopping motion with her arm.

Yuan’s eyes widened at these ladies’ viciousness that betrayed their gentle appearances.

Tan Songyun shook her head and said, “I found him, but the situation is much more complicated than I thought.”

“What’s so complicated about it? The man toyed with you and broke your heart. It’s as simple as it gets.”

Tan Songyun sighed, “I wish it were that simple. Don’t worry. I am handling it right now.”

“Well, it’s your Pact of Vengence, so we have no say in how you want to take revenge. However, if you ever need help, don’t hesitate to contact us. You’re Lady Wei’s favorite, after all,” Fairy Li said.

“Can you stop saying it like that? It has a weird tone to it.”

“But it’s true! The two of you are like real sisters,” Fairy Zhang remarked.

Tan Songyun and these two individuals continued chatting for the next several days, with Tan Songyun recalling her experiences of the last few years without mentioning Yuan directly. Yuan and Xi Meili merely sat together in silence, patiently waiting for the Immortal Monastery to open.

During the next few days, numerous people approached Yuan and Xi Meili, hoping to acquire an entry slot. However, both of them consistently denied possessing one, calmly explaining that they were only there to spectate.

While some individuals resorted to slightly aggressive questioning, they ultimately walked away without escalating the situation. Unlike in the outside world, their inability to use spiritual energy inside the Hidden Domain made any potential conflict far riskier. Additionally, acting like a bandit in such a public setting, surrounded by other famous cultivators, would severely tarnish their reputations.

“The Immortal Monastery will be open shortly! Everyone with an entry slot, please gather before the golden gates!” Sect Leader Bao announced shortly after he appeared on the peak.

Hearing this, all of the participants began gathering in front of the entrance.

Feng Yuxiang emerged from Yuan’s Dantian and said, “Good luck, you guys.”

Since she wasn’t participating, she had to stay outside with the others. seaʀᴄh thё novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“By the way, what about Xiao Hua?” Feng Yuxiang asked through voice transmission.

Yuan sighed, “Due to her status as an Exile, I do not want to expose her to so many people from the upper heavens here if I don’t have to. I’ll see if I can enter with her first. If I can enter with my Soul Weapons, there’s a good chance I can enter with Xiao Hua inside my Dantian.”

“In that case, I’ll come with you,” Feng Yuxiang said before returning to his Dantian again.

A few minutes later, the massive golden gates began to emit a profound and ancient aura. The energy spread outward in a ripple, sweeping across the entire area like an invisible wave.

The aura carried a weight of timelessness, as though it had existed for millennia, untouched by the flow of time. Those present could feel its immense power, leaving many in awe and silence as they braced themselves for what was about to unfold.

The aura continued to surge forth like a broken dam, each ripple growing increasingly powerful as it radiated across the area.

The sheer intensity of the energy made the air feel heavier with every wave, pressing down on everyone present. Some cultivators instinctively took a step back, their faces pale as they struggled to withstand the overwhelming force.

“This is Immortal Qi!”

“A mere gate is exuding Immortal Qi? I cannot imagine what the rest of the Immortal Monastery will look like!” The participants trembled with excitement.

Sometime later, the aura ceased its outward expansion, but it continued to radiate power around the golden gate.

Then, a deep, ancient voice echoed across the area, its tone imbued with an overwhelming sense of authority and timelessness. Each word resonated deeply, causing the souls of those present to tremble in reverence, as if they were before a god.

“Welcome to the Immortal Monastery. My surname is Xian, and I was once called the Ancient Immortal.”

“Ancient Immortal!” Ji Ran exclaimed in shock after hearing this name. “That’s the Immortal Monastery’s founder! However, this makes no sense. The Ancestor died long before the sect collapsed. Why would he create a place like this?”

Hearing this, Yuan said, “He could be an imposter for all we know.”

“That’s true. Even I don’t know what the founder’s voice sounds like, so this could very well be a fake.”

The voice continued, its tone unwavering and solemn, “I created this place shortly before my death, as a safeguard in the event that the Immortal Monastery is ever destroyed or ceases to exist—as pessimistic as that might sound. If the Immortal Monastery still stands to this very day, consider this an opportunity to glimpse what life was like during its earliest days.”

“You will also have the chance to earn rewards—powerful martial techniques, cultivation methods, treasures, and rare herbs that were available during the Primordial Era. However, do not think these rewards will come easily. Only by passing the trials I have created will you be deemed worthy of acquiring them.”

“If you somehow manage to pass all of my trials, I will even allow you to inherit my legacy, which includes the Immortal Monastery.”

The moment these words echoed through the air, the crowd’s collective breath hitched. Many swallowed hard, their eyes flickering with unmistakable greed and desire.

chapters.title: Chapter 1819 Entering The Immortal Monastery
chapters.body:
Chapter 1819 Entering The Immortal Monastery

“The Ancient Immortal’s legacy!” someone exclaimed, their voice trembling with excitement. “I’ve heard about him before! He was one of the earliest cultivators to reach immortality! If he could achieve the Immortal Realm during such an early period, when cultivation was barely developed, I can only imagine the kind of heaven-defying cultivation techniques he must have possessed!”

“Forget that! We get to inherit the Immortal Monastery! Doesn’t this mean we will inherit all of their techniques?! I’m willing to risk my life for this opportunity!”

After a moment of pause, the Ancient Immortal continued speaking, “Only those who have a token will be able to participate in my trials. I have left behind 20,000 tokens. Anyone without a token who tries to participate will be erased from existence. I will begin calling the number on the tokens in an hour.”

When the Ancient Immortal mentioned the numbers, a few people holding entry tokens instinctively glanced at them. To their surprise, a faint number now glowed on their tokens—something that hadn’t been there before.

However, not everyone’s token bore a number. The absence of this marking on some tokens caused unease to ripple through the crowd, as it likely meant those tokens were fakes.

“W-why don’t my token have a number?! What is the meaning of this?!” someone eventually shouted, his voice filled with anger.

“Mine is also missing a number!”

“Mine too! Does this mean our token is fake?!”

“Sect Leader Bao, what is the meaning of this?!” The people with a fake entry token immediately turned to Sect Leader Bao for answers, as the Heavenly Beast Sect was responsible for handing out the tokens.

“How dare you try to scam us with these fake entry slots?! Do you know who you’re messing with?!”

“Fuck! I paid a fucking fortune for this entry slot! I demand an answer, Heavenly Beast Sect!”

“Did you really think you’d get away scamming us?! I’ll burn down the Heavenly Beast Sect myself if that’s the case!”

Sect Leader Bao appeared genuinely shocked and confused by the sudden revelation about the tokens. However, his daze was short-lived as the situation quickly escalated.

When people began directing their anger and threats toward the Heavenly Beast Sect, his expression hardened. With so many powerful cultivators and influential figures from the upper heavens present, the sect could not afford to offend them. Doing so would undoubtedly spell disaster and potentially lead to the destruction of the Heavenly Beast Sect.

Sect Leader Bao straightened his posture, his voice steady but tinged with urgency as he addressed the crowd, “Everybody, please calm down and listen to me!”

“The tokens my Heavenly Beast Sect handed out are definitely real! Every single one was acquired directly from this place, so it’s extremely unlikely they are fake!” He paused, his gaze sharp as he swept across the crowd.

“Now, I have a question for those of you with a fake token!” he continued, his tone demanding yet composed. “Did you acquire the token directly from my sect, or did you obtain it through another source?”

“If you did not acquire a token directly through my Heavenly Beast Sect, there’s a good chance you were scammed. In such a case, it is not my Heavenly Beast Sect’s responsibility to compensate you!”

The area grew quiet, and those with fake tokens stood dumbfounded as they realized they had been scammed. The others, after hearing Sect Leader Bao’s explanation, couldn’t find a reason to blame the Heavenly Beast Sect and mostly sided with him.

If someone had purchased a token from outside the Heavenly Beast Sect, it was their responsibility to do their due diligence to ensure they weren’t buying a fake token. Scams like these were not uncommon, especially when dealing with something as valuable as entry tokens to a Hidden Domain.

Eventually, someone broke the silence.

“I-I got my token directly from the Heavenly Beast Sect!”

“That’s right! I got my token from the Heavenly Beast Sect, too! Or are you calling us liars?!”

“That’s exactly what I am calling you!” Sect Leader Bao roared. “I don’t care if you’re from the upper heavens or not! I have registered the identity of everyone who received a token from our sect! If you claim to have gotten your token from us, you should be willing to give us your name so that we can verify it!”

Once Sect Leader Bao mentioned a list of verified token holders, those who had been shouting moments ago fell instantly silent. The atmosphere grew still, and the area became quiet once again.

Meanwhile, everyone took out their token to verify they had the real ones. Yuan also did the same, and sure enough, all five of his tokens had a number on them.

“Here.”

After verifying the tokens, Yuan handed token number ‘11,923’ to Xi Meili, ‘7,129’ to Lan Yingying, and ‘17,441’ to Yingzi, leaving numbers ‘7,688’ and ‘2,025’ to himself and Ji Ran. Searᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Is anyone willing to sell their real token to me?! I’ll pay any amount!” one of the individuals with a fake token shouted, their voice brimming with desperation.

Others in the same predicament quickly joined in, their frantic pleas echoing through the area as they scrambled to secure a legitimate token, willing to offer anything to enter the Immortal Monastery.

A few individuals holding real tokens seized the opportunity to secure an immediate and guaranteed profit. Despite setting exorbitant prices, the desperation of those with fake tokens ensured that the real tokens were purchased almost instantly.

An hour later, the Ancient Immortal’s voice resounded once more, echoing across the area.

“The Immortal Monastery will now open. Those with numbers 1 to 1,000 may enter the Immortal Gates and begin their trial. You have three minutes to do so, or you’ll lose your chance to enter.”

As soon as the announcement ended, a wave of movement erupted. People rushed toward the gates where a massive portal shimmered, eager to visit the Immortal Monastery.

However, even at a glance, it was obvious that far more than a thousand individuals were rushing toward the gates.

The moment those without a real token—or any token at all—attempted to step into the portal, a terrifying sight unfolded. Their bodies and souls were instantly vaporized by an unseen force, leaving nothing behind. This shocked everyone, momentarily halting their movements.

chapters.title: Chapter 1820 Entering The Immortal Monastery(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1820 Entering The Immortal Monastery(2)

When those who thought about sneaking inside the Immortal Monastery despite the Ancient Immortal’s warning saw the brutal deaths of the first few deaths, they instantly changed their minds.

However, the Ancient Immortal had different plans.

“Foolish hoarders who ignored my warning, you all deserve death,” the Ancient Immortal spoke in a grim voice.

The next moment, the golden gates emitted another powerful pulse of energy, sending a ripple cascading across the area.

When the ripple reached those who had attempted to enter the Immortal Monastery without a token but hadn’t yet reached the gates, their bodies were instantly vaporized, erased from existence as if they had never been there.

The shocking display left everyone watching frozen in fear and disbelief. The ruthlessness of the Ancient Immortal gave a glimpse of what might be to come in the trials.

Some of the victims were Immortals who came from the Upper Heavens, with a few even being Immoral cultivators. However, they were still powerless before the Ancient Immortal’s powers.

“I will not repeat myself. Those who disrespect my authority will be erased. One minute left.” The Ancient Immortal spoke after a moment of silence.

After being reminded of the time limit, those numbered 1 to 1,000 snapped out of their daze and hastily rushed into the portal.

Once the remaining minute was over, the Ancient Immortal’s voice resounded again, “Numbers 1,001 to 2,000 may enter the Immortal Gates now. You have 3 minutes.”

This time, the exact number of people allowed—1,000—approached the Immortal Gates. The gruesome fate of the previous group had left an indelible impression on everyone present, and not a single person dared to attempt sneaking into the Immortal Monastery without a legitimate token.

Three minutes passed without any incident.

“Numbers 2,001 to 3,000 may enter the Immortal Gates now. You have 3 minutes.”

“I’ll send you directly to the front,” Yuan said to Ji Ran, who had number 2,025.

“Okay. I’ll see you inside the Immortal Monastery, Young Lord.”

Yuan grabbed the Baneful Abyss and tossed it directly toward the Immortal Gates with the token.

“What’s that?”

The people there first thought someone was trying to launch an attack, but the following scene shocked many people there.

When the Baneful Abyss reached the Immortal Gates, Ji Ran emerged from the Soul Weapon, and his God Ascension realm aura surged forth like a tidal wave.

“A spirit?”

“No, that’s a physical soul! He must be a God Ascension realm cultivator!” After coming out of the Baneful Abyss, Ji Ran didn’t immediately enter the Immortal Gates. Instead, he bowed before it, almost as if paying respects to it.

“I can sense a familiar energy coming from you,” the Ancient Immortal’s voice suddenly echoed.

The unexpected statement startled everyone still present, including Ji Ran and Yuan. The implication behind such words—that the Ancient Immortal was still conscious, perhaps even alive—sent a ripple of shock through the crowd.

“I remember now… This energy is from the Immortal’s Seal, something only disciples of the Immortal Monastery may receive… And judging by its powerful energy, you must’ve held a high position within the sect… State your identity, disciple.”

Ji Ran immediately responded, “This one is called Ji Ran, and I once held the position of Sect Leader within the Immortal Monastery! It’s an honor to be in your presence, Immortal Founder!”

Ji Ran’s words sent another wave of shock to those present.

“Immortal Monastery’s Sect Leader? But the Immortal Monastery should’ve ceased to exist since the Primordial Era!” someone muttered out loud.

“Does that mean he’s from the Primordial Era? I have never met someone so ancient before!”

“He’s a God Ascension realm cultivator, so he has an infinite lifespan. However, even with unlimited longevity, it’s incredibly rare to survive this long!”

The Ancient Immortal’s voice returned, its tone filled with intrigue, “So you were a Sect Leader, huh? In that case, what do you seek from my trial?”

“I wasn’t sure at first, but after hearing what you said—about experiencing the Immortal Monastery in its earliest days, I decided that’s what I wanted to do,” Ji Ran said.

“I see… I have many questions, but I am not in a position to ask them now. In any case, even if you were once the Sect Leader of my Immortal Monastery, I will not make the trials easier for you,” said the Ancient Immortal.

“Yes!” Sёarch* The NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Ji Ran entered the Immortal Gates shortly after.

After Ji Ran entered the Immortal Gates, the crowd’s attention shifted almost immediately to Yuan.

Whispers spread among the onlookers, their gazes filled with intrigue and curiosity. Yuan, who had been the one to bring Ji Ran out, now stood at the center of attention, with everyone wondering about his identity and his connection with Ji Ran and the Immortal Monastery.

“Maybe he’s that Sect Leader’s disciple?”

“So he’s a disciple of the Immortal Monastery?”

“The Immortal Monastery doesn’t exist anymore.”

“Not physically, but as long as one of its disciples remains, the Immortal Monastery is technically still alive.”

Many people there took note of Yuan’s appearance, but due to his mask, they were only able to memorize his outer appearance and those around him.

Sometime later, the Ancient Immortal said, “Numbers 7,001 to 8,000 may enter the Immortal Gates now. You have 3 minutes.”

Lan Yingying and Yuan began making their way to the Immortal Gates since they were numbered 7,129 and 7,688.

“Good luck.” Xi Meili said to them.

“I’ll see you inside,” he nodded.

“You too,” said Lan Yingying.

Once they reached the Immortal Gates, Yuan said to Lan Yingying, “You can go inside first. I have a question for the Ancient Immortal.”

“Okay.” Lan Yingying nodded and entered the portal.

Yuan then looked at the Immortal Gates and spoke, “I only have one token, but I am a Beast Tamer, and I have my servants with me. Can they enter without a token? They won’t be participating in the trial, of course.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1821 Entering The Immortal Monastery(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1821 Entering The Immortal Monastery(3)

“A Beast Tamer, huh? Indeed, I can sense several presences within you,” the Ancient Immortal responded shortly after. “You were with the Immortal Monastery’s Sect Leader. What is your connection with the Immortal Monastery and the Sect Leader?”

Instead of giving a response, the Ancient Immortal questioned Yuan.

Hearing this, Yuan decided to answer truthfully with some minor lies mixed within, “I helped the Sect Leader escape the Shadow Realm—a place where souls are damned to wander for eternity. In exchange for helping him, Sect Leader Ji agreed to make me his disciple and the successor of the Immortal Monastery.”

“The Shadow Realm!” The spectators present gasped in shock after hearing about this ominous place. seaʀᴄh thё NovelFire.net* website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“He actually released someone from the Shadow Realm? Does he not fear offending the heavens—the Celestial Emperor?”

“Why would it offend the Celestial Emperor? It’s not as if our current Celestial Emperor was responsible for what happened during the Heavenly War of Immortals or Gods.”

“No, but if the Celestial Emperor wanted to, he could easily free those in the Shadow Realm at any point. The fact he hasn’t means he doesn’t want them to leave for whatever reason.”

There were two main reasons why nobody dared to assist those within the Shadow Realm.

First, the place was incredibly dangerous. The majority of the souls dwelling there had long lost their rationality, their souls corrupted over time. They were no different from malevolent spirits, driven by pure instinct and malice, making it perilous for anyone to venture near them.

The second reason was that nobody wanted to offend the Celestial Emperor. Just like a certain individual had mentioned, the Celestial Emperor had the power and authority to release the souls within the Shadow Realm, but he hasn’t for whatever reason.

“So you saved the Sect Leader. Very well, I shall make this a special occasion and allow you to enter with those residing within you despite having only a single token. However, they will not be able to participate in the trial, nor will you be able to contact them until you are finished with the trial,” the Ancient Immortal finally said.

Yuan bowed to the Ancient Immortal, “Thank you, Senior.”

“Since you are now part of the Immortal Monastery, you should address me as Founder.”

“Yes, Founder.”

“Now get going. You have less than ten seconds to enter.”

“Yes, Founder.”

“Now get going. You have less than ten seconds to enter.”

Yuan nodded and entered the portal with just two seconds left.

As Yuan stepped through the Immortal Gates, his vision momentarily blanked, and an unsettling sensation washed over him.

It didn’t take long for him to notice a profound change within his body—and it was not a good one. His strength faded away, his talents sealed, and, most disturbingly, his cultivation had completely vanished. At this moment, Yuan realized he was no different from a mortal, stripped of all the power and talents he had.

When Yuan’s vision returned, he found himself standing in a serene courtyard nestled in the heart of a bamboo forest. The gentle rustling of the bamboo leaves in the wind contrasted starkly with the unsettling changes he had just experienced.

Around him were the other participants, all with tokens numbered 7,001 to 8,000. They stood scattered throughout the courtyard, their expressions varying between confusion, wariness, and curiosity as they took in their surroundings.

“Where the hell are we? This doesn’t look like the Immortal Monastery!” someone exclaimed.

“Fuck! My entire cultivation is gone!”

“I also cannot sense my physique or talents! What’s happening?! Did we get tricked?!”

The participants began to panic as they realized their powers had completely vanished, leaving them as vulnerable as mortals. Murmurs of confusion and fear spread through the group, their voices rising as they struggled to understand the puzzling situation.

While the others were consumed by their panic, Yuan stood quietly, his expression calm. His gaze wandered over the courtyard and bamboo forest with a nostalgic glint.

‘This place is the Bamboo Garden…’ Yuan thought to himself. ‘The very first trial where one must prove their worth before being allowed to even step foot into the Immortal Monastery as a disciple…’

Tian Yang’s memories inside this place began to resurface again, but he didn’t have the time to reminisce.

Yuan began inspecting his body. Sure enough, he was no different than a mortal. As for his connection with Feng Yuxiang and the others, he couldn’t even sense them, almost as if his connection to them was completely cut off.
