chapters.title: Chapter 1910 - 1910: Family Seal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1910 - 1910: Family Seal

“Is there anything I can say that will change your mind?” Elder Sun asked, still reluctant to let him visit her family.

Yuan smiled.

“You should know me well enough by now to know the answer to that.”

Elder Sun sighed, “Even if your life might be endangered?”

“I’m sure you’ll protect me.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Elder Sun said, “Fine, if that’s what you want to do. However, you must accept something from me.”

“Oh? A gift from you? Why would I refuse something like that?”

“In that case, take it.”

Elder Sun suddenly flicked his forehead, the motion swift yet oddly gentle. A warmth spread from the point of contact, washing over him like a comforting embrace.

Although Yuan couldn’t see it, a faint red symbol briefly appeared on his forehead, glowing softly before fading away as if it had never existed. The warmth lingered for a moment before dissipating, leaving behind an inexplicable sensation, as though something had subtly changed within him.

“What did you do?” Yuan asked as he rubbed his forehead.

“I gave you my family seal. It basically recognizes you as someone approved by me. As long as you have that, my family won’t kill you—probably.”

“A family seal?” Yuan raised his eyebrows, looking slightly surprised. “Isn’t that something one gives to someone who joins their family? We’re practically married now.”

“What kind of nonsense is that?” Elder Sun sighed out loud. “Family seals can be given to people outside the family, but only those from the main family are allowed to do so.”

Yuan chuckled, “Is that so?” Sёarᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Despite Elder Sun’s casual demeanor, the act she had just performed was anything but trivial. Bestowing a family seal was an incredibly significant and meaningful gesture—one that carried deep implications. It was not something given lightly, nor was it an act done on a whim. This was especially true for families with immense influence and reputation. Whether Yuan realized it or not, the family seal symbolized something far greater than mere sentiment.

For the next several days, Yuan would spend all of his time on the Immortal Sun Peak with Elder Sun. They would talk about many things, from their hobbies to their goals in life.

Time passed quickly, and in the blink of an eye, it was time for Yuan and his group to leave the Immortal Monastery.

“I’m going to enter seclusion today, Elder Sun. If you’re not here by the time I come out, I’ll definitely come visit you in the Holy Continent.”

Elder Sun nodded and silently watched as Yuan disappeared from the Immortal Sun Peak.

Sometime later, Yuan and the others went to meet with the Sect Leader.

Outside the Sect Leader’s headquarters, Ji Ran bid them farewell.

“Good luck out there, everyone.”

“You too. Since time flows much faster in this world, it’ll probably be a while for you before we return,” Yuan said.

Ji Ran smiled and replied, “Even if it takes millions of years, it still wouldn’t compare to my time inside the Shadow Realm.”

After saying their goodbyes to Ji Ran, Yuan and the others went inside the building to meet with the Sect Leader.

“Are you all leaving?” the Sect Leader asked.

“Yes.”

“Very well. Once you leave, you will wake up on an island. To leave, you just need to go outside and enter the portal,” said the Sect Leader.

The Sect Leader snapped his fingers, and a swirling purple portal materialized before them.

Without hesitation, Yuan stepped forward, entering the portal first, followed closely by Tan Songyun and the others.

The instant they crossed the threshold, their vision went blank. A strange weightlessness overtook them, their senses fading as their consciousness drifted away, much like when a player entered Cultivation Online.

A few moments later, Yuan’s eyes fluttered open. Blinking away the lingering haze, he slowly took in his surroundings. He was lying on a bed inside a modest yet unfamiliar room, the atmosphere calm and still.

Instinctively, he inspected his body, running his hands over himself. Sure enough, he was back in his original body.

Just as he got out of the bed, a notification appeared before him, something he hadn’t seen in years.

<You have received ‘Seal of the Eternal Sun’>

“Seal of the Eternal Sun? Is that Elder Sun’s family seal?” Yuan mumbled.

[Seal of the Eternal Sun: ???]

When he tried to look up its information, there were only question marks. However, what truly surprised Yuan was that the seal had carried over from the other world.

Sometime later, Yuan stepped out of the room and made his way outside. As he exited the building, his gaze was immediately drawn to his surroundings.

He found himself standing atop a small floating island, suspended in the vast sky. A gentle breeze swept past, carrying an air of mystique. However, what truly caught his attention was the sheer number of similar floating islands stretching across the horizon—thousands of them, each seemingly housing other participants.

Yuan attempted to step off the island, but the moment he did, he was met with an invisible force. A barrier surrounded the entire floating island, preventing him from leaving. He pressed his hand against it, feeling a faint resistance—solid yet intangible.

Its purpose was obvious. The barrier was likely there to prevent participants from interfering with one another.

Suddenly, Yuan’s attention was drawn to movement nearby. The door of the adjacent building opened, and a familiar figure stepped out.

It was Lan Yingying.

Her elegant form emerged into the open, her gaze sweeping across the floating islands before finally landing on Yuan.

Lan Yingying waved at him. Her mouth was moving, but Yuan couldn’t hear what she was trying to say.

Yuan pointed at the portal nearby before entering it, disappearing from the island. Lan Yingying followed suit and entered the portal on her island.

After emerging from the other side, Yuan and Lan Yingying found themselves back inside the City of Radiance.

Moments later, Tan Songyun and the others also appeared out of thin air beside them.

chapters.title: Chapter 1911 - 1911: Warning(End Of Immortal Monastery Arc)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1911 - 1911: Warning(End Of Immortal Monastery Arc)

“Looks like everyone is here,” Yuan said after Tan Songyun, Xi Meili, and Yingzi returned to the City of Radiance.

“Young Master, you’re back!”

Feng Yuxiang emerged from his dantian a moment later and immediately embraced him.

“Feng Feng? What happened to you after I entered the Immortal Monastery?” Yuan asked her. Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Well, after you entered the portal, I was forced to slumber. I didn’t wake up until just now,” she explained.

“I also just woke up. Where’s Ji Ran? I don’t see him with you,” Yu Ning’s voice resounded.

“Oh, Ji Ran decided to remain in the trial. Although we came out, we haven’t completed the trial yet. Of course, he’s not going to stay there forever. I will come back here once I create his new physical body,” explained Yuan.

“Is that so…”

“Now then, let’s figure out how long we’ve been inside the trial. If I consider what the Sect Leader told me, only about a year should have passed since we entered the Immortal Monastery.”

Yuan extended his divine sense, sweeping across the vast expanse of the City of Radiance in search of any presence. However, to his surprise, the once-bustling city was now eerily empty, and not a single soul remained apart from his group.

“There’s nobody here?” he muttered, his voice tinged with disbelief.

Before they had entered the Immortal Monastery, the city had been teeming with cultivators, all eagerly searching for hidden treasures or just touring the place. Even if it had become clear that there were no treasures, that wouldn’t have been enough to completely deter people from visiting this place—especially in the cultivation world, where people are the most persistent.

Yet now, only a year later, the city stood completely abandoned.

That didn’t make sense.

Something had happened. But what?

In the midst of his thoughts, Yuan noticed Tan Songyun was staring at her communication jade slip with a dumbfounded look on her face.

“Did something happen?” he asked her out of curiosity.

However, Tan Songyun didn’t immediately respond and continued staring at her communication jade slip. Clearly, she was still busy with it.

While Yuan waited for her, a notification abruptly appeared before him.

<You have received a message from Qiang Qing Yun>

‘Celestial Overlord Qiang?’ Yuan raised an eyebrow.

This was the first time he’d received such a notification.

‘Since it’s related to the Celestial Overlord…’

Yuan raised his arm and glanced at his faction symbol.

A faction symbol was more than just a means of identification—it also functioned as a communication tool, much like a communication jade slip. However, its usage was restricted. Unlike communication jade slips, it could only be used for announcements since the messages are sent to every member, and only the higher-ups of the faction had the authority to send messages.

After Yuan had infused some of his spiritual energy into the symbol, it pulsed faintly, and a voice echoed within his mind.

“This is a warning for all members. The Demon Worshippers, who have always acted from within the shadows, have recently increased their activity and started revealing themselves—an unusual shift in their behavior. Stay vigilant, and report any information or signs of their presence to the Demon Sealing Clan or the Demon Sealing Grotto immediately.”

Yuan frowned after hearing the message.

Demon Worshippers typically thrived in secrecy, operating from the shadows and avoiding unnecessary attention. Their entire existence revolved around manipulation, deception, and silent corruption. However, according to the Celestial Overlord’s announcement, they were now behaving in direct opposition to their usual methods—boldly revealing themselves instead of lurking unseen.

This was deeply out of character and worrying.

“Did you also receive a warning from your faction?” Tan Songyun’s voice suddenly resounded, snapping Yuan out of his thoughts.

He looked at her and asked, “About the Demon Worshippers’ unusual activities? You also received it?”

She nodded.

“Our faction usually doesn’t concern itself with matters unrelated to our vengeance. However, when we do pay attention, it’s because the situation is so significant that even we can’t ignore it.”

Xi Meili joined their conversation and asked Yuan, “I heard of them during your trial in the Stairway to Heaven, but I am still unfamiliar with their existence. What are they? And what are their goals?”

Since Demon Worshippers didn’t exist in the Ancient Dragon City, nor did they have to worry about them, Xi Meili was never taught about their existence.

“You know what demons are, right?” Tan Songyun asked.

“Of course.”

“Well, Demon Worshippers, as their name implies, are those who revere and serve demons,” Tan Songyun explained. “Some are mere admirers, those who idolize demons but take no real action. Then, there are those who go a step further—acting on behalf of demons, carrying out their will in the world.”

“I can’t fathom why anyone would revere demons, much less serve them,” Xi Meili said, shaking her head in disbelief. “Demons once ruled the world, didn’t they? And they committed countless atrocities against everyone. Why would anyone willingly follow them?”

To worship those who had once brought devastation to the world was unfathomable. Yet, the existence of Demon Worshippers proved that some people not only accepted demons but actively sought their favor.

“It’s quite simple, actually,” Yuan said, his voice calm yet firm. “For power.”

He met Xi Meili’s gaze as he continued, “Demons may be ruthless and unreasonable, but their power is undeniable. Some people are willing to sacrifice everything—morality, humanity, even their own souls—just for a taste of that power. To them, it doesn’t matter what demons have done in the past. All they care about is what they can gain in the present.”

“How does that work? How does one acquire the power of demons?”

In response, Yuan extended a finger, revealing a single drop of blood glistening at the tip.

“Their blood,” he said a moment later. “By consuming demon blood, one can gain immense regenerative abilities that rival those of demons themselves. Moreover, one’s natural limitations—their innate talents can be bypassed, allowing them to achieve feats that otherwise aren’t possible.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1912 - 1912: Extreme Yin Lake
chapters.body:
Chapter 1912 - 1912: Extreme Yin Lake

“I understand their desire for power, but what’s the point if you turn into a monster at the end?” Xi Meili shook her head after hearing about Demon Worshippers. She could still vividly recall the grotesque transformation she witnessed during Yuan’s trial.

“That usually only happens when one consumes too much demon blood. A few drops won’t turn one into a monster, but there is still a high chance it will affect their mind by making them more aggressive, hot-tempered, and unreasonable,” Yuan explained further.

“I was told this place was attacked recently by the Demon Worshippers, hence why it’s empty right now.” Tan Songyun suddenly revealed.

She turned to look at Yuan and asked, “So, what are your plans now?”

Yuan fell into deep thought, his expression unreadable as he pondered in silence.

He didn’t know the exact reason behind the Demon Worshippers’ sudden activity, but that didn’t mean he was clueless. His mind drifted back to his encounter with the Demon Emperor Carnage a few years ago. Could this surge in Demon Worshipper activity be connected to him?

‘If it’s really the Demon Emperor’s doing, something big will definitely happen soon, and I should get ready for it.’

Yuan looked at Tan Songyun and asked, “Do you know of any place with extreme yin element? The colder the place, the better.”

“A place of extreme yin element, huh? Does it have to be in the Sixth Heaven?” she asked.

“No, but it would be great if there is one here.”

Tan Songyun pondered for a moment before responding, “The Extreme Yin Lake. It’s located in the Sixth Heaven and is a very popular place for cultivators who specialize in the yin element.”

Yuan nodded and said, “Then that will be my next destination.”

He wanted to improve his cold immunity further so his physique could evolve.

“I don’t mind, but why? You don’t even cultivate Yin Qi.”

“I’m not going there to cultivate. It’s to temper by body and train my cold immunity,” he explained.

“Is that so…?”

Despite knowing his purpose, Tan Songyin still couldn’t understand why he needed to train his cold immunity now. It just seemed too random and out of the blue.

With their next destination in mind, Yuan and his group made their way to the Extreme Yin Lake.

The Extreme Yin Lake was located near the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect, one of the most prestigious and powerful sects in the Sixth Heaven.

Renowned not only for their peerless ice-based martial techniques, the sect was also widely recognized as a gathering place for the most beautiful women in the realm.

In the cultivation world, women are naturally attuned to the yin element, while most men are inherently aligned with yang energy. This natural balance follows the fundamental principles of Yin and Yang, where Yin represents coldness, tranquility, and the moon, while Yang embodies warmth, intensity, and the sun.

Because of this, female cultivators tend to naturally excel in Yin-based techniques, making the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect a predominantly female sect. However, there exist a few exceptions—men born with an extremely rare natural affinity for Yin energy. Their numbers are so few that they can be counted on one hand.

After traveling for several days, Yuan and his group arrived at their destination, the Extreme Yin Lake.

“Wow, it’s beautiful…” Xi Meili muttered, her eyes shimmering as she gazed at the breathtaking scenery before them.

The Extreme Yin Lake was nestled within a valley of towering snowy mountains, their peaks blanketed in pristine white. The air was crisp and cool, carrying a faint, almost ethereal chill that seemed to seep into the soul. The entire landscape radiated a serene, otherworldly beauty, untouched and pure, as if frozen in time. Sёarᴄh the nôvel_Fire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The temperature in the area was a staggering -200 degrees Celsius, an unbearable cold that could freeze cultivators unprepared for such extreme conditions.

Yet, despite the frigid atmosphere, the lake’s surface remained untouched by ice. The water flowed with an almost ethereal stillness, shimmering under the pale light, as if defying the very laws of nature.

If one looked closely, they would notice several individuals seated around the Extreme Yin Lake, immersed in deep cultivation.

Most of them remained near the snowy banks, absorbing the pure Yin Qi radiating from the lake. However, two figures stood out, and they were cultivating directly on top of the lake itself.

“How long are you going to stay here?” Tan Songyun asked.

“I don’t know yet since it’ll depend on how effective this training spot is, but I’ll be here for a few days, at the very least.”

“In that case, I’ll visit the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect. I know their Sect Leader.”

“Can I come with you? I’d like to see the sect, and I don’t like this coldness.” Xi Meili said.

“Sure.” Tan Songyun nodded. “Then we’ll be back in a few days. Try not to cause any trouble.”

“Yes, mother,” Yuan responded in a joking tone.

Tan Songyun trembled, and she immediately responded with an angry frown, “Don’t you ever call me that again, even as a joke.”

Yuan chuckled, “Sorry.”

After Tan Songyun and Xi Meili departed, Yuan made his way toward the Extreme Yin Lake, his steps steady despite the piercing cold.

With each step closer, the temperature plummeted even further, the air itself growing heavier with dense Yin energy. The chilling aura seeped into his skin, biting at his bones, but he pressed forward, undeterred.

As for Lan Yingying and Yingzi, one entered his Dantian while the other his shadow.

<Cold Resistance has improved>

<Cold Resistance has improved>

Yuan smiled after seeing the notification.

‘It’s nothing compared to the training grounds within the Primal Expanse, but I doubt I’ll find a training place that extreme in the Sixth Heaven.’

When Yuan got close enough, the cultivators who had been cultivating by the Extreme Yin Lake finally paid attention to him.

‘A man? Why is he wearing a mask?’

‘Who’s that? He’s not a disciple at our Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect…’

Although the disciples were curious about Yuan’s presence, they chose not to approach or disturb him. Instead, they merely observed in silence, their gazes carrying a mix of intrigue and caution.

It was rare for an outsider—especially a man—to approach the Extreme Yin Lake, let alone withstand its intense cold. Yet, Yuan walked forward with unwavering composure, as if the bone-chilling temperature had no effect on him.

chapters.title: Chapter 1913 - 1913: Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect
chapters.body:
Chapter 1913 - 1913: Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect

Although the Extreme Yin Lake was located beside the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect and primarily visited by its disciples, it was classified as a natural training ground rather than exclusive sect property. As such, outsiders were allowed to enter freely.

However, despite this openness, the lake’s unforgiving cold served as a natural barrier. The chilling Yin energy was so intense that only those who had cultivated the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s unique techniques could withstand it for extended periods. For anyone else, this training ground was a perilous area.

Because of this, while technically open to all, in practice, the Extreme Yin Lake remained a place almost exclusively occupied by the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s disciples, and even then, only their most talented and experienced disciples would dare to train there.

When Yuan reached about 300 meters from the Extreme Yin Lake, he came to a stop. The air around him was so frigid that even the moisture in his breath froze instantly.

Without hesitation, he sat down and closed his eyes, entering a state of cultivation. Unlike others who would instinctively shield themselves from the cold, Yuan chose not to protect himself with spiritual energy.

Instead, he allowed the bone-chilling Yin energy to penetrate his body freely, embracing the raw intensity of the Extreme Yin Lake’s environment. He was not merely cultivating—he was tempering his body, pushing himself beyond his natural limits.

<Cold Resistance has greatly improved>

<Cold Resistance has greatly improved>

<Cold Resistance has greatly improved>

Although the Extreme Yin Lake was indeed effective in improving his Cold Resistance, Yuan found the progress too slow for his liking.

A mere five minutes had passed when he opened his eyes, dissatisfaction flickering within them. Without hesitation, he stood up and began walking closer to the lake, stepping into the even denser Yin energy that surrounded the lake.

The disciples swallowed nervously when they realized Yuan wasn’t protecting himself with spiritual energy and enduring the cold with his bare body.

‘Is he insane? No… more importantly, how is he enduring the Extreme Yin Lake with his bare body? Does he have a heavenly yin physique?’

The disciples were shocked by Yuan’s audacity and mysterious physique.

A few moments later, when Yuan was only 200 meters away from the water, ice began forming around his body, slowly turning him into an ice statue. Yet, despite his seemingly dangerous state, Yuan did not feel like his life was in danger in the slightest.

‘This is kind of disappointing… or maybe I was spoiled by the extreme training grounds at the Divine Dragon Sanctuary…’ Yuan sighed inwardly as he recalled the truly perilous training grounds that could kill even immortals.

In the blink of an eye, Yuan closed the distance by another 100 meters. Now, he was standing right beside the disciples who were cultivating outside the lake.

“W-Who are you…?” one of them couldn’t help but ask.

Yuan turned to look at the dazed female disciple and said, “Xiao Yang.”

Without saying anything else, Yuan continued to walk closer to the lake. S~eaʀᴄh the nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“You should stop there if you value your life.”

One of the individuals cultivating on the late suddenly spoke out loud when Yuan was just 50 meters away.

“Don’t worry, my condition is actually better than it may appear,” Yuan said calmly.

The female disciple frowned and said, “Seriously. I don’t want to deal with your frozen corpse later. Unless you cultivate the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s cultivation technique or have cold immunity, you won’t survive the lake.”

“I appreciate your concern, but it’s really fine. If I somehow end up as a corpse, my companion will deal with it.”

“Your companion? Where?” The disciple looked around but couldn’t see anyone.

“They’ll be back in a few days. If I have not returned by then, please let them know where I am.”

“What are you saying? I don’t get it.” The disciple raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

Yuan, who had reached the lake, pointed at the water and said, “I’ll be inside.”

“What?! You can’t be serious—”

Before the disciple could finish her sentence, Yuan jumped into the air and dove directly into the lake’s center.

“Holy shit! He went inside the water!”

Shock rippled through the disciples as they stared, unable to believe what they witnessed.

Even the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s Sect Leader, a cultivator renowned for possessing Cold Immunity, would not last more than a few seconds submerged in the lake’s waters. Yet, Yuan went inside without any protection, treating it like an ordinary pool of water.

Meanwhile, Tan Songyun and Xi Meili arrived at the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect and were greeted by the Sect Leader herself when she heard Tan Songyun’s name.

“Senior Sister Tan! What a pleasant surprise! I haven’t seen you in forever!”

A beautiful woman with white hair and blue eyes approached them with a bright smile on her face.

“I was passing by the Extreme Yin Lake and decided to visit. How’s the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect lately?”

When the Sect Leader heard this question, her smile weakened, and she responded in a dispirited voice, “It was doing great… until last month.”

“What happened?” Tan Songyun inquired.

“I don’t know if you’ve heard, but the Demon Worshippers have been making a scene lately.”

“I was in the Hidden Domain until a few days ago, but I know of it. Did they attack your sect?”

The Sect Leader glanced around, scanning their surroundings with a discerning gaze. After a brief pause, she spoke, “Let’s go somewhere more private.”

“Sure.”

“By the way, who is your friend?” The Sect Leader asked as they walked back to her place.

“She’s Xi Meili. We’re traveling together at the moment. Don’t worry, she’s trustworthy.”

“I’m not worried about that. I was just curious since you’ve never brought a friend before.”

Sometime later, they went inside the Sect Leader’s room.

Once she shut the door, the Sect Leader looked at Tan Songyun with a serious face and revealed, “We don’t know if it’s related to the Demon Worshippers yet, but some of our disciples have gone missing lately.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1914 - 1914: Someone From The Demon Sealing Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1914 - 1914: Someone From The Demon Sealing Clan

“Missing… so you think your disciples are being kidnapped by the Demon Worshippers?” Tan Songyun asked after hearing this.

“That, or they were killed,” sighed the Sect Leader.

“What is the sect doing about this?”

“So far, we’ve recalled most of our disciples back to the sect, unless they’re on a really important mission. We have also restricted their movements to within the sect’s boundary. Other than that… I don’t know what else can be done.”

“What about the disciples at the Extreme Yin Lake? I saw a few of your disciples there,” Tan Songyun said.

“That’s a special location. Most people can’t even get close due to the extreme weather. Unless the Demon Worshippers have cold immunity or a special physique, they won’t be able to go near it. Also, it’s fairly close to the sect.”

“Sorry you have to deal with this.”

The Sect Leader shook her head and said, “I’m sure it’ll be over soon, especially once that person from the Demon Sealing Clan arrives.”

“That person?” Tan Songyun raised an eyebrow.

The Sect Leader smiled and said, “Yes, she’s very famous in the Demon Sealing Clan and the cultivation world. I’m sure you’ve heard of her before. I requested help from the Demon Sealing Clan as soon as I realized the Demon Worshippers were kidnapping my disciples, and they recently notified me that this individual would be coming to assist us. In fact, she should be arriving soon.”

“So, who is this individual?” Tan Songyun asked again.

The Sect Leader smiled and said, “Are you in a hurry to leave? If you are, I will tell you, but if you have some time, you should meet her. This is a really rare chance, after all. Hell, you’d probably stay after hearing her name.”

Tan Songyun was a little annoyed by the secrecy, but she was also very curious.

“I have a few days,” she said.

“Great! I’ll let you know when she arrives. Is there anything you would like to do in the meantime? How about I show you around the sect?”

Xi Meili immediately nodded and replied, “That would be great.”

Thus, the Sect Leader began showing them around the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect.

“I know you already have a Demon Sealer on the way, but I know someone who might also be able to help,” Tan Songyun said as they traveled.

“Are you talking about Yu—Xiao Yang?” Xi Meili asked.

Tan Songyun nodded, “What do you think? You know him better than me, but from what I’ve seen, he seemed very familiar with demons and demon worshippers.”

“I’m not too sure, but if it’s him, I’m sure he’ll be helpful no matter what it is.”

“Who is this Xiao Yang?” The Sect Leader inquired in a curious voice.

“He’s our traveling companion and the reason why we’re here. He’s currently training at the Extreme Yin Lake, though.”

“A man with Yin affinity? How rare.”

Although the Sect Leader misunderstood his situation, they didn’t bother correcting her.

A few hours later, midway through their tour, the Sect Leader suddenly halted her movements and retrieved a communication jade slip.

“I just received news that the Demon Sealer has arrived at the sect, so we’ll end the tour here.”

Learning this, Tan Songyun and Xi Meili followed the Sect Leader to meet this Demon Sealer.

Surprisingly, they didn’t head to the Sect Leader’s headquarters. Instead, they traveled to the 7th Frozen Cloud Peak, a public area accessible by all disciples.

When they arrived at their destination, their gaze was immediately drawn to a beautiful woman in black and white robes standing gracefully by a water fountain.

Her presence was captivating, exuding a refined elegance that demanded attention.

Surrounding her were excited disciples, their expressions filled with awe and admiration. Some eagerly spoke with her, while others simply stood nearby, as if just being in her presence was an honor in itself.

“That’s… that’s… who is that?” Tan Songyun turned to look at the Sect Leader after failing to recognize the Demon Sealer.

“Seriously?! You don’t recognize her?! That’s Sun Ling Cai, also known as Myriad Colors! Not only is she one of the best painters in the Nine Heavens, but she is also a Master Demon Sealer! Each of her paintings, no matter how simple, fetches tens of millions of spirit stones!” Searᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Myriad Colors? That’s the ‘Sublime Painter’? Although I have never seen her before, I have heard of her name,” Tan Songyun muttered with slightly widened eyes.

The Sublime Painter was renowned not only for her ability to create flawless masterpieces but also for the unique power her creations possessed. Unlike ordinary works of art, hers were capable of producing mystical effects akin to those of powerful treasures.

For example, one of her most famous works, Cultivator’s Ascension, was a painting that functioned like a real spiritual treasure, capable of gathering and enhancing spiritual energy in its surroundings. Just by placing it within a cultivation chamber, one could experience a significant boost in their cultivation speed.

Because of this, her art was highly sought after, not just for its beauty, but for its practical and mystical properties, making it priceless to both mortals and cultivators alike.

“She’s a painter? I didn’t know cultivators paid that much attention to such a profession,” Xi Meili remarked, as she considered painting a mortal’s profession.

“She’s not your ordinary painter. Her paintings function as treasures. Some have effects that benefit cultivation, while others can work as life-saving treasures. The only issue is that she cannot determine the effect of her paintings, so they vary greatly.”

The Sect Leader approached Sun Ling Cai and bowed, “It’s a real honor to have you here, Senior Sun. I am the Sect Leader of the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect, Yun Xi.”

“Nice to meet you, Sect Leader Yun. I was sent here by the Demon Sealing Clan after they reviewed your request. I will do everything in my power to assist you with your problem.”

“I can’t imagine having someone more reliable than you here, Senior Sun. If you’re ready, let’s go somewhere more quiet.”

“Let’s.” Sun Ling Cai nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1915 - 1915: Sublime Painter
chapters.body:
Chapter 1915 - 1915: Sublime Painter

Shortly after greeting each other, Sect Leader Yun led Sun Ling Cai to her headquarters. Xi Meili and Tan Songyun followed them after receiving the Sect Leader’s permission.

“Am I the only Demon Sealer here? What about the Demon Sealing Grotto?”

“You’re the only Demon Sealer here.”

“I see. I was half-expecting them to be here, too.”

A stiff smile appeared on the Sect Leader’s face as she spoke, “To be honest, I contacted both the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto. However, only the Demon Sealing Clan responded to my request. I apologize if I did something I wasn’t supposed to.”

Sun Ling Cai calmly shook her head and said, “No, you’re fine. There’s no rule stating that you can only contact one of us. I wouldn’t mind working with them either if it means dealing with these Demon Worshippers faster.”

She turned to look at Xi Meili and Tan Songyun and asked, “Then who are you guys? You don’t seem like disciples of this sect.”

“We’re just guests here. However, if you need help, we’re more than willing to lend a hand,” said Tan Songyun.

She wasn’t offering assistance just because of her friendship with Sect Leader Yun. This was a matter far greater than personal ties.

As a cultivator—as a human—she felt an obligation to stand against the Demon Worshippers, those who had forsaken their morals and humanity in pursuit of corrupt power. Their actions threatened not just sects or families but the balance of the world itself.

Once they arrived at the headquarters, Sect Leader Yun began explaining the situation to Sun Ling Cai in more detail.

“According to those who witnessed the kidnappings, these Demon Worshippers are specifically targeting maidens,” Sect Leader Yun stated grimly.

“Maidens?” Sun Ling Cai raised an eyebrow, her expression shifting to one of intrigue and concern.

Sect Leader Yun nodded, her voice steady as she continued, “At first, we believed the Demon Worshippers were simply kidnapping our disciples at random. However, we soon realized there was a clear pattern. All the disciples who managed to evade capture, despite being present at the time of the incidents, were not maidens. Is there a reason why they’re specifically targeting maidens?”

Sun Ling Cai pondered for a moment before responding, “Truth is, your Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect isn’t the only place being targetted by the Demon Worshippers. Female cultivators around the Nine Heavens are being kidnapped on a scale we’ve never witnessed before. Although we don’t know why, we do have some speculations.”

“The most obvious one is that they require sacrifices for something.”

“Could the Demon Worshippers be feeding them to the demons?” Xi Meili asked.

Sun Ling Cai nodded and responded, “It’s possible, but there’s no reason why they would only go after maidens since demons don’t have preferences and eat whatever they get their hands on.”

“If they’re only going after maidens, maybe they are doing a ritual that requires maidens. I know there are forbidden techniques out there that demand maidens because of their pure essence,” said Tan Songyun.

“Yes, the Demon Sealing Clan also believes the Demon Worshippers are gathering maidens for some kind of ritual. As for the purpose of such ritual… we can only speculate.”

“So, how can we stop these Demon Worshippers? Unless you know where they are hiding, we can only react to their actions,” Tan Songyun asked.

Sun Ling Cai retrieved a black metal compass and said, “This is a treasure that can detect nearby demonic energy.”

“What a convenient tool,” Xi Meili remarked.

“It was only developed recently, and its uses are still limited, especially its detection range. However, it’ll automatically improve as we use it,” said Sun Ling Cai.

“How far does its detection reach?” Sect Leader Yun asked.

“About a hundred miles.”

“That’s not much…”

“It’s not, but it can detect even the faintest demonic energy—”

In the midst of Sun Ling Cai’s speech, the compass suddenly emitted a red glow, pulsing with an eerie intensity.

A moment later, a red light flickered above it, rapidly condensing into the shape of an arrow, its tip unwavering as it pointed toward a specific direction. Sёarᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“The compass activated?! So it detected demonic energy nearby?!” Sect Leader Yun stood up in shock.

Sun Ling Cai nodded with a serious expression on her face.

“The arrow… isn’t it pointing in the direction of the Extreme Yin Lake?” Xi Meili asked after noticing this.

“It is. Let’s hurry to the Extreme Yin Lake,” Sect Leader Yun spoke with urgency.

Meanwhile, at the Extreme Yin Lake, the disciples have been staring at the lake for the last few hours.

“It’s been hours since he went inside. He’s definitely dead, right?”

“If he’s dead, why isn’t his corpse coming back up?”

“Then he’s probably not dead…”

The disciples were in disbelief. They couldn’t fathom how a man could endure so long inside the Extreme Yin Lake.

“He definitely has a heaven-defying Yin physique! Maybe he even possesses the legendary Arctic Saint Physique!” one disciple exclaimed in shock.

“That’s impossible,” another disciple immediately refuted, shaking her head. “Only women can be born with that physique. No man has ever possessed it in history.”

Suddenly, another disciple said, “Hey, look over there.”

The group instinctively turned their heads, their gazes locking onto the treeline.

There, several cloaked figures stood motionless, their dark robes barely shifting despite the cold wind.

“We sure have a lot of visitors today.”

“Snap out of it. They’re oozing with malice, and they’re not even hiding it.”

The two disciples who had been cultivating on the lake suddenly jumped out of it to confront these newcomers.

“Judging by your suspicious appearances and the recent incidents… you must be Demon Worshippers. Are you here to kidnap us?”

“Demon Worshippers?!”

The other disciples immediately became wary and nervous after hearing this.

Meanwhile, the Demon Worshippers wasted no time with words.

Without warning, they lunged forward, their dark cloaks billowing as they charged straight at the disciples, completely unfazed by the Extreme Yin Lake’s deadly cold.

Seeing this, one of the disciples immediately retrieved a red talisman and tore it up.

“I have notified the sect! We just need to last until they arrive!”

chapters.title: Chapter 1916 - 1916: Fighting The Demon Worshippers
chapters.body:
Chapter 1916 - 1916: Fighting The Demon Worshippers

The Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s disciples clashed with the Demon Worshippers. Due to the close distance between their sect and the Extreme Yin Lake, they only needed to defend themselves for several minutes before help arrived.

However, the disciples quickly realized that lasting even a minute would be challenging. The Demon Worshippers were incredibly powerful and fought without any regard for their own safety.

Moreover, despite having lower cultivation than the disciples, the Demon Worshippers displayed prowess that surpassed the disciples. This abnormal power is one of many reasons why Demon Worshippers abandon their humanity. Searᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I won’t be able to endure this much longer, Senior Sister!”

Aside from the two Core Disciples who cultivated on the Extreme Yin Lake, the rest of the disciples were merely Inner Court Disciples, and they were on the verge of being overwhelmed.

Despite their efforts, the Inner Court Disciples were struggling against the Demon Worshippers’ brutal assault, their techniques barely holding back the relentless attacks. One by one, they began to falter, their defenses crumbling under the sheer ferocity of their enemies.

As for the Core Disciples, while they were faring better, they were far from gaining the upper hand. Neither side was winning or losing, trapped in a deadlock where a single mistake could turn the tide.

Despite their desire to help their junior disciples, they simply weren’t in a position to do so—their opponents were too strong, forcing them to remain fully engaged in their own battles.

If this continued much longer, the Inner Court Disciples would fall first, and the Core Disciples would eventually fall as well.

“Ah!”

“Junior Sister!”

One of the Inner Court Disciples let out a sharp cry as she was overwhelmed, her defenses shattering beneath the relentless assault. Her weapon slipped from her grasp, clattering against the icy ground as she collapsed, exhausted and defenseless.

The Demon Worshipper standing over her wasted no time. With a flick of his hand, dark chains materialized from thin air, writhing like living shadows before wrapping tightly around her body.

The moment the black chains latched onto her, a profound force sealed her spiritual energy, suppressing it completely.

The disciple’s eyes widened in horror as she realized that her cultivation had been sealed. In an instant, she had been reduced to a mere mortal.

As soon as the first disciple was captured, the battle quickly turned one-sided.

One by one, the other Inner Court Disciples fell, unable to resist the overwhelming power of the Demon Worshippers. The black chains continued to manifest, restraining their spiritual energy and leaving them helpless as they were forcibly subdued.

Once all the Inner Court Disciples had been captured, the Demon Worshippers swiftly turned their attention to the Core Disciples. Though they had managed to hold their ground for a time, they were now outnumbered and outmatched.

Realizing they couldn’t win, the Core Disciples fought desperately, trying to break through and free their fellow disciples, but it was futile. The Demon Worshippers coordinated their attacks flawlessly, exploiting every weakness and overwhelming them with sheer force.

In the blink of an eye, the battle was over.

All the disciples—Inner Court and Core alike—had been captured, their spiritual energy sealed, their struggles rendered meaningless.

“No! I don’t want to die!”

The captured disciples began crying out, their voices filled with a mix of terror and shame.

They trembled, not just from fear, but from the crushing realization of their own weakness. They had only needed to hold out for a few minutes—just enough time for their sect’s reinforcements to arrive. Yet, they had failed.

In less than two minutes, they had been completely subdued, their spiritual energy sealed, their bodies restrained like helpless mortals.

Tears welled in their eyes as frustration and despair settled in. They had trained for years, striving to become stronger, yet when it mattered most, their strength had meant nothing.

Their captors, the Demon Worshippers, stood over them in silent mockery, as if their struggle had been nothing more than an inevitable conclusion.

“Bring them all away,” said one of the Demon Worshippers.

However, just as the Demon Worshippers began lifting the subdued disciples, carrying them like mere luggage, a voice suddenly echoed through the icy air.

“I thought I was smelling things, but sure enough, it’s the stench of demonic energy.”

The chilling words sent a ripple of unease through the Demon Worshippers. Their movements halted instantly, and their heads snapped toward the Extreme Yin Lake.

There—where no one had stood before—a masked figure had suddenly appeared out of thin air like a ghost.

However, when the Demon Worshippers noticed this figure was only at the second level of Spirit Enlightenment, they immediately relaxed.

“Hurry up and kill him. I’ll give you three seconds.”

In response, one of the Demon Worshippers suddenly dropped the disciple in his grasp and lunged at Yuan.

The Demon Worshipper reached Yuan in one second and struck the next. However, in the third second, the Demon Worshipper suddenly stopped moving and collapsed on the ground, his body quickly turning into stone.

The Demon Worshippers were instantly alarmed after seeing their companion turn into a stone statue.

“That power! He’s a Demon Sealer!”

Yuan then spoke in a calm voice, “Release those girls and surrender yourselves, or I’ll turn you all into stone. I’ll give you three seconds.”

Realizing they were against a Demon Sealer, the Demon Worshippers no longer dared to look down on Yuan and decided to fight him together.

They released the disciples and attacked Yuan together, surrounding him from every direction.

There were six Demon Worshippers, and they were all around the Divine Lord level.

Despite being surrounded, Yuan calmly said, “Your three seconds are up.”

In response, the Demon Worshippers attacked.

At the same time as the Demon Worshippers attacked Yuan, Sun Ling Cai and the others arrived at the scene.

“Who is that?! He’s fighting six Divine Lord Demon Worshippers by himself! And he’s only at the Spirit Enlightenment level!” Sun Ling Cai almost couldn’t believe her eyes when she saw the scene.

chapters.title: Chapter 1917 - 1917: Fighting The Demon Worshippers(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1917 - 1917: Fighting The Demon Worshippers(2)

Although Sun Ling Cai wanted to intervene and help the masked individual, her curiosity got the best of her, and she decided to watch him a little longer.

As for Sect Leader Yun, she couldn’t care less and immediately went to save her disciples the moment she noticed them.

“Sect Leader! You’re here!”

The disciples cried tears of joy when they saw her beautiful face.

“Are you all okay?!” Sect Leader Yun asked them as she destroyed the black chains that restricted their cultivation.

“Yes, we’re okay. It’s all because of him.”

The disciples pointed at Yuan with their gazes, their eyes filled with admiration and gratitude.

“That must be Senior Sister Tan’s companion…” Sect Leader Yun mumbled as she stared at Yuan, who was emitting a profound yin aura.

‘That aura! He’s attained Super Ice Resistance!’ she cried inwardly. Despite cultivating in the Extreme Yin Lake for decades herself, she’d only managed to achieve Powerful Ice Resistance, which was one tier weaker than Yuan’s Super Ice Resistance.

In just a few hours, Yuan had managed to upgrade his Weak Ice Resistance by two whole tiers, an unthinkable speed of progression for most.

After spending a few moments effortlessly dodging the Demon Worshippers’ attacks, Yuan finally countered—using nothing but his bare hands.

His movements were swift and precise, flowing like water, yet carrying the force of a raging tempest. With every shift of his body, he evaded strikes with almost inhuman ease, making it seem as though he was merely playing with his enemies.

Despite wielding no weapon, the Demon Worshippers didn’t dare to underestimate his attacks.

It wasn’t his physical strength that frightened them—it was the Demon Sealing Aura radiating from his very being. To them, that aura was more terrifying than any weapon, capable of sealing their very existence if they weren’t careful.

However, they quickly realized how futile their efforts were. Much like how the Inner Court Disciples struggled to fight back, the six of them were being pressured despite outnumbering him.

Soon, Yuan landed a strike on one of the Demon Worshippers, instantly turning him into stone.

“To seal his opponent so quickly—what powerful Demon Sealing Aura!” Sun Ling Cai cried out in shock, her eyes widening as she witnessed Yuan’s effortless suppression of the Demon Worshippers.

As a Master Demon Sealer, very few Demon Sealers surpass her in Demon Sealing Aura within the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto. Yet, this masked individual was clearly much stronger than her.

However, there was no way she wouldn’t recognize such a powerful Demon Sealer.

“Seriously, who is that? He’s definitely not from the Demon Sealing Clan, but I know of every Master Demon Sealer in the Demon Sealing Grotto, too.” Sun Ling Cai muttered in a dumbfounded voice.

“That’s our traveling companion, Xiao Yang,” Tan Songyun said.

“Xiao Yang? I don’t recognize that name. Where did he learn how to use Demon Sealing Aura?” Sun Ling Cai asked.

Tan Songyun shook her head.

“I don’t know. You’ll have to ask him.”

“There’s no way someone with such powerful Demon Sealing Aura isn’t affiliated with the Demon Sealing Clan or Demon Sealing Grotto!” Sun Ling Cai refused to believe that some random cultivator could have such a strong Demon Sealing Aura.

After all, while one could learn Demon Sealing Aura through techniques, it wouldn’t reach the level of Master Demon Sealer without guidance from an expert Demon Sealer.

By the time their conversation ended, Yuan had dealt with all but one Demon Worshipper.

“P-Please, spare me…”

The Demon Worshipper gave up resistance and began begging for his life.

“Oh? You’re begging for your life despite selling your soul to the demons? How ironic. Don’t worry, I won’t completely seal you. After all, I have some questions for you.”

Yuan suddenly raised his leg and kicked the Demon Worshipper in the groin. The Demon Worshipper cried out loud in pain, and soon, everything but his head turned to stone.

“Not only is his Demon Sealing Aura powerful, but his control over it is monstrous!” Sun Ling Cai swallowed nervously.

‘If he’s not affiliated with the Demon Sealing Grotto already, I must bring him over to our side!’

Once it was clear that Yuan had dealt with the Demon Worshippers, Sect Leader Yun approached him and bowed, “Thank you. If it weren’t for your assistance, more of my disciples would’ve been kidnapped by those bastards.”

“There’s no need to thank me. I was merely dealing with pests.”

Tan Songyun and the others appeared shortly after. Searᴄh the NovelFire.net* website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Before Xi Meili or Tan Songyun could even speak, Sun Ling Cai rushed over and introduced herself, “Hello, I’m Sun Ling Cai. You’re Xiao Yang, correct?”

‘She’s…’

Yuan instantly recognized Sun Ling Cai, as she was one of the Divine Paragon’s Fanatics. Moreover, she was the one who painted him at the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library, even giving him a necklace afterward.

‘What’s she doing in the Sixth Heaven?’ he wondered inwardly.

Yuan wanted to reveal his true identity to her, but he wasn’t sure if he could trust her, especially when he was being hunted as a demon, so he decided to continue acting as Xiao Yang for the time being.

“Yes, I am Xiao Yang.”

Sun Ling Cai continued speaking, “I am actually a Master Demon Sealer from the Demon Sealing Clan, and I cannot help but notice your powerful Demon Sealing Aura. Yet, I don’t recognize you. Are you affiliated with the Demon Sealing Clan or the Demon Sealing Grotto?”

Yuan pondered for a moment before nodding, “I am somewhat affiliated with the Demon Sealing Clan. Do you know Yan Hara?”

“Senior Sister Yan? Of course, I know her! We’re practically family! What is your relationship with her?” Sun Ling Cai was pleasantly surprised to learn that Yuan was not only affiliated with the Demon Sealing Clan but even knew Yan Hara, but this raised more questions.

“Our relationship is a little complicated, but we’re definitely quite close with each other. How’s she been lately? I haven’t seen her in a while.” Yuan decided to learn more about Yan Hara’s situation.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 1918 - 1918: Extracting Their Memories
chapters.body:
Chapter 1918 - 1918: Extracting Their Memories

“Senior Sister Yan’s situation, huh?” An awkward expression appeared on Sun Ling Cai’s face. “It’s a bit complicated.”

Seeing Sun Ling Cai’s expression, Yuan frowned and asked, “Did something bad happen to her?”

“What? Oh, it’s nothing like that, but… it might take some time to explain, so let’s continue this chat later.” Sun Ling Cai’s gaze shifted to the sealed Demon Worshipper.

Yuan realized what she was trying to say and nodded, “You’re right. Let’s continue this conversation after we deal with the Demon Worshippers. I was going to interrogate them, but if you have other plans, please let me know.”

“That’s exactly what I was going to do,” Sun Ling Cai said. “I need to know more about their activities.”

Sect Leader Yun then spoke, “In that case, you can interrogate them at my sect. I’ll prepare a place for you.”

“Thank you.”

While they brought the Demon Worshippers to the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect, Tan Songyun asked Yuan, “What about your training?”

“It’s done. Even if I continued training at the Extreme Yin Lake, my cold resistance wouldn’t improve further… No, to be more accurate, it’ll take too long to see any decent improvements.”

“Already? It’s only been half a day since you started training at the Extreme Yin Lake…” Tan Songyun looked at him with a dumbfounded face.

Yuan silently shrugged.

Upon arriving at the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect, Sect Leader Yun sent all of the disciples to medical to ensure they were not injured before arranging a place for Sun Ling Cai and Yuan to interrogate the Demon Worshippers.

“N-No matter what you do to me, I won’t say anything!” the partially sealed Demon Worshipper exclaimed, his voice trembling with defiance as he was forcibly placed inside a prison. His body felt heavy, and his spiritual energy was completely suppressed by Yuan’s Demon Sealing Aura.

“I don’t doubt it. You probably can’t reveal anything even if you wanted to,” Sun Ling Cai calmly remarked.

It was common practice among Demon Worshippers to curse themselves, a ruthless safeguard ensuring their secrets remained buried.

These self-inflicted curses acted as a deadly failsafe—if a Demon Worshipper even attempted to reveal any information, their body would detonate instantly, reducing them to nothing but blood and ash before a single word could leave their lips. Because of this, interrogating Demon Worshippers was notoriously difficult.

Sun Ling Cai continued in a cold voice, “Unfortunately for you, I have plenty of ways to extract information from you without even needing you to open your mouth. However, these methods are quite cruel and have a high chance of killing you. Even if you somehow manage to survive, you’ll most certainly be crippled for life. These methods are too cruel to use on humans, but you’re a Demon Worshipper—pests that cannot be considered humans.”

The Demon Worshipper’s face twisted with fear and horror after hearing such words.

Sun Ling Cai turned to Yuan and asked, “You don’t mind, right?”

Yuan shook his head and said, “You can skin him alive for all I care. Once you’re done with him, I’ll unseal the others so that you can interrogate them as well.”

“Thank you for your assistance.”

In the next moment, Sun Ling Cai retrieved a massive paintbrush, its size comparable to that of a greatsword. With a swift, deliberate motion, she pressed the tip of the brush against the Demon Worshipper’s forehead.

For a brief moment, nothing happened. Then—she suddenly swiped the brush in a perfect arc, and from the Demon Worshipper’s forehead, a black orb of light was forcibly dragged out, writhing with a sinister energy.

Without hesitation, Sun Ling Cai guided the orb toward an empty scroll she had prepared. The moment the black orb entered the painting, the scroll came to life, ink flowing across its surface as a picture quickly formed. S~eaʀᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

As for the Demon Worshipper, the moment the black orb of light was forcibly extracted, he let out a bloodcurdling scream, his voice laced with sheer agony.

His face convulsed violently, with veins bulging as if something within him was being ripped apart. It was as though the very essence of his existence was being forcefully unraveled.

The hysterical screams lasted for only a brief moment before his head suddenly went limp. Just like that, the Demon Worshipper perished.

“I have extracted the memories of that Demon Worshipper, but I could only extract about ten years—his last ten years. Hopefully, there is something useful.” Sun Ling Cai said as she poured her spiritual energy into the painting.

A glowing string appeared from the painting and attached itself to Sun Ling Cai’s forehead, showing the Demon Worshipper’s last ten years of memories.

Sometime later, Sun Ling Cai opened her eyes and shook her head with a disappointed expression.

“He was just a lackey. Besides being ordered to capture female maidens, he didn’t know anything. Do you want to take a look? It’s quite nasty, so I don’t really recommend it,” Sun Ling Cai looked at Yuan.

“No, it’s fine.” Yuan politely refused. Although Sun Ling Cai acted nonchalantly, reading another’s memory was a dangerous act.

Memories were not just images and thoughts; they carried the essence of a person’s experiences, emotions, and even their will.

To extract and absorb them was to open oneself to the lingering traces of the person’s soul, an act that could be incredibly dangerous, especially when dealing with Demon Worshippers, whose very existence was steeped in corruption.

If Sun Ling Cai wasn’t careful, the malice and twisted beliefs of the Demon Worshipper could seep into her consciousness, corrupting her own thoughts and emotions.

However, Yuan wasn’t worried the memories could corrupt him. He just didn’t want to subject himself to such unpleasant memories, especially when it wasn’t necessary.

“But if it gets too hard for you, I can look at them instead,” he said.

Sun Ling Cai smiled and said, “You don’t have to worry about me. I’ve extracted hundreds of Demon Worshippers’ memories before without any issues.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1919 - 1919: Yan Hara'S Situation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1919 - 1919: Yan Hara'S Situation

“You’re a Master Demon Sealer. I don’t qualify to worry about you,” Yuan chuckled.

“That’s not true. Your Demon Sealing Aura is powerful enough to qualify as a Master Demon Sealer—if you aren’t one already.”

“I’m definitely not a Master Demon Sealer.”

“If you say so…”

Sun Ling Cai continued to extract the memories of the next Demon Worshipper after Yuan partially unsealed them.

Once again, the Demon Worshipper released an ear-piercing scream when Sun Ling Cai extracted his memories with a calm expression on her face, clearly unaffected by it.

A few moments later, the Demon Worshipper went limp, his life extinguished.

This continued for several more times—until Sun Ling Cai extracted the memories from all of the captured Demon Worshippers. In the end, only one of them had managed to survive, but he had been crippled so badly that he couldn’t even utter a word properly. However, he didn’t live long, as Sect Leader Yun ended his misery shortly after.

“Did you learn anything?” Sect Leader Yun asked afterward.

Sun Ling Cai nodded and said, “It appears there are several backgrounds involved. The Ling Family, Cheng Family, Red Saber Mountain, and the Purple Rain Monastery. Are you familiar with these names?”

Sect Leader Yun’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing these names, and she quickly said, “Of course! The Ling Family is a prominent name in the business industry. The Cheng Family are renowned for their sword techniques and for producing many powerful swordmasters. As for the Red Saber Mountain and Purple Rain Monastery, they’re both above-average sects. How are they involved?”

“From the memories I extracted, the Demon Worshippers we’ve captured belong to these places. Their positions within these places aren’t very high except for one of them, who turned out to be a sect elder of the Purple Rain Monastery.”

“Their mission was to capture females who are still maidens and bring them to a certain area within the Smoldering Desert. I’ll be heading there soon. Hopefully, I will find your missing disciples there.”

“I will come with you!” Sect Leader Yun declared without hesitation.

Sun Ling Cai raised an eyebrow and said, “I don’t doubt your strength, but the environment there is quite harsh, especially for yin-attribute cultivators such as yourself. There’s also a good chance we might encounter a demon there.”

“T-That’s…”

“I also need someone to deal with the Ling Family and the other three. You can deal with them while I deal with the Smoldering Desert.”

“I’ll come with you,” Yuan suddenly said.

Sun Ling Cai immediately nodded, “I can definitely use your help.”

Despite Yuan’s low cultivation, Sun Ling Cai couldn’t underestimate him, who could fight cultivators several realms above his cultivation.

“What about us?” Xi Meili asked.

“You should assist Sect Leader Yun,” Yuan said.

“Alright.”

“Lead the way,” Yuan said to Sun Ling Cai, who retrieved a flying treasure and gestured for him to ride it.

Yuan jumped onto the flying treasure, which took the appearance of a painting and flew away with Sun Ling Cai.

While they traveled, Sun Ling Cai spoke, “Since we have some time now, let’s continue our conversation.”

“Is Yan Hara in trouble?” Yuan asked.

“Before I answer that, I must ask you… What exactly is your relationship with the Demon Sealing Clan and Senior Sister Yan?”

“This is my relationship.”

Yuan retrieved his Elite Demon Sealer’s identification badge and showed it to her.

“You’re an Elite Demon Sealer? With that level of Demon Sealing Aura? I don’t believe it.”

“It’s been a while since I visited the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library, so I haven’t gotten the chance to improve my rank. As for my relationship with Yan Hara… Sorry if this seems rude, but I cannot fully trust you.” seaʀᴄh thё nôvel_Fire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Sun Ling Cai narrowed her eyes at Yuan’s face—mask—and fell silent.

After a moment of silence, she spoke, “Senior Sister Yan has started gathering people for her own faction within the Demon Sealing Clan—one that directly opposes the current leader. Of course, she didn’t openly admit this, but her intentions are clear to everyone.”

“I don’t know how much you know about the Demon Sealing Clan’s current leader, but he is someone who fought desperately to reach his position, and someone like that won’t let his position be threatened. Fortunately, because of the Demon Worshippers’ activity, as well as the Demon Sealing Grotto’s pressure, he has his hands full right now and cannot deal with Senior Sister Yan.”

“However, I’m afraid that once things calm down, he will start clashing with Senior Sister Yan. Although Senior Sister Yan is very powerful, with connections to many influential figures in the clan, the leader has about 90 percent of the Demon Sealing Clan on his side. It won’t be an easy fight.”

Hearing this, Yuan asked, “What about you? Are you with Yan Hara?”

“Of course. We’ve known each other even before we joined the Demon Sealing Clan. The only thing preventing us from being real sisters is our blood. That’s why I am very offended to hear you doubt me.”

“Sorry, but as you said, Yan Hara has essentially made herself a target within the Demon Sealing Clan.”

“It’s not that extreme… yet. At this moment, Senior Sister Yan is just gathering trustworthy people to her side, which isn’t against the rules or anything, and until she does something that directly clashes with the Clan Leader, she’s not in danger.”

“Where is she right now?” Yuan then asked.

“I’m not sure, but she’s definitely on a mission to deal with the Demon Worshippers right now. Hell, we’re still looking for some ridiculously powerful demon that was last spotted in the Fourth Heaven.”

Yuan’s body stiffened after hearing Sun Ling Cai’s last several words.

“Ridiculously powerful demon in the Fourth Heaven?” he repeated.

“Yes. This demon was first spotted a few years ago in the Fourth Heaven by some people from the Nine Heavens Law and Order,” Sun Ling Cai started with a shrug.

“However, the description they gave is ridiculous—and quite frankly, nobody in the Demon Sealing Clan truly believes it exists.”
