chapters.title: Chapter 1920 - 1920: Smoldering Desert
chapters.body:
Chapter 1920 - 1920: Smoldering Desert

“This is my first time hearing about such a demon. Can you tell me more about it?” Yuan asked.

Sun Ling Cai nodded and said, “Then let me tell you what the Nine Heavens Law and Order told us. They claimed this demon was only at the Spirit King level when they first encountered it. Yet, this demon was able to overpower several immortals who have restricted their cultivation to the Divine King realm.”

“The demon wielded two Soul Weapons, a spear and a sword. They even claimed the demon was able to use Enhanced Sword Aura. They also claimed that this demon was able to regenerate from complete annihilation and even had the aura of a beast. Ridiculous, isn’t it? Who would believe that such a demon would exist?”

It was common knowledge that demons don’t use weapons, let alone Soul Weapons, and if they did, these weapons would be crafted from their own blood. Moreover, a demon using Sword Aura was unheard of, much less Enhanced Sword Aura.

“I have never heard of a demon using Soul Weapons before,” Yuan remarked.

“Right?”

Sun Ling Cai suddenly fell silent.

After a slight pause, she looked at Yuan with a doubtful expression and spoke, “Now that I think about it, are you suppressing your true cultivation? There’s no way you’re only at the second level of Spirit Enlightenment, right? After all, you were able to overpower those Demon Worshippers, who were all Divine Lords.”

“I’ll leave that to your imagination,” Yuan calmly responded.

“…”

‘Even if he’s suppressed his cultivation, it still wouldn’t make sense how he was able to fight several Demon Worshippers who were four realms stronger than him…’ Sun Ling Cai thought to herself.

Even if Yuan were a Cultivation God, since he suppressed his cultivation to Spirit Enlightenment, his prowess would only be around that level. Even if his techniques were superior, it would be extremely hard to compensate for four entire realms of power difference.

‘His prowess is similar to that demon…’

Although Sun Ling Cai compared Yuan’s fighting prowess to the demon, she didn’t entertain the possibility that they were the same individual, even for a split second.

Sometime later, they arrived at the Smoldering Desert, a vast, desolate expanse where the air shimmered with intense heat and the landscape stretched endlessly with blazing sand dunes.

There was no sign of civilization—only scorching winds, searing sunlight, and the ever-present danger of magical beasts that had long adapted to the harsh environment.

“How are you feeling? If the heat becomes too unbearable, let me know,” Sun Ling Cai said as she veiled her body with a thick layer of spiritual energy that was constantly being melted away from the intense heat.

“I’ll be fine,” Yuan calmly responded. Due to his Fire Harmony, not only did he not feel the heat, but it was even comfortable for him.

‘The way the heat is naturally being absorbed into his body… no way… he’s achieved Fire Harmony…?’ Sun Ling Cai swallowed nervously after realizing this.

There was almost no doubt in her mind that Yuan was hiding his true cultivation, as she couldn’t fathom someone at the Spirit Enlightenment achieving Fire Harmony.

“Once we arrive at our destination, if we encounter any demons, please let me handle them. I’m not doubting your ability to seal demons, but I am on a mission, and I am technically your senior. However, if there is more than one demon, I’ll probably need your help,” Sun Ling Cai said as they neared their destination.

Yuan nodded, “I’ll deal with the Demon Worshippers and secure the kidnapped victims—if they are still alive—while you deal with the demon.”

Sometime later, Sun Ling Cai stopped the flying treasure above an empty area and said as she looked down, “They are beneath the sand. I can sense them. Out of the 42 presences I sense, only three of them have demonic energy. It’s safe to assume that the other 39 are all kidnapped individuals.”

She continued, “As for the three with demonic energy… I’m certain that one of them is a demon. The other two are just Demon Worshippers, but they are both at the Divine Ancestor level. I’ll leave them to you.”

“Sure,” Yuan agreed without hesitation.

“I’m going in.”

Sun Ling Cai tilted the flying treasure, angling it sharply until its front pointed directly toward the ground. S~eaʀᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Without hesitation, it plunged downward, cutting through the sand like a blade through silk.

As they descended, the surface layer of sand gave way to reveal a hidden subterranean chamber—a spacious area filled with towering pillars of compressed sand, sculpted over time.

To their surprise, the scorching heat of the desert vanished almost instantly upon entry. Down here, the air was cool and still, a stark contrast to the inferno above, as if they had crossed into an entirely different world beneath the sand.

The 42 individuals also came into view.

Thirty-nine of them were bound tightly by black chains, their spiritual energy completely suppressed, leaving them weak and unable to resist. They sat or lay scattered across the sandy ground, expressions filled with exhaustion, fear, or quiet defiance.

Not far from them, two humans stood watch. However, they seemed relaxed, their postures casual and unfocused—as if they didn’t see the captives as a threat, nor expected any kind of interruption.

“I’ll leave them to you,” Sun Ling Cai said.

“Of course.”

Yuan immediately jumped off the flying treasure, landing between the two Demon Worshippers and the captives. Meanwhile, Sun Ling Cai flew toward the demon, who was slumbering not too far away.

“What the fuck?! Who are you?! And how did you find this place?!” The Demon Worshippers were immediately alerted by their sudden invasion, but they quickly relaxed upon seeing Yuan’s cultivation base.

“Your friends from the Red Saber Mountain told us,” Yuan said as he retrieved Number One Under Heaven, seemingly intent on finishing this as soon as possible.

“The Red Saber Mountain?! Bullshit! There’s no way they would betray us!” The Demon Worshippers exclaimed as they also withdrew their weapons.

chapters.title: Chapter 1921 - 1921: Rescuing The Captives
chapters.body:
Chapter 1921 - 1921: Rescuing The Captives

The moment the Demon Worshippers retrieved their weapons, Yuan wasted no time attacking them. Coincidentally, the Demon Worshippers were also wielding swords, and they were bewildered when they failed to activate their swords’ ability to block Yuan’s attack, almost as if an unseen force had sealed their swords.

“Wha—?!”

“My sword?!”

Yuan took advantage of their brief moment of shock and landed two swift and seemingly non-lethal strikes.

At first, the Demon Worshippers didn’t think much of the superficial injury they had received—a minor scratch at best. With a sneer, they quickly backed away from Yuan, unconcerned.

But mere moments later, their expressions began to change.

A strange stiffness crept through their limbs, slow at first, then rapidly intensifying. Their movements grew sluggish, their muscles locking up as an unnatural weight settled over their bodies.

Panic flickered in their eyes as they realized the truth. Their bodies were turning to stone—they were being sealed.

“T-This is—?!”

The two Demon Worshippers couldn’t believe how quickly they had been defeated. And it was done by a mere Spirit Enlightenment cultivator. Yet, neither of them appeared too concerned for some reason.

They looked at Yuan and sneered coldly, “Don’t think this is the end. You’ll realize what a horrible mistake you’ve made so—”

“Our Master will not let this—”

Their sentence was interrupted when they were fully sealed.

After sealing the Demon Worshippers, Yuan went to help the captives by removing the black chains that suppressed their cultivation.

Meanwhile, Sun Ling Cai immediately began working on sealing the demon after disturbing its rest.

The demon didn’t have any visible crystal on its outer appearance, meaning it was at least a Demon General. It also had the cultivation of a peak Immortal Ascension.

Sun Ling Cai was also at the peak of Immortal Ascension since that was the limit of the Sixth Heaven. Yet, she wasn’t worried about her opponent being a Demon General.

Sun Ling Cai fought with an elegance that bordered on artistry, almost as if she were painting. Her massive brush moved through the air in fluid, sweeping strokes, each motion resembling the graceful flourish of a master painter.

But instead of ink and parchment, her canvas was the demon before her, and each stroke of the brush carried the weight of Demon Sealing Aura.

Unlike Yuan, whose Demon Sealing Aura was powerful enough to seal demons near his level in a single strike, Sun Ling Cai had to slowly accumulate it in the demon, slowly sealing it.

While Sun Ling Cai wasn’t overwhelming her opponent, she wasn’t pressured by the demon either and even had the leisure to speak while she fought it.

“You demons and your worshippers have been lurking in the shadows since the Demon Sealing Clan defeated most of the demons during the Demonic Era,” Sun Ling Cai said coldly, her brush continuously striking the demon.

“What changed? Why are your worshippers suddenly acting so bold and brazen?”

The demon, despite slowly being sealed, smiled provocatively and responded in an amused tone, “A new era is coming.”

“What?” Sun Ling Cai frowned at such ominous words. “Do you really think demons will rule the world again? How foolish! The only reason you demons even managed to rule humans during the Demonic Era was due to the massive casualties caused by the Heavenly War of Immortals and Gods that greatly diminished our population of powerhouses!”

“Our numbers are not only much greater than before, but our strength has also improved drastically since then—incomparably so! Meanwhile, all you demons have done since your defeat is hide and starve in the shadows!”

The demon laughed uncontrollably at Sun Ling Cai’s words, which wasn’t wrong. If the Heavenly War of Immortals and Gods hadn’t claimed so many lives, humans might not have been enslaved by the demons.

Although demons are immortal, that immortality does not equate to limitless power. Like humans and beasts, demons require resources to grow—specifically flesh and blood, the raw essence of life. Without it, their strength stagnates and slowly decays.

Since the end of the Demonic Era, demons have been forced into hiding with few opportunities to feed or cultivate their strength. Meanwhile, humankind has flourished, building powerful sects, expanding across realms, and growing ever stronger with access to abundant spiritual resources.

“What you experienced and witnessed during the Demonic Era was only a fraction of our Demon Clan’s true power!” the demon roared, its voice laced with hatred and madness.

“Soon, you livestock will learn what true fear is—and once again, you will live beneath our feet like the worms you are!”

Its eyes gleamed with a sinister light as it stepped forward, unfazed by the Demon Sealing Aura assaulting it.

“You may have defeated us back then, but it was only because of the Divine Paragon! Without him, you humans are nothing! Worthless insects!”

Then, its gaze narrowed on Sun Ling Cai and continued with a mocking sneer.

“And you so-called Demon Sealers… You’ve grown weak—so much weaker than before! Your end is coming… and this time, there will be no one to save you! Hahahaha…!”

A chilling laugh erupted from its throat right before it was fully sealed.

Although she had successfully sealed the demon, Sun Ling Cai felt no relief, no sense of victory or pride. If anything, a heavy unease settled over her heart.

She took a deep breath and sighed, “Whatever these bastards are up to, we’ll definitely stop it!”

Meanwhile, moments before Sun Ling Cai sealed the demon.

“Everything will be fine now,” Yuan said to the captives as he released them.

“W-What about the demon?” one of them asked.

“I came here with a Master Demon Sealer from the Demon Sealing Clan, and she’s dealing with it now. There won’t be any issue since there’s only one of them.”

“N-No!” The young lady’s face exclaimed, her voice filled with horror. “There is more than one demon here!”

Just as Sun Ling Cai turned to leave, her body suddenly froze mid-step.

A new presence had emerged—not far from where she stood—and it was like a crushing wave of malice crashing down upon her senses. Sёarᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Her instincts screamed in alarm. This wasn’t just another demon. Its aura was unlike anything she had ever encountered. It was cold, suffocating, and utterly overwhelming, filled with a primordial hatred that seemed to rot the very air around it.

A chill ran down her spine.

Something terrifying had arrived.

chapters.title: Chapter 1922 - 1922: The Sudden Appearance Of A Demon Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 1922 - 1922: The Sudden Appearance Of A Demon Emperor

After sensing the overwhelming malicious presence, Sun Ling Cai had to force herself to turn around. Every instinct in her body warned her to run, yet she stood her ground.

At first, she saw nothing—just the empty, distorted air ahead, heavy with oppressive energy. But then, a silhouette began to take shape, emerging slowly from the shadows as if reality itself were bending to make way for it.

Each step it took was deliberate, and with every step, the pressure grew heavier, colder—like a noose tightening around her soul.

‘A second demon? How is that possible?! My divine sense has been active and surrounding this place ever since we entered, yet I only just sensed it! Did it teleport in here just now?’ Sun Ling Cai couldn’t fathom how she could’ve missed this demon, who was emitting a ridiculous amount of demonic energy.

“To think I’d return to intruders… I only left for a couple of minutes, too.”

The silhouette spoke as it emerged from the shadow, revealing its appearance.

Just like the Demon General, this demon did not appear to have a crystal outside its body. However, its demonic energy and presence was several times stronger than the Demon General, which could only mean one thing.

“A Demon Emperor…” Sun Ling Cai muttered under her breath, her voice barely audible, laced with tension.

She clenched her fists tightly, her jaw locking as pure fear crawled down her spine.

After spending just a few moments in its presence, she began to recognize the undeniable familiarity in the aura. It was nearly identical to the one she had felt during the Supreme Demon Sealer Examination a few years ago.

The Demon Emperor before Sun Ling Cai was not the same one at the examination. It was a new one that she’d never heard of or seen before.

‘Fuck, what should I do?’ Sun Ling Cai could only stand there and ponder her limited options as the Demon Emperor slowly approached.

Although both she and the Demon Emperor had their cultivation sealed to the peak of Immortal Ascension, Sun Ling Cai wasn’t confident she’d be able to face this Demon Emperor even if she had her full cultivation and the demon didn’t.

She could try running away, but that would bring shame to her status as a Master Demon Sealer, not to mention Yuan and the captives were not far away from her. If she ran, they would most certainly die.

The Demon Emperor suddenly stopped before the sealed Demon General. After a brief pause, it placed its palm on the sealed demon and began infusing demonic energy into it. A moment later, cracks began appearing on the sealed demon.

‘It’s unsealing the other demon?!’

Seeing this, Sun Ling Cai instinctively attacked the Demon Emperor.

Without even glancing in Sun Ling Cai’s direction, the Demon Emperor casually flicked its hand, a motion so effortless it almost seemed dismissive.

In that instant, a massive wave of demonic energy surged forth—thick, black, and seething with malice.

It struck Sun Ling Cai like a collapsing mountain, the sheer force of it leaving no room to resist. Her body was violently repelled, sent flying through the air as if she were nothing more than a leaf caught in a storm.

By the time Sun Ling Cai stabilized herself, which only took a second, the sealed demon was released from its stone prison.

“I apologize for my incompetence, Emperor.” The Demon General kneeled before the Demon Emperor.

The Demon Emperor calmly gazed upon the demon and spoke, “Take care of the other invader. I’ll deal with this livestock myself.”

“As you command.” Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The Demon General left without any delay, ignoring Sun Ling Cai, who could only watch as it disappeared.

‘He should be fine…’ Sun Ling Cai wasn’t too worried for Yuan, who she was sure was hiding his true cultivation.

‘I won’t be able to defeat this Demon Emperor by myself, so I can only try to hold it off until he defeats that Demon General…’

Sun Ling Cai could only rely on Yuan’s assistance to defeat this Demon Emperor, but even that seemed unlikely.

According to what she heard from others in the Demon Sealing Clan, it took their Clan Leader, as well as two Supreme Demon Sealers, to capture the other Demon Emperor, and they were all at full strength.

Meanwhile, she was only a Master Demon Sealer. It’d take a miracle for her to hold off the Demon Emperor for a few minutes, let alone seal it.

‘Let’s just focus on defense until Xiao Yang arrives…’ Sun Ling Cai thought to herself as she prepared to fight the Demon Emperor, who merely stood there in silence.

At the same time, the Demon General appeared before Yuan and the captives, who were trembling in fear due to the Demon Emperor’s presence that filled the entire space there. The pressure was so terrifying and suffocating that the captives couldn’t even stand up despite their cultivation returning, let alone escape.

“Spirit Enlightenment? How did a weakling like you seal those two idiots?” The Demon General narrowed its eyes at Yuan after accessing the situation, its gaze filled with confusion.

“Don’t worry, you’ll experience it for yourself in just a moment,” Yuan calmly said.

“Hmph! Ridiculous!” The Demon General sneered.

Yuan turned to face the captives and said, “Close your eyes. All of you. It’ll be over in just a moment.”

Hearing his soothing voice, calm and steady like a gentle breeze, the captives felt an unexpected sense of comfort wash over them. Without realizing it, they all began to close their eyes tightly.

“Normally, I’d use you to entertain me before I kill you. Unfortunately, I don’t have the time today since I need to help that girl as soon as possible, so if you don’t mind—”

The Demon General interrupted with a cold laugh, “Using me as entertainment? I should be saying that to you!”

In a fit of rage, the Demon General lunged toward Yuan—or at least, it tried to.

But the moment it moved, its body suddenly froze, locked in place by an unseen force.

Confusion flashed across its face as it looked down to find several sword-shaped lights piercing through its body, glowing with pure, radiant energy that sizzled against its flesh.

“W-What? Where did this come from?” it muttered in a daze, eyes wide with disbelief as it sensed an unfamiliar sensation—pain.

[Swords of Eternal Torment]

When the Demon General lifted its head up again, Yuan was already standing right before it with a humble-looking red sword in his grasp.

The Demon General’s eyes widened some more after sensing the terrifying Demon Sealing Aura being emitted by the sword, and its body trembled in fear as it recalled this forgotten feeling called fear.

“T-That sword! Why do you—”

Yuan flicked his arm, and with a surreal grace, he swung the blade in a perfect horizontal arc, its path tracing cleanly across the demon’s neck.

The movement was strange. Slow and fast all at once. There was no wasted motion, no dramatic flourish, and the demon’s head came cleanly off, its body completely sealed in an instant, long before its head could even reach the ground.

Thud.

The demon’s head hit the sand with a muffled thud, sinking slightly into the soft grains, and its face was locked in a terrified expression, wide eyes filled with the dawning horror of realization.

chapters.title: Chapter 1923 - 1923: Facing The Demon Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 1923 - 1923: Facing The Demon Emperor

After sealing the Demon General, Yuan said to the captives trembling in fear behind him, “You can open your eyes again. I have dealt with the demon.”

When the captives opened their eyes, their faces became filled with shock after seeing the sealed demon. After all, only a few seconds had passed since they were told to close their eyes.

‘He sealed a Demon General in seconds?! How is that even possible?!’

The captives couldn’t comprehend it.

Yuan continued, “Unfortunately, there is still a demon that needs to be dealt with. Stay here and don’t move until I return. Otherwise, I won’t be able to guarantee your safety.”

Without waiting for their response, Yuan left the scene and moved toward the Demon Emperor’s presence.

Meanwhile, Sun Ling Cai started her battle with the Demon Emperor shortly after the Demon General left.

“If you’re not going to attack, I’ll come to you,” the Demon Emperor spoke as it slowly approached Sun Ling Cai.

Gritting her teeth, Sun Ling Cai quickly pulled out several paintings and threw them into the air. They floated around them, forming a circle. Moments later, the scrolls opened up, releasing a wave of Demon Sealing Aura that created a protective barrier.

The purpose of the barrier was to weaken any demons caught within, much like Yuan’s Demon Sealing Zone. However, the Demon Emperor didn’t so much as flinch, standing unaffected as though the aura had no effect on it at all.

“You Demon Sealers have truly fallen. Compared to those of the past, your Demon Sealing Aura is laughably weak,” the Demon Emperor said, his voice laced with both mockery and disappointment.

Sun Ling Cai did her best to ignore the Demon Emperor’s taunts and continued to bombard it with Demon Sealing Aura. But with the Demon Emperor treating her attacks as no more than empty air, the vast gap between them quickly became undeniable—and overwhelming.

The Demon Emperor suddenly vanished from Sun Ling Cai’s sight, and in the blink of an eye, before she could even react, she found herself sprawled on the ground, her limbs twisted at unnatural angles.

Sun Ling Cai coughed up a mouthful of blood.

‘W-What just happened?!’ she screamed inwardly.

Lifting her gaze, she saw the Demon Emperor standing over her, its eyes cold and condescending—like it was merely observing a crushed insect.

“Consider yourself fortunate. I’d kill you, but your Pure Yin Essence will serve my goals far too well,” the Demon Emperor remarked coldly.

“What do you need my Pure Yin Essence for…?” she asked, her voice strained.

Despite the seemingly brutal state of her body, such injuries were superficial for an Immortal like Sun Ling Cai. As long as her Dantian and soul remained unharmed, she could easily recover from such injuries.

“What do you think? I’m going to use your Pure Yin Essence to refine the ‘key’.”

“Key…? Are you trying to unseal the Demonic Realm?!” she exclaimed.

“Oh? I guess you’re not that clueless, after all.”

“Your plan won’t succeed! The Demon Sealing Clan will never allow the Demonic Realm to open!” Sun Ling Cai gritted her teeth.

“Hahaha! The Demon Sealing Clan? You speak as if you guys have the same power as before! Without the Divine Paragon, you guys are powerless!”

“W-We’re not alone!”

“Are you talking about the Demon Sealing Grotto? Even if there are a hundred of you, it won’t change anything. If you want to stop us, you better dig the Divine Paragon out of his grave or something!”

‘Dig the Divine Paragon from his grave? We never even saw his corpse…’ Sun Ling Cai sighed inwardly.

The Divine Paragon had vanished without a trace at the end of the Demonic Era. No matter how many resources were poured into searching for him, not a single clue was ever found.

“It may sound insane, but I truly regret that the Divine Paragon is no longer around,” the Demon Emperor said, eyes fixed on the sand-carved ceiling. “The only reason I’ve made it this far is because of the hatred I bear for that man. I’d give anything to watch him on his knees, begging for his life…”

“That’s not quite right.”

A new voice echoed through the air, calm yet unmistakably firm.

The Demon Emperor’s eyes narrowed as he turned toward the masked figure standing silently in the distance.

“You’re only standing there now because you ran away—tail tucked between your legs like a frightened cat.”

Yuan spoke calmly as he walked toward them, his eyes fixed on Sun Ling Cai, who was lying on the ground with all of her limbs twisted in a gruesome manner. However, he wasn’t too concerned about her condition since he could tell that her life wasn’t in danger.

“What happened to the demon I sent after you? I can’t sense his presence—but there’s no way he would’ve fled,” the Demon Emperor questioned Yuan after realizing this, not even entertaining the possibility that the Demon General had been sealed in such a short time by a Spirit Enlightenment cultivator.

“And I can’t just ignore what you said. Ran away like a frightened cat? You’ll regret those words.”

The Demon Emperor remained composed, its calm demeanor betraying utter confidence—like it could crush an insect like Yuan whenever it wished.

“Isn’t it the truth, though? Once you realized you couldn’t defeat me, you—along with ‘Carnage’ and that other Demon Emperor—fled like cowards.”

The Demon Emperor’s expression darkened. It wasn’t because Yuan had struck a nerve with facts or that he’d mentioned Demon Emperor Carnage by his demon name, something only very few knew, but because he spoke as if they had been the ones retreating from him. seaʀᴄh thё ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Xiao Yang…? What are you…?” Sun Ling Cai muttered, her eyes wide with disbelief.

Yuan met her gaze and said softly, “Thank you for not running away and risking your life to face this Demon Emperor. I’ll take it from here.”

Then, without another word, he raised his hand to his mask and slowly removed it.

The moment the Demon Emperor saw Yuan’s handsome face, its expression contorted with shock and instinctive fear. Without hesitation, it leapt away from Sun Ling Cai’s side and distanced itself.

“D-Divine Paragon?!” it exclaimed afterward.

chapters.title: Chapter 1924 - 1924: Demon Sealing Sword
chapters.body:
Chapter 1924 - 1924: Demon Sealing Sword

Sun Ling Cai stared at Yuan’s face, stunned. It was identical to the statue enshrined in the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library—handsome, timeless, and unmistakable.

It had been years since Sun Ling Cai last saw Yuan, and in that time, he had matured. He no longer merely resembled the Divine Paragon—he was his exact likeness, down to the last detail.

“F-Founder…? That’s impossible…”

Sun Ling Cai had spent many years gazing at the Divine Paragon’s statue since she joined the Demon Sealing Clan, and as a painter who painted countless pieces of him, there was no way she could mistake his features. Yet even now, she struggled to believe what her eyes were showing her.

“Your first instinct is to run again? I thought you wanted to see me, Demon Emperor ‘Slaughter’,” Yuan said coldly as he walked toward Sun Ling Cai’s battered body.

When Yuan reached her side, he retrieved a recovery pill and gently placed it in her mouth. Just as Sun Ling Cai had once been forced to watch the Demon Emperor free the Demon General from his seal, Demon Emperor Slaughter could now only watch helplessly as Yuan healed her—its body paralyzed by fear.

“Founder… is it really you? Where have you been this entire time?” Sun Ling Cai asked, her voice trembling as tears streamed down her face.

A faint smile touched Yuan’s lips as he replied calmly, “I’ll explain everything once I’ve handled our little situation here.”

After treating Sun Ling Cai, Yuan turned to face the Demon Emperor, who looked like it had just seen a ghost.

“Why do you look so surprised to see me? Didn’t Demon Emperor Carnage tell of my return?” Yuan asked, having assumed the news would’ve spread after their brief encounter years ago.

“Carnage knew?! Impossible! We spoke not long ago! He never mentioned anything about your return!” Demon Emperor Slaughter exclaimed.

“He didn’t? How odd. Well, whatever reason he has for keeping it to himself, I’m not complaining. This works better for me anyway.”

“You hateful bastard… how the fuck are you still alive?! It’s been hundreds of millions of years since the Demonic Era ended! No human should live that long!”

Yuan narrowed his eyes. “If you demons—beings beneath me—can live that long, then why shouldn’t I? In case you forgot, let me remind you something—that I won’t take my last breath until I eradicate every single demon in existence!”

Sure enough, Demon Emperor Slaughter can still vividly recall the Divine Paragon always uttering such words whenever they clashed.

“No! I don’t know why you look like the Divine Paragon, but you can’t be him! Right! You’re only at the level of Spirit Enlightenment, and I can tell that’s your real cultivation!”

“Since when did my cultivation ever matter?” Yuan scoffed. “I wiped out half your race before I even stepped into the Immortal Realm.”

Demon Emperor Slaughter clenched its jaw in frustration, the bitter memory surfacing once more. During the Demonic Era, the Divine Paragon had defied all logic—slaughtering demons with a cultivation far beneath theirs, turning their pride into shame.

Unlike battles between cultivators, fighting demons hinged on one thing—the strength of one’s Demon Sealing Aura, and in that regard, no one even came close to the Divine Paragon.

“Since you refuse to believe I’ve truly returned, there’s only one option left… I’ll beat the reality into you.”

Yuan retrieved the crimson blade from his spatial ring again—the very sword he had used to seal the Demon General.

“T-That sword! The Demon Sealing Sword!”

Demon Emperor Slaughter trembled at the sight of it. That weapon had sealed countless demons, and its presence alone stirred deep, ancestral fear within its core.

“The Demon Sealing Sword?! The strongest of the Three Demon Sealing Treasures?!” Sun Ling Cai gasped, just as stunned by its appearance. No one had seen the legendary weapon since the Divine Paragon vanished, and most had long believed it was lost with him.

Upon sensing the Demon Emperor’s presence, the Demon Sealing Sword began to tremble, as if quivering with anticipation, eager to taste demonic blood once more.

“It’s been too long since you had a proper meal, hasn’t it?” Yuan murmured to the blade. “Don’t worry… I’ll give you a feast soon.”

Yuan then retrieved another treasure from his spatial ring, and it was another ring.

“That ring…”

It took her a moment, but Sun Ling Cai recognized the ring. It was a mysterious treasure that had long rested within the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library treasury. Though its purpose remained unknown, it had astronomical value because it was once owned by the Divine Paragon.

Sure enough, this ring was the Unknown Sealed Ring Yuan had acquired in the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library treasury for 2,000 points after passing his Elite Demon Sealer Examination, and other than the Divine Paragon himself, nobody knew of its true purpose.

Once the ring was in his grasp, Yuan bit his lip until blood welled up, then pressed a gentle kiss to the surface.

<Unknown Sealed Ring has absorbed your blood>

<Unknown Sealed Ring’s seal has been lifted> S~eaʀᴄh the novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<Unknown Sealed Ring reveals its true self>

The Unknown Sealed Ring suddenly shattered into countless fragments, each shard drawn toward the Demon Sealing Sword before being absorbed into the blade as if returning to where it truly belonged.

After absorbing the ring, the Demon Sealing Sword erupted with an overwhelming surge of Demon Sealing Aura. The aura was so potent that it began sealing Demon Emperor Slaughter merely by proximity.

<Demon Sealing Sword has awakened>

<Demon Sealing Sword has changed name, becoming Demon Devouring Sword>

<Demon Devouring Sword’s Grade has increased>

[Demon Devouring Sword]

[Grade: Celestial]

[Quality: Peak]

[Requirement: Can only be wielded by Player Yuan.]

[Description: Once wielded by the Divine Paragon. Not only does it seal demons, but it can also devour them, using their demonic energy to strengthen itself.]

[Unique Ability: When wielding the Demon Devouring Sword, all Demon Sealing Techniques will have their power amplified by 666,666%.]

[Unique Ability(2): The Demon Devouring Sword can devour demons and their souls, turning them into its own strength.]

[Unique Ability(3): You can summon the soul of demons devoured by the Demon Devouring Sword. Qi consumption increases based on the summoned demon’s strength.]

[Unique Ability(4): When Demon Devouring Sword is out, all demons within its vicinity will be greatly weakened, and all demons below Demon Emperor will have their abilities sealed.]

chapters.title: Chapter 1925 - 1925: Demon Devouring Sword
chapters.body:
Chapter 1925 - 1925: Demon Devouring Sword

“Did you know? The Demon Sealing Sword was once an ordinary sword that evolved through the demon blood it was constantly soaked in.”

Yuan calmly revealed this information as he looked at the sword in his grasp. If it hadn’t been protected by his Demon Sealing Aura, the Demon Sealing Sword would’ve most likely evolved into a cursed treasure instead.

‘I need to run!’

At the sight of the Demon Sealing Sword, the Demon Emperor abandoned all thoughts of battle. Fear took hold, and it began desperately searching for a way to escape.

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the Demon Emperor, a grin tugging at his lips.

“That look… Trying to run again?”

The Demon Emperor’s entire being trembled at his words, shock rippling through it. Instinct took over, and it bolted—only to crash against an invisible barrier. Yuan had activated the Demon Sealing Zone long before, silently trapping it within.

“HAHAHAHA!!!”

Yuan suddenly burst out laughing, his voice loud enough to shake the space around them.

“F-Founder…?” Startled, Sun Ling Cai’s body jumped slightly.

“This is too hilarious! If only you had remained inside your hole after I allowed you to escape the last time, you wouldn’t be in this situation, Demon Emperor! If you think you can escape from me a second time, think again!”

Yuan chased after the Demon Emperor with the Demon Devouring Sword in his grasp, its blade trembling from anticipation.

“Devour this coward!”

Yuan swung his sword from a distance. In the very next moment, the Demon Emperor’s right arm vanished—erased from existence, as if devoured by an unseen force.

Normally, a wound like this would be meaningless to a demon, as their god-like regeneration would restore the damage in an instant. But this time, the Demon Emperor’s arm remained gone, as if it had been erased from existence itself.

“Fuck! You goddamn bastard, Divine Paragon!” The Demon Emperor roared angrily. Knowing it could not escape, it decided to fight to the death instead and lunged at Yuan.

“That’s more like it!”

[Dragon Transformation!]

Although Yuan activated Dragon Transformation to bridge the gap in cultivation, the Demon Emperor still held the advantage, remaining slightly faster and stronger than him. Fortunately, the Demon Emperor was missing a limb, and Yuan’s regeneration rivaled demons.

Of course, Yuan’s most significant advantage was his overwhelming Demon Sealing Aura, which greatly weakened the Demon Emperor’s combat prowess. Without it, Yuan wouldn’t have stood a chance against it, much less dominate it.

Meanwhile, Sun Ling Cai watched their clash with a dazed, almost dreamlike gaze. When she snapped out of it, she retrieved a blank scroll and her brush. Despite the lingering pain in her still-healing arms, she began to paint, capturing the battle unfolding before her.

‘This is it! I was born to witness and paint this scene!’

Sun Ling Cai’s eyes sparkled with fervor, her heart pounding, her blood ablaze. Every detail of the battle seared itself into her soul as she poured it all into her painting.

As Yuan and the Demon Emperor continued clashing, the sheer force of their battle grew overwhelming. The surrounding landscape began to crumble under the pressure, collapsing in on itself as their power tore through everything in its path.

Seeing the devastation unfold, Sun Ling Cai quickly used her spiritual energy to shield the captives from afar, even transporting them to safety. Yet she herself remained in the heart of the battlefield, unmoving—unwilling to miss a single moment, even if it meant risking her life to witness and capture history in the making.

“Stop toying with me, Divine Paragon!”

The Demon Emperor suddenly roared. It knew Yuan was only messing around since he hasn’t used ‘devour’ again after the first one.

“Are you in that much of a hurry to die? Then allow me to fulfil your wish!”

[Devour!]

[Devour!]

[Devour!]

In the blink of an eye, the Demon Emperor’s remaining limbs were devoured by the Demon Devouring Sword.

‘What kind of ability is that?! Does it have no limit?!’

Sun Ling Cai swallowed hard, her nerves trembling at the Demon Devouring Sword’s godlike power. She had believed herself well-versed in its history, having studied every record available. But witnessing it in action shattered that illusion. There was still so much the world didn’t know about the Divine Paragon and his Demon Sealing Treasures.

“THIS ISN’T OVER YET, DIVINE PARAGON!”

Even with all its limbs erased from existence, the Demon Emperor refused to yield. Its mutilated form still radiated overwhelming menace and bloodlust. Hovering in the air, its body twisted unnaturally, and from its back, two massive, blood-soaked wings burst forth, beating with raw, defiant fury.

“ENDLESS SLAUGHTER!”

A crimson barrier, formed from blood and eerily reminiscent of Demon Emperor Carnage’s Blood Sphere, suddenly materialized, encasing both Yuan and the Demon Emperor within its ominous glow.

However, before the barrier could fully form, Yuan slashed through it with ease, tearing it apart like fragile paper.

“What do you think you’re doing?” he said coldly, his voice laced with offense. “That trick might’ve worked on other Demon Sealers, but who do you think you’re facing? Are you looking down on me, you demon bastard?”

“You fucking monster…” The Demon Emperor gritted its teeth.

Yuan grinned at its words. Sёarch* The NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Let me show you what a real monster looks like…”

With a single vertical slash, Yuan tore through the air, rending open a gap in space itself.

In the next moment, a figure emerged from the rift, its presence radiating an ominous aura eerily similar to the Demon Emperor’s, heavy with malice and bloodlust.

Demon Emperor Slaughter’s eyes widened in shock the moment it saw the figure. It wasn’t just familiar—it was someone it had once fought alongside, a former comrade from an era long past.

“Demon Emperor Malice…” Demon Emperor Slaughter muttered in a dazed voice.

The presence Yuan had summoned with the Demon Devouring Sword was none other than Demon Emperor Malice, one of the Nine Demon Emperors he had slain during the Demonic Era.

chapters.title: Chapter 1926 - 1926: Devouring The Demon Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 1926 - 1926: Devouring The Demon Emperor

‘That level of demonic energy… another Demon Emperor has appeared?! What is happening?!’ Sun Ling Cai’s jaw dropped after seeing Demon Emperor Malice’s sudden appearance. It was so shocking that it forced her to stop painting briefly.

“You probably already know this, but in case you don’t, ‘Malice’ was the first Demon Emperor to be slain by me. Although Malice wasn’t the strongest amongst the Demon Emperors, he came the closest to defeating me—mostly because I was still inexperienced back then.” Yuan looked back at their battle as his gaze stared at Demon Emperor Malice, who stood there with a blank look on its face, resembling a soulless puppet.

He gave a simple gesture, beckoning the figure forward.

In response, Demon Emperor Malice stepped toward him—and without hesitation, dropped to its knees, then lowered itself further until it was bowing low, its forearms to the ground in a show of absolute submission.

Demon Emperor Slaughter’s eyes widened in a mix of shock and fury as it watched Yuan calmly sit atop Demon Emperor Malice’s back—using its fellow Demon Emperor as if it were nothing more than a chair.

“How dare you!” Demon Emperor Slaughter roared, his aura skyrocketing from rage.

“Why wouldn’t I dare? Once I devour you in a moment, I’ll make you wash my clothes or something,” Yuan casually mocked it.

“YOU AND I—THE DEMON CLAN—CANNOT COEXIST UNDER THE SAME SKY!”

Demon Emperor Slaughter roared, unleashing a torrent of bloodlust so overwhelming it seemed to shake the very air around them.

A blood-red aura erupted around Demon Emperor Slaughter as it launched itself at Yuan, recklessly hurling its body forward—completely disregarding its own safety in a frenzy of rage.

“Why are you stating the obvious? The first thing you demons did after appearing in our world is treat humans as livestock.” Yuan sneered as he stood up from his seat and prepared to face the approaching Demon Emperor.

[Demon Devouring Strike]

As the Demon Emperor closed in, Yuan swung the Demon Devouring Sword without hesitation. In the blink of an eye, mid-attack, the Demon Emperor vanished—its entire existence seemingly erased, as if it had never been there at all.

<Demon Devouring Sword has devoured Demon Emperor Slaughter>

<Demon Emperor Slaughter can now be summoned through the Demon Devouring Sword>

<Demon Devouring Sword has refined the demonic energy into spiritual energy, which can be absorbed at any time>

“D-Did you defeat the Demon Emperor?” Sun Ling Cai suddenly asked, breaking the silence.

“Yes, it’s dealt with now,” Yuan calmly responded.

“No way…” Sun Ling Cai swallowed nervously.

Despite witnessing it herself, she still couldn’t believe how easily Yuan had defeated the Demon Emperor, an existence that could threaten the entire Demon Sealing Clan.

“Are you okay?” Yuan asked as he approached her.

“Y-Yes!” When Sun Ling Cai realized that she was still sitting on the floor, she quickly stood up. “Thank you for saving my life, Founder.”

“About that founder thing…”

Yuan retrieved the necklace Sun Ling Cai had given him before and showed it to her.

“Huh? That necklace…” Sun Ling Cai’s eyebrows rose, looking confused, but not because she didn’t recognize the necklace.

“But I gave that to the Little Founder…” she muttered.

Yuan smiled at her words and explained, “That’s right. It’s me, Yuan.”

“W-What…?” Sun Ling Cai stared at his handsome face with a dumbfounded expression on her face.

“I’m Yuan,” he repeated in a firmer tone. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“But…”

“I know what you’re thinking. I am Yuan, but I am also the Divine Paragon. I am his reincarnation.”

Sun Ling Cai gazed at Yuan, her face blank with disbelief—until the weight of what she’d just heard sank in, and her expression shifted into utter shock.

At that moment, all Sun Ling Cai could think about were the jokes made about Yuan being the reincarnation of the Divine Paragon because of his uncanny resemblance. What once seemed like idle talk amongst the Divine Paragon Fanatics had turned out to be the truth.

“I apologize for tricking you. I had to make sure you were trustworthy since my existence could drastically affect everyone in the Demon Sealing Clan.”

Sun Ling Cai shook her head and said, “You don’t have to apologize. I completely understand. However, I must ask… does Senior Sister Yan know about this?”

“Yes, she knows of my true identity, and you’re the second person within the Demon Sealing Clan to know.”

“Hm? Wait a second… Is that why she started gathering people?”

He nodded and explained, “We plan on removing the current Clan Leader from his position; that’s why she’s gathering trustworthy people.”

“Qian Chu? Is there any particular reason why? Of course, I don’t have any issues with it. In fact, I believe you should lead the Demon Sealing Clan again.”

“Do you remember that incident during the Supreme Demon Sealer Examination?”

“Of course. It only happened a few years ago, and because of that incident, our relationship with the Demon Sealing Grotto has hit rock bottom. We’re on the verge of war right now… No, we’re already past that point…”

“Well, I don’t know why, but Qian Chu planned that to happen. Moreover, there’s something off about him. I can’t pinpoint it, but that’s what my instincts are telling me.”

“A-Are you saying that Qian Chu, the current leader of the Demon Sealing Clan, is a demon?!” Sun Ling Cai exclaimed.

“No, there’s no proof that he’s a demon, nor am I saying he’s a demon. However, there’s definitely something odd about his existence. I’ll try to look deeper into it once I reach the Seventh Heaven.”

“Um… Is there anything I can do to help you? Although my abilities as a Demon Sealer may be lacking, there should be something I can do to help! Since Senior Sister Yan has decided to fight against the Clan Leader, I cannot sit back and do nothing!”

“You want to help, huh? I’m not sure what to tell you, but if you want, you can go talk to Yan Hara. I’m sure she’ll have an idea.”

“Okay!” Sun Ling Cai eagerly nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1927 - 1927: Returning To The Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect
chapters.body:
Chapter 1927 - 1927: Returning To The Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect

“By the way, why are you hiding your identity, even going as far as to wear a mask?” Sun Ling Cai asked Yuan when he wore his mask again.

“Do you remember that demon the Nine Heavens Law and Order warned you about?”

“That ridiculous and unbelievable existence? What about it?”

“Well, that ‘demon’ is me,” Yuan calmly revealed.

“WHAT?!” Sun Ling Cai exclaimed loudly, her eyes wide with shock.

“They mistook me for a demon because of my regenerative abilities,” Yuan shrugged.

“Did you really kill one of them? If you hadn’t done that…”

“Yes, I did kill one of them. They wouldn’t listen to me and got on my nerves.”

Sun Ling Cai then spoke in a cold voice, “Those bastards dared to call the Founder of the Demon Sealing Clan a demon! You should’ve killed all of them!”

Yuan smiled and said, “Let’s continue our conversation later. There are still people waiting for us to return.”

“Okay.”

Sometime later, Yuan and Sun Ling Cai returned to the captives’ side. Moreover, before then, Yuan devoured the Demon General, who was only sealed.

“The demons have been dealt with. Right now, we’ll take you to the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect, where we will sort everything out and help you return to your home,” Sun Ling Cai said to the captives, who immediately began crying from an overwhelming sense of relief.

Sun Ling Cai retrieved her flying treasure, which could have its size adjusted and made it spacious enough to carry everyone there.

Once the captives were on the flying treasure, they began their journey back to the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect.

It didn’t take long for them to uncover solid evidence implicating the entire Cheng Family and Ling Family in the cult’s activities. As for Red Saber Mountain and Purple Rain Monastery, the investigation revealed that only a handful of individuals were involved, and the sects themselves were not wholly complicit.

Due to the incident, both the Cheng Family and the Ling Family were executed.

Once Yuan and Sun Ling Cai arrived at their destination, the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect immediately began assisting the captives in reuniting with their families. Fortunately, those who had been kidnapped from the sect were still alive and among the rescued.

“I cannot thank you enough for your help, Senior Sun.” Sect Leader Yun bowed to her.

“I didn’t do much… In fact, Foun—Xiao Yang here did most of the work,” Sun Ling Cai said.

“That’s not true. It was Senior Sun who defeated the demons. I merely assisted her,” Yuan shook his head.

“Demons?! You encountered demons?!” Sect Leader Yun exclaimed in shock.

“Yes, there were two of them. Fortunately, they weren’t anything special, or we wouldn’t have been able to return in one piece.”

Yuan and Sun Ling Cai agreed to keep the appearance of the Demon Emperor a secret for now. After all, no one would believe that the two of them had defeated both a Demon Emperor and a Demon General, and it would attract unwanted attention to Yuan.

As for the captives, they knew almost nothing about demons and couldn’t distinguish their ranks. Because of that, Yuan wasn’t concerned about them revealing the truth.

“Anyway, I have dealt with the demons, and I even learned something about the reason behind their unusual activities, so I will be returning to the Demon Sealing Clan soon,” Sun Ling Cai said.

Before leaving, Sun Ling Cai and Yuan had one last private conversation.

“I am allowed to let the Demon Sealing Clan know about the demon’s motives, right? Of course, I won’t mention anything about the Demon Emperor,” Sun Ling Cai asked.

Yuan nodded. “Yes, go ahead and tell them.”

“We must stop the demons from unsealing the Demon Realm at all costs…”

However, Yuan then said, “Actually, regarding the Demon Realm, I also plan on unsealing it.”

“What?! Why would you do something like that? Ah, I am not questioning your decision. I just want to know your reasoning so that I can better understand it. Sёarch* The Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Regardless of the demon’s motives, it’s true that the seal to the Demon Realm isn’t everlasting. It will eventually break, and once it does, we’ll have another war with the demons. Unless all demons are defeated—including those in the Demon Realm—we will never truly be safe. Since it will eventually open, I might as well open it myself. Also, the demons will continue to kidnap and sacrifice humans until they achieve their goal.”

“I see… That makes sense… In that case, maybe it’d be better to not let the Demon Sealing Clan know about it? They will definitely increase the security of the Demon Realm once they learn about the demon’s plan to unseal it.”

“It would make for a good distraction. I don’t intend on unsealing the Demon Realm until I take care of Qian Chu, anyway.”

“Ah, I cannot wait to see you lead the Demon Sealing Clan again…” Sun Ling Cai muttered with a dreamy look on her face.

Yuan chuckled and said, “But I don’t have any intention of leading the Demon Sealing Clan.”

“What?! If you don’t, who will replace Qian Chu?!”

“Yan Hara. I am going to make her the next leader.”

“No way… then what are you going to do?”

“I have countless other things to do. Moreover, the Demon Sealing Clan will become obsolete once there are no longer any demons left.”

“That won’t happen! Even if there are no more demons left in this world, the Demon Sealing Clan will not perish!”

“I didn’t say they’ll disappear. They’ll just lose their purpose and will have to find other things to focus on.”

“That’s…” Sun Ling Cai couldn’t refute this inevitability.

“I guess we’ll have to figure it out once we get there. Anyway, is there really nothing I can do to help you before I leave?”

“Unless you can help me improve my Cold Immunity or enter the Seventh Heaven in complete secrecy, I can’t think of anything else,” he said.

“Your Cold Immunity?” Sun Ling Cai raised her eyebrows.

chapters.title: Chapter 1928 - 1928: Yuan'S Heavenly Tribulation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1928 - 1928: Yuan'S Heavenly Tribulation

“Is that why you were at the Extreme Yin Lake? To improve your Cold Immunity?” Sun Ling Cai asked.

“That’s right. I need to train my body in a place with extremely strong Yin Qi.”

Sun Ling Cai pondered for a moment before speaking, “Actually, I might be able to help you in this regard.”

She retrieved a scrolled painting from her spatial ring and continued, “This is one of my masterpieces. It’s called ‘The Cold That Cuts Heaven’, and I painted it when I visited the White Hell, one of four forbidden zones within the Nine Heavens.”

“White Hell? What kind of place is that?” Yuan asked, his curiosity sparked upon learning it was one of the four Gateways to Hell, much like the Vanishing Valley.

“It’s a land where the snow never stops falling—a world buried in endless white, which is how it got its name,” Sun Ling Cai explained. “It’s also one of the coldest places in the Nine Heavens. Even Immortals can freeze to death there.”

“I see… So how exactly will that painting help me improve my Cold Immunity?” Yuan asked, raising an eyebrow.

In response, Sun Ling Cai unfurled the scroll. The moment it opened, a wave of chilling aura surged out, instantly filling the room with an icy presence so intense that frost began creeping across the walls, freezing everything in sight. seaʀᴄh thё novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

As for the painting itself, it depicted the peak of a towering snowy mountain—majestic and desolate—its summit shrouded in swirling frost, capturing the very essence of an eternal winter.

“This is one of many snowy mountains within White Hell,” Sun Ling Cai explained. “I can send people inside, and the environment and temperature are nearly identical to the real thing. If you’re looking to train your Cold Immunity, this is the perfect place.”

“Training inside a painting, huh? That’s a first for me,” Yuan said, raising an eyebrow with mild amusement.

Sun Ling Cai offered an awkward smile. “To be honest… this painting was originally meant to trap enemies. It’s more of a torture chamber than a training ground…”

“That’s fine by me. The harsher the environment, the faster my Cold Immunity will improve.”

Sun Ling Cai added, “Also, you’re trying to reach the Seventh Heaven without drawing attention, right? I haven’t tested it before, but in theory… I might be able to smuggle you in using this method.”

“Oh?”

“Since I don’t know if it’ll actually work, I am going to try it out on someone else first,” she continued.

“I’ll just find someone who’s willing to participate in this experiment and take them to the Seventh Heaven with me when I visit the Demon Sealing Clan’s headquarters. After I finish reporting to the Demon Sealing Clan, I will return here to help you with your Cold Immunity training,” she explained.

“Alright, then I will wait for you here at the sect. How long do you think it’ll take for you to return?”

Sun Ling Cai pondered for a moment before responding, “It shouldn’t take longer than a month.”

“Then I will see you in a month.”

Sun Ling Cai left the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect shortly after.

Before heading to the Seventh Heaven, Sun Ling Cai went to look for a guinea pig for her experiment.

After some thinking, she went to a prominent family in the Sixth Heaven and offered one of her treasured paintings to whoever was willing to assist her experiment.

Due to Sun Ling Cai’s reputation, she didn’t have any issue gaining their trust, and with such an attractive offer, it didn’t take long before she acquired a volunteer.

Once prepared, Sun Ling Cai transferred the volunteer into one of her paintings before making her way to the Stairway to Heaven.

However, she was unable to ascend to the Seventh Heaven because the Stairway to Heaven had detected the volunteer’s presence within her treasure.

‘So I can’t use the Stairway to Heaven, huh?’

Fortunately, Sun Ling Cai was not out of options.

After the attempt to smuggle a volunteer through the Stairway to Heaven failed, Sun Ling Cai retrieved a rare and valuable treasure—one that allowed her to return directly to the Seventh Heaven without relying on the Stairway to Heaven.

It was a one-time-use teleportation treasure, capable of sending its user to any previously marked location. Unlike most teleportation treasures, which were restricted to the same or lower realm, this one had no limitations. Despite its rarity and staggering cost, Sun Ling Cai didn’t hesitate to use it for her experiment.

Sometime later, Sun Ling Cai returned to the Seventh Heaven.

After her return, she immediately retrieved her painting and the volunteer from it.

‘It worked!’ she cried inwardly after seeing the volunteer in perfect condition. ‘Now I just need to return to the Sixth Heaven and do it again with the Founder!’

Meanwhile, as he waited for Sun Ling Cai’s return, Yuan absorbed the spiritual energy refined by the Demon Devouring Sword.

<You have reached the Ninth Level Spirit Enlightenment>

After reaching the peak of Spirit Enlightenment, Yuan pondered whether he should attempt to enter the Divine Realm.

‘If I’m going to enter the Seventh Heaven soon, I should enter the Divine Realm before I do so. Hopefully, my physique won’t prevent me from doing so.’

Yuan closed his eyes and began attempting to enter the Divine Realm.

<You are attempting to enter the Divine Realm>

<You have evoked Heavenly Tribulation(Minor)!>

Hearing the notifications, Yuan opened his eyes.

“Heavenly Tribulation, huh?”

He wasn’t surprised that he had evoked a Heavenly Tribulation since every cultivator had to face a minor tribulation to ascend from the Spirit Realm to the Divine Realm.

Just as Yuan stood up and prepared to leave his living quarters to confront the Heavenly Tribulation, another notification appeared.

<Celestial Emperor’s Curse has been activated>

<Due to the Celestial Emperor’s Curse, the Heavenly Tribulation has been affected, becoming more fierce!>

<Heavenly Tribulation(Major) prepares to descend!>

Meanwhile, the skies above the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect darkened as ominous black clouds gathered overhead. Thunder rumbled in the distance, its echo rolling across the land, audible from miles away.

“H-Heavenly Tribulation! Someone triggered a Heavenly Tribulation!” The disciples were shocked and alerted by the phenomenon.

chapters.title: Chapter 1929 - 1929: Entering The Divine Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 1929 - 1929: Entering The Divine Realm

Yuan stared at the new notifications with raised eyebrows. He didn’t know the Celestial Emperor’s Curse had such an effect since it wasn’t mentioned in its description.

<Heavenly Tribulation(Major) descends!>

Before Yuan could even step outside or prepare himself, the Heavenly Tribulation struck without warning.

The dark clouds above the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect roared with fury as a colossal bolt of lightning—large enough to engulf an entire building—crashed down, striking both Yuan and his house in a blinding explosion of light and power.

The house was instantly vaporized by the Heavenly Tribulation. Yet, despite the overwhelming power of the strike, the nearby buildings remained untouched. The destruction was eerily precise—confined solely to Yuan’s house, as if the Heavenly Tribulation possessed a will of its own, targeting only its intended prey.

“Heavens! That power! This is a Major Heavenly Tribulation! Is someone attempting to break their mortal shackles right now?!”

The disciples scattered in panic, fleeing as far as they could from the terrifying display in the sky.

Normally, only those who had committed grave offenses against the heavens—or those attempting to ascend into the Immortal Realm—would trigger a Major Heavenly Tribulation. The sheer scale of what they were witnessing filled them with dread and awe.

“Yuan?! Are you okay?!”

Xi Meili and Tan Songyun, who lived in separate buildings beside Yuan’s, rushed out the moment they sensed the commotion.

Their eyes widened in shock when they saw that Yuan’s house had vanished—replaced by a smoldering crater where it once stood.

“It’s a Heavenly Tribulation! A major one, at that!” Tan Songyun exclaimed after seeing the darkened sky. “But why did one appear now?! And why did it target him?!”

A moment later, a figure suddenly leapt from the crater, rising into the sky with effortless grace.

It was Yuan, and to everyone’s astonishment, he looked completely unscathed, as if the Heavenly Tribulation hadn’t touched him at all.

<Flawless Heavenly Physique has negated Heavenly Tribulation(Major)!>

<Flawless Heavenly Physique has absorbed Heavenly Tribulation(Major)!>

Because the Heavenly Tribulation was lightning-attributed, it was useless against Yuan’s Flawless Heavenly Physique. The divine energy that would have devastated others merely washed over him without leaving a mark. seaʀᴄh thё novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Don’t worry, I am fine. I have experienced worse in the Primal Expanse,” Yuan said.

<Heavenly Tribulation(Major) prepares to descend again!>

As if provoked by Yuan’s words, the Heavenly Tribulation rumbled furiously in response, gathering its power once more as it prepared to unleash a second, even more furious strike.

Seeing this, Yuan said to the others, “It’ll attack again soon. You should leave.”

Xi Meili and Tan Songyun listened to his advice and got away right before the second lightning bolt descended.

BOOM!

The second bolt struck with far greater fury than the first, its destructive power obliterating everything around Yuan. The ground was reduced to scorched, blackened earth.

Yet, Yuan remained unfazed even after being struck by such a powerful force.

<Flawless Heavenly Physique has negated Heavenly Tribulation(Major)!>

<Flawless Heavenly Physique has absorbed Heavenly Tribulation(Major)!>

In fact, it only made him stronger.

<You have stepped into the Divine Realm and reached First Level Divine Warrior!>

Yuan could feel his spiritual energy and overall prowess skyrocketing. The leap from the Spirit Realm to the Divine Realm was staggering—an unfathomable gap, like the distance between heaven and earth.

He looked toward the sky and spoke, “Is this all you got? If so, get lost.”

<Due to Player Yuan’s provocation, Heavenly Tribulation(Major) has increased in fierceness!>

<Heavenly Tribulation(Supreme) prepares to descend!>

Purple lightning danced across the dark sky, writhing around like snakes.

When the spectators saw this, their eyes widened with disbelief.

“Supreme Heavenly Tribulation! I have only ever heard about this level of Heavenly Tribulation from others! I never thought it would appear in the Sixth Heaven!” Sect Leader Yun nearly fell on her buttocks from shock.

A Supreme Heavenly Tribulation was powerful enough to obliterate even Immortals, after all.

Eventually, the third strike descended—an immense bolt of lightning so vast it lit up the entire sky before crashing down on Yuan. The impact was catastrophic, destroying every building and object within a ten-mile radius.

Fortunately, the disciples had evacuated long before, and despite the destruction, there were no casualties.

<Flawless Heavenly Physique has negated Heavenly Tribulation(Supreme)!>

<Flawless Heavenly Physique has absorbed Heavenly Tribulation(Supreme)!>

<You have reached Second Level Divine Warrior>

“Ahaha!” Yuan suddenly burst out laughing. “Thanks for the boost, you sore loser! Keep coming at me if you dare!”

The clouds rumbled once more, echoing the heavens’ fury at Yuan’s continued provocation. But no further strike came. As much as the Heavenly Tribulation burned with the desire to smite him again, it was bound by its own laws.

Yuan had withstood its wrath and survived the tribulation. If the heavens wished to challenge him again, they would have to wait for the next appointed time. For now, he had earned his qualifications to be in the Divine Realm.

<You have earned the title ‘One Who Dares to Provoke the Heavens’>

<You have earned the title ‘One Who Dares to Mock the Heavens’>

Eventually, the dark clouds looming over the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect began to fade, slowly dissolving into the heavens. Moments later, a clear blue sky emerged.

Xi Meili, Tan Songyun, and Sect Leader Yun approached Yuan shortly afterward.

“Y-You! What did you do to summon a Supreme Heavenly Tribulation?!” Tan Songyun demanded answers, her voice trembling with disbelief. “And how are you completely unscathed?!”

As an Immortal herself, Tan Songyun had endured a Supreme Heavenly Tribulation—and had barely survived. The memory of it still haunted her, making Yuan’s effortless survival all the more unfathomable.

“The Heavenly Tribulation appeared because I was attempting to enter the Divine Realm,” Yuan calmly explained.

“Divine Realm?! That would only summon a Minor Heavenly Tribulation—Major Heavenly Tribulation at most for those with heaven-defying talents. However, what you summoned was the Supreme Heavenly Tribulation, which only appears when a Bronze Immortal attempts to become a Silver Immortal!” Tan Songyun said.

chapters.title: Chapter 1930 - 1930: A Month Of Cultivation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1930 - 1930: A Month Of Cultivation

Yuan shrugged and said, “It became more powerful when I provoked it.”

“Y-You provoked the Heavenly Tribulation…? Not even a madman would dare to do something so insane…” Tan Songyun’s jaw dropped at this information.

“Anyway…” Yuan turned to face Sect Leader Yun and bowed to her.

“I apologize for summoning the Heavenly Tribulation right in the middle of your sect, even destroying a portion of it.”

Sect Leader Yun snapped out of her daze and shook her head before saying, “It’s fine. The destroyed property can be fixed. As long as nobody was hurt, I couldn’t care less. Moreover, you saved several of our disciples. The least I can do is overlook something this minor.”

“Thank you,” Yuan said.

“Now then… since all of our guest houses are destroyed, you can stay at my place for now. It’s big enough to accommodate all of you with ease, too.”

“Okay.”

Sometime later, Yuan and the others moved into Sect Leader Yun’s living quarters.

“I can’t believe you’re already at the second level of Divine Warrior when you were only at the second level of Spriit Enlightenment yesterday,” Tan Songyun sighed in a heavy voice.

Yuan smiled and said, “It’s only the Divine Warrior realm. I’m going to push my cultivation as much as possible before we go to the Seventh Heaven.”

“Isn’t it bad for your body to rush through so many cultivation levels so quickly?” Xi Meili asked. Searᴄh the nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“For normal people, yes. However, my physique is unique, and I have already reached these levels before—several times, at that.”

“Haaaa… looks like our distance will widen even more now…” she sighed.

Hearing this, Yuan retrieved a monster core that once belonged to an Immortal-level magical beast and offered it to her.

“You can cultivate too.”

“That’s an Immortal-level monster core! Where did you get such a thing? That level of magical beasts shouldn’t exist below the Seventh Heaven.” Tan Songyun asked.

“No, there is one place where you can find Immortal-level magical beasts… the Vanishing Valley.”

“The forbidden zone?! You actually visited such a dangerous place?” Tan Songyun swallowed nervously. If she was given the opportunity to go to the Vanishing Valley, she would, without a doubt, refuse.

Sometime later, Yuan entered his room and resumed his cultivation.

Instead of consuming monster cores, Yuan chose to absorb the spiritual energy stored within the Demon Devouring Sword first since the sword held an enormous reserve of spiritual energy gathered during the Demonic Era.

<You have reached Third Level Divine Warrior>

<You have reached Ninth Level Divine Warrior>

<You have reached Fifth Level Divine Master>

<You have reached Ninth Level Divine Master>

<You have reached First Level Divine Grandmaster>

Although Yuan had reached the Divine Grandmaster realm, absorbing spiritual energy manually from the Demon Devouring Sword was a slow process. Unlike consuming monster cores, which could instantly boost his cultivation, this method required patience and time.

Of course, that was only considered slow by Yuan’s standards. Compared to other cultivators, his cultivation speed was nothing short of absurd—practically defying common sense.

<You have reached Fourth Level Divine Lord>

<You have reached Ninth Level Divine Lord>

<You have reached First Level Divine King>

In just a single month, Yuan had advanced from the Spirit Enlightenment realm all the way to the Divine King realm—a feat that would be unthinkable for even geniuses. And he had done it while under the effects of the Celestial Emperor’s Curse, which slowed his cultivation speed by 99 percent.

However, Yuan wasn’t the only one whose cultivation surged. Lan Yingying, who received a portion of the spiritual energy Yuan absorbed through her Supreme Seal, had also advanced rapidly—reaching the first level of the Divine Lord realm.

But unlike Yuan, such rapid progress came with risks. Advancing too quickly could destabilize her foundation and harm her cultivation. To protect her, Yuan sealed her Supreme Seal, halting her progress while he continued to cultivate.

As for Feng Yuxiang, her cultivation had risen to the peak of the Divine Emperor realm. However, this wasn’t newfound growth but merely the restoration of the strength she had once lost, and the Supreme Seal only quickened that recovery.

Meanwhile, Sun Ling Cai returned to the Demon Sealing Clan and reported her findings. She revealed her encounter with two demons in the Sixth Heaven and their disturbing plan to unseal the Demon Realm using a key forged from the sacrifices of innocent maidens.

This revelation sent a wave of alarm through the entire Demon Sealing Clan. Anything concerning the Demon Realm was considered highly sensitive—and potentially catastrophic.

Thus, to prevent the demons from achieving their goal, the Demon Sealing Clan declared the eradication of the Demon Worshippers their top priority. While they had kept a watchful eye on the cult in the past, they had never fully committed their resources.

As for the search for the so-called ‘demon’ who had killed a cultivator from the Nine Heavens Law and Order, the Demon Sealing Clan chose to suspend the investigation for now. With the threat of the Demon Realm’s unsealing looming, their priorities had shifted.

After completing her report, Sun Ling Cai wasted no time and went to look for Yan Hara. With the Demon Sealing Clan having recalled most of their Demon Sealers to address the crisis, it didn’t take long for her to locate her.

“Senior Sister Yan! Can you spare me some of your time? I have something important to tell you!”

“Sure. Let’s go to my office.”

Once they entered Yan Hara’s office and closed the door, Sun Ling Cai retrieved a painting from her spatial ring and said, “I painted this during my travel to the Sixth Heaven—when I encountered the demons.”

When she revealed the painting of Yuan fighting the Demon Emperor, Yan Hara’s eyes widened with surprise.

“Huh? Wait a second… Isn’t that…”

The first thing Yan Hara noticed was Yuan’s handsome face.

“Yes, it’s the Founder… or should I call him Little Founder?”

Yan Hara’s entire body stiffened at such words.

She swallowed nervously before asking in a shaky voice, “W-What are you trying to say…?”

“Judging by your reaction, I think you know exactly what I’m trying to say,” Sun Ling Cai responded with a teasing smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 1931 - 1931: Demon Sealing Clan'S Highest Priority
chapters.body:
Chapter 1931 - 1931: Demon Sealing Clan'S Highest Priority

“I—I don’t know what you’re talking about!” Yan Hara exclaimed, slamming her hand on the desk as she shot to her feet. Her voice trembled, and the panic in her eyes was impossible to miss.

Sun Ling Cai chuckled, “You’re really cute when you panic, Senior Sister Yan. You’re also very bad at acting. Who are you trying to fool with that kind of reaction? Anyway, I know that the Little Founder is the Divine Paragon’s reincarnation.”

Yan Hara’s entire body trembled at such words.

“H-How did you…?”

“Relax, the Little Founder told me himself.”

“You met Yuan?! Where and when did you meet him?!”

“I was with him right before I returned to the Demon Sealing Clan. Ah, he’s in the Sixth Heaven right now.”

“He’s in the Sixth Heaven already?!”

Despite knowing that Yuan was the Divine Paragon’s reincarnation, Yan Hara was still surprised by his fast progress.

“Yes, and he’ll be arriving at the Seventh Heaven soon.”

“In that case, I must head down to the Seventh Heaven to meet him as soon as possible!”

“Slow down a bit, Senior Sister Yan. I still have a lot to tell you.”

“Oh, right… I heard you dealt with two demons before returning. Was he with you at that time?”

Sun Ling Cai nodded with a somewhat bitter look on her face and said, “Honestly, he was the one who dealt with the demons.”

“Eh?” Yan Hara’s eyebrows rose in a questioning manner.

“I altered my report to protect the Little—the Founder. In truth, the demons weren’t ordinary demons. One was a Demon General, and the other… was a Demon Emperor.”

“A Demon Emperor?!?!?!” Yan Hara exclaimed, nearly falling on her buttocks from shock.

“The Founder called it Demon Emperor Slaughter. If it weren’t for the Founder, I would’ve died that day.”

“So he’s powerful enough to defeat a Demon Emperor by himself now…” Yan Hara clenched her fists in excitement.

“He didn’t just defeat the Demon Emperor. He practically toyed with it. I have never seen anyone mess with a demon like that before, much less a Demon Emperor.” Sun Ling Cai sighed as she recalled their fight.

“I am going to need you to tell me everything that happened from the beginning to end without missing a single detail,” Yan Hara spoke with a serious look on her face.

Sun Ling Cai nodded.

Once they were seated, tea in hand, Sun Ling Cai began recounting everything to Yan Hara in vivid detail. She left nothing out—starting from her arrival at the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect, to witnessing Yuan confront the Demon Worshippers, and finally, his battle with and victory over the Demon Emperor.

Yan Hara’s body trembled with delight as she listened.

“Ah… he’s truly grown into an unfathomable individual since I last saw him… I cannot wait to see him again,” she muttered in a daze.

“Also, I can’t believe he turned out to be the demon we’ve spent the last several years searching for… It’s no wonder he’d hide his identity from you.”

Sun Ling Cai then spoke, “Oh, right. When I asked him if I could be of any assistance, he asked me to ask you.”

“Assistance, huh? Actually, I do need some help. I have been thinking about recruiting people outside our clan to my group, and you, who have more influence than me outside the Demon Sealing Clan, can help me do that.” Sёarch* The Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“You’re trying to dethrone Qian Chu, right? Why would outsiders matter in the Demon Sealing Clan’s affairs?” Sun Ling Cai asked.

“You’re not thinking far enough. While an outsider may have no say in our affairs, do you think Qian Chu will peacefully hand over his position to us?”

“Probably not… We all know how obsessive he is with his position. He might even fight us to death if it means protecting his position.”

Yan Hara nodded and continued, “And once he starts fighting, those on his side will join. Qian Chu has over half of the Demon Sealing Clan’s support. If we want to defeat him, we’ll need external help.”

“That might be true now, but if the Founder reveals himself, I’m sure most of them would switch sides,” said Sun Ling Cai.

“That’s very possible, but it’s still better to be safe than sorry.”

“That makes sense…”

“When will you be returning to the Sixth Heaven?” Yan Hara then asked.

“Right after the Demon Sealing Clan’s meeting.”

“Alright.”

A few days later, the Demon Sealing Clan started their major meeting, drawing participation from nearly all of its Demon Sealers regardless of rank or status. The topic was about Sun Ling Cai’s report on the demons’ plans to unseal the Demon Realm.

“During my encounter with the demons, I questioned their reason for kidnapping maidens, and it told me that they were making a ‘key’ to open the Demon Realm.”

A tense atmosphere filled the place when Sun Ling Cai mentioned the Demon Realm.

If the Demon Realm were ever unsealed, the Nine Heavens would, without a doubt, experience another Demonic Era, and nobody there wanted such a thing to happen.

“W-We must stop that from happening at all costs! If the seal to the Demon Realm ever unseals, countless people will die!”

“That’s right! We have to do something about this!”

“Isn’t that obvious?”

The meeting was brief and to the point. Qian Chu issued a firm directive: the Demon Worshippers were now the highest priority. All Demon Sealers were to remain vigilant and travel the Nine Heavens while giving special attention to any cases involving kidnappings and places with high numbers of missing people.

At the end, Qian Chu also announced that he would be away from the Demon Sealing Clan for some time, but he didn’t go into details or even mention why he’d be away. However, nobody there questioned him.

Yan Hara wanted to question him, but she didn’t want to attract more attention to herself right now. However, she did task someone from her group to follow Qian Chu and report all of his activities to her.
