chapters.title: Chapter 1932 - 1932: Supreme Cold Immunity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1932 - 1932: Supreme Cold Immunity

After the meeting, Sun Ling Cai and Yan Hara met up one more time before Sun Ling Cai made her way back to the Sixth Heaven.

In the Sixth Heaven, before heading back to the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect to meet with Yuan, Sun Ling Cai dropped off the volunteer back at his home.

“Wow, what happened here? Did a demon attack your sect while I was gone?” Sun Ling Cai was dumbfounded by the destruction in the sect and asked Sect Leader Yun.

“No… A Supreme Heavenly Tribulation occurred when Xiao Yang attempted to breakthrough to the Divine Realm.”

“Supreme Heavenly Tribulation?! But that’s usually only reserved for Immortals! Is he okay?!” Sun Ling Cai was greatly shocked by this news. Although she was confident in Yuan’s abilities as the Divine Paragon’s reincarnation, she was still worried he might not be able to handle a Supreme Heavenly Tribulation that threatened even Immortals like her.

“He’s fine. Hell, he emerged from it unscathed.”

“Unscathed…? Is that even possible?” Sun Ling Cai stared at the Sect Leader with a dumbfounded look on her pretty face. “How did he do it?”

“You’re asking the wrong person.” Sect Leader Yun shrugged before continuing, “I don’t know how he did it, but he treated the Supreme Heavenly Tribulation as if it was a joke.”

Sun Ling Cai pondered for a moment before muttering, “I can only think of one possibility… he’d achieved the legendary Heavenly Physique that grants immunity to lightning.”

“Heavenly Physique?! That’s impossible!” Sect Leader Yun exclaimed, her eyes wide with disbelief. Most cultivators only cultivate a single elemental immunity in their life because it takes a lifetime to train even one. She already knew Yuan possessed Cold Immunity, so the thought of him having another defied all reason.

Moreover, lightning immunity was the hardest immunity to train, and the number of people who have achieved Heavenly Physique since the beginning of time could be counted on one hand.

“Impossible? Nothing is impossible for the Found—him.”

‘Found?’ Sect Leader Yun raised an eyebrow.

“Anyway, where’s Xiao Yang?”

“He’s currently staying at my place.”

Sect Leader Yun brought Sun Ling Cai back to her living quarters shortly after.

“Welcome back. How did it go?” Yuan asked her once they were alone.

“It worked! I managed to bring someone from the Sixth Heaven to the Seventh Heaven—even the Eighth Heaven! I tried going through the Stairway to Heaven at first, but that didn’t work out, so I had to use a treasure.”

“Anyway, when do you want to go to the Seventh Heaven?”

“I’ll go right after I finish training my Cold Immunity.”

“…”

Sun Ling Cai suddenly went silent.

“Is something wrong?” Yuan asked.

“Huh? Oh, nothing’s wrong. I was just wondering if you truly have the Heavenly Physique or not.”

“Heavenly Physique? I have it.” Yuan admitted.

“How old are you in this life, again?”

“I’m still in my twenties in this life.”

“Your twenties?! And you already have the Heavenly Physique and Cold Immunity?!” Sun Ling Cai exclaimed in a terrified voice.

Seeing how she reacted, Yuan didn’t dare to reveal that he also had Poison Immunity and Fire Immunity.

Sometime later, Sun Ling Cai said, “By the way, I spoke with Senior Sister Yan. She said that she’d like to meet you once you arrive at the Seventh Heaven.”

Yuan nodded, “Sure.”

“Alright, when do you wish to start your training?”

“I’d like to start now if you don’t mind.”

“I understand.” seaʀᴄh thё ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Sun Ling Cai proceeded to retrieve the painting ‘The Cold That Cuts Heaven’ and opened it, instantly turning the room into an ice chamber.

“I’ll be watching over you constantly, so if you need anything, just shout it out loud,” she said.

Yuan nodded and said, “Alright. Don’t take me out unless I tell you to, even if it looks bad.”

“Okay. I’ll send you inside the painting now.”

Sun Ling Cai lightly tapped Yuan’s shoulder with her brush, its tip glowing with profound energy. Then, with a graceful motion, she drew the brush toward the painting. In the next instant, Yuan’s figure was pulled into the scroll—vanishing into the painted world as if swallowed by it.

<You have entered ‘The Cold That Cuts Heaven’>

After entering the painting, Yuan found himself standing atop the very snowy mountain depicted in the scroll. The world around him was blanketed in endless white—sky, ground, and horizon blending together in a frozen stillness.

Despite having Super Cold Resistance, he could feel the bitter chill biting into his skin, the cold so intense it began to creep into his veins, threatening to freeze his blood solid. Yet, he did nothing to fight it.

<Your Cold Resistance has improved significantly>

Within moments, the frigid air overwhelmed him. A thick layer of ice rapidly formed over his body, sealing him completely. In the blink of an eye, Yuan stood frozen—transformed into a statue of ice, motionless amidst the endless snow.

Sun Ling Cai, who was watching this, immediately wondered if she should take Yuan out of the painting.

‘No… although his entire body is frozen, his heart is still beating.’

Time flowed quietly in the frozen world, and three full months had passed in the blink of an eye. Yet Yuan remained unchanged—still encased in ice, unmoving, as though time itself had forgotten him.

<Your skill ‘Super Cold Immunity’ has developed into ‘Supreme Cold Immunity’>

At long last, the ice encasing Yuan began to crack—thin fractures spreading like spiderwebs across its surface. Moments after attaining Supreme Cold Immunity, a surge of energy pulsed through his body, and with a sharp burst, he shattered the frozen shell around him, breaking free from his long, silent imprisonment.

‘If I want to reach the next level, I’ll need to visit the real White Hell.’

While he could continue training inside the painting and achieve the next level, it would take too much time, hence why he decided to stop there.

“I’m ready to leave!” Yuan lifted his head toward the sky and spoke loudly.

A moment later, Yuan felt a profound energy wrap around him, and in the blink of an eye, the icy world vanished. When he opened his eyes, he was back in his room. Before him, Sun Ling Cai sat quietly in front of the painting with a relieved smile on her face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1933 - 1933: Arriving At The Seventh Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1933 - 1933: Arriving At The Seventh Heaven

“How did it go? I hope you’ve achieved satisfactory results,” Sun Ling Cai said.

“How long have I been inside the painting?” Yuan asked.

“A little over three months.”

“I see… It wasn’t too bad. However, if I want to advance further, I’d need to visit the real White Hell personally.”

“Seriously…? I know you have Cold Immunity, but that place is so dangerous that even those with Cold Immunity aren’t safe.”

“Then how did you manage to paint this painting there?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows.

“Did you think I went there alone? I had assistance from the Sun God’s Temple. They have a treasure that can protect you from the weather in White Hell. However, even that treasure has its limits. Otherwise, I would’ve gone deeper inside the White Hell.”

“Is that so? Well, I am going there to train, so any protection will only hinder my progress. Where can I find the place?”

“You can find it in the Seventh Heaven.”

“That’s great. I’ll head over there once I am finished with my other business,” said Yuan.

“So, are you ready to head to the Seventh Heaven?”

“I am, but let me talk to Xi Meili and Tan Songyun too.”

Sometime later, Yuan revealed his plan to enter the Seventh Heaven through Sun Ling Cai.

“Are we leaving now?” Xi Meili asked.

Yuan nodded.

Tan Songyun then said, “In that case, I’ll have to leave your group for some time. I need to speak with my Faction Leader, so I’ll return to my faction for some time. Once I am done, I’ll find you again.”

“Are you sure? What if I do something bad while you’re away?” Yuan teased.

Tan Songyun stared at him with narrowed eyes for a moment before sighing. Then, she spoke in a defeated tone, “Fine, I admit that I was wrong about you. However, I will need some time to accept it, as I have spent the last several thousand years blinded by hatred.”

This is one of the main reasons why she decided to leave the group temporarily, as she needed time to clear her mind. Sёarᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I don’t blame you. The only one at fault is the individual who started spreading those false rumors.”

Tan Songyun nodded.

“By the way, since I have already been to the Seventh Heaven, I can go there by myself, so I won’t be coming with you,” she said a moment later.

“I understand. Until next time.”

Tan Songyun left the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect shortly after.

Sun Ling Cai was very curious about their relationship, but not wanting to appear nosy, she decided to stay silent.

“Stay here for a bit. I have to go outside for a moment.” Yuan said to them before leaving the house.

“Yin. Yang. Are you here?”

A moment later, two cloaked figures appeared before Yuan.

“We are at your service.”

“I will be heading to the Seventh Heaven soon. Will you be able to follow me there?” he asked.

“We can follow you all the way to the Ninth Heaven.”

“That’s good. By the way, do you know anything about the Dragon Spiral Mountain located within the Seventh Heaven?”

Yang spoke first. “The Dragon Spiral Mountain in the Seventh Heaven is currently under the control of the Holy Dragon Clan. Their bloodline ranks among the top three royal bloodlines of all dragon bloodlines.”

Yin followed. “They’re extremely reclusive and seldom leave their territory. Naturally, that also means they rarely accept visitors.”

“Is that so?”

After his conversation with Yin and Yang ended, Yuan returned inside the house and said, “Alright, I am ready. Let’s head to the Seventh Heaven.”

Sun Ling Cai retrieved a painting shortly after and sent Yuan and Xi Meili inside it.

“I’ll let you know when we arrive. It won’t take long.”

After putting away the painting, Sun Ling Cai notified Sect Leader Yun of their departure before retrieving a treasure that brought her to the Seventh Heaven and used it without hesitation.

Once they arrived in the Seventh Heaven, Sun Ling Cai brought Yuan and Xi Meili out of the painting.

“Welcome to the Seventh Heaven,” she said to them.

“Wow… I thought the spiritual energy was already impressive enough in the Sixth Heaven, but it cannot even compare to the spiritual energy in the Seventh Heaven.” Xi Meili muttered with a dazed look on her face. She could feel her cultivation improve significantly with every breath.

“Not just spiritual energy. The Immortal Qi here is also incomparably more abundant,” said Sun Ling Cai.

“Immortal Qi… I guess we’ll be encountering Immortals now.” Xi Meili swallowed nervously.

“That’s right. After all, there is no longer any restriction to one’s cultivation from the Seventh Heaven and onwards. In fact, you could even encounter a Cultivation God in the Seventh Heaven.”

Sun Ling Cai continued, “Also, unlike the First Heaven to Sixth Heaven, traveling between the Seventh Heaven and Ninth Heaven is much easier and much less restrictive.”

“So, what are you going to do now?”

Sun Ling Cai and Xi Meili looked at Yuan.

“As much as I want to head straight to the Dragon Spiral Mountain immediately, I need to prepare myself a little.”

Yuan looked at Sun Ling Cai and continued, “Can you help me with my training?”

“Of course. What kind of training?”

“I want to be able to use Immortal Qi.”

“Huh? But Immortal Qi is something you can use only after you become an Immortal. It’s not something that can be attained from effort and training alone.”

Yuan smiled and exuded some of his incomplete Immortal Qi.

“?!?!”

Sun Ling Cai nearly jumped from shock.

“Immortal Qi?! It’s not perfect, but it’s definitely Immortal Qi! How is that even possible?”

“Nothing is impossible for him,” Xi Meili chuckled.

After snapping out of her daze, Sun Ling Cai asked, “How do I help you?”

“It’s quite simple. You just need to keep attacking me with your Immortal Qi,” he said.

Her eyes widened with disbelief.

“You want me to attack you with Immortal Qi?! I can’t do something like that!”

“Don’t worry, I won’t die.”

“That’s not the issue! You’re telling a Divine Paragon Fanatic to attack the Divine Paragon! How will I be able to face the others after knowing what I have done?!”

“That’s your worry…?” Yuan was speechless.

“Please! I’ll do anything but that!”

“But you’re the only one here who can train me. Or are you telling me to wander this place that’s teeming with Immortals without being able to use Immortal Qi?”

“That’s… Oh, right! Senior Sister Yan will be here soon! You can ask her to help you! Her Immortal Qi is much stronger than mine, too!”

“Yan Hara? She’s coming here?”

“Yes. I spoke with her when I returned to the Demon Sealing Clan, and she said that she wanted to see you. I gave her this location before returning to the Sixth Heaven. She should be here soon.”

Just as Sun Ling Cai finished her sentence, Yuan could sense a presence speeding toward their location.

Moments later, a beautiful woman halted right above the island to look at them.

When Yan Hara saw Yuan, she immediately landed right before him and gave him a firm embrace.

“I’ve missed you!”

Sun Ling Cai’s jaw dropped. However, it was not because she was jealous.

“How can you touch the Founder so casually?!” she exclaimed.

Yan Hara’s body froze at her words. After a moment of silence and stillness, Yan Hara released Yuan and immediately kowtowed.

“I am so sorry! I got too excited and instinctively acted on my old habits!” she apologized.

“I don’t mind.” Yuan removed his mask and showed her a handsome smile.

“It’s been a while. How have you been?”

Yan Hara didn’t respond—her attention was completely consumed by Yuan’s face. It was a perfect reflection of the Divine Paragon, so strikingly identical that she couldn’t look away.

“Ahem.” Sun Ling Cai cleared her throat.

Yan Hara quickly snapped out of her daze and spoke with a red face, “If you visited the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library right now, it’d definitely cause a commotion. I also want to see Qian Chu’s reaction when he sees you.”

“I’ll visit the library as soon as I finish my other business,” he said.

“I can’t believe it hasn’t even been a decade since you first visited the library. I thought I would have to wait several decades before I could see you outside of the library,” Yan Hara said.

“Anyway, I have a lot to talk to you about. Let’s change our location first.”

“Wait. Before we leave, I need your help.”

“I’ll do it,” Yan Hara instinctively responded without any hesitation.

“Good. Then attack me with your Immortal Qi.”

Yan Hara’s body trembled at his words, and her eyes widened as she spoke, “W-What? I think I misheard—”

“Attack me with your Immortal Qi,” Yuan repeated. “It’s for my training.”

“What kind of training are you doing?!” she exclaimed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1934 - 1934: Learning Immortal Qi
chapters.body:
Chapter 1934 - 1934: Learning Immortal Qi

“I’m trying to learn—complete my Immortal Qi.” Yuan showed Yan Hara his incomplete Immortal Qi.

“You can already use Immortal Qi? But you’re only at—Divine King?! How did you improve your cultivation so quickly?! You were only around Spirit King a few years ago!”

Yan Hara just noticed his cultivation and was greatly bewildered by his progress. If she knew that he’d gone from Spirit Enlightenment to Divine King in the past month, who knows how she’d react.

As for Sun Ling Cai, her mouth and eyes were wide open. She knew Yuan was only Spirit Enlightenment last month, and she knew he’d triggered a Supreme Heavenly Tribulation to enter the Divine Realm, but she didn’t realize he was already Divine King.

‘What kind of outrageous cultivation speed is this?!’ she cried inwardly.

“Why are you so surprised? Have you forgotten who I am?” Yuan chuckled.

“Anyway, attack me with your Immortal Qi. I’ll be fine. I promise.”

Despite her hesitation, Yan Hara eventually nodded her head and agreed to train him.

Sun Ling Cai and Xi Meili flew off the island a moment later and distanced themselves from Yuan.

Sometime later, Yan Hara released her Immortal Qi and attacked Yuan with it.

“It’s not strong enough! Don’t hold back and come at me as if you’re trying to kill me!” Yuan shouted after barely feeling it.

Yan Hara gritted her teeth and increased her Immortal Qi output tremendously.

Boom!

Yan Hara’s Immortal Qi pressed down on Yuan like a mountain.

Yuan coughed up a mouthful of blood afterward.

“Yuan!” Yan Hara instinctively retracted her Immortal Qi when she saw his blood.

“Don’t stop! It’s just a little blood!”

“…”

Yan Hara was reluctant to continue, but seeing Yuan’s fierce eyes, she gritted her teeth and continued blasting him with Immortal Qi.

<Your comprehension of Immortal Qi has improved tremendously!>

<Your comprehension of Immortal Qi has improved tremendously!>

<Your comprehension of Immortal Qi has improved tremendously!>

Yan Hara was a Golden Immortal—on the verge of becoming a True Immortal—so her Immortal Qi was immensely powerful.

Although Yuan could have trained his Immortal Qi in the Sixth Heaven, the process would’ve been painfully slow. While Immortal Qi could still be used by Immortals even when their cultivation was suppressed, its power would be significantly weakened.

This is why he waited until he arrived at the Seven Heaven, where Immortals could use their Immortal Qi properly.

Yuan’s entire body was screaming in pain from Yan Hara’s Immortal Qi, but it wasn’t enough to put his life in danger. If anything, his body was getting stronger by the moment due to his physique. Searᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“You can increase the power a little more now,” Yuan said to Yan Hara a few days later.

Yan Hara, who’d been using the power of a Bronze Immortal, slowly increased her strength to that of a Silver Immortal.

After two weeks of training, the Immortal Qi that Yuan was exuding was no longer incomplete and resembled that of a true Immortal.

<You have fully comprehended Immortal Qi>

<You are now able to use the powers of an Immortal!>

“What do you think?” Yuan asked as he released his Immortal Qi.

Yan Hara swallowed nervously. It wasn’t just the fact that Yuan could use Immortal Qi that left her stunned—it was the sheer mastery with which he wielded it. His control, precision, and efficiency surpassed even her own as a Golden Immortal. It was as if he had been using Immortal Qi for far longer than she had, despite only recently learning it.

However, then she remembered that he was the Divine Paragon’s reincarnation.

“You’re really something, Yuan.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “Immortal Qi is just the beginning. Until I can use Celestial Qi, I am still vulnerable.”

“Celestial Qi?” Yan Hara echoed, her brows raising. “There have been rare cases of cultivators using Immortal Qi before reaching the Immortal Realm, but there’s no precedent—none—of anyone below the God Ascension realm comprehending Celestial Qi.”

“No precedent—yet.” Yuan smiled, leaving her speechless.

Xi Meili and Sun Ling Cai returned to the island shortly after.

“He actually managed to learn Immortal Qi…” Sun Ling Cai muttered under her breath.

Sometime later, Yuan and the others jumped on Sun Ling Cai’s flying treasure and sped away.

After arriving at a city, Yan Hara brought them to a tea house.

“This is Tea Paradise, one of the most famous teahouses in the Nine Heavens.”

Once they were seated, Yan Hara said to the server, “Give us your best tea.”

“Our best…?” The server raised an eyebrow before asking, “Only those with—”

Before the server could even finish her sentence, Sun Ling Cai retrieved a green medallion and placed it on the table.

“Please wait a moment! I will bring your tea right away!”

The server hurriedly left the room.

“What was that about?” Xi Meili asked.

Yan Hara chuckled and said, “Unless you have a membership, your options in this place are limited. However, even with a membership, you won’t be able to experience their best tea without a certain rank. I’m just an ordinary member, but Junior Sister Sun here…”

“I am not a member but an honorable guest. This medallion was given to me by the owner of this teahouse himself,” she explained.

“I don’t know if you know this, but she’s a bigshot in the Nine Heavens. In fact, I am a nobody when compared to her,” Yan Hara said with a smile on her face.

“I heard that she’s a famous painter,” said Yuan.

“Not just any famous painter! She’s the most famous!” Yan Hara bragged as if she was talking about herself.

“It’s really nothing that amazing…” Sun Ling Cai said, appearing somewhat bashful.

Their tea arrived a few minutes later, and the server explained the tea’s background.

“This is the Starry Dream Infused Tea, brewed using million-year-old leaves from a Starry Tree, which can only be found in the Starry Sky, as well as a drop of Celestial Dew.”

Once the server left, Xi Meili said, “I know I am supposed to be impressed by the ingredients, but I don’t know anything about them…”

Sun Ling Cai then explained, “The Starry Tree is an exotic plant that only grows in the Starry Sky. The Celestial Dew is a precious treasure that can only be found in—”

“Let’s just say that the cost of this tea is enough to purchase an Empyrean-grade treasure,” Yan Hara interjected.

“The price of an Empyrean-grade treasure?!” Xi Meili exclaimed, not daring to believe her ears. How could a pot of tea be comparable to an Empyrean-grade treasure?

chapters.title: Chapter 1935 - 1935: Demon Sealing Grotto True Identity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1935 - 1935: Demon Sealing Grotto True Identity

Xi Meili stared at her cup of tea and hesitated to touch it, much less drink it.

“What are you waiting for? Go ahead and drink it,” Yan Hara said right before downing her cup of tea in one motion.

Sun Ling Cai also drank the tea without any hesitation.

Xi Meili swallowed nervously before grabbing the cup. Then, with one swift and forceful movement, she drank it.

“Wow! My cultivation increased by an entire level just from drinking that small cup! And I can still feel a tremendous amount of spiritual energy in my body that is slowly being absorbed!” Xi Meili exclaimed with a shocked face.

“That amount of spiritual energy will probably be enough to raise your cultivation by an entire realm once you fully absorb it. However, due to your lack of cultivation, you’ll only be able to drink one cup of tea for now. If you try to drink more, you’ll absorb too much spiritual energy for your body to handle and… boom—your Dantian will explode.” Yan Hara warned her.

“It’s not just your cultivation that has improved. I’m sure you’re starting to notice it, but all of your senses have also improved greatly. Your body should have strengthened, as well.” Sun Ling Cai added.

“A single cup of tea is able to benefit me so much? It’s hard to believe even though I am experiencing its effects as we speak…” Xi Meili swallowed nervously.

“Ah, thank you very much for this opportunity, seniors.”

“There’s no need to be so formal with us. Just call us Senior Sisters,” Yan Hara said.

Meanwhile, Yuan also consumed his cup of tea. However, unlike Xi Meili, it’d barely affected him. S~eaʀᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“The tea has a really unique flavor. I quite like it,” said Yuan as he placed his cup down.

“Here’s another cup.” Yan Hara quickly refilled his cup.

Without hesitation, Yuan drank the whole thing again.

“Here’s some more.”

Yan Hara poured him another cup; her movements were so natural that it felt like she’d done this a million times.

In the blink of an eye, Yuan drank half of the tea in the pot.

“The two of you should drink too,” he said to them.

“It’s fine. We’ve been here several times. And this is mostly to celebrate your arrival in the Seventh Heaven. You can drink all of it—if you can handle it.”

“If you say so…”

Yuan didn’t hesitate to drink the rest of the tea. Although he liked the taste, it wasn’t the only reason he was drinking it.

<Your comprehension of Celestial Qi has increased slightly>

After his third cup, his Celestial Qi comprehension began improving with every cup. The progress was minimal, nearly imperceptible, but it was progress nonetheless—and for something as elusive as Celestial Qi, even the smallest advancement was invaluable.

As Yuan continued to drink his tea, Yan Hara revealed the situation within the Demon Sealing Clan to him, as well as her current activities.

“The drama between the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto has only gotten worse since your last visit to the library—much worse. We’re at the point where we start fighting the moment we see each other without so much as exchanging any words.”

“Has Qian Chu done anything to calm the situation?” Yuan asked.

“He claims to be working on it, but we haven’t seen any noticeable results even several years later. Most recently, right before I came to meet you, he disappeared somewhere after giving us the order to prioritize the Demon Worshippers.”

“Disappeared?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“He said he had some business, but he didn’t go into any details. However, I have someone following him, so we should know his activities soon.”

Sun Ling Cai then spoke, “By the way, the Demon Sealing Grotto’s headquarters are in the Seventh Heaven, so you’ll most likely run into them while you’re here.”

“Are you worried that I might become a target?” Yuan asked with a smile.

“While that is a concern, I’m pretty confident you’ll be able to defend yourself. After all, you managed to defeat a Demon Emperor alone.”

“Don’t worry, even if I encounter them, they won’t know I’m with the Demon Sealing Clan, and I have no reason to reveal myself.”

Yan Hara then spoke, “Honestly, I was hoping you could speak with the leader of the Demon Sealing Grotto.”

“Hmm? Why?” Yuan asked.

“If we’re going to fight Qian Chu, I think it would benefit us to have the Demon Sealing Grotto on our side,” she admitted.

“I don’t really know much, if anything at all, about them. I know that they’re essentially a second Demon Sealing Clan, but that’s about the extent of my knowledge on them.”

Yan Hara sighed and said, “The Demon Sealing Grotto may have come after the Demon Sealing Clan and was even created after the Demonic Era, but to tell you the truth, they qualify to be the Demon Sealing Clan more than us.”

“I don’t get it.”

“The Demon Sealing Grotto was founded by Demon Sealers who originally belonged to the Demon Sealing Clan,” Yan Hara explained. “After you disappeared, the clan lost its direction. Disagreements over leadership led to internal strife, and people fought to become the next Clan Leader, causing a lot of bloodshed. This led to the creation of the Demon Sealing Grotto, a haven for those who wanted to stay true to the Divine Paragon’s teachings.”

Yuan couldn’t help but notice the similarities between this situation and what the Celestial Overlords were currently facing—internal division and power struggles after their founder left.

Yan Hara continued, “The reason the Demon Sealing Grotto has surpassed the Demon Sealing Clan in strength is simple—they stayed true to the old traditions and rigorous training. Meanwhile, the Demon Sealing Clan… what can I even say? We’ve become pathetic.”

She let out a quiet sigh. “Honestly, I considered joining the Demon Sealing Grotto at first. But in the end, I chose to stay with the ‘original’.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “I understand the situation now. In that case, we should definitely get the Demon Sealing Grotto to be on our side.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1936 - 1936: Seventh Heaven'S Dragon Spiral Mountain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1936 - 1936: Seventh Heaven'S Dragon Spiral Mountain

“When are you going to visit the Demon Sealing Grotto? I would love to go with you, but my position within the Demon Sealing Clan would only make things more difficult for you,” Yan Hara said.

“I have some business at the Dragon Spiral Mountain, so I will be going there first. After that, I want to check up on my friends, but that won’t take long. If nothing else comes up, I’ll visit the Demon Sealing Grotto.”

Yan Hara raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner and inquired, “The Dragon Spiral Mountain? What business do you have with the Holy Dragon Clan? I thought they didn’t accept visitors.”

“You’re aware that my current body is only an avatar of my soul, right?”

“Yes, I am aware. The Celestial Emperor told us about you guys, but so far, you’re the only person I’ve met who’s from that world.”

“Well, I intend on coming to the Nine Heavens with my real body, and the Dragon Spiral Mountain is the key to making that a reality.”

“Coming here with your real body?! Such a thing is possible?” Yan Hara exclaimed in shock.

“The Dragon Spiral Mountain is actually a massive teleportation device that connects with the other Dragon Spiral Mountains, and one of those mountains is in my world. I’m not sure how it works, but I’ll figure it out once I get there.”

“That’s only if the Holy Dragon Clan even allows you entrance,” said Yan Hara.

Yuan pondered for a moment before asking, “At what level is the strongest individual there?”

“If you’re thinking about forcing your way inside, forget about it. They have several God Ascension Realm powerhouses there,” Sun Ling Cai said, shaking her head.

“Do you think I am a brute or something?” Yuan chuckled. “I just wanted to know their prowess.”

Yan Hara then spoke, “If you’re asking about their prowess, the Holy Dragon Clan is within the top 20 most powerful backgrounds in the Nine Heavens, and in terms of Dragon Clans, they’re top 3.”

“Top 20 in the entire Nine Heavens? That’s quite impressive.”

“If you need help, I can ask around,” Sun Ling Cai said.

“No, it’s fine. I have several plans in mind already.”

“If you say so… but if you ever need my help, just ask. Here is my communication jade slip for contacting me.” Sun Ling Cai placed her jade slip on the table before Yuan.

“Thanks.”

Yuan accepted the jade slip.

Sometime later, after Yuan finished drinking all of the tea and they finished their conversation, Yan Hara spoke, “As much as I would like to travel with you, I don’t want to bring unnecessary attention your way, especially from Qian Chu and his followers.”

Yuan nodded. “I understand.”

Sun Ling Cai then spoke, “Well, good luck with the Holy Dragon Clan. They’re renowned for their stubbornness.”

Sun Ling Cai and Yan Hara left first after paying for the tea.

“Let’s head to the Dragon Spiral Mountain now, shall we?” Yuan said to Xi Meili, who eagerly nodded.

After leaving the tea house, they made their way to the nearest teleportation device.

Many teleports later, they arrived at Silverfoam Harbor, a bustling city located near the Sea of Dragons.

The Sea of Dragons was a vast, unique ocean in the Seventh Heaven, renowned for its abundance of sea beasts that bore the appearance of dragons. However, despite their fearsome looks, most of these beasts were not true dragons, and only a rare few possessed actual dragon blood in their veins.

No one truly knew why the sea beasts resembled dragons, but legend had it that a mighty ancient dragon once perished within those waters. Its blood seeped into the sea, tainting the depths and altering the creatures that dwelled within—giving rise to the dragon-like beasts that now roamed the Sea of Dragons.

As for the Dragon Spiral Mountain, it was located on a secluded island somewhere in the middle of the Sea of Dragons. Its remote and treacherous location was a natural deterrent to visitors, especially with powerful Immortal-level sea beasts prowling the surrounding waters.

While Yuan didn’t fear the sea beasts, Xi Meili was a different story.

“Maybe I should stay in this place while you head to the Dragon Spiral Mountain. I don’t want to be a burden,” said Xi Meili as they stood before the Sea of Dragons.

Yuan shook his head.

“Don’t say that. Also, your presence might help me convince the Holy Dragon Clan to assist us, so I cannot leave you behind. Just stay close to me. I’ll protect you.”

“Young Master, you’re not alone.”

Feng Yuxiang suddenly appeared before them.

“I’ll also help out.”

“Thank you…” Xi Meili nodded.

“We also have Yingzi, who is as powerful as a Golden Immortal,” Yuan added.

Feng Yuxiang then spoke, “Instead of flying around like we usually do, it’s about time we start making use of flying treasures.”

With that, she retrieved a small crimson ship from her storage and tossed it into the air. The ship began expanding midair until it was large enough to accommodate a thousand people with ease. The sudden appearance of such a massive flying treasure left everyone at the harbor utterly dumbfounded.

“This is the Soaring Phoenix,” Feng Yuxiang introduced the flying treasure to them as she jumped on it. “Although it’s not the fastest flying treasure out there, it has what many ordinary flying treasures lack—defensive formations that will keep its riders safe. As long as we don’t encounter any Immortal-level threat, we’ll be safe while riding it.”

Yuan and Xi Meili entered the ship a moment later.

“This is quite something. Why didn’t you bring this out before? We could’ve used something like this during some of our long travels.”

Feng Yuxiang then said, “Because it doesn’t operate with just spiritual energy. It also requires Immortal Qi, which can only be found in the Seventh Heaven and above.” S~eaʀᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Moments later, the Soaring Phoenix stirred to life, and in the blink of an eye, it shot into the distance, vanishing from the harbor’s view, leaving only ripples in its wake and stunned silence behind.

chapters.title: Chapter 1937 - 1937: Sea Of Dragons
chapters.body:
Chapter 1937 - 1937: Sea Of Dragons

After entering the Sea of Dragons, it didn’t take long for the sea beasts to launch their assault on the Soaring Phoenix. Even though the flying treasure flew high above the waves, the creatures below unleashed powerful long-range attacks. Some even went as far as jumping out of the water to strike them using their entire body.

The sea beasts of the Sea of Dragons were notoriously aggressive, attacking anything that moved without hesitation. This was one of many reasons why few dared to traverse its waters. Navigating the sea required not only specialized equipment but also a vast amount of resources to defend against the relentless onslaught of these powerful creatures.

“Why are the sea creatures here so aggressive? It wasn’t nowhere this bad at the Purple Sea,” Xi Meili wondered out loud as she watched them bounce off an invisible barrier that protected the flying treasure.

“Well, dragons are aggressive creatures by nature,” Feng Yuxiang said with a casual shrug.

“That’s not true… at least not for the dragons in the Ancient Dragon City,” Xi Meili responded, a hint of defensiveness in her tone.

“I’m not saying all dragons are violent,” Feng Yuxiang replied calmly. “But many are. It’s in their blood—just like how phoenixes are naturally prideful creatures. It’s part of who we are.”

“…”

Xi Meili fell silent, unable to refute Feng Yuxiang’s words, especially with the evidence right before her eyes. The sea beasts, dragon-like in form and ferocity, were living proof of the aggression woven into their bloodline. All she could do was watch in quiet contemplation.

“So, how do you plan on approaching the Holy Dragon Clan, Young Master? I can’t say I’ve dealt with them before, but I’ve heard many stories about them.” Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan.

“Are you going to disguise yourself as a Dragon Emperor like you usually do?”

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “No, that trick probably won’t work with them, especially if they’re one of the top dragon clans in the Nine Heavens. Moreover, their God Ascension experts will most likely see through my disguise.”

“Then how are you going to trick them? I doubt they’d pay attention to a human.” Feng Yuxiang said.

“Since there’s a good chance they have a connection with Dragon Goddess Yeyou, I can use that to my advantage. After all, I have Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s soul with me.”

“I see… so you’re going to use Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s identity…”

“If that doesn’t work, I’ll think of something else.”

The Sea of Dragons was so vast that even with a high-speed flying treasure, crossing from one end to the other would take years. The Dragon Spiral Mountain was located at the very heart of the Sea of Dragons, and Yuan estimated that their journey would take several months at the very least.

With an abundance of time, the group dedicated themselves to cultivation. Thanks to the rich spiritual energy of the Seventh Heaven, Xi Meili’s cultivation advanced at an astonishing pace.

Lan Yingying and Yingzi appeared to cultivate. While Lan Yingying focused on stabilizing her cultivation, Yingzi’s cultivation soared at an unimaginable speed.

After a few weeks at sea, Feng Yuxiang returned to Yuan’s Dantian and entered slumber. Fortunately, the Soaring Phoenix required no active supervision to operate. With a destination already set and absorbing the natural spiritual energy and Immortal Qi around it, the ship continued sailing steadily toward Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Meanwhile, Yuan continued to absorb the spiritual energy within the Demon Devouring Sword. It didn’t take long for his Divine King cultivation to enter the Divine Emperor realm. However, after reaching the peak of Divine Emperor, Yuan encountered his bottleneck.

<An unknown force prevents your cultivation from advancing further>

<Any additional Qi you consume will be used to strengthen your body instead>

After reaching his bottleneck, Yuan focused on improving his Soul Strength.

Unlike spiritual energy, which could only be enhanced by absorbing more of it, Immortal Qi functioned differently. While it could be strengthened through conventional cultivation, it also grew stronger through one’s Soul Strength, hence why Immortals spend more time cultivating their Soul Cultivation Technique than their Spiritual Cultivation Technique.

Of course, due to the rarity of Soul Cultivation Techniques, not all Immortals had the luxury of strengthening their Soul Strength. This was where factions became crucial. Beyond offering protection and resources, many powerful factions possessed Soul Cultivation Techniques, making them invaluable to those seeking to advance further.

‘Heaven’s Indomitable Soul… truly an unfathomable technique. I wonder how it’s related to my past lives…’ Yuan thought to himself as his Soul Strength strengthened with every passing second.

As the Soaring Phoenix drew closer to Dragon Spiral Mountain, the sea beasts grew noticeably more aggressive and far more powerful. Their appearances also resembled dragons more and more.

At first, the Soaring Phoenix effortlessly absorbed all incoming attacks, its defenses unfazed by the sea beasts’ aggression. But now, the sea beasts were powerful enough to shake the flying treasure with their attacks.

“The barrier looks like it’s struggling… It’s not going to break, is it?” Xi Meili asked, a worried look on her face as the ship shuddered again.

“Feng Feng said the flying treasure can withstand attacks up to the Silver Immortal level,” Yuan replied calmly. “The sea beasts are still only in the Immortal Ascension realm, so we’re fine—for now.”

“Is that so?” Feeling relieved, Xi Meili returned to her cultivation.

In just eight months, Xi Meili increased her cultivation to the 8th level of Divine Master from Divine Warrior. Lan Yingying had stabilized her cultivation at the 1st level of the Divine Lord level and had begun cultivating toward the next level by herself. As for Yingzi, her cultivation saw the most significant improvements, reaching the 3rd level of Divine Grandmaster.

As their journey entered its ninth month, the Soaring Phoenix came to a sudden halt for the first time since they had set sail.

Sensing the shift, Yuan and the others paused their cultivation to investigate, expecting to see their destination. But to their confusion, the Dragon Spiral Mountain was nowhere in sight.

“Young Master, several powerful presences are blocking our path,” Feng Yuxiang reported calmly. “But it doesn’t seem like they’ve noticed us. In fact, it looks like they’re currently fighting among themselves.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1938 - 1938: Ancient Monolith
chapters.body:
Chapter 1938 - 1938: Ancient Monolith

After hearing Feng Yuxiang’s words, Yuan expanded the range of his divine sense. Although he kept it active at all times, he usually limited its scope to avoid distracting others—or worse, being mistaken for spying, a common source of conflict among cultivators.

Several hundred miles away, Yuan’s divine sense locked onto a large gathering of individuals. They were unmistakably dragons—humanoid in form but marked by prominent horns and swaying dragon tails.

There were around twenty of them, split into two distinct factions. One group was dressed in elegant white and gold robes, radiating a noble aura. The other wore black and light blue garments, their presence colder and more ominous.

“The Holy Dragon Clan has monopolized the Dragon Spiral Mountain for far too long!” One of the black and blue-clothed individuals shouted angrily.

“Shut up! We’ve been protecting the Dragon Spiral Mountain since the Primordial Era! What right do you have to tell us to leave?!”

“That’s right, you greedy bastards!”

“Your intentions for wanting the Dragon Spiral Mountain aren’t good, either! We’ll never hand it over to you!”

The gold-robed individuals responded.

“Young Master, should we intervene?” Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan.

“Not yet. If we intervene now, we might offend both sides. Let’s wait a little longer,” he calmly responded.

Soon, the Holy Dragon Clan and the opposing faction resumed their battle.

The combatants were all powerful, most being at the Immortal Ascension realm, with each side led by a single Bronze Immortal. At first, the two forces seemed evenly matched, their attacks shaking the very air with dragon techniques. Searᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

But as the battle dragged on, it became clear that the dragons in black and blue robes were gaining the upper hand, steadily pushing back the Holy Dragon Clan.

“I’m going to intervene now.” Yuan suddenly left the Soaring Phoenix and flew toward the battle.

Sometime later, once Yuan arrived at the scene, he cleared his throat loudly and spoke, “Excuse me, can I have a moment of your attention?”

“Huh? Who the hell is that?”

Both sides abruptly halted their battle and turned their attention to Yuan. At first, they tensed with suspicion, assuming he was a reinforcement from the opposing side. But as they caught his scent and realized he was human, their expressions shifted from wariness to confusion.

“I’m trying to find the Dragon Spiral Mountain, but I’m lost,” Yuan continued speaking in a calm and clear despite the tension in the air.

“T-This crazy human bastard actually interrupted us over some bullshit like that…?”

“You’ll pay for your sin with death!”

One of the black-robed dragons suddenly flew at Yuan with clear killing intent radiating from his aura.

“What’s with the hostility? I’m just trying to ask for directions…”

Yuan sighed as he retrieved Dragon God’s Vengeance.

“?!?!?!”

The instant the Dragon God’s Vengeance appeared, the black-robed dragon charging toward Yuan came to an abrupt halt, his instincts screaming at him to stop.

Gasps rippled through the others, their faces etched with instinctive terror. A primal fear gripped them—one they couldn’t suppress—as the weapon exuded a deadly pressure that made their very blood run cold. From the Dragon God’s Vengeance, they felt an overwhelming aura of destruction, one that threatened their lives just by existing.

“What’s wrong? Weren’t you about to punish me for my sins?” Yuan asked calmly, his gaze fixed on the dragon who had just charged at him.

“T-That’s…” the dragon stammered, his voice trembling. He hesitated to speak further, terrified that a single wrong word might provoke the figure before him and cost him his life.

Instead, a voice from the Holy Dragon Clan spoke a moment later.

“What business do you have at Dragon Spiral Mountain?”

The speaker was their Bronze Immortal—a woman whose tone carried both authority and unease. Though Yuan’s cultivation appeared negligible at first glance, her eyes were fixed on the ominous weapon in his grasp. Something about it unsettled her deeply. It radiated a presence that felt inherently hostile to dragons, perhaps even a bane to their kind.

The others from the Holy Dragon Clan quickly grasped the intent behind their Bronze Immortal’s question. Their expressions sharpened, and subtle shifts in their stances revealed their readiness. If Yuan’s next words hinted at any ill intent toward Dragon Spiral Mountain, they were prepared to act—no matter how ominous the weapon in his hand appeared.

“It’s complicated.”

“I don’t care how complicated it is. Explain.”

Yuan’s gaze sharpened, a chill threading through his voice as he addressed the Bronze Immortal. “I don’t like your tone.”

Sensing the undercurrent of danger, the Bronze Immortal instinctively shifted into a defensive stance. The others reacted similarly and prepared to face Yuan.

Seeing this, Yuan stowed away the Dragon God’s Vengeance and said calmly, “My business with Dragon Spiral Mountain concerns Dragon Goddess Yeyou. That’s all I’ll say.”

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou?!”

Gasps rippled through the crowd, shock evident on every face. The mere mention of her name from a human’s lips left them stunned.

Even among the dragon clans, Dragon Goddess Yeyou was a name spoken rarely, known only to a few. That a human would mention her made the moment all the more startling.

Meanwhile, deep within the Dragon Spiral Mountain, a towering monolith of unknown origin stood in silent majesty. Moments after Yuan took out the Dragon God’s Vengeance, the monolith stirred, releasing a profound aura that swept across the mountain like a silent storm. The sudden pulse of unfathomable power startled every being in its reach, even those at the God Ascension Realm.

Within moments, many powerful figures appeared before the monolith.

“W-What is going on with the Ancient Monolith?! It hasn’t reacted like this since Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s disappearance!”

“Is it sensing Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s presence?! Has she finally returned to us?!”

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou has returned!”

“Quickly! Notify the Holy Dragon Emperor and the rest of the clan!”

Unbeknownst to Yuan, he’d accidentally stirred up a storm within the Dragon Spiral Mountain, sending the Holy Dragon Clan into a frenzy.

chapters.title: Chapter 1939 - 1939: Holy Dragon Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1939 - 1939: Holy Dragon Clan

“Well, if you’re not going to tell me where to find the Dragon Spiral Mountain, I’ll take my leave now.” Yuan said shortly after putting away the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

Without giving the Holy Dragon Clan an opportunity to speak, Yuan turned around and flew away.

“W-Wait a second—!”

The Bronze Immortal from the Holy Dragon Clan tried to chase after Yuan but was blocked by the black-robed dragons.

“Where do you think you’re going?! We’re not finished here yet!”

“Get lost!”

Their battle continued, but in a sudden shift, the tides turned. The Holy Dragon Clan, who had been on the brink of defeat just moments earlier, now surged with newfound strength, quickly overpowering the black-robed dragons.

Realizing they could no longer stand against the Holy Dragon Clan, the black-robed dragons retreated. But instead of taking to the skies, they plunged into the sea below, vanishing beneath the waves.

“We need to return to the Dragon Spiral Mountain and report what happened here to the others,” said the Bronze Immortal.

Meanwhile, Yuan returned to the Soaring Phoenix and continued to watch them fight from a safe distance.

“Are you sure you should’ve just left them like that?” Feng Yuxiang asked. She thought he was going to save the Holy Dragon Clan.

“It’s fine. I doubt they’d believe anything I say. My purpose was just to reveal my presence to them and make them question my identity so when I show up at the Dragon Spiral Mountain later, they won’t immediately turn me away.”

“I see… but what if they perish at the hands of those black-robed dragons?”

Yuan then responded in confidence, “Although they are fighting with each other, none of them are showing real killing intent, so it is unlikely they are going to kill each other. If things get real dangerous, I’ll intervene again.”

In the end, events unfolded just as Yuan had predicted. The clash between the Holy Dragon Clan and the black-robed dragons concluded without any true casualties.

Shortly after the Holy Dragon Clan left, the Soaring Phoenix began moving again, slowly following behind them.

At the same time, the rest of the Holy Dragon Clan was in an uproar due to the Ancient Monolith’s sudden activity.

Shortly after the Ancient Monolith sensed the Dragon God’s Vengeance, nearly every single bigshot and important figure within the Holy Dragon Clan appeared at the monolith to witness the phenomenon for themselves.

“I-It’s true! The Ancient Monolith has awakened!”

“Does this mean that Dragon Goddess Yeyou is really back?”

“According to our ancient records, the Ancient Monolith would pulsate with a unique aura when it senses Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s presence nearby. However, it hasn’t happened since her disappearance.”

“What should we do now?”

The Holy Dragon Emperor then spoke.

“We must prepare for Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s return!”

Though it remained uncertain whether Dragon Goddess Yeyou had truly returned—or when she might appear—the Holy Dragon Clan dared not take any chances. They began preparing in earnest for her grand return.

“It stopped!”

Moments later, the Ancient Monolith fell silent once more, its presence returning to stillness. However, that did not change their plans, and the Holy Dragon Clan went on to prepare for Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s return regardless.

Shortly after, the Holy Dragon Clan received news from the group that Yuan had encountered in the Sea of Dragons.

“What? A human who knows about Dragon Goddess Yeyou was looking for the directions to our Dragon Spiral Mountain?” The Holy Dragon Emperor almost couldn’t believe his ears.

Besides those living in the Dragon Spiral Mountain and those who lived near it, knowledge of Dragon Goddess Yeyou was almost nonexistent.

‘Does this human have something to do with the Ancient Monolith’s activation?’ he wondered inwardly as he received this news.

“Where is this human now?” he asked the messenger.

“According to the scouts, he left before they could stop him.”

“What? How could a mere human escape from their grasp?” Holy Dragon Emperor questioned out loud with raised eyebrows.

“They were stopped by those sea bastards, whom they encountered and was fighting before meeting the human.”

“Seriously…?” The Holy Dragon Emperor rubbed his eyes and continued, “How long before they return?”

“They should return in 45 days.”

“Send them directly to me once they return. I need to talk with them personally.”

“As you wish, Your Majesty.”

Once the messenger left, the Holy Dragon Emperor lifted his head to look at the tall ceiling and mumbled, “Are you truly back, Dragon Goddess…?”

Forty-three days later, the dragons who had encountered Yuan finally returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain. They were summoned without delay by the Holy Dragon Emperor, who demanded they recount every detail of their encounter.

‘Their encounter with that human coincides almost perfectly with the activation of the Ancient Monolith… And a spear of such unfathomable power, is it?’ The Holy Dragon Emperor mused silently, his thoughts deep and unreadable.

“Your Majesty, what should we do about them? They’re becoming more brazen by the day,” asked the Bronze Immortal, who was still unaware of the Ancient Monolith’s activation.

“The Azure Dragon Clan, huh? Stubborn as ever—they won’t rest until the Dragon Spiral Mountain is theirs,” the Holy Dragon Emperor muttered, his gaze distant. “I have no desire for war, but neither can we surrender the mountain.” He let out a weary sigh.

“So war is unavoidable, huh?” The Bronze Immortal shook her head. S~eaʀᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Unfortunately. Anyway, you are dismissed. Thank you for the report.”

“I do not deserve your recognition, Your Majesty.”

Time passed swiftly, and within a week, the Soaring Phoenix had brought Yuan close enough to see the Dragon Spiral Mountain rising on the horizon.

Unlike its counterpart on Earth, the Dragon Spiral Mountain in the Seventh Heaven was vastly more expansive—so immense that the one on Earth would amount to barely ten percent of its size.

After another day of travel, the Soaring Phoenix finally reached the Dragon Spiral Mountain. However, before they could get close to the land, several figures emerged from the skies, surrounding their flying treasure and blocking any further approach.

chapters.title: Chapter 1940 - 1940: A Real Dragon Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 1940 - 1940: A Real Dragon Emperor

“Identify yourselves!”

A group of golden-robed dragons surrounded the Soaring Phoenix.

“My name is Xiao Yang, and this is my traveling companion, Xi Meili.”

“What purpose do you have at our Dragon Spiral Mountain?”

“That doesn’t concern you,” Yuan calmly said.

“Doesn’t concern us?! We, the Holy Dragon Clan, are the rightful owners of the Dragon Spiral Mountain! Everything related to the mountain is of our concern!”

“If you’re looking to cause trouble here, get lost before we forcefully remove you!”

Hearing their threats, Yuan laughed out loud, “I heard the Holy Dragon Clan are Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s loyal followers. What if I told you that I am Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy? Will you still tell me to leave then?”

“What?! Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy?!” seaʀᴄh thё Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

A wave of shock swept through the crowd, but it quickly faded, replaced by rising anger, as though Yuan’s words had been a deliberate insult to them.

One of them sneered, “I was wondering what sort of excuse you’ll give, but Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy? What absolute rubbish!”

Another spoke with contempt in his voice, “Who do you think you’re trying to fool?! Why would Dragon Goddess Yeyou choose a lowly human as her envoy?!”

Yuan turned to look at the one who had just questioned him and responded with narrowed eyes, “That’s a funny thing to say, considering that Dragon Goddess Yeyou was once a loyal servant to a lowly human.”

Yuan’s words instantly shifted the atmosphere, drawing frowns from the surrounding dragons, yet not one of them could offer a rebuttal.

“If you claim to be Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy, you must have proof. Let us see it.”

Yuan fell silent, mostly because he didn’t have anything that could be used as evidence.

“What? You don’t have anything to prove your identity? Why am I not surprised? How dare you try to take advantage of us using Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s name! You’ll pay for your crimes with death! Kill them!”

The next moment, all of the dragons lunged at the Soaring Phoenix.

Seeing this, Yuan activated his Dragon Transformation and retrieved Dragon God’s Vengeance.

However, he only intended to scare them, so he didn’t attack them. Of course, the dragons were all frozen from fear the moment they sensed the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

Yuan then spoke, “It’s not that I don’t want to show you proof. Information about Dragon Goddess Yeyou is highly sensitive, and you’re simply not qualified to hear it. Take me to your leader—the head of the Holy Dragon Clan.”

The dragons exchanged uncertain glances, but before any could speak, a powerful presence descended upon them.

A figure emerged, radiating a domineering aura that commanded instant reverence. He, too, was a dragon, clad in a resplendent golden robe nearly identical to the Golden Dragon Robe in Yuan’s possession.

Yuan couldn’t discern the depth of his cultivation, but instinct told him this was a being who had stepped into the God Ascension Realm.

At the sight of him, the surrounding dragons immediately bowed in unison.

“We greet His Majesty, the Holy Dragon Emperor!”

In the next instant, several more dragons appeared, each radiating a profound and majestic presence—just a shade beneath that of the Holy Dragon Emperor himself.

Seeing this, the dragons hastily greeted them, “W-We greet the Holy Dragon Ancestor, Supreme Elder, Holy Princess, Holy Prince, Holy—”

“Enough.”

The Dragon Emperor suddenly interrupted. If he’d allowed them to continue the greetings, they’d be there all day.

He then turned to face Yuan with slightly narrowed eyes.

‘He’s clearly human, yet he possesses the ability to transform into a dragon… Only those with a pure and high-quality dragon bloodline can achieve such a feat. And yet, such individuals usually carry a strong dragon scent—but from him, I sense nothing of that sort, ‘ the Holy Dragon Emperor mused inwardly, his gaze sharp as he studied Yuan’s Dragon Transformation.

When he attempted to probe deeper into Yuan’s body, a sudden chill crept down his spine. He couldn’t quite identify it, but within Yuan’s very bones, he sensed the presence of something ancient… something unfathomably profound, lying dormant yet quietly watching.

‘Among the scarce fragments of knowledge surrounding Dragon Goddess Yeyou, there was one rumor that stood out. It was said she possessed the power to bestow her talents upon any being, dragon or not. With her blessing, even the weakest of creatures, an ant, could wield the might of dragons,’ he recalled, the memory surfacing like a forgotten echo from a bygone era.

“Human, I heard your earlier words,” the Dragon Emperor said, his tone calm yet laced with innate pride. “I may not be the leader of the Holy Dragon Clan, but my status within it is no less significant. Tell me—am I, the Dragon Emperor, worthy to see your so-called proof?”

‘So that’s a real Dragon Emperor, huh?’

This was Yuan’s first encounter with a real Dragon Emperor. However, after encountering many ancient dragons within the Primal Expanse, the Dragon Emperor before him did not feel anything special.

“I don’t mind showing you.”

Holy Dragon Emperor nodded and spoke, “We can continue our conversation somewhere more private. Follow me.”

He turned around and flew inside the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Seeing this, Feng Yuxiang, who was still inside Yuan’s Dantian, continued the Soaring Phoenix to follow the Holy Dragon Emperor.

Soon, they entered the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Although the other dragons didn’t like the idea of letting someone as suspicious as Yuan enter their sacred territory, none of them could defy the Dragon Emperor’s decision.

Once the Soaring Phoenix entered the Dragon Spiral Mountain, the other powerhouses followed behind to ensure Yuan wouldn’t try anything funny. Though their cultivations far surpassed Yuan’s and they could probably kill him with a sneeze, an unshakable unease settled over them in the presence of the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

Meanwhile, the moment Yuan entered the Dragon Spiral Mountain, he could feel something stirring within him.

‘This feeling… the Nine Dragon Symbols? Is it reacting to something within the Dragon Spiral Mountain?’ he wondered inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1941 - 1941: Destroying The Ancient Monolith
chapters.body:
Chapter 1941 - 1941: Destroying The Ancient Monolith

After entering the Dragon Spiral Mountain, the Dragon Emperor led Yuan to the mouth of a colossal cave, its entrance veiled in a faint, ancient pressure. As they approached, the Nine Dragon Symbols within Yuan stirred restlessly, trembling with excitement. Whatever it was resonating with was inside the cave.

“This is the first time an outsider has visited this place,” said Holy Dragon Emperor as they reached the entrance.

“It’s an honor,” Yuan smiled.

“However, if you fail to prove your identity, you won’t be able to leave this place alive.”

“…”

“Let’s go.”

The Dragon Emperor entered the cave, and Yuan calmly followed him. Xi Meili quietly trailed behind Yuan, staying close to him. Due to her lack of cultivation, just being in the presence of so many powerful beings was a challenge for her.

After traversing a shadowed tunnel, they emerged into a vast, dimly lit cavern. At its center stood a towering monolith, ancient and imposing, its presence dominating the chamber in solemn silence.

‘That monolith must be the cause for the Nine Dragon Symbol’s unrest…’ Yuan narrowed his gaze at the Ancient Monolith.

The Dragon Emperor came to a halt about a dozen meters from the monolith, then turned to face Yuan.

“Only those who hold the highest status within the Holy Dragon Clan are permitted to enter this place,” he said calmly. “So rest assured—everyone present is just as qualified as I am to witness your proof.”

Yuan turned around to look at the others who followed them to the monolith and nodded, “I don’t mind.”

“Then whenever you are ready.”

The Dragon Emperor and the others fell into a heavy silence, their gazes fixed on Yuan as they patiently awaited the proof of his claim as the envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“…”

‘Since it has come to this point, let’s just try summoning her…’

Yuan retrieved the Dragon God’s Vengeance again, unaware of the Holy Dragon Clan’s true intentions.

They alone knew of the Ancient Monolith’s ability to sense the presence of Dragon Goddess Yeyou. And with Yuan’s arrival coinciding perfectly with its recent awakening, they had a strong suspicion that he was the cause. They only needed to confirm their suspicions with their own eyes.

However, when Yuan retrieved the Dragon God’s Vengeance, the Ancient Monolith remained silent.

This caused the Dragon Emperor and the others to raise their eyebrows.

‘He’s not the cause for the Ancient Monolith’s awakening?’

‘If not him, then who was responsible?’

The Dragon Emperor let out a quiet sigh, his voice laced with disappointment as he spoke,

“He’s not the one. Kill him.”

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened in shock at the sudden decree. He hadn’t even had the chance to call upon Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“I’ll handle him,” said a girl with the appearance of a refined young lady.

In the next moment, her aura flared—powerful and sharp—revealing her cultivation at the Silver Immortal Realm.

“You dare exploit the name of Dragon Goddess Yeyou? You wretched human… you’ll pay for your insolence!”

With a flash of movement, she lunged at Yuan, her body partially transformed. Her right arm morphed into a massive, white-scaled limb that gleamed with draconic power as it tore through the air toward him.

Yuan did not dare to underestimate her and immediately activated Dragon Transformation.

The moment Yuan transformed, the Ancient Monolith behind the Dragon Emperor began trembling. However, nobody there noticed it since they were fully absorbed with Yuan’s transformation.

“Get behind me!” Yuan shouted, stepping forward to shield Xi Meili as his gaze locked onto the charging young lady.

A heartbeat later, their clash erupted. The sheer force of their collision sent shockwaves through the cavern, causing the very walls to tremble beneath the pressure.

Though Yuan managed to block the attack, the impact sent him hurtling through the air, and he was thrown directly toward the Ancient Monolith, crashing into its surface a moment later with a resounding thud.

“Yuan!” Xi Meili exclaimed.

“I’m fine…” Yuan muttered as he rose to his feet. Though he hadn’t suffered any injuries, the situation was far from ideal. No matter how talented he was, there was no way for him to defeat so many experts at once. Even if he could escape with his life intact, there was still Xi Meili, who was his biggest concern.

“Huh?”

However, just as Yuan braced for the young lady’s next strike, he paused. Something was wrong. She, along with the other dragons, stood frozen, their eyes locked onto him, wide with disbelief, as if they were witnessing the impossible.

It was then that Yuan noticed a subtle vibration behind him—the Ancient Monolith was trembling beneath his touch.

“Why is the Ancient Monolith reacting now?! Is Dragon Goddess Yeyou nearby?!”

The Dragon Emperor immediately spread his divine sense as far as possible, covering the entire Dragon Spiral Mountain and much more. However, he was unable to sense Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

As the Dragon Emperor and the others anxiously scanned the area for any sign of Dragon Goddess Yeyou, a sudden flash of movement drew their eyes back to Yuan.

The Dragon God’s Vengeance slipped free from his grasp, rising into the air to hover before him, gleaming with an eerie light.

“Yeyou…?” Yuan murmured, confused.

The spear slowly tilted—its tip turning not toward the crowd, but toward Yuan… or more precisely, the Ancient Monolith behind him.

Whoosh!

In the blink of an eye, the Dragon God’s Vengeance surged forward without hesitation, transforming into a streak of light. Before anyone could react, it pierced the Ancient Monolith and rooted itself within—a structure long believed by the Holy Dragon Clan to be utterly indestructible.

<Dragon God’s Vengeance has absorbed a fragment of Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Soul>

<Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Soul has regained some of its strength>

“Y-You destroyed the Ancient Monolith?! Are you insane?!” Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The Dragon Emperor roared in anger after snapping out of his daze. Even if Yuan were Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy, he wouldn’t be spared for such blasphemy.

But in the very next moment, the Dragon God’s Vengeance released a wave of profound aura—deep and ancient. From within its radiant glow, a figure slowly began to emerge, cloaked in divine presence.

Once she fully materialized, she was revealed to be a woman of breathtaking beauty. Yet, none among the dragons spared a thought for her appearance, as her overwhelming presence was so overbearing that it engulfed the entire Dragon Spiral Mountain.

And though none of the dragons recognized her appearance, not a single one of them doubted her identity for even a split second.

She was none other than Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“D-Dragon Goddess—”

The Dragon Emperor began to speak, but his words faltered and died in his throat as Dragon Goddess Yeyou turned to face Yuan without even sparing them a glance.

Yuan stood frozen, eyes wide in disbelief.

Unlike her previous appearance, she was no longer a semi-transparent apparition. The Dragon Goddess standing before him radiated presence and substance, as though she had truly descended into the world once more.

“Yeyou…” Yuan breathed her name, the word slipping from his lips without thought.

Without a word, Dragon Goddess Yeyou stepped forward. In one fluid motion, she grasped his collar with a firm hand and pulled him into her before pressing her soft lips against his in a deep, passionate kiss that left everyone there in stunned silence.
