chapters.title: Chapter 1942 - 1942: Four Pieces
chapters.body:
Chapter 1942 - 1942: Four Pieces

“W-What does this vixen think she’s doing to my Young Master?!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed, her voice filled with envy as she watched this unfold from Yuan’s Dantian.

The members of the Holy Dragon Clan stood frozen in place, eyes wide and jaws agape, as if the very ground had been pulled from beneath them. Some looked as though their souls had momentarily left their bodies, stunned beyond words by what they were witnessing.

After what felt like an eternity, Dragon Goddess Yeyou finally released Yuan and spoke, “This doesn’t mean I have forgiven you, so don’t think for a second that you’re out of the hot water.”

However, Yuan ignored her words and changed the topic, asking, “I didn’t know your soul wasn’t in one piece. Why didn’t you mention this before?”

“Did you think a mere treasure could contain my soul as a whole? In order to survive, I had to split my soul into several pieces. I am fated to recover my soul, so there’s no point mentioning it,” she explained.

Only a being of Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s caliber could speak of splitting her soul with such casual ease. Even cultivators at the God Ascension Realm wouldn’t dare attempt such a feat, and realistically, not even a Cultivation God might possess the ability to do what she had done.

“How many more pieces of your soul are still out there?” Yuan then asked.

“I split my soul into four pieces, so there are still two pieces.”

“Do you know where I can find them?”

“No, since it was the Dragon Ancestor who safeguarded my soul. If you want to know, ask him. However, you’ll eventually find them even if you don’t.”

“Because of fate, huh?”

“Anyway… I’ve reached my limit,” Dragon Goddess Yeyou said softly, glancing down at her hand as it began to fade, turning slowly transparent.

Her form had taken shape thanks to the recovery of a second fragment of her soul—but it was only temporary.

Before she returned to the Dragon God’s Vengeance, Dragon Goddess Yeyou turned to face the Holy Dragon Clan and said, “If I had a little more time, I would’ve punished you for baring your fangs at him, but I don’t, so I’m willing to give you a second chance. Treat him as you would treat me.”

“A-As you wish, Dragon Goddess!” the Dragon Emperor responded, bowing his head low in deference.

The others followed suit, dropping to their knees in solemn reverence.

As for the young lady who had attacked Yuan, she stood frozen in place, mainly because Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s gaze was directly on her. The sheer pressure of such a gaze caused her body to tremble nonstop, her clothes drenched in sweat.

Without saying another word, Dragon Goddess Yeyou returned inside the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

A deafening silence settled over the cavern in the wake of Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s departure. Not a single member of the Holy Dragon Clan rose to their feet even long after her presence had faded. It was as if they dared not move, awaiting Yuan’s permission.

“You guys can stand up now…” Yuan eventually said, breaking the silence.

The dragons slowly rose to their feet, nodding in silent acknowledgment. Their gazes lingered on Yuan, filled with questions they clearly wished to ask, yet none of them dared to speak them aloud.

Knowing that his identity would be revealed sooner or later, Yuan decided to just reveal it himself.

“When I said that I was Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy… I lied, sorry. I’m actually the master of the Nine Divine Supreme—the Immortal Monarch. I know this is hard to believe, so I am willing to prove my identity.”

However, to his surprise, the Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “There’s no need. We believe you. After all, the Dragon Goddess Yeyou would only do something like that to the Immortal Monarch.”

Of course, despite his confident tone, his face was full of shock and disbelief.

“The Holy Dragon Clan is at your service, Immortal Monarch. If you wish, we’d even be willing to go to war for you.”

Yuan shook his head and quickly said, “That’s not necessary. I didn’t come to the Dragon Spiral Mountain to take advantage of the Holy Dragon Clan.”

“In that case, why did you come here? Was it to retrieve the Dragon Goddess’ soul from the Ancient Monolith?”

“No, that was just a coincidence. I came here because I need to use the Dragon Spiral Mountain’s teleport formation.” seaʀᴄh thё Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“The teleport formation…? May I ask for what purpose? It hasn’t been activated ever since the Divine Heaven became the Nine Heavens.”

“Do you know about ‘Players’?’

“You mean those from the missing fragment of the Divine Heaven who travel here with the help of the Celestial Emperor—those like you?”

Yuan calmly nodded. He was not surprised that the Dragon Emperor saw through his avatar since it had happened before.

“My real body is currently in the other world, but due to special circumstances, I am unable to return to it. In other words, I am stuck here as a soul avatar, and in order to return to my body, I will need to activate the Dragon Spiral Mountain’s teleport formation and connect this world to my world, where the other Dragon Spiral Mountain exists.”

The Dragon Emperor nodded his head as he digested the information.

“I understand the situation. Unfortunately, we have a slight issue. The key to activating the formation is currently in the Azure Dragon Clan’s possession…”

“Azure Dragon Clan?” Yuan raised his eyebrows. He recalled the dragons in black and blue robes from before.

“Yes, they’re the dragon clan that commands the Sea of Dragons, and they’ve been trying to acquire the Dragon Spiral Mountain for centuries now. We lost the key to them several decades ago after losing to them in a certain battle.”

“Why are they trying to acquire this place?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“They never say it out loud and refute anything we say, but it’s clear that they want the Dragon Spiral Mountain to appease the Great One,” explained the Dragon Emperor.

“Appeasing the Great One…?” Yuan repeated in a mumble.

chapters.title: Chapter 1943 - 1943: Azure Dragon Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1943 - 1943: Azure Dragon Clan

“Appeasing the Great One?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

The Dragon Emperor gave a solemn nod and began to explain, “Just as we are devoted to Dragon Goddess Yeyou, the Azure Dragon Clan offers their loyalty to the Great One. They would go to any lengths just to earn even a sliver of his attention.”

“Unfortunately for them, the Great One rarely shows interest in anything, as he already possesses everything one could desire. However, there is one exception—he always shows interest in matters related to Dragon Goddess Yeyou. And while our Dragon Spiral Mountain may not hold any true connection to her… It’s enough to pique the Great One’s interest.”

“Fortunately for us, the Great One is too prideful to personally take over this place, hence the Azure Dragon Clan’s motivation. Anyway, if we want to activate the teleport formation, we will need to retrieve the key from them, but they definitely won’t let it go without a fight.”

After taking a moment to digest the information, Yuan asked, “How did you lose it in the first place?”

A stiff smile made its way on the Dragon Emperor’s face as he spoke, “I’m ashamed to even say this, but we lost it in a bet.”

Yuan looked at him with a dumbfounded face.

“A bet?”

He nodded and explained, “Every five hundred years, all dragon families with royal bloodlines gather to have their youngest evaluated for talent. During the last gathering, we made a wager with the Azure Dragon Clan—staking the key on our belief that our family would produce the most gifted genius. But in the end… they won.”

“In other words, they won the key fair and square, huh?” Yuan sighed. He was hoping that the Azure Dragon Clan had acquired the key by force, giving him justification to reclaim it by force.

“Anyway, I’ll think of something to reclaim the key. Is there a way to contact the Azure Dragon Clan? I’d like to have a conversation with them.”

“Of course. There’s no way the Azure Dragon Clan would refuse audience with the Immortal Monarch,” said the Dragon Emperor.

Yuan quickly shook his head and said, “No, I don’t want to reveal my identity. In fact, I expect everyone here to keep my identity a secret. It’s not time for the world to know that I have returned… not yet.”

“I understand. In that case, while it might not be peaceful, we can still set up a meeting with them. However, we’ll need a reason to do so. They won’t just accept a meeting for no reason.”

Yuan began pondering in silence. A few moments later, a sly smile appeared on his face. The Dragon Emperor and the other subconsciously swallowed nervously after seeing his smile. S~eaʀᴄh the novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Alright, this is what we’ll do…” Yuan began explaining his plan to the Holy Dragon Clan.

Sometime later, the Dragon Emperor smiled at Yuan’s plan and nodded, “Alright, I’ll follow your plan. Since it might take some time for them to respond, we’ll prepare a living quarter for you to stay.”

He turned to look at the young lady who had fought with Yuan and said, “Sheng Qing, take them to our best living quarters and stay with them while I contact the Azure Dragon Clan.”

“Y-Yes!” she hastily nodded.

The Dragon Emperor turned to Yuan and said, “There’s still much I’d like to discuss with you… but let’s continue this conversation later—after I’ve had time to fully process everything that’s happened today. To be honest, I’m still overwhelmed. It’s difficult to believe any of this is real…”

“Take your time.”

Yuan and Xi Meili followed Sheng Qing to their living quarters shortly after.

Once Yuan was gone, the Dragon Emperor and the others could finally breathe properly. Some of them even fell onto their buttocks from fatigue despite being Immortals.

“What do you think of that human? Is he really the Immortal Monarch?”

“I don’t know about that human, but that woman was definitely Dragon Goddess Yeyou. I have never felt my blood tremble like that before—not even before the Great One.”

The Dragon Emperor spoke with a thoughtful expression on his handsome face, “He appears far too young to be the Immortal Monarch, and his cultivation is too low. But that doesn’t rule out the possibility. In the end, none of those details truly matter. What does matter is his direct connection to Dragon Goddess Yeyou… close enough for her to kiss him without hesitation. Aside from the Immortal Monarch himself, I can’t imagine her showing such intimacy to anyone else.”

After a brief pause, the Dragon Emperor continued, “Furthermore, Dragon Goddess Yeyou herself commanded that we treat him as we would treat her. So even if he’s not the Immortal Monarch, we already have more than enough reason to honor him as one.”

The surrounding dragons exchanged glances, but none voiced dissent—nor did any show signs of disapproval. Dragon Goddess Yeyou had spoken, and to her loyal followers, her word was absolute.

“What should we tell the rest of the clan if we have to keep his identity a secret?”

“Just follow the original script and tell them he’s Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy. As for Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s return… we should let them know since they didn’t say anything about that.”

“Let’s do that then.”

Sometime later, the Dragon Emperor and the others left the cave.

Meanwhile, after departing from the cave, Sheng Qing led Yuan and Xi Meili toward the residential area. The Holy Dragon Clan rarely received visitors, so there were no designated guest quarters—only the private residences of its members. Fortunately, there were still plenty of unoccupied buildings, and Yuan was given the most luxurious building in the Dragon Spiral Mountain while Xi Meili was given the building next to his.

“If you need anything, you can find me here,” Sheng Qing said to them.

“Thank you.”

“Um… Senior Immortal…” Sheng Qing suddenly became fidgety, looking like she was hesitant about something.

Yuan could guess what she wanted to say and spoke with a smile on his face, “If you’re worried about attacking me, you don’t have to worry. I wasn’t hurt, and I don’t blame you for your actions.”

“T-Thank you!”

She immediately bowed, feeling an immense relief, as if a mountain had been lifted off her shoulders.

chapters.title: Chapter 1944 - 1944: Young Dragons
chapters.body:
Chapter 1944 - 1944: Young Dragons

A few hours after Yuan and Xi Meili were given a living quarter, the Dragon Emperor showed up at their place.

“I have contacted the Azure Dragon Clan, but it may take a few days, if not weeks, before we get a response,” he said. “In the meantime, you can treat the Dragon Spiral Mountain as if it’s your own home.”

“I will do just that,” Yuan nodded.

“Would you like to walk with me? I’ll give you a tour around the place. There’s also a lot I would still like to discuss with you.”

“Sure.”

“Can I come along?” Xi Meili asked.

“I don’t see why you cannot.”

As they walked through the residential grounds with the Dragon Emperor leading the way, he suddenly spoke, his tone laced with concern, “How is the Dragon Goddess? Is she… well?”

Yuan nodded and responded, “She’s fine.”

“May I ask what happened to her? She disappeared right after the Divine Heavens split into the Nine Heavens and hasn’t made an appearance until today.”

“Let’s just say we faced a powerful opponent.”

The Dragon Emperor fell silent. He couldn’t fathom a being stronger than Dragon Goddess Yeyou, who was so powerful that not even the Dragon Ancestor and the Great One could hold a candle against her.

“I’d like to ask you something as well,” Yuan said, breaking the silence. “Why does the Holy Dragon Clan devote itself to her?”

The Dragon Emperor glanced at him, then replied, “It is in a dragon’s nature to follow strength, and Dragon Goddess Yeyou stands among the most powerful beings in existence. For many, that alone would be reason enough to revere her. But for us, there is another reason we worship her.”

“Back then, the Holy Dragon Clan was of no great lineage—just an ordinary bloodline, struggling to survive. We stood on the brink of annihilation.”

He continued in a solemn voice, “But Dragon Goddess Yeyou descended in our darkest hour. She gave us a second chance, blessing us with a drop of her divine blood. That single act transformed our lineage, elevating our bloodline to rival even those of royal descent.”

“Oh, I think I remember when that happened.” Yuan suddenly said with a reminiscing expression on his face.

“Really? It was a time long before I was even born,” the Dragon Emperor looked at him with surprise.

Even now, he could hardly believe Yuan was the Immortal Monarch.

As they wandered the Dragon Spiral Mountain, its residents would stare at them from a distance while mumbling to each other.

“That must be Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy. Why do you think he’s here?”

“I’m more interested in why Dragon Goddess Yeyou chose a human to be her envoy.”

“It’s not that surprising if you consider that she used to follow a human around.”

“You mean the Immortal Monarch? People claim he’s human, but I do not believe it for a second. He was definitely a dragon disguising himself as a human, and you cannot convince me otherwise!”

The Dragon Spiral Mountain in the Nine Heavens closely resembled its counterpart on Earth—a towering peak with a spiraling path winding around its body. However, it was vastly larger in scale, both in height and breadth, soaring several times higher and far more expansive.

Its colossal size was no coincidence—it was a deliberate tribute to Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s overwhelming power. The mountain had been purposefully shaped to surpass the others, a testament to her unmatched divinity.

The foot of the mountain was filled with stores, restaurants, and all the amenities one would expect from a thriving city. A little higher up the mountain lay the residential area, sprawling across most of its vast expanse and housing the majority of its inhabitants.

The praying area and training grounds were situated above the residential area, and beyond that point, only those with significant status within the clan were permitted to ascend any higher.

“How many individuals does the Holy Dragon Clan hold?” Yuan asked as they arrived at the training grounds, where many young dragons could be seen sparring with each other while supervised by Immortal experts.

“We have around 20,000 dragons within the Holy Dragon Clan. It may seem like a lot, but we’re actually one of the smallest Dragon Clans in the Nine Heavens. The Azure Dragon Clan, for example, has over 100,000 members within its clan. Moreover, only about 50 of us actually have the Holy Dragon Bloodline.”

The young dragons at the training grounds quickly noticed the Dragon Emperor and bowed to him with respect. A few of them approached him and asked, “Your Majesty, who is this person? He also has a unique scent on him that I don’t recognize,” one of the young dragons asked as he looked at Yuan with curiosity.

The Dragon Emperor smiled and declared, “This individual is a human—and the envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy?!”

The young dragons stiffened in shock, and the realization hit them like thunder. Without hesitation, they bowed deeply to Yuan.

“We greet the envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou!”

Though they didn’t fully understand what the title entailed, the mere presence of her name within it was enough. That alone made it sacred.

Yuan gave a casual wave of his hand and said, “No need for formalities. Just treat me like a friend.”

“Then… what exactly is an envoy?!” one of the young dragons suddenly asked, curiosity gleaming in their eyes.

“It’s like a messenger or representative,” Yuan replied with a faint smile. “In this case, I’m a messenger for Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

“Wow! Does this mean that Dragon Goddess Yeyou sent you here?!”

“Why did she send you here? Does she have a message for us?”

The young dragons bombarded Yuan with questions.

“Enough. You’re bothering him,” the Dragon Emperor said sharply, his tone firm and commanding, bringing an immediate hush over the young dragons.

“It’s fine,” Yuan said, and he began answering their questions one by one. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“That’s right, I was sent here by Dragon Goddess Yeyou. As for why I am here… I cannot tell you the full details, but it is related to the Azure Dragon Clan.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1945 - 1945: Young Dragons(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1945 - 1945: Young Dragons(2)

“The Dragon Azure Clan! Did the Dragon Goddess send you here to get rid of those pests trying to steal our home?!”

The young dragons’ eyes sparkled when Yuan mentioned the Dragon Azure Clan.

“Who knows?” Yuan shrugged.

A few of the young dragons then approached Xi Meili and asked her, “Are you also Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy?”

“Hm? No, I am not. I am just his traveling companion.”

“How old are you, Senior Sister?” one of them suddenly asked.

“I stopped counting after 10,000 years,” she calmly stated.

“Huh? You’re only at that level even after 10,000 years? You must be very lazy, Senior Sister.”

“What?! Who are you calling lazy?!”

Xi Meili’s expression froze in disbelief after being called lazy.

Though she was still considered young by dragon standards, the dragons before her were even younger—all under a thousand years old. And yet, each one had a cultivation level that surpassed her own.

Because of this, the young dragons could only view Xi Meili, who was older yet weaker than them, as lazy in their eyes.

“If you’re not lazy, why is your cultivation so much lower than ours?” asked one of the young dragons, and the others stared at her with a suspicious gaze.

“T-That’s because I grew up in a place with much less spiritual energy than you all!” Xi Meili explained.

Even though she knew she wasn’t lazy, Xi Meili couldn’t help but feel uneasy under the weight of so many young dragons’ doubtful gazes.

Yuan then spoke, “We actually just arrived in the Seventh Heaven.”

“Ohh…”

The young dragons nodded their heads as if they understood the situation.

“Then, would you like to train with us, Senior Sister?”

“Of course!” Xi Meili didn’t hesitate to accept their offer.

“Have fun,” Yuan said with a smile on his face.

Xi Meili nodded and followed the young dragons to the arena, where she began sparring with them first.

Due to their difference in cultivation, they agreed not to use any martial techniques and focused purely on their skills.

“I guess she really wasn’t lying. Although her cultivation is lacking, she’s very experienced and skillful.”

Xi Meili quickly earned the young dragons’ respect with her skillful performance.

However, despite her ten thousand years of experience, she was unable to secure a single victory. Out of ten matches, she lost them all.

It wasn’t just their superior cultivation—the young dragons possessed exceptional technique, battle sense, and even physique. They were simply incomparably stronger than the dragons she would spar with within the Ancient Dragon City.

“Damn it… I knew I was weak, but I didn’t expect myself to be this weak… Is this what it means to be a frog in a well?” Xi Meili mumbled as she lay on the arena, her body soaked in sweat.

“Don’t look so down. You’re very talented. I’m sure you’ll catch up soon,” Yuan said as he approached the arena.

Xi Meili turned her head to look at him without getting up and asked, “If I have your ‘Seal’, would it make it stronger?”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan nodded, “Yes, you would undoubtedly become stronger with the Immortal Monarch’s Supreme Seal.”

He answered with a serious face.

“However, I won’t give you the seal if that’s your reason for wanting it.”

Xi Meili showed a bittersweet smile.

“I know. I was only joking.”

She immediately stood up and spoke loudly, “Alright! Let’s spar again!”

Yuan turned to the Dragon Emperor.

“This is just a personal request of mine, but do you mind letting her train with you guys?”

“Of course. The young ones seem to have taken a liking to her as well,” the Dragon Emperor said with a faint nod. “While her cultivation and experience are certainly lacking, I can sense a remarkable talent within her. My instincts also tell me her bloodline is unique—perhaps even one that could rival our own Holy Dragon Bloodline.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Have you heard of the Ancient Dragon City?”

The Dragon Emperor’s eyes widened.

“You mean to tell me that she’s from that place?”

“Not only that, but she’s also from a royal bloodline.”

The Dragon Emperor turned to look at Xi Meili and asked, “What was her name again?”

“Xi Meili.”

“Surnamed Xi!”

The Dragon Emperor trembled.

“You recognize it?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

He nodded thoughtfully and said, “There is another Dragon Emperor who bears the surname Xi. His clan resides in the Eighth Heaven. It may be nothing more than coincidence… but there’s a chance their bloodlines are connected.”

“The Eighth Heaven, huh? I’ll be sure to visit them in the future.”

Some time later, a young female dragon approached Yuan and bowed politely.

“Senior Envoy, may I respectfully request a spar with you?” she asked with clear determination in her voice.

Yuan turned to look at her. Though her appearance resembled that of a human teenager, she could very well be over a thousand years old. Her cultivation was nothing to scoff at either, firmly at the peak of Immortal Ascension.

The Dragon Emperor frowned, but before he could say anything, Yuan nodded with a smile.

“Why not?” seaʀᴄh thё NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Thank you!” The female dragon’s face immediately brightened.

Yuan followed her to an empty arena shortly after.

“Huh? Look over there! Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy is going to fight Senior Sister Sheng Ran!”

Almost everyone at the training ground shifted their attention to Yuan.

“Sheng Ran? Are you from the main bloodline?” Yuan asked the female dragon after hearing her name.

“That is correct. I am the 4th youngest in the family, Sheng Ran. Let’s have a great match, Senior Envoy!”

“Let’s.”

“…”

Yuan and Sheng Ran stood motionless on the stage. Neither made a move even moments later, almost as if they were waiting for the other to strike first.

“Umm… Senior Envoy, are you not to release the suppression on your cultivation?” Sheng Ran asked.

“Hm? This is my real cultivation, though.”

“What?! You’re only at the peak of Divine Emperor?!” she exclaimed, not daring to believe him.

chapters.title: Chapter 1946 - 1946: Young Dragons(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1946 - 1946: Young Dragons(3)

“There’s no way Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy is only a Divine Emperor! You must be teasing me!” Sheng Ran refused to believe that would be Yuan’s true cultivation.

Yuan smiled and said, “But it’s the truth.”

“No way…”

After a moment of awkward silence, Sheng Ran spoke again, “Okay… I’ll suppress my cultivation to match yours.”

However, Yuan shook his head and said, “There’s no need for you to do that.”

“What? But that wouldn’t be fair.”

“Why not? Because I cannot use Immortal Qi?”

Sheng Ran quietly nodded.

The next moment, Yuan exuded his Immortal Qi, shocking Sheng Ran and the spectators.

“He can use Immortal Qi even though he’s only a mortal?!”

In the Seventh Heaven and above, all cultivators who have yet to breakthrough Immortal Ascension and reach Bronze Immortal are considered mortals.

Sheng Ran could only stand there with a dumbfounded look on her face.

“Are you still going to underestimate me? I promise you that I am Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy for a reason,” said Yuan, snapping Sheng Ran out of her daze.

“I-In that case, let’s spar without any restrictions…” she muttered.

Yuan nodded, “That’s more like it.”

“By the way, I won’t be using any techniques or weapons,” he added.

“So you’ll only be using your physique? I’ll do that as well!”

“Whenever you are ready,” Yuan said calmly.

After a brief moment of stillness, both sprang into action, moving almost at the same instant. Yuan, however, was a split second slower, having deliberately waited for Sheng Ran to make the first move.

Their bodies shrouded in Immortal Qi, Yuan and Sheng Ran collided with explosive force, sending a powerful ripple surging through the arena. Fortunately, a protective formation surrounded the battlefield, containing the shockwave and preventing it from spilling into the surrounding area.

A look of surprise appeared on Sheng Ran’s face when she noticed how powerful Yuan’s physique was. Searᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

‘I have never seen such a sturdy and powerful physique before! Can humans really achieve this level of physique as a mortal?!’ she cried inwardly.

Sheng Ran was a genius in her own right, hence why she was able to use Immortal Qi while only being at the peak of Immortal Ascension. However, when she compared herself to Yuan, she felt like an ordinary mortal who had no talents.

As the battle wore on, the outcome became increasingly clear. Sheng Ran was drenched in sweat, her breaths growing heavy and uneven, exhaustion evident in every movement.

In stark contrast, Yuan stood calm and composed, without so much as a single drop of sweat marring his body.

The Dragon Emperor watched with intense focus, not daring to miss even the slightest detail.

‘His experience and prowess are far above what it appears… This is beyond the realm of geniuses… So he’s a True Reincarnator, huh? That explains everything…’ The Dragon Emperor finally pieced together the truth and understood the situation.

“Y-You win!” Sheng Ran declared her own defeat as she fell to the ground in exhaustion. Although Sheng Ran could wield Immortal Qi, doing so drained her energy and stamina at an alarming rate, especially under the relentless pressure from Yuan. As a result, the match lasted just over an hour before she was completely exhausted, unable to continue.

“It was a good match,” said Yuan.

Before Yuan could even step off the stage, another figure leapt onto it, his eyes gleaming with excitement.

“Senior Envoy, she wasn’t even enough to count as a warm-up, was she?” he said with a grin. “How about a match with me, Sheng Ming?”

Yuan turned to look at the handsome young man standing before him. His cultivation had reached Bronze Immortal, and his body exuded an aura that was much stronger than Sheng Ran’s.

“Why not?” Yuan said with a smile.

Seeing this, Sheng Ran quickly got off the stage so they could begin their fight.

The moment Sheng Ran stepped off the stage, Sheng Ming lunged at Yuan with fierce momentum, his aura surging so violently that the very air around them trembled without end.

Sheng Ming had decided to go all out from the very beginning. Not wanting to disappoint him, Yuan activated his Dragon Transformation before clashing with him.

“He can use Dragon Transformation even though he’s a human?!” Sheng Ran and the other dragons were greatly shocked to see this.

“That must be Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s powers! There’s no doubt that he’s her envoy!”

“I knew you were holding back… but I didn’t expect you to be hiding this much power,” Sheng Ming muttered through gritted teeth.

He had intended to press the offensive, but the overwhelming pressure from Yuan made it impossible. Forced onto the defensive, Sheng Ming could only hope to spot an opening amidst the relentless assault.

However, his hopes never materialized. Within half an hour, Sheng Ming was thoroughly defeated, unable to withstand Yuan’s relentless pressure.

“I haven’t experienced this level of defeat since I graduated from this place…” Sheng Ming sighed out loud as he lay on the ground.

Just like Sheng Ming, several more dragons jumped onto the stage. However, they weren’t as powerful as Sheng Ming. Yet, they still wanted to experience fighting with Yuan.

“I’ll face all three of you at once. Whenever you’re ready.” Yuan beckoned them.

“Thank you for this opportunity, Senior Envoy!”

The young dragons bowed to him before attacking simultaneously from different directions.

Although Yuan didn’t deactivate his Dragon Transformation, he didn’t go all out either, allowing the young dragons to fight a satisfying match.

Time passed, and in the blink of an eye, Yuan spent the rest of the day sparring with the young dragons in the training grounds.

“I am going to train here a little longer. You can leave without me.” Xi Meili said to them at the end of the day.

Yuan nodded and continued ascending the Dragon Spiral Mountain with the Dragon Emperor.

“Sorry that took so long. I only planned on a few matches.”

“There’s nothing to apologize for. I was even expecting it to take a few days. The young dragons enjoyed it. That’s all that matters.”

Yuan looked at him and smiled, “You know, a certain friend told me how the Dragon Emperors have a terrible personality.”

The Dragon Emperor burst out laughing at such unexpected remarks and said, “Well, your friend isn’t wrong.”

“Huh?” Yuan looked at him with raised eyebrows.

chapters.title: Chapter 1947 - 1947: Seven Dragon Emperors
chapters.body:
Chapter 1947 - 1947: Seven Dragon Emperors

“Don’t look at me like that. I’m not talking about myself.”

The Dragon Emperor shook his head with a weak smile on his face.

“Since you’ve only recently arrived in the Seventh Heaven, you probably haven’t heard of any rumors on the Dragon Emperors since they rarely get passed down to the lower heavens.”

“There are only seven Dragon Emperors across the entire Nine Heavens—including myself,” the Dragon Emperor said solemnly. “Yet most of them are steeped in controversy. Take the Blood Dragon Emperor, for example. He is infamous for his uncontrollable bloodlust, going on murderous rampages every few years. Entire cities and bloodlines have been reduced to ruins beneath his wrath.”

“Another example is the Poison Dragon Emperor,” the Dragon Emperor continued. “A master of venom and toxins, he shows no regard for anything around him when he unleashes his power. Every battlefield he touches is left tainted for centuries, poisoning the land, the waters, and all life nearby. Many innocent beings have perished because of his recklessness.”

“Why do they behave like that? Hell, how did they even become Dragon Emperors?” Yuan asked.

“How much do you know about Dragon Emperors? How they are chosen, for example?”

Yuan shook his head and said, “I admit that I don’t know much about Dragon Emperors since they weren’t really a thing during the Immortal Monarch’s Era.” Searᴄh the Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I see. Then allow me to explain this system to you.”

“There are two main requirements to becoming a Dragon Emperor,” the Dragon Emperor explained. “First, you must be born into one of the ten recognized royal bloodlines. Second, even if you possess a royal bloodline, it must reach a certain threshold of purity. Without fulfilling these two requirements, one cannot ascend to the title of Dragon Emperor.”

“There are many self-proclaimed Dragon Emperors out there, but only seven of us are true Dragon Emperors.”

“If someone is born meeting both conditions, they must still undergo the Dragon Emperor Ceremony, where they are tested through a series of rigorous trials,” the Dragon Emperor continued.

“However, that is not the final step. Even after passing the ceremony, they must receive the approval of at least six of the ten recognized royal bloodlines, along with the endorsement of at least two current Dragon Emperors.”

“If there are fewer than four Dragon Emperors in existence, one may ascend without needing their approval. However, there can never be more than seven Dragon Emperors at any given time.”

“If there are already seven Dragon Emperors, anyone who meets all the prior requirements may challenge an existing Dragon Emperor to a deathmatch—fighting for their title.”

Hearing this, Yuan asked, “Have you ever been challenged for your title before?”

“I have only been challenged twice since I became a Dragon Emperor 120,000 years ago,” the Dragon Emperor replied with a faint smile. “After all, a dragon who meets all the qualifications is born only once every few thousand years, and they tend to challenge the weakest Dragon Emperor out there.”

“Do Dragon Emperors face no consequences for their actions? What are the other dragon clans doing?” Yuan then asked.

The Dragon Emperor sighed and said, “Unfortunately, that’s the reality we live in. Dragon Emperors aren’t just powerful individuals—they have the full backing of their clans, along with the support of various other forces. Unless someone possesses overwhelming strength and an unshakable reputation, it’s nearly impossible to challenge or hold a Dragon Emperor accountable.”

“Even if someone qualifies to challenge them, it’s rarely worth the risk, as such actions could easily ignite wars,” the Dragon Emperor added with a heavy tone.

“What about the Dragon Ancestor and the Great One? What are they doing now?” Yuan then asked about the Dragons of Origin.

“The Great One is the very embodiment of aloofness and selfishness,” the Dragon Emperor said. “He has no interest in the affairs of the Dragon Emperors—his only concern lies in expanding his personal collection of treasures. As for the Dragon Ancestor… he hasn’t been seen for many thousands of years.”

“Is that so… I guess I’ll do something about it.”

The Dragon Emperor looked at Yuan with wide eyes, but he didn’t object. After all, Yuan was the Immortal Monarch, and he had Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s backing. If anyone could deal with the Dragon Emperors, it would be Yuan.

Unfortunately, Yuan lacked the capabilities to deal with the Dragon Emperors at this moment, so he had to wait until he did.

Some time later, they arrived at the area situated just above the training grounds, where a single large building stood. It served a singular purpose, used exclusively by the higher-ups of the Holy Dragon Clan for important meetings and discussions.

Since there was nothing of importance inside, they simply walked past the building and continued their ascent toward the mountain’s peak.

Eventually, they arrived at the summit, where an elevated circular platform stood, resembling a grand stage. In front of it stood a stone tablet, distinguished by a uniquely shaped hole carved through its center.

“Is this…?”

The Dragon Emperor nodded and confirmed Yuan’s suspicions, “Yes, this is the teleportation device. Although it’s capable of extremely long-distance teleportation, it’s limited to the other Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

“But alas, the key used to operate it… is currently in the Azure Dragon Clan’s possession.”

“Speaking of the Azure Dragon Clan, could you tell me more about them?” Yuan asked. “I’d like to better understand their situation before I confront them.”

The Dragon Emperor nodded and replied, “Of course. The Azure Dragon Clan once lived peacefully, hidden away in the Sea of Dragons. For countless years, they remained secluded, causing no conflict. However, for reasons unknown, they began making aggressive moves toward our Dragon Spiral Mountain a few thousand years ago, trying to appease the Great One. We tried asking for an explanation, but they refused to reveal anything. It’s likely the Great One has something to do with it, but it’s not like we can confront him. The Great One has a short temper and is easily offended, after all.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1948 - 1948: Frozen Palace
chapters.body:
Chapter 1948 - 1948: Frozen Palace

“The Great One, huh? Do you know his location?” Yuan asked.

The Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “The number of individuals who may know his location can be counted on one hand. He loves traveling the Nine Heavens in search for treasures and can be anywhere. When he’s not traveling, he’s most likely hidden within his own realm or slumbering somewhere in the Starry Sky.” seaʀᴄh thё novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I see.”

“Anyway, going back to the Azure Dragon Clan. They are one of the ten recognized royal bloodlines, but they are ranked last in terms of prowess and influence.”

After a slight pause, he added, “In case you are wondering, the Holy Dragon Clan is ranked sixth.”

“Their bloodline grants them complete mastery over water, so they established their main dwelling deep on the floor of the Sea of Dragons.”

The Dragon Emperor continued to talk about the Azure Dragon Clan.

Sometime later, Yuan said, “I have a good understanding of the Azure Dragon Clan—somewhat. Until I meet and speak with them personally, I can’t say I know them. Anyway, it’ll take some time before they respond to our offer, right?”

“That’s correct,” the Dragon Emperor replied. “The fastest we’ve ever received a response was five weeks, and the longest took nearly seven months. Their timing is unpredictable.”

“I’d like to train in the meantime,” Yuan said. “Does the Dragon Spiral Mountain have any unique training grounds? Specifically, something related to the yin attribute. I’m working on strengthening my Cold Immunity.”

The Dragon Emperor nodded and replied, “Of course. We have special training grounds for those who have graduated from their initial training—what you experienced earlier.”

The Dragon Emperor led Yuan back down the mountain, retracing their steps until they returned to the training grounds.

However, instead of stopping at the familiar arenas, where Xi Meili was still engaged in spirited sparring with the young dragons, they continued deeper, heading toward a more secluded area.

When they reached their destination, Yuan found himself standing before five towering pagodas, each emanating a distinct and ancient aura.

After taking a moment to admire their imposing beauty, Yuan shifted his gaze to the large signs hanging above each entrance.

“Four Levels of Inferno, Frozen Palace, Endless Tribulations, Venomous Altar, and the Unyielding Playground, huh?” Yuan mumbled the names written on each sign with intrigue.

The Dragon Emperor began introducing each pagoda in detail, “Each of these pagodas contains a realm of its own, far more vast than their appearance suggests.

This one is the Four Levels of Inferno. It is designed to temper one’s resistance to fire, with four ascending levels of difficulty. You can challenge a higher floor as your endurance improves.”

He continued, “The first level is intended for beginners with no fire resistance. The second is suited for those with Weak Fire Resistance. The third is for those with Powerful Fire Resistance.

As for the fourth level, it should only be attempted by those who possess Super Fire Resistance. Anything less would be courting death.”

“The Frozen Palace follows the same structure,” the Dragon Emperor continued, “but it’s designed to train one’s resistance to cold, which should suit your needs.”

He gestured to the next pagoda.

“The Endless Tribulation is for tempering one’s body against lightning.”

Then to another, “The Venomous Altar sharpens one’s resistance to poison.”

Finally, he pointed to the last pagoda, “And last but not least, the Unyielding Playground—where gravity is greatly intensified to strengthen one’s raw physique and endurance.”

After learning about the five pagodas, Yuan asked, “So the Frozen Palace only trains one’s cold resistance to Supreme Cold Resistance?”

“Actually, the Frozen Palace has five levels, so you can train your cold resistance past Supreme Cold Resistance. The Unyielding Playground has the most levels since we prefer training our raw physique,” the Dragon Emperor clarified.

Yuan nodded, “Alright, I’ll be training on the fifth level within the Frozen Palace. By the way, do you have a way to contact me when the Azure Dragon Clan responds?”

“Don’t worry, I’ll personally come get you when they respond.”

“I understand. Have fun.”

Without any delay, Yuan stepped into the pagoda dedicated to cold resistance training.

The moment he crossed the threshold, his surroundings shifted. He found himself standing in a vast, snowy expanse, where icy winds howled across an endless frozen plain.

Far in the distance, towering against the pale horizon, stood a massive castle sculpted entirely from shimmering ice—its presence both majestic and foreboding.

Since he already had Supreme Cold Resistance, Yuan didn’t feel any discomfort, but it was clear that the training had yet to truly begin.

Without hesitation, Yuan began making his way toward the ice palace.

Even before reaching the gates, Yuan could see a figure sitting in front of the entrance.

When Yuan got near, this figure opened his eyes and spoke, “Since this is your first time here, allow me to give you a brief introduction.”

“There are five floors in the Frozen Palace. You can increase the difficulty of your training by heading to the next floor. On each floor, you will not only have to endure the cold, but you’ll also have to fight enemies. If they hit you, your body becomes more prone to the cold. If you need to leave, just shout for help and I will come rescue you.”

Yuan nodded before asking, “Are you with the Holy Dragon Clan?”

The middle-aged man shook his head and said, “No, I am from the Glacial Dragon Clan—the true owners of this realm. The Holy Dragon Clan merely borrows this training ground from us.”

“I see…” Yuan murmured thoughtfully. “So the pagodas are actually teleportation devices.”

The Dragon Emperor had purposefully skipped this detail to surprise Yuan.

“What are the rules?” Yuan then asked.

“There is only one rule here,” the middle-aged man said firmly. “You are strictly prohibited from disturbing others who are training on the same floor. Violate this, and you will be immediately expelled from the training ground—and banned from returning for one hundred years.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and muttered, “There will be others?”

chapters.title: Chapter 1949 - 1949: Frozen Palace(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1949 - 1949: Frozen Palace(2)

“Of course, there will be others. This is a public training ground, after all,” said the middle-aged man. “That said, the majority of these individuals are from the dragon clans, so you won’t be seeing many humans.”

“I see. Understandable.”

“Whenever you’re ready to begin your training, simply pass through the gates behind me,” the middle-aged man instructed.

Yuan nodded and, without hesitation, stepped through the gates, arriving at the first floor of the training area.

The moment he crossed over, the temperature plummeted far lower than it had been outside, as if he had stepped into the heart of a frozen world. Of course, since this floor was meant for beginners, it didn’t affect Yuan, who had reached Supreme Cold Resistance.

Without lingering, Yuan made his way straight toward the staircase leading to the second floor.

As he walked, he couldn’t help but notice just how vast the floor truly was—the space seemed endless. Aside from the entrance wall behind him, the castle’s outer walls were nowhere in sight, swallowed by the seemingly infinite expanse of snow and ice.

Moreover, at a glance, there appeared to be several thousand individuals scattered across the vast expanse, each focused intently on their training. As the middle-aged man had mentioned, the overwhelming majority of them were dragons, and those who didn’t belong to the dragon clans were mostly from other beast races.

Suddenly, Yuan sensed a surge of killing intent hurtling toward him.

Turning swiftly to face the threat, he spotted a knight clad in icy armor, its entire form sculpted from frost. The knight charged at him with relentless momentum, its sword raised high, gleaming coldly under the pale light.

Without even bothering to draw his weapon, Yuan casually sidestepped the knight’s sword and swung his fist.

The moment his strike connected with the icy armor, the knight’s entire body shattered into countless glittering fragments, scattering like snow in the air.

After destroying the knight, Yuan noticed his Cold Resistance and spiritual energy increasing slightly.

<Your Cold Resistance has improved slightly>

“So they’re not just an annoyance, huh?”

Eventually, Yuan reached the staircase leading to the next floor—only to find that it was actually a teleportation device designed to transport him directly upward.

Upon arriving on the second floor, he immediately noticed the change. The number of individuals training had thinned considerably, with only a few hundred scattered across the vast frozen landscape. Moreover, the cultivators on the second floor were far stronger than those on the first. While the first floor was filled with individuals still in the Spirit Realm, the ones here had already stepped into the Divine Realm and beyond.

Once again, Yuan immediately made his way to the next teleportation device.

Some time later, Yuan arrived on the third floor, where the number of individuals training had been cut in half.

On the third floor, the ice knights were not only more powerful than on the previous floors, but they also made more frequent appearances. By the time Yuan made it to the teleportation device to the fourth floor, he had to defeat six ice knights. Sёarch* The NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

On the fourth floor, the number of individuals had dwindled to fewer than a hundred. Most of them possessed cultivation levels around Immortal Ascension, and a few among them had already stepped into the Bronze Immortal Realm.

Eventually, Yuan arrived at the teleportation device to the fifth floor, and right beside the device was a warning that read, “Only those who have achieved Supreme Cold Resistance should proceed to the fifth floor.”

After glancing at the warning, Yuan, without hesitation, entered the teleportation device and teleported to the fifth floor.

The moment Yuan stepped onto the fifth floor, a bone-chilling cold that he hadn’t been able to experience in the previous four floors immediately washed over him. For the first time, he didn’t need to fight the ice knights to feel his cold resistance being tempered, and simply standing there was enough to push his body toward growth.

The fifth floor was noticeably less spacious than the previous levels, its confined space concentrating the cold into a much smaller area. As a result, the frigid temperature bore down on everything with a far greater intensity. Moreover, a massive ice structure stood at the very center of the training room, its towering presence radiating an even deeper, more penetrating chill.

As for the number of people training on the fifth floor, there were only four, and three of them were female. Unsurprisingly, the vast majority of people training inside the Frozen Palace were females.

When Yuan arrived on the fifth floor, none of the others spared him so much as a glance, each completely absorbed in their own training.

Yuan wanted to start his training immediately, but he was somewhat disappointed by the training grounds.

‘This place is only slightly better than Sun Ling Cai’s painting,’ he sighed inwardly as he made his way to an empty spot and began his training.

An hour into his training, Yuan had to deal with an incoming ice knight. The ice knight did not have a cultivation base, but its raw strength was comparable to someone at the peak of Immortal Ascension, and it was very skilled with its ice sword.

However, Yuan was still able to completely destroy them with his bare fists.

<Cold Resistance has improved>

After defeating the ice knight, Yuan stood there with a pondering look on his face.

‘What a second… That guard said if I get hit by these ice knights, my body becomes more prone to the cold. If I purposefully let myself get hit and weaken myself, would that increase the speed of my training?’

He had to test out his theory and waited for the next ice knight to appear with anticipation.

An hour later, another ice knight appeared before Yuan.

This time, instead of destroying it instantly, Yuan allowed it to land a strike on him.

<You have been temporarily cursed with Glacial Trace>

<Your Cold Resistance has been weakened>

chapters.title: Chapter 1950 - 1950: Glacial Traces
chapters.body:
Chapter 1950 - 1950: Glacial Traces

After being struck by the ice knight and being cursed with Glacial Trace, Yuan inspected his body.

‘My body definitely feels colder now, but it’s not enough.’

Since a single curse wasn’t enough to see any difference, Yuan decided to let the ice knight strike him a few more times.

<You have been cursed with Glacial Trace>

<You have been cursed with Glacial Trace>

<You have been cursed with Glacial Trace>

Although Yuan took no real damage or injuries from the ice knight’s attacks, a glowing blue chain-like tattoo would appear across his body with each strike, each mark representing a single Glacial Trace.

After accumulating ten Glacial Traces, Yuan finally sensed a change. His cold resistance had weakened to the point where the fifth floor’s environment now felt genuinely threatening. However, in exchange, his cold resistance was strengthening at a noticeably faster rate than before.

Naturally, Yuan wasn’t the first individual to have considered this reckless action. However, he was definitely the first to weaken himself so much.

The middle-aged man standing guard outside the palace had been watching Yuan out of curiosity and was dumbfounded by Yuan’s actions.

“Ten Glacial Trace! His Supreme Cold Resistance is now only as strong as Super Cold Resistance! What a reckless human!”

He’d seen impatient individuals purposefully receiving Glacial Traces to speed up their training before, but most of them only dared to receive one or two Glacial Traces. Even the most reckless individual to train at the Frozen Palace has only accepted seven Glacial Traces. Yet, Yuan went ahead and purposefully received ten Glacial Traces at once.

However, that was only the beginning of the guard’s shock, as Yuan decided to receive even more Glacial Traces, racking up twenty Glacial Traces in total.

“This is more like it.”

A smile tugged at Yuan’s lips as he shattered the ice knight after accumulating twenty Glacial Traces.

By now, his cold resistance had dropped to the level of Powerful Cold Resistance—a full two stages lower than the recommended threshold for surviving on the fifth floor. Yet despite the overwhelming cold, his excitement only grew.

The other four individuals training on the fifth floor finally turned their attention to Yuan, their faces filled with shock and disbelief as they realized what he had done to himself.

Lowering his cold resistance to such a dangerous level in this environment was nothing short of madness—and yet he stood there, unyielding.

“Who is that? I’ve been training here for over a hundred years, and this is my first time seeing him,” one of them questioned out loud.

“He’s a human, too.”

“Hmph. Whoever he is, he won’t last long with that many Glacial Traces on him. I’ll give him a few days at most before he is forced to leave or dies,” one of them sneered.

After showing brief interest in Yuan, the four individuals quickly returned to their own training.

Hours turned into days.

A week had passed since Yuan arrived on the fifth floor and started training his Cold Resistance. The others believed that he would only last a few days, but he persisted for an entire week and didn’t appear to be giving up anytime soon. seaʀᴄh thё novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

A month later, the middle-aged man who usually guarded the entrance unexpectedly appeared on the fifth floor, drawing puzzled looks from those training there. It was the first time any of them had seen him abandon his post.

The middle-aged man approached Yuan after finding him.

“I have a message from the Holy Dragon Clan’s Dragon Emperor,” he said.

“The Azure Dragon Clan has responded. They have agreed to meet you. It will happen in three months on a specific island. You can continue training here for another two and a half months since it’ll take us two weeks to arrive at the meeting place.”

“That’s the end of his message. Do you have anything to say to him?”

Yuan then said, “Tell him that I’ll see him in two months.”

The middle-aged man silently nodded and left the next moment.

“He received a message from the Holy Dragon Clan’s Dragon Emperor! What’s his relationship with the Dragon Emperor?”

“His background must be quite extraordinary.”

The four individuals itched to approach Yuan and strike up a conversation, curiosity burning in their eyes. However, bound by the strict rules of the training ground, they didn’t dare disturb him, knowing that even a simple word could be seen as interfering with his training.

Even when they spoke aloud, it was little more than muttering to themselves, carefully avoiding direct conversation with one another to stay within the rules.

Once the middle-aged man left, Yuan returned to his training.

However, when the ice knight appeared half an hour later, Yuan allowed it to strike him, giving himself several more Glacial Traces.

“Heavens! He’s at 30 traces now! Is he actually trying to kill himself?!”

The others were in disbelief at Yuan’s recklessness.

Unbeknownst to them, Yuan would’ve died countless times already if it weren’t for his regeneration ability and powerful physique.

‘I need to achieve Cold Immunity before I meet the Azure Dragon Clan!’

With that determination, Yuan increased his Glacial Traces until he reached forty.

With forty Glacial Traces suppressing his cold resistance, Yuan was essentially left without any defense against the cold, enduring a temperature intended for those with Supreme Cold Resistance.

“Holy shit! How is he not dead?!”

“His cultivation is only at Divine Emperor, too!”

“Look closely! His body is recovering as it is being frozen and shattered! I cannot imagine the pain he must be experiencing right now!”

“What horrifying regenerative abilities. He must have some kind of god-like physique. Or maybe he consumed a miracle treasure before coming here.”

The four individuals training on the fifth floor were so shocked—and unsettled—by Yuan’s reckless training that they found themselves unable to concentrate on their own.

One by one, they abandoned their training, their full attention drawn irresistibly to Yuan instead.

They all wanted to see his limit.

chapters.title: Chapter 1951 - 1951: Cold Immunity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1951 - 1951: Cold Immunity

An entire month has passed since Yuan accepted forty Glacial Traces on his body. Although his training progress has sped up tremendously, Yuan could tell that he would still not be able to achieve Cold Immunity within what little time he had left.

Thus, he decided to push himself further and accepted another ten Glacial Traces, bringing his total to fifty.

His recklessness was so mindboggling that it indirectly disturbed the others’ training. There was simply no way they could focus on their own training when there was such a madman in their presence.

With only one week remaining, Yuan suddenly began making his way toward the massive ice structure at the center of the floor—the very source of the coldest, most concentrated energy in the entire training ground.

The others could only watch in silence, occasionally swallowing hard as tension gripped them.

Each of them was a Golden Immortal—one even a True Immortal—yet not a single one dared to approach the ice structure. Its oppressive cold was something even they instinctively feared.

And yet, Yuan walked toward it without hesitation.

“He’s actually approaching the Heavenfrost Essence… That thing could instantly freeze an Immortal even if they have Cold Immunity…”

There was a reason why everyone was training far away from the Heavenfrost Essence.

<Cold Resistance has improved tremendously>

<Cold Resistance has improved tremendously>

<Cold Resistance has improved tremendously>

Yuan’s cold resistance was improving at a tremendous rate, but his body was also experiencing unspeakable pain. His skin, blood, and even his bones were constantly freezing and shattering with every passing second.

A few days later—

<Your skill ‘Supreme Cold Resistance’ has developed into ‘Cold Immunity’>

A unique aura enveloped Yuan’s figure the moment he achieved Cold Immunity.

“H-He’s actually achieved Cold Immunity!”

<You have achieved four different types of immunity>

<You have earned the title ‘True Impervious Physique’>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun fusing with Cold Immunity>

<Progress: 1%>

As Yuan had anticipated, his Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique began to merge with his Cold Immunity.

However, unlike his time in the Primal Expanse, the training ground here, despite its intensity, was ultimately too weak to yield any significant progress.

Yuan turned his gaze toward the towering ice structure in front of him, the thought of taking a few bites out of it briefly crossing his mind as a way to accelerate his progress.

But after a moment of consideration, he shook the idea away. This training ground didn’t belong to him, nor even to the Holy Dragon Clan. Claiming something so central to its design would be crossing a line he wasn’t willing to step over.

‘Looks like I will have to visit White Hell to complete its fusion,’ he sighed inwardly.

Since he’d achieved his goals, Yuan decided to end his training there and began walking toward the teleportation device.

“E-Excuse me! Are you done with your training?” One of the four Immortals suddenly called out to Yuan.

If he’d finished his training, it wouldn’t be against the rules to disturb him.

Yuan halted his steps and turned to face the four Immortals.

“Yes, I am done here.”

“May I ask for your name? I’m Bing Xin from the Glacial Dragon Clan!”

Yuan pondered for a moment whether he should use his real identity or a fake one.

In the end, he spoke with a calm smile, “My name is Yuan, and I’d like to thank the Glacial Dragon Clan for sharing this training ground.”

Bing Xin didn’t know what else to say and could only watch as Yuan entered the teleportation device and disappeared from the fifth floor.

Sometime later, Yuan left the ice palace.

Noticing his presence, the middle-aged man could only shake his head and mumble, “You’re a monster.”

“Thank you. By the way, how do I leave this place?”

“There is only one teleportation device. It’ll take you back to the place you came from.”

“Alright.”

Sometime later, Yuan returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

The Holy Dragon Emperor was waiting for him in front of the pagoda when he returned and said, “How was your training?”

“I have achieved one of my goals, but I am still far from reaching my final goal. Anyway, when will we head out to meet the Azure Dragon Clan?”

“We can immediately leave.”

“Let’s do it.”

“Very well.”

The Dragon Emperor suddenly took a deep breath before speaking in a loud voice that rippled throughout the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“We’re leaving immediately!”

A few moments later, a massive flying treasure in the shape of a boat appeared in the sky.

The Holy Dragon Clan had been preparing for their departure the moment they received the Azure Dragon Clan’s response and was only waiting for Yuan’s return.

Without any delay, Yuan and the Dragon Emperor flew onto the flying treasure, where Xi Meili and other Holy Dragon Clan members were waiting. Moreover, there was a small group of elite warriors, looking like they were prepared to go to war.

“Welcome back, Yuan.” Xi Meili approached him with a smile on her face.

Yuan smiled back and said, “Looks like you’ve been working hard these past few months.”

Xi Meili’s cultivation had not only soared to the peak of Divine Grandmaster, but her aura was also much sharper than before.

“The Holy Dragon Clan was generous enough to provide me with a personal instructor, and I have been training pretty much every day since then,” she revealed. “I heard you’ve been training inside the Frozen Palace. I plan on training at the Unyielding Playground once we return.”

“Senior, are we allowed to leave now?” asked the owner of the flying treasure.

Yuan nodded, “Yes, we can leave now.”

Soon, the flying treasure began moving, flying with immense speed.

“We’re headed to the Dragon Assembly Hall. It’s a building built on an unnamed island specifically for meetings between the Holy Dragon Clan and the Azure Dragon Clan,” the Dragon Emperor explained as they traveled.
