chapters.title: Chapter 1952 - 1952: Meeting The Azure Dragon Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1952 - 1952: Meeting The Azure Dragon Clan

“Are they soldiers?” Yuan asked, his gaze settling on the group of elite warriors stationed around the ship, standing like silent sentinels.

“Yes,” the Dragon Emperor replied. “We’re not expecting a battle with the Azure Dragon Clan, but it’s a precaution. They’ll be doing the same on their end.”

“I see.”

Their journey to the Dragon Assembly Hall took just over a week, so they still had a few days to spare before their scheduled meeting with the Azure Dragon Clan.

The Dragon Assembly Hall itself was an imposing dome-shaped structure, constructed at the heart of a barren, isolated island.

Normally, sea beasts roamed these waters in abundance, but Yuan couldn’t sense a single presence near the island. It was as if something on the island itself was repelling them.

“Can I explore the place before the Azure Dragon Clan’s arrival?” Xi Meili asked.

However, the Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “Since this is a formal meeting, we can only enter the island on the scheduled date, even if we’ve arrived early.”

“Oh, okay.”

The Azure Dragon Clan didn’t make an appearance until a day before the scheduled meeting. Unlike the Holy Dragon Clan, who traveled by air, the Azure Dragon Clan moved through the sea depths, emerging gracefully from the sea, as if the ocean itself parted to welcome them.

“So that’s the Azure Dragon Clan, huh?” Xi Meili murmured, her eyes scanning their features with quiet curiosity.

Unlike the Holy Dragon Clan, who bore black hair and golden eyes, the Azure Dragon Clan stood out with their light blue hair, shimmering like the sea under sunlight, paired with the same striking golden eyes that marked their royal heritage.

However, just like the Holy Dragon Clan, they did not set foot on the island and merely camped out right outside it.

Just as the Dragon Emperor predicted, the Azure Dragon Clan also brought their own warriors. In fact, they brought thrice as many soldiers as the Holy Dragon Clan.

The next day, both the Azure Dragon Clan and the Holy Dragon Clan entered the island and made their way to the building.

Neither attempted to speak with the other until they were inside the building.

Once they were seated, the leader of the Azure Dragon Clan, Jiao Zhenhai, spoke, “What’s the meaning of this, Holy Dragon Emperor? Why did you bring a human to such an important meeting?”

“Watch your mouth, Jia Zhenhai. He’s not your typical human.”

Yuan then spoke, “Allow me to introduce myself. My name is Yuan, and I was the one who arranged our meeting today.”

“What? You’re…”

“Yes, I am Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy.”

“Hahaha!” Jiao Zhenhai suddenly burst out laughing.

“Do you really expect me to believe that you, a human, are the Dragon—”

Before Jiao Zhenhai could even finish his sentence, Yuan activated his Dragon Transformation, unleashing a surge of aura that flooded the entire room with overwhelming pressure.

“?!?!”

The soldiers of the Azure Dragon Clan reacted instantly. Sensing the sudden release of power, they instinctively shifted into combat stances, their bodies tensing as if bracing for a battle.

Seeing this, the soldiers of the Holy Dragon Clan also braced themselves.

However, the tense atmosphere quickly calmed down when Yuan remained still.

“So you can use Dragon Transformation. I fail to see how that proves you are Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy,” Jiao Zhenhai sneered in disbelief.

“It proves you know nothing about Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

Someone from the Holy Dragon Clan sneered.

“What?” Jiao Zhenhai frowned at such provocative words.

The Dragon Emperor smiled and elaborated, “Dragon Goddess Yeyou has the ability to grant abilities unique to dragons to other beast races, including humans. The fact that he can use Dragon Transformation is proof that he was blessed by Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

Jiao Zhenhai narrowed his eyes and asked, “When did you become her envoy? As far as I am aware, Dragon Goddess Yeyou has not made any appearances since the Immortal Monarch’s era.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Recently.”

“What?!”

Many from the Azure Dragon Clan showed surprise, even shock, upon hearing this.

“Are you implying that Dragon Goddess Yeyou has returned?!” Jiao Zhenhai asked. Although he tried to hide it, his voice was still trembling slightly.

“That’s exactly what I am trying to say.”

“Bullshit!”

“I have witnessed evidence of her return with my own eyes,” said the Dragon Emperor.

“Me too.” Searᴄh the nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Those who were there to witness Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s appearance in the Dragon Spiral Mountain joined in.

“…”

Jiao Zhenhai remained silent for a good moment before he finally responded, “Since the Holy Dragon Clan vouches for him, I’ll take your word for it. So, Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Envoy, why did you organize this meeting?”

“I’ll get straight to the point. The key you took from the Holy Dragon Clan. I need it.”

“You mean the key that our Azure Dragon Clan won during a bet? I don’t mind lending it to you, but it won’t be free, even if you’re Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Envoy.”

“Of course, I don’t intend to take it for free,” Yuan said calmly. “What do you want in exchange?”

“I want the Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

“…”

The room fell into a suffocating silence.

The members of the Azure Dragon Clan struggled to maintain their composure, faint smirks tugging at the corners of their lips and amusement flickering in their eyes.

Meanwhile, the expressions of the Holy Dragon Clan darkened, their brows furrowed deeply in barely restrained indignation.

“Unfortunately, I do not own the Dragon Spiral Mountain, so I cannot give it to you. However, I can give you something else.”

“And what can you possibly give me that’s worth more than the Dragon Spiral Mountain?” Jiao Zhenhai sneered in disdain.

“The reason you want the Dragon Spiral Mountain is because you want the Great One’s attention, right? Do you need something from him?”

“Even if I do need something from the Great One, it’s not something you can provide.”

Yuan smiled and said, “You’re right. I may not be able to provide it, but Dragon Goddess Yeyou might.”

“What…?”

Jiao Zhenhai’s expression stiffened.

chapters.title: Chapter 1953 - 1953: Azure Dragon Clan'S Predicament
chapters.body:
Chapter 1953 - 1953: Azure Dragon Clan'S Predicament

Yuan continued, his voice steady and firm, “You may not know much about Dragon Goddess Yeyou—but surely, you should be aware that among the Three Dragons of Origin, she stands as the strongest. Whatever the Great One can offer, there’s little—if anything—that Dragon Goddess Yeyou cannot match or surpass.”

Jiao Zhenhai fell silent to ponder Yuan’s words, and the rest of the Azure Dragon Clan began exchanging glances with each other.

Eventually, Jiao Zhenhai spoke, “I am willing to negotiate, but I will only continue speaking if you, Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Envoy, are alone. That means the rest of the Holy Dragon Clan must leave.”

“That’s unreasonable!”

“There’s no way we could leave Senior Envoy alone with you bastards!”

The Dragon Emperor then spoke, his tone calm yet resolute, “I must agree with the others—he holds as much importance to us as Dragon Goddess Yeyou herself. We cannot, in good conscience, allow him to be left in the hands of your Azure Dragon Clan, especially after the aggression you’ve already shown.”

“This matter pertains to information meant solely for the Azure Dragon Clan,” Jiao Zhenhai said firmly. “It is not something to be shared with outsiders, especially the Holy Dragon Clan.”

Holy Dragon Emperor pondered for a brief moment before responding, “In that case, I alone will stay here with him. I may be with the Holy Dragon Clan, but I have my own reputation to uphold as a Dragon Emperor. I can even make an oath if you’re not satisfied.”

Jiao Zhenhai nodded and said, “If it’s just you, and you’re willing to swear an oath you will keep it to yourself, I am willing to let you stay.”

The Dragon Emperor turned to glance at the members of the Holy Dragon Clan behind him. Without a single word exchanged, they quietly took their leave, offering no complaints or objections. With the Dragon Emperor present, the strongest among them, they had no reason to doubt Yuan’s safety.

Once the Holy Dragon Clan left, the Dragon Emperor solemnly swore an oath, binding himself to secrecy until death regarding anything discussed in the conversation that followed.

Only after the oath was spoken did Jiao Zhenhai finally open his mouth to speak.

“I’ll get straight to the point,” Jiao Zhenhai said, his expression turning grave. Sёarᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Our clan’s bloodline is cursed. With each passing generation, its purity diminishes. If this continues, the Azure Dragon Clan will eventually fall beneath the threshold required to be recognized as a royal bloodline. To stop this decline… we need the assistance of the Great One. However, to even get his attention, we need something he’s interested in, and that’s the Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

“How can you be so sure it’s a curse that’s causing your bloodline to decline?” Dragon Emperor asked with a doubtful look on his face.

“Because that’s what the bastard who did it to us said!” Jiao Zhenhai shouted angrily as he slammed the desk.

“That bastard?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“I don’t know who or what he truly is,” Jiao Zhenhai said, his voice low with lingering unease. “He simply appeared before our headquarters one day and demanded our family heirloom. When we refused and attempted to drive him off, he warned us… that he would curse our bloodline. That was many thousands of years ago. Since then, our lineage has steadily declined. At first, the changes were subtle—barely noticeable. But now… It’s growing worse with each generation.”

“I see. So this curse is the source of your problem, and if I get rid of it, you’ll lend me the key?” Yuan said.

“If you can solve our predicament, let alone lend you the key, we’ll give it to you!”

Yuan smiled and said, “That’s a promise. As for your curse, I have a simple solution that doesn’t even require Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s involvement.”

“What?”

In response to the Azure Dragon Clan’s puzzled expressions, Yuan retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification and explained, “If you pour a few drops of your blood into this chalice, it’ll get rid of your curse.”

“Th-That…? What kind of treasure is that?” Jiao Zhenhai asked, swallowing nervously as he felt the profound and unfamiliar aura radiating from the chalice.

Even the Dragon Emperor’s eyes narrowed with intrigue. The aura it emitted was unlike anything he had ever sensed—ancient, otherworldly, and utterly unique. No one present could recall encountering anything quite like it before.

“It belongs to Dragon Goddess Yeyou,” Yuan explained briefly. “Are you worried it might do something to you?”

Jiao Zhenhai hesitated, then shifted his gaze to the Dragon Emperor, seeking silent reassurance, as if needing his approval before proceeding.

“If anything happens to you, I’ll offer you my life.” The Dragon Emperor spoke without an ounce of hesitation in his voice.

Jiao Zhenhai nodded and fed several drops of his blood to the chalice.

Yuan and everyone in the room waited in tense silence, a mix of anticipation and unease hanging heavily in the air.

However, nothing happened even after many moments, causing Yuan to raise an eyebrow.

“What’s wrong?” Jiao Zhenhai could no longer keep silent and questioned.

Yuan inspected the chalice to ensure nothing was wrong with it before looking at Jiao Zhenhai and speaking with a serious face, “You’re not cursed.”

“What?! How is that possible?!” Jiao Zhenhai exclaimed in disbelief. “You must be messing with me!”

“What would I gain from lying to you?” Yuan shook his head. “This is a profound treasure that can remove even the strongest curses out there. The fact that it’s not responding to your blood is simply because you are not cursed!”

“Then how do you explain what’s been happening to my clan’s bloodline?!” Jiao Zhenhai roared, his voice trembling with frustration. “Are you telling me my entire family has been wrong all this time?!”

He refused to believe it was all just a misunderstanding—there had to be more behind the slow collapse of his lineage.

Yuan shrugged and said, “I have no way of knowing that unless I take a closer look for myself. How about I come to your place and investigate it further?”

chapters.title: Chapter 1954 - 1954: Visiting The Azure Dragon Clan'S Territory
chapters.body:
Chapter 1954 - 1954: Visiting The Azure Dragon Clan'S Territory

“Did I hear you correctly? You want to visit the Azure Dragon Clan’s headquarters?” Jiao Zhenhai asked with a deep frown.

“Yes,” Yuan replied with a nod.

“And what exactly do you hope to accomplish?” Jiao Zhenhai pressed. “Our clan has lived there for tens of thousands of years. We’ve searched every corner, and nothing we’ve found has ever explained our situation or hinted at a solution.”

“Who knows? I can’t tell you until I see it for myself. Maybe you missed something. What do you have to lose, anyway?”

Jiao Zhenhai rubbed his eyes and sighed, “Dragon Emperor, will you be joining him? I won’t allow anyone else from the Holy Dragon Clan to join.”

Holy Dragon Emperor nodded and said, “Naturally. Wherever he goes, I will follow.”

“Since this involves the entire clan, I cannot make this decision myself. Let me return to my clan to speak with the others first. I will contact you within two months.”

“Very well.”

The meeting ended shortly after with the Azure Dragon Clan and the Holy Dragon Clan returning to their own homes. Sёarᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“So? What’s happening?”

The Holy Dragon Clan asked the Dragon Emperor as they rode their flying treasure back to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“I cannot go into details since I made an oath, but Senior Envoy and I will be making a trip to the Azure Dragon Clan soon.”

“What?! You’re heading directly to their headquarters?! That’s too reckless even for you, Your Majesty!”

“It’s fine. Even if they try to do something, I will take down at least half of their clan before I perish.”

Yuan shook his head and tried to reassure the others that it would be fine.

“The Azure Dragon Clan is in a unique situation. I doubt they’ll do anything to us.”

“I don’t know what’s happening, but what if they’re tricking you, Senior Envoy? What if it’s an ambush?”

Yuan chuckled and said, “Do you really think we’ll be going there alone? Have you forgotten who else is with me?”

They quickly came to a realization.

“That’s right! You have Dragon Goddess Yeyou!”

Yuan nodded.

“Indeed. With Dragon Goddess Yeyou at our side, not even the Great One could do anything to us,” he said.

Hearing this, the Holy Dragon Clan was no longer worried about their safety. If anything, they hoped the Azure Dragon Clan would try something so they could be erased from existence by Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

Once they returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain, Xi Meili immediately returned to her training. As for Yuan, he also returned to the Frozen Palace to progress his physique, even if it was just a little.

“You’re back? Why?” The middle-aged man was puzzled by his return and said, “Didn’t you already achieve Cold Immunity? It won’t go any further than that even if you train for a thousand years.”

“I know.”

Without offering any explanation, Yuan entered the fifth floor.

“Hm? There’s nobody here?”

Yuan had expected to see the four Immortals who were training there during his last visit. With only two weeks having passed, they should still have been in the midst of their training.

Unbeknownst to him, however, the four had been so shaken by Yuan’s reckless training that they chose to take a break from their own cultivation

Some time later, Yuan seated himself beside the Heavenfrost Essence.

Despite his Cold Immunity, the presence of the ice crystal radiated an intense chill that was still bitterly cold, even for him.

When the ice knight appeared an hour later, Yuan purposefully allowed it to attack him, quickly stacking up Glacial Traces again.

This time, Yuan pushed himself directly to fifty Glacial Traces. However, even after an entire hour, his physique showed no signs of improvement.

Curious, he attempted to push further, stacking additional Glacial Traces onto his body. Yet as he reached sixty, he realized there was a limit to their effectiveness. Beyond fifty, the additional traces had no noticeable impact; it felt no different than before.

An entire week later, Yuan finally noticed some improvements to his physique, but it was incredibly minimal.

<Progress: 2%>

After an entire week of relentless training, Yuan’s progress had increased by only a single percent.

At this rate, it would take him nearly two full years to reach completion—but knowing the nature of his physique, he understood all too well: the closer he came to perfecting it, the slower and harder the progress would become.

Realistically, no matter how much time he spent here, it was unlikely he would ever fully complete his physique in this place alone.

Still, Yuan wanted to make the most of the opportunity. He devoted all his time to training on the fifth floor while awaiting a response from the Azure Dragon Clan.

A month and a half passed before the long-awaited message finally arrived. In that time, he had managed to increase his physique’s progress by only another 3 percent.

“The Dragon Emperor has a message for you.”

Before the man could convey the Dragon Emperor’s message, Yuan walked toward the teleportation device and said, “No need. I’ll hear it from him personally.”

Sometime later, Yuan met up with the Dragon Emperor.

“The Azure Dragon Clan has responded. They agreed to let us visit their territory, and they’ll pick us up from this place in a month.”

“Sounds good.”

With a month remaining before his visit to the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory, Yuan spent his days sparring with the young dragons at the training grounds.

Time passed in the blink of an eye, and a month later, Jiao Zhenhai and a small group of elite guards showed up at the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Yuan and the Dragon Emperor wasted no time and grouped up with them.

“Before we depart, do you need any water-breathing pills?” Jiao Zhenhai asked, aware that humans couldn’t breathe underwater.

“I’ll be fine as long as I cover myself with spiritual energy,” said Yuan.

Jiao Zhenhai raised an eyebrow and said, “What if you run out of spiritual energy? Don’t underestimate the Sea of Dragons. It can be quite rough even for an Immortal—Ah, whatever. Even if you exhaust your spiritual energy, we have some water-breathing pills prepared for you, so you won’t die from drowning.”

“Thank you for your consideration.” Yuan smiled.

chapters.title: Chapter 1955 - 1955: Underwater City
chapters.body:
Chapter 1955 - 1955: Underwater City

After forming an airtight barrier of spiritual energy around himself—functioning like a self-contained air tank—Yuan stepped forward and followed Jiao Zhenhai into the depths of the Sea of Dragons.

The waters of the Sea of Dragons were remarkably clear, allowing Yuan to see his surroundings with ease, even without the aid of any special techniques or artifacts.

Once they had descended deep enough into the sea, one of the soldiers accompanying Jiao Zhenhai suddenly shifted into his true form—a massive, blue-scaled dragon whose presence seemed to ripple through the surrounding waters with raw majesty and power.

“Hop on.”

Jiao Zhenhai said to them as he stood on the dragon’s back, treating it like a mount.

Flying treasures that functioned underwater were rare and not as fast as they were in the sky, but for the Azure Dragon Clan, such tools were unnecessary. Masters of the sea, they traversed the waters with ease, relying on their natural prowess rather than external aid.

“We will arrive at our destination in a month,” said Jiao Zhenhai.

“A month? Your base isn’t as far as I expected,” Yuan remarked.

Jiao Zhenhai laughed at Yuan’s remark and said, “It only takes a month because we’re taking a shortcut. If we travel normally, it’d take several years to reach our place.”

“By shortcut, do you mean the underwater currents?”

“A few of those and a natural teleporter.”

Just as the Primal Expanse possessed natural teleporters, so too did the Nine Heavens, though most of them were hidden deep beneath the sea.

As for the underwater currents, they functioned like one-way tunnels, allowing anything within them to travel at incredible speeds with minimal effort.

A few days later, they reached their first underwater current. The moment the dragon entered it, a powerful force surged around them, and their speed increased severalfold, propelling them through the depths like an arrow loosed from a bow.

However, these water currents are not without drawbacks. One of the drawbacks to these underwater currents was their sheer intensity. Some were so powerful that they could tear a body apart if one wasn’t strong enough to withstand the force.

‘It’s been a while since I entered one of these…’ Yuan thought to himself as he reminisced about the previous times he’d traveled underwater as Tian Yi, the Immortal Monarch.

The Azure Dragon Clan wasn’t the only one to call the ocean depths their home. Countless species and races had carved out their own domains beneath the waves.

While the underwater world wasn’t nearly as densely populated as the lands above, it was far from empty, filled with a wide variety of beings who had adapted to life in the vast, mysterious deep. When he was still living as the Immortal Monarch, he’d even visited cities that were entirely built under water.

After spending several days riding the current, they finally reached its end.

However, it wasn’t long before they came across another one just a few hours later—and without hesitation, they entered and let it carry them onward through the depths once more.

As for Yuan’s barrier, it was under constant assault from the powerful currents. If not for his physique that constantly replenished his spiritual energy and experience, maintaining it would have been impossible for someone at his cultivation level.

Half a month later, they arrived at their first natural teleporter, which existed in a suspicious-looking fissure at the sea floor.

Throughout their journey, Yuan encountered many unique sea beasts, some bearing a striking resemblance to dragons, although lacking the distinct, commanding aura that true dragons possessed.

Yet despite their size or appearance, these creatures would always steer clear once they sensed the group’s presence, instinctively avoiding the path of the Azure Dragon Clan. Hell, Yuan even came across a few Flying Leviathans, but like the rest of the sea beasts, they veered away the moment they sensed the group’s presence.

After passing through the deep fissure, Yuan’s group emerged into an entirely different region of the sea.

The terrain had shifted so drastically that without thousands of years of experience living in these waters, it would be nearly impossible to navigate or know where to go next. Sёarᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

After passing through several more currents and one final natural teleporter, Yuan and his group finally arrived at the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory.

Nestled deep within the tranquil blue of the sea, the territory resembled a small, self-contained city—its structures crafted from a combination of gleaming coral, crystal, and flowing stone that shimmered with life and energy.

Towering spires curved like sea serpents, and wide pavilions were anchored to the seabed by living roots that pulsed faintly with energy.

Glowing flora swayed with the tides, illuminating the surroundings with a gentle bioluminescence. At the same time, aquatic beasts native to the region glided peacefully between structures, coexisting in harmony with the Azure Dragons.

Despite being underwater, the city exuded a quiet, regal grandeur—an ancient calm that reflected the pride and heritage of the Azure Dragon Clan.

Unlike the Holy Dragon Clan, which remained largely self-contained within the Dragon Spiral Mountain, the Azure Dragon Clan operated more like a traditional royal house, complete with its own city, infrastructure, and citizens. They didn’t just command a bloodline; they ruled a domain.

As for the inhabitants of the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory, they weren’t limited to dragons alone. A diverse array of sea races could be seen moving through the city, most of them bearing the human form.

Just like most land-dwelling beasts, sea beasts had adopted human forms for convenience, making interaction, movement, and daily life far easier within a structured society.

As Yuan’s group passed over the city from above, the scene below shifted. Everyone, regardless of race or status, paused what they were doing and looked up. With reverent expressions, they offered respectful bows or gestures, honoring the presence of the Azure Dragon Clan.

“Is this your first time seeing an underwater city?” Jiao Zhenhai suddenly asked Yuan.

He shook his head and responded with a slight smile, “No, but it’s definitely been a while since I’ve visited one.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1956 - 1956: Azure Dragon Clan'S Three Elders
chapters.body:
Chapter 1956 - 1956: Azure Dragon Clan'S Three Elders

After passing through the underwater city, Yuan’s group continued their journey for several more hours, gliding deeper into the heart of the Azure Dragon Clan’s domain.

Eventually, they arrived at their destination—a massive underwater courtyard residence that stretched across several miles of seafloor. Its grand architecture blended seamlessly with the surrounding ocean, featuring sweeping halls made of reinforced coral, shimmering crystal pillars, and roofs shaped like flowing waves. The entire structure radiated an aura of nobility and ancient power, befitting the status of a royal dragon clan.

Yuan’s group landed right at the entrance, where a group of individuals from the Azure Dragon Clan stood in orderly formation, awaiting their arrival.

“The Azure Dragon Clan welcomes the envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou and the Holy Dragon Emperor to our humble domain.”

“Thank you for having us,” Yuan said politely as he leapt down from the dragon’s back, landing lightly on the sea floor.

“Let’s head inside first,” Jiao Zhenhai added, gesturing toward the grand entrance as he began walking ahead.

Once inside, Jiao Zhenhai led them through a series of ornate halls before guiding them into a spacious chamber where three more individuals awaited.

Unlike those outside, these figures radiated a far greater presence—each one exuding a level of cultivation and authority that rivaled the upper echelons of the Holy Dragon Clan’s command. Their gazes were sharp, their auras refined.

“Let me give you a brief introduction. They are the Three Grand Elders. Their position and authority within the Azure Dragon Clan is only below mine and our ancestor.”

“I’m the first elder, Jiao Wenyan.”

“Second elder, Jiao Yansheng.”

“Third elder, Jiao Hanchen.”

“I’m Yuan, envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

“We’ve heard everything from the Clan Leader. You claim we’ve not been cursed, is that correct?” said the Second Elder.

Yuan nodded and said, “If you don’t believe me, you can give it a try as well.”

He retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification and gestured at them.

“Don’t worry, we’ll definitely do so. However, we won’t be the ones doing it. Instead, it’ll be someone who is, without a shadow of doubt, cursed,” said the First Elder as he made a gesture with his hand.

The next moment, a middle-aged man entered the room—his wrists bound in thick chains like a prisoner, and his complexion pale as moonlight, as though drained by time or torment.

The Third Elder explained, “We’ve placed a powerful curse on him right before your arrival, so we know he’s definitely cursed. If you’re able to remove that curse with your treasure, we’ll believe your claims.”

Yuan nodded and said to the man, “Feed this chalice several drops of your blood.”

The man gave a faint nod and followed the instructions with slow, weakened movements.

Once the man offered his blood to the chalice, Jiao Zhenhai and the three elders observed in solemn silence, their expressions tense and unreadable.

Moments later, the two dragons etched into the chalice stirred to life, gliding around its surface in fluid motion, as though awakened by the blood itself. They circled the chalice with growing excitement, their movements swift and vibrant, reacting to something within the offering.

“Now drink this.”

Yuan gathered the liquid from the chalice with his spiritual energy and hovered it above the man, who opened his mouth and drank it without question.

Before long, a visible change came over the sickly man. Color began to return to his pale complexion, and the weakness in his limbs slowly faded. Sёarᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

His breathing steadied, his posture straightened, and a faint glow returned to his eyes.

Jiao Zhenhai and the three elders stared in astonishment, their solemn expressions giving way to surprise and wonder as they witnessed the transformation before them.

After a brief moment of stillness, the Third Elder suddenly sprang into action and began inspecting the man.

“I-It’s real! His curse has been lifted!” he revealed in a trembling voice.

“What?! But we’d placed a Supreme Curse on him! How could it be removed with such ease?!” exclaimed the First Elder, his voice filled with disbelief.

“Satisfied?” Yuan asked them.

“Just a second! We still need to use the treasure on us to confirm that we’re not cursed!” said the Second Elder.

Yuan offered no objection to their request and began testing their blood one by one.

However, by the end of it all, the chalice remained completely still, showing no reaction to any of them.

“How do we know that you’re not controlling the treasure so that it only works with your permission?” the Third Elder suddenly questioned.

Yuan shrugged and said, “That’s impossible to prove, so you’ll just have to believe me.”

“You’re overestimating yourselves a little too much. The envoy has no reason to harm your Azure Dragon Clan,” The Dragon Emperor suddenly spoke in a somewhat disdainful tone.

“T-That’s…”

“I just want the key you took from the Holy Dragon Clan. If I wanted to harm the Azure Dragon Clan, I would’ve taken it by force,” said Yuan.

“And we would’ve helped him.” The Dragon Emperor added with a smile.

“Alright, we get it.” Jiao Zhenhai sighed as he rubbed his eyes.

“We’ll trust you. What are you going to do now?”

“Well, I’ve been thinking about it for the past month, but there’s really nothing I can say or do until I take a look around the place.”

“In that case, we’ll show you around our territory.”

“Sure thing.”

“I have something to do, so I’ll assign some guards to be with you. It’s not that I don’t trust you, but it’ll make traversing our territory much more convenient,” said Jiao Zhenhai.

“I understand.”

“Then please wait here for a moment.”

Jiao Zhenhai and the Three Grand Elders left Yuan and the Dragon Emperor alone inside the room while they gathered a small group of guards.

About half an hour later, two guards arrived at their room, a young man and a young woman, both Golden Immortal cultivators.

“My name is Xuanbing,” said the young man.

“I’m Binglan, and we’ll be your guide,” said the young woman.

“I’m Yuan. Let’s get along.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1957 - 1957: Investigation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1957 - 1957: Investigation

“Where would you like to visit first, envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou?” Xuanbing asked.

“That’s too long. Just call me Yuan.”

“Then, Senior Yuan, where would you like to go?”

Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking, “Let’s take a look around this place first since we’re already here.”

“Very well.”

Rather than searching within the Azure Dragon Clan’s residence, Yuan chose to explore the area surrounding their living quarters.

Xuanbing and Binglan followed quietly a short distance behind Yuan. While their presence served as a protective escort, it was clear they were also there to keep a close eye on him, ensuring he didn’t do anything deemed suspicious.

The area surrounding the Azure Dragon Clan’s residence was vast and open, teeming with vibrant marine life. Colorful corals sprawled across the seafloor alongside smooth stones and swaying seaweeds, creating a natural tapestry rich with motion and life.

‘Does the Azure Dragon Clan use this space for anything?” Yuan asked.

“Not that I am aware of. However, they do keep this place organized, treating it like an outdoor garden,” said Binglan.

“No wonder it looks so beautiful,” Yuan said as he swept the entire area with his divine sense.

However, he was unable to find anything out of the ordinary.

“I don’t see anything here. Let’s check out the city.”

Some time later, Yuan made his way back to the city they had passed through not long ago.

“This place is even more magnificent from up close.”

The underwater city bore a striking resemblance to those on land, complete with bustling businesses, residential buildings, and streets paved with smooth seastone. Even food stalls lined the pathways, their colorful banners swaying gently in the currents, offering all manner of underwater delicacies to passersby.

Yuan approached one of the nearby stalls with quiet intrigue. On land, such stalls typically sold skewers, steamed buns, or sizzling grilled dishes. But here, beneath the sea, every vendor offered only raw seafood and vegetables. It wasn’t surprising, of course. In a place where fire was useless, raw delicacies were the norm.

“How much for that one?” Yuan asked, pointing to a piece of seafood neatly wrapped in what looked like blue seaweed.

“Two ordinary shells,” said the vendor.

Yuan raised his eyebrows at this unfamiliar currency.

“We have our own currencies down here,” said Xuanbing. “Allow me to pay for you.”

Xuanbing produced two beautiful and identical shell-like objects and handed them to the vendor, who immediately accepted them before handing Yuan his food.

As soon as it was in his hand, Yuan took a bite. The texture was smooth and delicately soft, almost melting on his tongue. A strong, briny flavor filled his mouth—a rich, oceanic taste that was clean and refreshing, the kind only found in the freshest, highest-quality fish. The blue seaweed used to wrap the seafood offered a pleasant contrast, slightly tougher in texture, but with a satisfying crunch that complemented the softness of the filling.

“What kind of food is this?” Yuan asked the vendor.

“That is Flying Leviathan meat wrapped with Spiritual Seaweed.”

“What?”

Surprise and bewilderment flashed across Yuan’s face the moment he learned the identity of what he’d just eaten.

Flying Leviathans were known as the king of the seas by mortals, especially back in the Primordial Era. And yet, here they were… reduced to affordable stall food, casually served and consumed as though they were no more special than common fish.

“Is there something wrong with the food?” the vendor asked with a worried look on his face, clearly afraid to offend someone being escorted by two soldiers from the Azure Dragon Clan.

“Oh, there’s nothing wrong. The food was delicious. Thank you.”

Yuan continued to wander through the city, his expression calm and almost leisurely, like that of a curious traveler taking in the sights.

But beneath that relaxed exterior, he remained vigilant. His divine sense was constantly at work, quietly sweeping through the surroundings as he diligently inspected every corner for anything unusual.

Due to the city’s immense scale, it took Yuan nearly two full weeks to explore it in its entirety. He left no alley, district, or passage unchecked—his divine sense meticulously combing through every layer of coral, stone, and structure.

Yet, despite his thorough search, he found nothing out of the ordinary. Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Anything?” Binglan asked him.

Yuan shook his head.

“Not surprising. The Azure Dragon Clan has already searched through the city… several times, at that.”

Yuan then said, “You said the Azure Dragon Clan fought with the individual who cursed your clan, right?”

“Yes, that’s right.”

“Where did the fight take place? Can you take me there?”

“What’s the point? The fight occurred many thousands of years ago. I doubt there are anything left from that event.”

“We won’t know until we see it for ourselves.”

“…”

Xuanbing and Binglan exchanged glances with each other. Eventually, Xuanbing said, “Alright, we’ll take you there.”

The group made their way back toward the Azure Dragon Clan’s residence, but instead of stopping, they continued past it.

After traveling several hundred miles through the serene, light-dappled waters, Xuanbing and Binglan came to a halt before a strange and desolate field.

The area stretched wide and eerie, marked by hundreds of deep, irregular craters etched into the seabed—each one like a scar carved into the ocean floor.

“The battle took place here,” Xuanbing said solemnly. “Though the Azure Dragon Clan fought against a single individual, that person held their ground for an entire month against all of us. And in the end… they still managed to escape with their life.”

“…”

Yuan didn’t respond. He simply stood there, eyes fixed on the cratered landscape, a faint frown settling on his face.

“Do you see something?” the Dragon Emperor asked, noticing Yuan’s expression. His voice was calm but curious—he himself couldn’t sense anything unusual.

“Yes…” he nodded.

“What is it? What do you see?!” Binglan asked, her voice filled with urgency and excitement.

<Perfect Poison Immunity has resisted ‘???’>

“It’s poison. This place is filled with poison,” he revealed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1958 - 1958: Unknown Poison
chapters.body:
Chapter 1958 - 1958: Unknown Poison

“Poison?!” Binglan suddenly burst out, her voice sharp with disbelief. “Are you saying poison is what caused the decline of our Azure Dragon Clan’s bloodline?! That’s outrageous!”

Her eyes flared with fury as she stepped forward. “I’ve tolerated this farce only because it was my father’s wish—but I can no longer endure it! You’re nothing but a fraud!”

“E-Elder sister… please, calm down,” Xuanbing said softly, stepping in with a concerned expression, trying to ease the rising tension.

Yuan looked at her with raised eyebrows.

“Father? You’re the Clan Leader’s daughter?”

“That’s right! My full name is Jiao Binglan, and I am the eldest daughter in the Azure Dragon Clan!”

Xuanbing sighed and spoke next, “I’m Jiao Xuanbing, the second eldest son. Also, this isn’t our real appearance.”

Now that their identities had been exposed, there was no longer any need for concealment. In the next moment, they shed their disguises, revealing their real appearances.

“Senior Envoy, are you sure it’s poison? I don’t mean to doubt you, but I also cannot sense anything,” said Dragon Emperor with a doubtful look on his face.

“I’m certain. Unfortunately, I cannot tell you what kind of poison it is.”

“Why are you so certain?” Jiao Xuanbing asked.

Yuan looked at him and said without any hesitation, “Because I have Perfect Poison Immunity, and it reacted the moment I entered this place.”

“Perfect Poison Immunity?!” Jiao Binglan exclaimed, her voice rising with disbelief. “Isn’t that the highest level of poison resistance—one that only exists in legends?! And you expect us to believe you, a human, have actually acquired such an ability?!”

Yuan gave a light shrug. “Whether you believe me or not is your choice. And for the record, there’s no definitive proof yet that this poison is tied to your clan’s predicament. It’s just a possibility.”

After learning about the poison, Yuan and the others hurried back to inform Jiao Zhenhai and the Three Grand Elders about the discovery.

“Poison?! Are you sure…?”

Unsurprisingly, Jiao Zhenhai and the Three Grand Elders met the news with skepticism. Doubt flickered across their faces, their expressions tightening as they struggled to accept such an improbable explanation.

“I’ve never heard of a poison capable of weakening a royal bloodline,” said the First Elder.

As the Azure Dragon Clan’s foremost poison expert, his skepticism carried weight.

“We’ve examined our bodies and blood thoroughly in the past, but there were no traces of any poison.”

“This might be something entirely new,” Yuan replied. “A poison specifically designed to target your bloodline without leaving behind the usual signs.”

“You can’t be serious! Do you even know what it would take to create something like that? And why would they target our Azure Dragon Clan? We’ve always lived here in peace without offending anyone!” Jiao Binglan exclaimed.

“You said he came here for your family heirloom, right? Do you think I can take a look at it?” Yuan asked.

“That must be your true purpose here!” Jiao Binglan snapped, her eyes blazing with fury. “Are you working for that bastard?!”

Without hesitation, she withdrew her weapon and leveled it at Yuan, her killing intent sharp and immediate.

But before she could take another step, the Dragon Emperor moved. In an instant, he stepped between them, and the room was flooded with his overwhelming pressure, so heavy it made the walls groan and the very water around them tremble.

“Stop!”

Jiao Zhenhai roared.

“Binglan, what are you doing?! Lower your weapon right this instant and apologize to our guest!”

“B-But—” she trembled with hesitation.

“I will not repeat myself!”

Jiao Binglan gritted her teeth in frustration as she lowered her weapon before putting it away.

“I apologize for her unruly behavior.” Jiao Zhenhai said with a weary sigh. “This is a deeply sensitive matter for our clan, and emotions are running high. I ask for your patience and understanding.”

Yuan shook his head lightly. “It’s fine. I understand how you feel. I don’t expect you to place your trust in me so easily, either.”

He paused for a moment before continuing, “How about this—let me return to that area and investigate further. The poison doesn’t affect me, so I’m in no danger.”

“Honestly, we can only enter that area up to a certain point before we’re forced to retreat,” Jiao Zhenhai admitted. “The deeper we go, the more nauseating it becomes, and if we push any further, we eventually lose consciousness, regardless of our cultivation.”

“Why didn’t you tell me that before I wasted two weeks wandering the city?” Yuan sighed. Searᴄh the novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Jiao Zhenhai fell silent. He didn’t have any excuses for this blunder.

Yuan turned to look at the Dragon Emperor and said, “I should do this alone. I don’t want you to become poisoned.”

“There’s a good chance that I am already affected by it.”

“Yes, but I believe it’s unlikely,” Yuan replied. “The poison may be unique and potent, but it seems to act very slowly. If it were truly aggressive, the Azure Dragon Clan’s bloodline would have begun deteriorating much earlier. More likely, it’s a poison that requires prolonged exposure, something that seeps in gradually over time before it begins to take effect.”

“In that case, I’ll listen to your advice and stay here for now.”

“Allow me to accompany you,” said the First Elder, stepping forward. “I possess the most extensive knowledge of poisons among us.”

Yuan considered mentioning that the First Elder likely wouldn’t be of much help, given that even he couldn’t detect the poison.

But he held his tongue and simply gave a quiet nod, choosing not to worsen his relationship with them.

Sometime later, Yuan and the First Elder made their way to the poisoned area.

<Perfect Poison Immunity has resisted ‘???’>

“My poison immunity starts reacting from this point,” Yuan said.

The First Elder didn’t respond. Instead, he calmly retrieved a set of tools from his spatial ring—treasures designed for detecting poison.

He activated them and tested the surrounding water. After a few moments, he shook his head and said grimly, “Nothing.”

Yuan then said, “I’ll be going deeper. I suggest you stay here.”

“No, I’m coming with you.”

“Do as you please.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1959 - 1959: Source Of The Poison
chapters.body:
Chapter 1959 - 1959: Source Of The Poison

As Yuan ventured deeper, the waters around him grew increasingly murky and dim. The clarity faded into a thick, shadowy haze, and a foul stench began to fill the area, seeping through even the protective barrier around him.

It was the pungent, unmistakable smell of decay, as if he were swimming through water steeped in rot and corruption.

“Heavens… It was nothing like this when we visited this place for the last time a hundred years ago…” The First Elder remarked with a disgusted expression on his face, even pinching his nose, trying to fight off the pungent smell.

“The last time you visited this place was a hundred years ago?” Yuan asked to confirm.

“Yes. Since nobody can venture into this place without fainting, we eventually gave up on exploring this place. We even had people from outside the Azure Dragon Clan attempt it, but the results remained the same.”

“Of course, nobody could explain the phenomenon, either. By the way, I am starting to feel my consciousness slipping away, so this is the furthest I can go. Good luck.”

The First Elder halted his movements for a second before turning around and walking back to the Azure Dragon Clan’s residents.

“I’ll see you back at the house,” said Yuan before he continued walking even deeper, his Perfect Poison Immunity blocking the effects of the poison every second.

Soon, the waters became so dirty that it was nearly impossible to see anything with his bare eyes, and even his divine sense was of little help.

Since divine sense no longer worked, Yuan had to use divine gaze, which consumed more spiritual and mental energy, but that wasn’t a problem for him.

After wandering for days, Yuan eventually noticed something and hurried his pace.

A few moments later, Yuan came upon a massive corpse resting on the seafloor—its presence dark and unsettling.

The remains appeared to belong to some sort of serpent-like creature, but its body was so deformed and ravaged that its original identity was nearly impossible to discern. Twisted flesh, torn scales, and warped bones made it a grotesque shadow of whatever it once was. Sёarᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

At the center of the corpse, nestled deep within its mangled remains, Yuan spotted a large black orb—its surface so dark and smooth that it nearly vanished into the surrounding murky water.

It pulsed faintly, as if absorbing the decay around it, and gave off a presence that felt both unnatural and deeply ominous.

It didn’t take even a second before Yuan determined this black orb to be the source of the poison. He carefully picked it up and inspected it.

The poison from it wasn’t as deadly as the poisons he’d encountered in the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp, but it had a really unique property. If he had to compare it to the poison within the swamp, it’d be somewhere in the inner zones.

‘This must be a new type of poison, specifically created to mess with the Azure Dragon Clan’s bloodline. Whoever created this is incredibly skilled.’

Now that Yuan had acquired the source of the poison, he had to deal with it. Fortunately, he had Perfect Poison Immunity and the Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique, which allowed him to absorb and refine the poison.

Although he wanted to bring the poison back to the Azure Dragon Clan to show the Clan Leader and the Three Great Elders as proof, he’d only endanger their lives.

With no other option, Yuan sat down and began to absorb the poison, refining it into his own power.

‘There’s a lot of poison in this thing, likely built up for tens of thousands of years… It’ll take some time before I fully purify it…’

As Yuan began purifying the poisonous orb, a faint glow surrounded his body, spreading out like ripples through the murky water. At the same time, he drew in the lingering poison around him, steadily absorbing and neutralizing its corruptive essence.

Bit by bit, the oppressive darkness faded, and the once-foul water began to clear.

Time flowed quietly, and after several days of tireless effort, Yuan was nearly halfway through purifying the poison.

But just as he continued his work, a sudden surge of killing intent flared from behind him. Instinctively, he halted, his body tensing as he turned to face the threat.

Whoosh!

A sharp, needle-like object tore through the water with blinding speed, aimed straight at Yuan. He reacted instantly, raising his arm to intercept it, but the weapon was shrouded in powerful Immortal Qi, disrupting his defense and slipping past his guard.

It struck his body with a jarring force, but thanks to his powerful physique, it failed to pierce through completely. Had he been any weaker, the needle would have bored into his heart.

Even so, it still wouldn’t have been enough to kill him.

<Perfection Poison Immunity has resisted Whisper of the Abyss>

“What is the meaning of this?” Yuan spoke to the approaching figure.

A few moments later, a familiar-looking old man appeared before him.

“First Elder.”

The one who’d attacked him was none other than the First Elder.

“So it was true that you have Perfect Poison Immunity. What a pity. You could’ve had an easy death if you didn’t.”

Although his poisoned attack had failed to kill Yuan, the First Elder showed no sign of concern. His expression remained calm, almost indifferent.

After all, Yuan was merely at the peak of the Divine Emperor Realm, while he himself stood at the 2nd level of the God Ascension Realm. From his perspective, the outcome was already decided.

“Before you kill me, how about giving me an explanation?” Yuan said calmly, his gaze steady. “Why are you doing this?”

“Why?” the First Elder sneered, his eyes narrowing. “It’s simple. You’re interfering with our great project—something that’s taken thousands of years of planning and a fortune in resources. We won’t let one outsider ruin it all.”

“That doesn’t really explain anything. What’s the plan? Who are you working for?” Yuan continued to question the First Elder, trying to acquire as much information as he could.

chapters.title: Chapter 1960 - 1960: Fighting The First Elder
chapters.body:
Chapter 1960 - 1960: Fighting The First Elder

“Why do you even care about such things when you’re about to die?” The First Elder sneered. “You should be worrying about your own life.”

Yuan shrugged and replied, “Why bother worrying about something that’s out of my control? You’re a God Ascension Realm cultivator, while I am still a mortal. The outcome should be obvious. And if I am going to die, I want to at least know why.”

“It’s simple. The reason you’re going to die is because you stuck your nose into something you shouldn’t have.”

Yuan sighed, “What else am I supposed to do? I need the key you took from the Holy Dragon Clan. By the way, aren’t you afraid of offending Dragon Goddess Yeyou? In case you forgot, I am her envoy.”

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou? Hahaha!” The First Elder began laughing out loud. “Who’d be foolish enough to believe such a lie? There’s no way you’re Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy. She died long ago!”

“Why are you so certain that she died? Did you witness her death with your own eyes?” Yuan asked.

“She hasn’t been seen since the Immortal Monarch Era. That’s more than enough proof! Anyway, enough yapping. I’m going to kill you now.”

“Are you sure you want to do that? The others might become suspicious of you if you return alone.”

“I’m going to tell them that you’d never returned. They can’t prove that I’d killed you if your corpse is in a place where nobody can reach. Even the Holy Dragon Emperor, who came here with you, won’t be able to reach this area.”

“Is that so? What a pity. Unfortunately for you, it won’t be that easy to kill me.”

“We’ll see about that.”

The First Elder raised his hand, palm facing Yuan, and unleashed a wave of Immortal Qi. The pressure surged through the water, seeking to suppress him with overwhelming force.

Yuan, however, remained unfazed. A faint smile curved his lips.

“Immortal Qi?” he said calmly. “What—afraid to use Celestial Qi? Worried you might alert the others?”

“…”

Although the First Elder had reached the second level of the God Ascension Realm, he had yet to fully master Celestial Qi. Because of this, he was reluctant to risk using it, especially when doing so might alert the others to his betrayal.

“Looks like I hit the nail on its head,” Yuan smiled. “You won’t be able to kill me with Immortal Qi alone, even if you’re at the God Ascension Realm.”

Yuan activated Dragon Awakening, causing his aura and strength to surge tremendously.

It was so powerful that it took the First Elder by surprise.

‘How can a mortal human release such pressure? And why does he seem so… confident? Does he really think that he can defeat me with the difference in our cultivation?’

“WHAT A JOKE! I’LL RIP THAT HEAD OFF THOSE SHOULDERS WITH MY BARE HANDS!”

The First Elder flew at Yuan.

Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and began fighting the First Elder.

‘A God Ascension realm cultivator, huh? Need my help?’ Zi Xuan’s voice suddenly resounded inside Yuan’s head.

‘So you’re finally awake. Not yet. I want to test my current limit,’ he said.

‘If you say so…’

Hundreds of exchanges later, a grim realization settled over the First Elder—he couldn’t overpower Yuan through brute strength.

The revelation was nothing short of shocking. He was a God Ascension Realm cultivator, a dragon no less, beings renowned for their overwhelming physical might and dominance in close combat.

And yet, he was being steadily pushed back by a mere human… one who stood several cultivation realms below him.

“Is this the strength of a God Ascension Realm cultivator? What a disappointment.” Yuan mocked.

However, he knew that the First Elder was holding back most of his strength.

“Imprudent brat!”

Suddenly, the First Elder’s appearance began to shift. Blue scales rippled across his skin like living armor. His eyes burned brighter, and his aura surged. He had activated Dragon Awakening, though not in its complete form. Even so, the partial transformation greatly amplified his power, making his already formidable presence even more oppressive.

After activating Dragon Awakening, the First Elder’s power surged, and he began to push Yuan back—but only slightly.

“I guess you don’t want a painless death! In that case, allow me to assist you!”

Realizing brute force alone wouldn’t be enough, the First Elder finally began unleashing his martial techniques, skills he had deliberately withheld until now.

“Current Cleaving Strike!”

The First Elder swung his claws with such immense force that the very water around him split apart.

Trapped by the water’s resistance, Yuan’s movements were heavily restricted, making it impossible to evade the devastating blow.

The First Elder’s claws tore through him with brutal precision, rending his body into three separate parts—blood clouding the water as shockwaves rippled through the depths.

“Hmph.”

Believing he had finally ended the battle, the First Elder let out a breath and deactivated his Dragon Awakening.

But just as the mist of blood began to settle, a gleam of silver light tore through it—a spear, surging forth with deadly precision.

“What?!” the First Elder gasped, caught off guard. He moved to evade, but his guard was down, and the strike was too sudden.

The spear pierced through him before he could fully react.

Though the spear struck him, it hadn’t landed a fatal blow. The First Elder jumped backward through the water, blood trailing from the wound as he clenched his teeth in pain and disbelief.

As the blood mist fully cleared, the First Elder’s eyes widened in disbelief.

Yuan stood there with the Dragon God’s Vengeance in his grasp, completely unharmed.

“H-How are you still alive?!” the First Elder roared, his voice echoing with disbelief and fury. “I know I tore your body to pieces!” Searᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“You sure? Maybe it was just your imagination,” Yuan calmly spoke. “After all, you’re quite old already.”

“Bullshit! I know my technique landed! There was even blood! What the hell are you?!” The First Elder clenched his fists in frustration.

chapters.title: Chapter 1961 - 1961: A Taste Of His Own Medicine
chapters.body:
Chapter 1961 - 1961: A Taste Of His Own Medicine

“Don’t get ahead of yourself and think you’ve already defeated me! Although you managed to land a hit on me, this amount of damage is nothing—”

“No, it’s over for you.” Yuan suddenly interrupted.

“What are you—”

Just as the First Elder opened his mouth to speak, he froze—his expression contorting in agony.

A searing pain suddenly erupted from within, spreading through his body like wildfire. It felt as though his very blood was boiling, his insides being scorched from the inside out.

He staggered, clutching his chest before coughing up a mouthful of dark blood.

“W-What did you do to me?!” he shouted, his voice a mix of panic and disbelief.

“Since you tried to kill me with poison, I’m giving you a taste of your own medicine.”

“Poison?! You poisoned me?! But I also have poison immunity! The Ten Thousand Poison Immunity!” The First Elder was in disbelief.

Yuan chuckled and responded in a mocking tone, “Just the Ten Thousand Poison Immunity? You’d need at least True Poison Immunity to deal with the Heaven’s Bane.”

“Heaven’s Bane?! You mean to tell me that you can use the most potent poison in the Nine Heavens?! Impossible! Not even the Poison Dragon Emperor can use that level of poison!”

“Believe what you want, but you’ll die soon.” Yuan shrugged.

Since his life was in jeopardy, the First Elder no longer cared about being caught and used his Celestial Qi to not only attack Yuan but also to resist the poison in his body.

Although Heaven’s Bane was regarded as the most potent poison in existence, Yuan’s current cultivation was still lacking.

As a result, he couldn’t instantly kill someone as powerful as the First Elder, a God Ascension Realm cultivator whose resilience and vitality far surpassed even immortals.

Even so, the poison was already doing its work—slowly, but relentlessly.

Yuan coughed up a mouthful of blood after being struck by Celestial Qi.

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi has increased slightly>

“H-How in heaven’s name are you still standing?! You’re just a mortal!”

The First Elder was greatly baffled to see Yuan withstanding his Celestial Qi, something even immortals could not resist.

After all, Celestial Qi didn’t just assault the body—it attacked the soul as well.

‘Even if he had a strong body, his soul should be a different story! Unless his soul strength is just as monstrous as his physique, he shouldn’t be alive after being hit by Celestial Qi, much less standing!’

The First Elder coughed up another mouthful of blood when his focus was broken for a second due to shock.

“I told you that killing me wouldn’t be easy…” Yuan stared at the First Elder with a cold grin on his face.

Though he was resisting the First Elder’s Celestial Qi, it wasn’t easy. In fact, he was barely standing.

‘If I don’t kill him quickly, I’ll definitely die here!’ The First Elder cried inwardly. Sёarch* The novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan could see the fear and anxiety in the First Elder’s eyes and provoked him even further, “Hm? Is that fear I sense? A God Ascension Realm cultivator fearing a mortal like me? That’s quite hilarious…”

“SHUT UP!”

The First Elder let out a furious roar as he unleashed his Celestial Qi to its absolute limit, so recklessly that it began to tear into his own body, inflicting internal damage in a desperate attempt to purge the poison.

Yuan was unable to withstand the overwhelming Celestial Qi and finally fell to his knees.

“Haha… That was a foolish thing to do…”

Despite the searing pain coursing through him, Yuan let out a low chuckle, his voice calm and cutting.

“Using your Celestial Qi so recklessly…” he said, eyes fixed on the writhing elder, “…will only make the poison spread faster.”

Sure enough, the poison within the First Elder’s body suddenly surged in response, spreading like wildfire through every vein and organ.

Within moments, his strength crumbled, and he collapsed to the ocean floor—his body trembling, his breath ragged.

But he wasn’t dead. Even with his entire body consumed by poison, a cultivator at the God Ascension Realm didn’t perish so easily.

However, he was essentially paralyzed. In such a condition, he couldn’t even lift a finger, much less use Celestial Qi.

Once the pressure on him was gone, Yuan stood up and slowly approached the First Elder with a grin on his face.

“Looks like the one who’ll be dying today will be you…”

Yuan raised his sword and prepared to decapitate the First Elder.

But just as he swung, a sudden surge of force swept through the water. In the next instant, something struck his blade mid-swing with a sharp clang, deflecting it before it could strike the First Elder.

The force of the clash also sent Yuan skidding backward through the water. Regaining his balance, he quickly turned his gaze toward the source—only for his eyes to narrow in recognition.

A familiar figure stood right in front of the First Elder, clearly trying to protect him.

“So,” Yuan said calmly, raising an eyebrow, “you were in on this too, Third Elder?”

The Third Elder didn’t immediately respond and turned to look at the First Elder, who was staring at him and clearly wanted to speak but couldn’t.

He picked up the First Elder before speaking, “You have no idea who you’re messing with. You’ll regret messing with our plan.”

“I sure don’t. Why don’t you enlighten me so I know who I am dealing with?”

“Hmph.” The Third Elder coldly sneered.

“Don’t worry, you’ll find out eventually when they come for you.”

“Whoever they are, they won’t find out if I kill you two here.” Yuan prepared to use the Immortal Monarch’s techniques.

However, before he could do so, the Third Elder activated some sort of teleportation treasure, teleporting the First Elder and himself away.

“Tch. They ran away, huh?”

Yuan sucked his teeth in annoyance.

“What a pity. If you had let me fight, I could’ve killed both of them in an instant,” said Zi Xuan.

Yuan shook his head.

“That would be overkill. Save your energy for something that’s actually worth it.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1962 - 1962: The Elders' Betrayal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1962 - 1962: The Elders' Betrayal

After the First Elder and the Third Elder left, Yuan returned to purifying the poison.

A week later, the pitch-black taint that once clouded the waters had completely vanished. The sea now shimmered with clarity, indistinguishable from the untouched stretches around it—serene, pure, and free of corruption.

Once he purified all of the poison, Yuan made his way back to the Azure Dragon Clan’s residence while taking the now-purified orb with him.

However, he didn’t even make it halfway back to the Azure Dragon Clan’s living quarters before he encountered Jiao Zhenhai, Holy Dragon Emperor, and the others.

“Senior Envoy! Are you okay?” The Dragon Emperor asked him.

“Yes, I am fine.”

“W-What did you do to this place?” Jiao Zhenhai asked, his expression one of utter disbelief. “It used to be filthy, and we could barely get this far without blacking out…”

Yuan held out the now-purified orb and spoke calmly, “This was the source of the poison. But I’ve already cleansed it. If you go a bit further down, you’ll see a corpse.”

“I see… I’ll have the First Elder analyze it,” said Jiao Zhenhai. “By the way, where’s the First Elder? He came out here with you, correct? We came all the way out here because we sensed his Celestial Qi and thought he was in a fight.”

“…”

Yuan didn’t immediately respond.

“What’s wrong?” Jiao Zhenhai asked.

Yuan looked at the Second Elder, who was still with the Azure Dragon Clan, and said, “You won’t like what I am about to say. Hell, you might even get angry at me for even saying it.”

After taking a deep breath, Yuan continued, “The First Elder betrayed the Azure Dragon Clan. He had a hand in the poison, and he attacked me while I was trying to purify it.”

“WHAT?!”

Everyone in the Azure Dragon Clan exclaimed loudly, their faces filled with disbelief.

“It’s not just the First Elder, either. The Third Elder is also involved.”

Jiao Zhenhai subconsciously took several steps back from absorbing the shock.

“T-There’s no way! You’re making that up!” Jiao Binglan suddenly shouted. “The First Elder and the Third Elder are some of the most loyal members of the family! They wouldn’t do something like betray us!”

Yuan shrugged and said, “Why don’t you listen to the details before you come to a conclusion? Here is what happened…”

Yuan recounted the entire series of events in detail—from the moment he first began purifying the poisonous orb, to the fierce battle that followed with the First Elder, and how the Third Elder had appeared unexpectedly, intervened at the last moment to save his fellow conspirator before vanishing without a trace.

“Hahaha! Do you really expect us to believe that you’d defeated the First Elder, who was at the second level of God Ascension and forced the Third Elder to escape, when you’re only at the peak of Divine Emperor?!” Jiao Binglan laughed out loud, finding his claims humorous and ridiculous.

“If you don’t believe it, I can show it to you? How I defeated the First Elder, that is. However, it’s going to be a very painful experience.” Yuan narrowed his eyes at her. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Jiao Binglan immediately erupted in anger, causing her aura to surge.

“STOP!” Jiao Zhenhai suddenly roared.

“B-But father—”

“If the First Elder and the Third Elder are innocent, they will appear before us to explain everything,” he said before looking at Yuan and continuing, “If what you claim is true, they probably won’t return to the Azure Dragon Clan, right?”

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, they will undoubtedly return. However, it’ll be to destroy the Azure Dragon Clan or finish whatever I intervened.”

Jiao Zhenhai clenched his fists in frustration.

“We don’t know that for sure. For all we know, they could’ve already returned to the residences and are waiting for our return.”

“Then why don’t we return to the residence first?” Yuan suggested.

Jiao Zhenhai nodded, and they began making their way back to the Azure Dragon Clan’s living quarters.

“I’m sorry you have to experience this, Senior Envoy…” The Dragon Emperor apologized to him as they moved. “If only we hadn’t gambled away the key…”

“It’s fine.”

Sometime later, they returned to the Azure Dragon Clan’s living quarters.

“Has anyone seen the First Elder or the Third Elder?! Did they return yet?!”

Jiao Zhenhai questioned every single individual they passed by in the courtyard.

“No, Your Majesty. The First Elder and the Third Elder have not returned yet. We can send them a message requesting their return.”

“Do it immediately! If they don’t respond, let me know!”

The servants hurriedly went to contact the First Elder and the Third Elder through their communication jade slip. However, neither of them responded.

Sometime later—

“Your Majesty, we tried contacting them several times, but neither of them is responding.”

“No way…”

Jiao Zhenhai suddenly staggered backward, his face pale with disbelief. His legs gave out, and he collapsed onto the floor, breath shallow, eyes wide. The reality that two of the clan’s highest elders, pillars of the Azure Dragon Clan, had betrayed them from within was sinking in.

“Father, there must be an explanation!” Jiao Binglan cried out, her voice trembling. “First Grandpa and Third Grandpa would never betray us!”

Tears streamed down her face as she shook her head in denial. They had been there since the day she was born, watching over her, guiding her, even treating her as their grandchild. The thought that they could turn against the Azure Dragon Clan—against her was too painful to accept.

Xuanbing and the Second Elder remained silent, but their pale faces and trembling forms betrayed the storm of emotions churning within them.

“Why…?” Jiao Zhenhai muttered, his voice cracking under the weight of disbelief. “Why would they betray the Azure Dragon Clan? They’ve been supporting the clan for longer than even I have…”

His fists clenched, and a hint of denial lingered in his tone. “It doesn’t make any damn sense…”

Instead of sticking it in their faces, Yuan remained silent and patiently waited for them to accept the situation.
