chapters.title: Chapter 1963 - 1963: Absorbing The Poison
chapters.body:
Chapter 1963 - 1963: Absorbing The Poison

Eventually, the Azure Dragon Clan calmed down.

“We’ll deal with the First Elder and the Third Elder later. For now… since you purified the poison, does that mean our problem has been fixed?” Jiao Zhenhai asked Yuan.

He shook his head.

“Hard to say. Since the Azure Dragon Clan has been exposed to the poison for thousands of years, it might have a permanent effect on your bloodline. However, it’s also possible for your bloodline to improve in future generations.”

“Can’t you just purify the poison in our body?” the Xuanbing asked.

“Like I said, the poison is already deeply rooted in your body. It’d be like trying to remove salt that has already been dissolved in water. It’s not impossible, but it’s going to be very difficult and time-consuming.”

“How long would it take for you to cleanse one person?” Jiao Zhenhai asked.

“I won’t know until I try it,” Yuan said.

“Can you try it? Only the younger generation really needs to be healed since they’re going to be the ones carrying on our bloodline and legacy.”

“I’ll give it a try, but if it takes too long or is too risky, I won’t be able to help you.”

Jiao Zhenhai nodded and turned to look at Xuanbing.

“Xuanbing, are you willing to be first?”

He nodded and said, “Of course. Anything for the family.”

“Now lend us a quiet place with little to no disturbance,” said Yuan.

Jiao Zhenhai brought them to his own room and said, “I’ll forbid anyone from approaching this place so you can do the treatment in complete silence.”

Yuan looked at Xuanbing and asked, “Are you ready?”

“Whenever you are.”

They sat down with Yuan sitting behind Xuanbing.

“I’m going to examine your body first. Do not resist my spiritual energy.”

Yuan placed his palms on Xuanbing’s back and began spreading his spiritual energy around to examine the interior of Xuanbing’s body.

“The poison isn’t affecting your physical strength, and your internal organs remain unharmed,” Yuan said as he carefully examined Xuanbing. “As for the poison itself, it’s deeply embedded within your body, from your blood to your cells, to the point that it’s almost undetectable. I’m going to begin the purifying process now. Let me know if it hurts.”

Without hesitation, Yuan began the process of removing the poison from Xuanbing’s body.

Unlike traditional purification techniques, his method was far more direct. He wasn’t neutralizing the poison but absorbing it into himself.

Xuanbing’s body began to tremble uncontrollably, his muscles tensing as the poison was drawn out. Yet not a single sound escaped his lips. He clenched his jaw and bore it in silence, choosing to endure rather than show weakness.

Seeing this, Yuan decided to continue without saying a word.

Exactly one week later, Yuan paused the process and examined Xuanbing’s condition, comparing his current condition to what it had been when they first began to see how long it’d take to fully heal him.

“At this rate, it will take around four months to remove all of the poison in your body, and the pain will only get worse,” Yuan said to Xuanbing.

“F-Four months…?” Xuanbing swallowed nervously. “I-It’s okay! I can endure it! No! I will endure it!”

“Alright, let me speak with your father before we continue.”

Sometime later, Jiao Zhenhai appeared before them.

“I can heal him in four months.” Yuan went straight to the point.

“That’s great! Then—”

“However, I will be done once I heal him.” Yuan suddenly interrupted. “I have already solved the source of your problem, so I am technically already done. Once I heal him, I’ll need the key. If you cannot accept this… then things might get complicated.” Searᴄh the novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“That’s…”

The excitement on Jiao Zhenhai’s face immediately disappeared when he remembered that Yuan wasn’t helping them for free. Regardless of their situation, none of it was Yuan’s problem. Moreover, it was not as if the Azure Dragon Clan’s lives were in danger.

“Is there no room for negotiation? I have five children, which would only take a little under two years to cure.”

In Jiao Zhenhai’s eyes, two years was nothing.

“Two years may be nothing in your eyes, but don’t forget that I am a human. I also have many things to do. Two years is a long time for me. However, if you make it worthwhile, I guess I can stay here for two years.”

“T-The treasury! I’ll let you take one treasure from our Azure Dragon Clan’s treasury for every child of mine you cure after Xuanbing!” Jiao Zhenhai said.

Yuan narrowed his eyes, but he didn’t immediately respond.

“Let me think about it. We can continue this conversation once I heal your son, since we still don’t know if he can endure it until I completely remove the poison from his body. It’s a painful process, after all.”

“Okay.” Jiao Zhenhai nodded, hope flickering within his gaze.

After speaking with Jiao Zhenhai, Yuan spoke with the Dragon Emperor.

“I’ll be here for at least another four months to heal Xuanbing. After that, I may or may not heal the others, which could take another two years. I’m not going to ask you to stay here with me, so you can return to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

“Are you sure you want me to leave? What if…”

“It’s fine. I doubt the Azure Dragon Clan would do anything to me at this point. Even if they want to do something, Dragon Goddess Yeyou is at my side. Moreover, my friend, Xi Meili, should know about my situation. I don’t want her to worry.”

“I understand. But instead of leaving immediately, I’ll leave in four months, just in case.”

Some time later, Yuan resumed the process of cleansing the poison from Xuanbing’s body.

Time passed swiftly, and in the blink of an eye, four months had gone by.

“I’ve successfully removed ninety-nine percent of the poison from your body. While a trace amount remains, it’s insignificant—far too weak to have any effect on you.”

Yuan said to Xuanbing, who was drenched in sweat.

chapters.title: Chapter 1964 - 1964: Azure Dragon Clan'S Treasury
chapters.body:
Chapter 1964 - 1964: Azure Dragon Clan'S Treasury

“Thank you!” Xuanbing stood up once he regained enough energy and bowed to Yuan.

“No problem.”

They contacted Jiao Zhenhai and the others shortly after.

“So? How are the results?” Jiao Zhenhai asked.

“I have purified ninety-nine percent of the poison in his blood. As for the remaining percent, it’s too little to have any effect on him, so he’s essentially fully healed,” Yuan explained.

“Thank you!” Jiao Zhenhai said with a slightly lowered head. “Now, about the others…”

Yuan then said, “Let me rest for a few days, then I want to take a look at your treasury. If the contents interest me, I’ll accept your offer.”

“In that case, contact me whenever you are ready. I have already prepared a room for you.”

Yuan was led to his room shortly after.

“I’ll head back to the Dragon Spiral Mountain now,” said the Dragon Emperor in front of his room.

Yuan nodded.

“I’ll return as soon as I am done here. Thanks for coming here with me.”

The Dragon Emperor left Yuan alone shortly after, but he didn’t leave the Azure Dragon Clan yet.

“Jiao Zhenhai, I will be returning to the Dragon Spiral Mountain now.”

“Already? Does that mean the envoy won’t—”

“That’s not it. I will be returning alone. Senior Envoy will remain here. In fact, I believe that the Senior Envoy has already decided to help your family, hence why he’s sending me away.”

“Is that so…” Jiao Zhenhai sighed in relief.

“The Senior Envoy’s safety is now your Azure Dragon Clan’s responsibility. If anything happens to Senior Envoy while I am gone, you’ll have to answer the entire Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

Jiao Zhenhai sneered, “Relax, Dragon Emperor. He’s our Azure Dragon Clan’s savior. We’ll return him to the Dragon Spiral Mountain safely once he’s done here.”

The Dragon Emperor nodded and left without saying another word.

A few days later, Yuan emerged from his room and was personally escorted by Jiao Zhenhai to the Azure Dragon Clan’s treasury.

Unlike most treasuries confined to physical space, this one was hidden within a separate dimension—Jiao Zhenhai’s personal world. Upon entering, Yuan found himself standing before a massive, square-shaped structure that resembled an ancient museum, grand and imposing.

The entrance was sealed behind a powerful level 9 formation, a barrier that only Jiao Zhenhai could unlock.

Inside, the building opened into a single vast chamber, and Yuan was greeted by an overwhelming sight. Rows upon rows of treasures, each one neatly displayed in elegant cases and along finely carved wall shelves.

The sheer number of treasures stored within the chamber caused the air itself to pulse with concentrated spiritual energy.

The pressure was immense, so dense and overwhelming that a cultivator at the Spirit Enlightenment Realm would be crushed the moment they stepped inside.

“Take your time looking around, and if you see anything that piques your interest, don’t hesitate to speak up,” said Jiao Zhenhai as he gestured for Yuan to enter.

Yuan wasted no time and immediately began looking at the treasures.

The vast majority of the treasures within the treasury were weapons, armor, and artifacts. Most were of Mythic-grade or higher.

The shelves dedicated to pills held a dazzling array of Tier 8 elixirs, and among them, a handful of exceedingly rare Tier 9 pills.

“Only the best and most powerful treasures make it to our treasury,” said Jiao Zhenhai in a prideful tone.

“I can see that,” Yuan said with a smile.

The more Yuan explored the treasury, the more he was tempted to accept Jiao Zhenhai’s offer.

‘Two years… While it’s not a lot of time for most people, it’s a lot to me…’

With his talents, Yuan could easily achieve what most people take centuries to achieve in two years, not to mention his real body was still stuck on Earth.

After several hours of meticulously examining each treasure, Yuan finally reached the far end of the room. There, set apart from all the others, was a single display—elevated slightly and encased in its own reinforced barrier.

Inside sat a breathtaking blue crystal, glowing faintly with a serene yet powerful light. Despite its relatively small size, no bigger than a drop of water, its luster shimmered like moonlight on still water, and its aura was unlike anything else in the room.

“What is this treasure?” Yuan asked, his gaze lingering on the luminous blue crystal.

Jiao Zhenhai’s expression stiffened slightly before he offered a nervous smile and said, “That… is our family heirloom, the Tear of the Azure Heavens.”

“Tear of the Azure Heavens? Never heard of it. So this is the treasure that the person who poisoned your clan wanted… What does it do?”

Jiao Zhenhai shook his head and said, “I’m being completely honest when I say this, but I have no clue what it does.”

“Huh? So you have an unknown crystal as your family heirloom?” Yuan raised his eyebrows in puzzlement.

“This treasure has been with our family since ancient times. It is said that it used to belong to our ancestor.”

“…”

Yuan stared at the crystal in silence. Its unique aura wasn’t the only reason he took an interest in it.

<Nine Dragon Symbols is reacting to something!>

The Nine Dragon Symbols have been acting up since he entered the treasury, and it was currently having the strongest reaction.

“Is there any chance you’d be willing to give me this treasure for the treatment? I’m willing to treat all of your children for this one treasure,” Yuan said.

“T-That’s… I’m sorry, but that’s impossible.” Searᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking, “What if I give you a few drops of Immortal Monarch’s blood?”

“What?! The Immortal Monarch’s blood?!” Jiao Zhenhai couldn’t believe his ears and almost fell down from shock.

Among the beast race, there existed a widely famous legend, one that had endured for generations.

It was said that the blood of the Immortal Monarch possessed a miraculous property—the ability to elevate the bloodline of any who absorbed it.

For beasts of royal lineage, who valued the purity of their bloodline more than life itself, such a thing was beyond priceless.

Jiao Zhenhai swallowed nervously as he seriously contemplated Yuan’s offer.

chapters.title: Chapter 1965 - 1965: Tear Of The Azure Heavens
chapters.body:
Chapter 1965 - 1965: Tear Of The Azure Heavens

After Yuan offered the Immortal Monarch’s blood for the Tear of the Azure Heavens, Jiao Zhenhai began to seriously contemplate his offer.

The Immortal Monarch’s blood would not only help the Azure Dragon Clan recover their weakened bloodline but might even strengthen it past their original purity.

“H-How many drops of the Immortal Monarch’s blood do you possess?” Jiao Zhenhai asked in a hesitant tone.

Yuan smiled and said, “Enough for you and all of your children.”

“That much?!” Jiao Zhenhai almost couldn’t believe his ears.

However, Jiao Zhenhai didn’t immediately agree and pondered.

‘The Tears of Azure Heavens has been with our family since ancient times, but if I’m being honest, we have no use for it. Hell, we don’t even know what it does. However, if we have the Immortal Monarch’s blood, it would solve our predicament and might even strengthen our bloodline.’

‘But for him to offer something as valuable as the Immortal Monarch’s blood, the Tears of Azure Heavens must be as valuable, if not even more valuable, than the Immortal Monarch’s blood… Damn it! Do I take his offer or not?!’

Eventually, Jiao Zhenhai made up his mind and spoke, “I’m—”

However, Jiao Zhenhai’s voice abruptly stopped, and his eyes widened with shock and disbelief when Yuan suddenly coughed up a mouthful of blood.

Both Jiao Zhenhai and Yuan simultaneously looked down, only to see a gleaming blue trident jutting clean through Yuan’s chest, right where his heart was located.

The next moment, Yuan collapsed to the ground.

“W-What?!” Jiao Zhenhai exclaimed in bewilderment, mostly because he recognized the tridant.

He subconsciously turned to look at the entrance and saw a lone figure standing there.

“Second Elder?! How did you get in here?! No, more important, what do you think you’re doing?!”

“What does it look like I am doing?” the Second Elder calmly responded.

“Don’t tell me… you’ve betrayed the Azure Dragon Clan too?!” Jiao Zhenhai’s voice trembled with disbelief as shock flooded his face.

After the First Elder and the Third Elder’s sudden betrayal, they had tirelessly interrogated the Second Elder for the past four months to ensure that he wasn’t working with them since the three of them were like peas in a pod. And after many hours of questioning, the Second Elder had been cleared of any suspicion.

But alas…

“Why did the three of you betray the Azure Dragon Clan?!” Jiao Zhenhai roared as his cultivation erupted furiously.

The Second Elder shrugged and said, “It’s quite simple. The Azure Dragon Clan is no longer worth our protection and loyalty.”

“There’s no way you’d be stupid enough to betray us over such a reason!”

The Second Elder smiled and said, “You’re right. We found a place that’s worth our loyalty.”

“Who?! Who are you working for?!” Jiao Zhenhai roared as he pounced at the Second Elder.

“Does it even matter? The Azure Dragon Clan will soon cease to exist, anyway.”

The Second Elder calmly raised his hand and deflected Jiao Zhenhai’s furious strike.

The clash sent shockwaves rippling through the treasury, but with both of them being at the fourth level of the God Ascension Realm, they stood locked in place, equally matched.

“I’ll force everything out of you even if it’s the last thing I do!” Jiao Zhenhai roared.

“I’d like to see you try!”

Jiao Zhenhai and the Second Elder clashed fiercely, their battle shaking the very foundation of the dimension. Blades of energy tore through the air, waves of Celestial Qi collided, and spatial distortions cracked around them like glass.

Yet, despite the overwhelming power of their techniques, not a single treasure in the room was disturbed. Every weapon, pill, and artifact remained untouched.

After nearly a thousand intense exchanges, the tide of battle began to shift, and the Second Elder’s cultivation suddenly increased to the fifth level.

The Second Elder, though evenly matched at first, immediately started to gain the upper hand.

“Hahaha! How do you like this?! I, who have always been slightly behind you, am now stronger! This is the result of betraying the Azure Dragon Clan!”

“You despicable bastard!”

Eventually, the Second Elder overpowered Jiao Zhenhai and sent him flying across the treasury.

“Once I kill you, acquire the Tear of the Azure Heavens, and accomplish our goal, we’ll receive even more benefit from the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan!”

Jiao Zhenhai trembled after hearing the name ‘Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’. seaʀᴄh thё nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“You were colluding with the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan this entire time?! You low-life bastards!” Jiao Zhenhai roared, his voice thunderous with fury.

The Second Elder covered his mouth with a hand and spoke in a teasing tone, “Oops. I wasn’t supposed to reveal that. Well, it doesn’t really matter since you’re going to die so—”

In the midst of his sentence, the Second Elder suddenly stiffened, a sharp jolt of pain lancing through his back, like a blade piercing straight through him.

His eyes widened in disbelief as he slowly turned around… only to see Yuan standing mere inches behind him, a dagger firmly gripped in his hand.

“You—! How are you still alive?!” the Second Elder gasped, staggering slightly as a burning pain rippled throughout his body.

However, what stunned him even more than the fact that Yuan was still alive after taking a spear through the heart was how he had managed to slip behind him completely unnoticed.

“Just like the First Elder, you seem to like attacking people from the back, so I’m letting you experience it for yourself. How is it? Do you enjoy it?”

Snapping out of his daze, the Second Elder roared loudly and lashed out with a wild, powerful swing of his arm.

Splat!

Yuan’s head was torn clean from his shoulders before it hurtled across the room, where it struck the wall with a sickening splatter of blood.

“That son of a bitch… I don’t know how he survived being struck in the heart, but he’s definitely dead now. No mortal can survive without their head!”

However, as the Second Elder turned away from the blood-smeared wall and back toward Yuan, his eyes widened in shock and his complexion paled instantly, almost as if he’d seen a ghost.

“Sup,” Yuan said with a smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1966 - 1966: A Glimpse Of Zi Xuan'S Prowess
chapters.body:
Chapter 1966 - 1966: A Glimpse Of Zi Xuan'S Prowess

As if struck by a sudden nightmare, the Second Elder recoiled in horror. With a sharp gasp, he leapt backward instinctively.

“W-What the hell are you?!” the Second Elder exclaimed, his voice riddled with fear. “How are you still alive?!”

Before Yuan could even respond, the Second Elder spoke again, “It can’t be! That regenerative ability! You’re a demon!”

“Hahaha!”

Yuan laughed at the Second Elder’s assumptions.

“Are those your last words?”

The Second Elder clenched his fists and roared, “So what if you’re a demon?! You’re still a mere Divine Emperor! I can crush you with a finger!”

The Second Elder raised a trembling finger at Yuan, gathering Celestial Qi with murderous intent blazing in his eyes.

But the moment the Celestial Qi began to surge through him, a searing pain erupted from deep within his body, far more intense than before.

His expression twisted in agony as the burning sensation multiplied, coursing through his veins like wildfire, threatening to tear him apart from the inside.

“This is… poison?! You poisoned me?!” The Second Elder immediately retrieved a handful of pills and swallowed them simultaneously after realizing Yuan had poisoned him.

“Those pills won’t save you,” Yuan said coldly. “Heaven’s Bane is one of the most lethal poisons in the universe. Unless you have specifically prepared for it, you won’t get rid of it.”

Moreover, Yuan had struck the Second Elder with Baneful Abyss, his Soul Weapon that not only amplified the potency of Heaven’s Bane but also accelerated its effects to a terrifying degree.

Despite having a much higher cultivation than the First Elder, it took less time for the Second Elder to succumb to the poison. Sёarch* The Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The Second Elder fell to his knees a moment later. However, despite his seemingly hopeless situation, he smiled and said, “So you managed to kill my physical body, but as long as my soul is intact, I am not dead!”

“I know,” Yuan said. “I also know that I don’t have the power to destroy your soul. However, I know someone who can.”

“Hahaha!” the Second Elder laughed, even as pain twisted his features. “If you’re talking about the Clan Leader, not even he could destroy my soul! Don’t underestimate a God Ascension Realm cultivator!”

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly, his tone calm and dismissive.

“God Ascension?” he sneered. “I’ve killed more of them than I can count.”

Yuan immediately put away Baneful Abyss and retrieved Number One Under Heaven.

“Zi Xuan, I’ll leave him to you.”

“What happened to using my energy on something worth it? He’s hardly worth my energy,” Zi Xuan said.

“I know, but unfortunately, we cannot let him live.”

Suddenly, Number One Under Heaven began to tremble, its entire form radiating an intense golden light. The brilliance grew blinding, until the light itself began to shift, condensing, reshaping.

Moments later, standing where the weapon once hovered was the beautiful figure of an adult woman. Her presence was regal and imposing, her voluptuous form wrapped in flowing black and golden robes that shimmered like starlight.

“His sword transformed into a human…?” Jiao Zhenhai watched the scene before him with a dumbfounded expression.

Zi Xuan examined her body for a moment before turning to look at Yuan with a dissatisfied face and said, “I am able to take this form since you’ve improved your cultivation to Divine Emperor, but it’s not good enough. I can barely last a few minutes right now, and that won’t be anywhere near enough to satisfy me. I will need to be able to remain in this form for at least a month to have a proper ‘session’ with you.”

“A whole month? Please spare me…” Yuan said with a stiff smile.

“I refuse. This is non-negotiable, especially since you left me in that depressing place for so long.”

The Second Elder stood frozen, his eyes locked on Zi Xuan as she casually chatted with Yuan, but not out of infatuation. It wasn’t her devastating beauty that left him dazed, but the overwhelming, unfathomable aura radiating from her presence.

“Anyway, let me take care of this trash first.”

Zi Xuan slowly turned her gaze toward the Second Elder.

The moment her eyes met his, his body began to tremble uncontrollably.

The sheer weight of her presence, conveyed through nothing more than a casual glance, was enough to crush his will.

In that single moment, all hope drained from the Second Elder’s eyes. He knew he wasn’t facing a cultivator but something far beyond his comprehension.

“How dare a lowly rat lay its filthy paws on my beloved?”

Zi Xuan’s voice rang out.

She took a single step toward the Second Elder, graceful yet absolute.

The moment her foot touched the ground, a ripple of overwhelming force erupted outward, infused with Supreme Sword Qi.

The entire dimension quaked in silent awe, as if bowing to the sheer majesty of Zi Xuan’s presence.

All around them, the display cases that had remained undamaged even through the cataclysmic battle between Jiao Zhenhai and the Second Elder began to crack.

As Zi Xuan took her second step, every display case within the treasury dissolved into countless fragments impossible to see with the naked eye.

The Second Elder’s body met the same fate. One moment he was there, and he was gone the next. No blood, no remains. Not even a speck of dust lingered. He had been erased, utterly and completely.

Yet, the Second Elder was still alive, as his soul still existed.

However, that wouldn’t last long, lasting only until Zi Xuan took her third step.

Realizing that he was a goner, the Second Elder spoke to Jiao Zhenhai through voice transmission before he was erased from existence, “Even if you kill me, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan is already on their way here. It’ll only be a matter of time before the Azure Dragon Clan falls to them!’

After the Second Elder was reduced to nothingness, Zi Xuan turned on her heel and began walking toward Yuan. Her steps, though silent, carried a presence that made the very air part before her.

When she stood before him, her body glowing, she leaned in and gave him a passionate kiss with her soft lips.

“I’m going to take a nap. Work hard to improve your cultivation so we can have more fun later,” she said to him before returning to her sword form and going silent.

chapters.title: Chapter 1967 - 1967: Consuming The Tear Of The Azure Heavens
chapters.body:
Chapter 1967 - 1967: Consuming The Tear Of The Azure Heavens

After putting away Number One Under Heaven, Yuan turned to look at Jiao Zhenhai, who was sitting on the floor with a dazed look on his face, and said, “I apologize for the destruction of this place.”

Jiao Zhenhai looked around with a dumbfounded look on his face before responding, “I-It’s fine. As long as the treasures themselves aren’t damaged.”

“Thank you. Now then, about what you were trying to tell me about our offer…”

Jiao Zhenhai slowly stood up before speaking in a solemn tone, “I am willing to give you the Tear of the Azure Heavens for the Immortal Monarch’s blood, but I want one more thing from you.”

“What is it?”

“Help me defend my Azure Dragon Clan,” said Jiao Zhenhai with a serious frown on his face.

“Defend the Azure Dragon Clan? What about you talking about?” Yuan raised an eyebrow, unaware of what the Second Elder said to Jiao Zhenhai through voice transmission.

Jiao Zhenhai immediately explained the situation.

“The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, whom the elders have been working with, are coming here as we speak. The Second Elder said this to me right before his death through voice transmission. Without the Three Elders, our chances of defeating the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan are slim to none. However, with you—and your sword’s assistance—we might be able to win.”

Yuan didn’t immediately respond. Zi Xuan had expended most of her energy just now and would probably take several weeks, if not months, to fully recover. If the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan showed up before she recovered, it might get a bit dangerous.

Of course, he still had Dragon Goddess Yeyou, but he wasn’t sure how much she could actually do in her current state.

After contemplating for some time, Yuan nodded and said, “Alright, I’ll help the Azure Dragon Clan. However, I will need the Tear of the Azure Heavens now. My sword is exhausted after killing the Second Elder and needs to recover.”

Jiao Zhenhai nodded, “Okay.”

Some time later, Jiao Zhenhai handed the Tear of the Azure Heavens to Yuan.

“I’m going to use this right now. You should alert the rest of your family.”

“I know this isn’t the proper time to ask this, but… what does it do?” Jiao Zhenhai couldn’t contain his curiosity.

However, Yuan shrugged and responded in a genuine voice, “I don’t know.”

“What? Then why do you want it?”

“My instincts,” he said.

“…”

Jiao Zhenhai fell silent.

After a moment, he asked, “Are you going to do it here?”

“Preferably. Since nobody can disturb me here. Don’t worry, I won’t steal your treasures, if that’s what you’re worried about.”

“I trust you.”

Without saying anything else, Jiao Zhenhai left the treasury and summoned everyone from the Azure Dragon Clan.

Meanwhile, Yuan sat on the floor with the Tear of the Azure Heavens in his grasp.

<Your Cold Resistance has improved significantly> Sёarᴄh the ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<Progress: 7%>

Yuan’s cold resistance has been increasing since he touched the Tear of the Azure Heavens, which was a pleasant surprise.

“Do any of you guys know what this is?” Yuan asked the others.

“No.” Lan Yingying quickly responded.

“I have also never seen or heard of this treasure before,” said Yu Ning.

“That is the heart of the Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“This is the heart of a phoenix?” Yuan repeated, raising an eyebrow in surprise.

“Yes,” Feng Yuxiang confirmed. “The Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix is a rare and powerful species whose flames don’t burn, but freeze everything they touch. Their hearts contain immense yin energy and are considered priceless among yin-attribute cultivators.”

“Honestly, I’m surprised one managed to be preserved for this long,” Feng Yuxiang remarked. “The Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix has been extinct for ages, after all.”

“So, what kind of effect does it have?” Yuan asked.

“It’s quite extraordinary,” Feng Yuxiang explained. “For cultivators, it grants the ability to cultivate and wield yin-attributed energy, even if they had no prior affinity for it. As for beasts, consuming it can transform their bloodline into one aligned with yin. And if they already possess a yin-attributed bloodline, it will elevate their lineage to an entirely new level.”

“No wonder the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan is trying to destroy the Azure Dragon Clan just so they can get their hands on this treasure,” Yuan mumbled.

“Anyway, I am going to consume this now.”

Without a moment of hesitation, Yuan brought the heart of the Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix to his lips and took a bite. Though it had the hardness of crystals, his teeth sank into it effortlessly, as if biting into a piece of delicate cake.

The moment it touched his tongue, the crystallized heart melted away like cotton candy, releasing an intense wave of chilling energy that spread through his body.

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix’s Heart has been refined by Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique>

Suddenly, Yuan’s entire body erupted in a blaze of azure-colored flames, radiating an intense cold so profound it felt as though he were being burned and frozen simultaneously.

The surrounding air trembled, and in the blink of an eye, the entire treasury was coated in a thick layer of glistening ice. Shelves, walls, even the very ground beneath him crystallized under the overwhelming yin energy now pouring from his body.

Yuan himself also turned into an ice statue.

As for his Cold Resistance, it improved at a frightening rate.

<Progress: 23%>

<Progress: 66%>

<Progress: 81%>

In just a few days, his progress skyrocketed to 99 percent.

Meanwhile, the Azure Dragon Clan began mobilizing for war against the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

With a population exceeding 100,000, including those residing in the city, their forces were vast. While not every member was suited for combat, the overwhelming majority had already reached at least the Divine Realm.

The city, once bustling like any ordinary settlement, transformed into a fortress overnight.

Barricades rose where markets once stood, defensive formations were activated across the seafloor, and patrols of armored warriors now roamed the area around the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory.

chapters.title: Chapter 1968 - 1968: Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique
chapters.body:
Chapter 1968 - 1968: Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique

“Where’s Senior Envoy?” Jiao Xuanbing asked Jiao Zhenhai a month after they began preparing for war.

“He’s in seclusion.”

“Are you sure he’ll even be of any help? He’s only at the peak of Divine Emperor…” Jiao Binglan said, expressing her doubts. “And for the record, I still don’t believe he managed to defeat the First Elder.”

Jiao Zhenhai looked at his foolish daughter and said, “I didn’t mention this, but it was he who killed the Second Elder. If it weren’t for him, I wouldn’t be standing here right now.”

“What?! He killed the Second Elder, who is the strongest amongst the Three Elders?!”

The others from the Azure Dragon Clan expressed shock and bewilderment after learning of this.

“Binglan, you need to start respecting him, or you’ll come to regret it later,” Jiao Xuanbing said to her. “Your hate is unreasonable in the first place. He didn’t do anything to offend you, and he’s helped our clan tremendously. If it weren’t for him, the Three Elders would’ve continued to stab us in the back.”

“That’s…” Jiao Binglan fell silent.

Suddenly, someone rushed into the room and shouted, “We’ve spotted the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan! They’ll arrive in our territory in two weeks!”

“How many people do they have?” Jiao Zhenhai questioned.

“Around fifty thousand!”

“They only brought a fourth of their forces? These bastards are underestimating us a little too much!” Jiao Zhenhai gritted his teeth in frustration.

Yet, he didn’t dare to underestimate their opponents. The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan was a royal bloodline just like them, after all.

At the same time, Yuan had just finished absorbing the Tear of the Azure Heavens.

The moment Yuan completed his Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique, the bottleneck that kept him at the peak of Divine Emperor shattered, and his cultivation began soaring to the next level.

In the blink of an eye, Yuan’s cultivation surged to the peak of the Divine Ancestor Realm, just a single step away from Immortal Ascension.

However, entering the Immortal Ascension Realm wasn’t as simple as accumulating enough spiritual energy. A cultivator needed exceptional Soul Strength and, more importantly, profound insight into the nature of cultivation itself. This was the barrier that trapped countless experts at the Divine Ancestor level.

Of course, Yuan was no ordinary cultivator. His Soul Strength already rivaled, if not surpassed, that of Immortals, and with the vast experience and insight he’d accumulated from his past lives, he only needed to attempt entering it… No, he could enter Immortal Ascension even in his sleep.

After taking a deep breath, Yuan began his attempt to break through to Immortal Ascension, the first step on his path toward true immortality.

Moments later, a surge of Immortal Qi erupted from Yuan’s body, flooding the air with boundless pressure.

<You have successfully taken your first step towards true immortality, entering First Level Immortal Ascension>

To ascend to the next level, one must continually refine their Soul Strength and deepen their insights into the Dao. However, for someone like Yuan, it was merely a question of whether he chose to ascend.

Mere seconds after stepping into the first level of Immortal Ascension, another surge of Immortal Qi burst forth from Yuan’s body as his cultivation soared into the second level.

<You have successfully entered Second Level Immortal Ascension>

From that moment on, Yuan advanced through each level as if it were mere child’s play. In the blink of an eye, he reached the ninth level of Immortal Ascension—just one step away from becoming an Immortal. Yet that final step was unimaginably vast, a chasm where countless cultivators had faltered and fallen.

Of course, if Yuan wanted to take that step, he could easily do so. However, when he attempted to do so, he was met by a familiar barrier.

‘So my physique still has room to grow, huh?’ Yuan mused. ‘Come to think of it, my Fire and Cold Immunities are only at the first stage, while my Poison and Lightning Immunities have already reached their peak.’

“Congratulations on reaching the peak of Immortal Ascension, Young Master.” Feng Yuxiang cheered loudly.

“You’re now only a step away from becoming a Bronze Immortal,” said Lan Yingying.

“I have never seen or heard anyone going from the first level to the peak in one sitting…” Yu Ning muttered in a dazed voice.

“It’s not just my cultivation that benefited from the Tear of the Azure Heavens. I also acquired new abilities.”

Yuan flipped his palms and summoned a blue flame.

“That’s the Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix’s unique flames, the Heaven-Freezing Flame!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed.

“This is just one of the few abilities I acquired. I’ll show you the rest later,” Yuan said with a smile.

“Anyway, how long have I been cultivating?” he then asked.

“A little over a month.”

“Since nobody disturbed me, I guess the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan has yet to arrive. Alright, let’s go check out the situation.”

Yuan left the treasury shortly after.

The moment Yuan exited the treasury, Jiao Zhenhai sensed it and immediately stopped everything he was doing to meet with Yuan.

“Senior Envoy! Are you done with your seclusion?” Jiao Zhenhai asked him.

Yuan nodded and said, “Yes.”

He released some of his cultivation.

“Ninth Level Immortal Ascension?! Did you really reach that level from the peak of Divine Emperor in just a month?!” Jiao Zhenhai cried out loud, not daring to believe his eyes.

“It’s mostly thanks to the Tear of the Azure Heavens.”

“W-What? Tear of the Azure Heavens…? What is he talking about, Father?” Jiao Binglan inquired with a dazed look on her face.

Jiao Zhenhai, who kept their deal a secret this entire time, finally explained the full situation to everyone there.

chapters.title: Chapter 1969 - 1969: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion
chapters.body:
Chapter 1969 - 1969: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion

“WHAT?! YOU GAVE OUR FAMILY HEIRLOOM TO HIM?!” Binglan exclaimed with a horrified expression after hearing Jiao Zhenhai’s explanation.

It wasn’t just Jiao Binglan. Jiao Xuanbing and the others were also just as shocked. None of them could’ve expected Jiao Zhenhai would be willing to part with their family heirloom.

“I understand your feelings, but what use is our family heirloom if our clan is destroyed in the process of trying to protect it? Moreover, we will receive the Immortal Monarch’s blood! This is a blessing none of you can even begin to fathom!” Jiao Zhenhai said.

“The Immortal Monarch’s blood?” Binglan and the others turned to look at Yuan with suspicious gazes.

Yuan smiled back and said, “Thank you for letting me consume the Tear of the Azure Heavens. Here is the Immortal Monarch’s blood, as promised.”

Before coming out of the treasury, Yuan collected several drops of his blood and stored them in small glass vials.

He retrieved a wooden box and handed it over to Jiao Zhenhai, who accepted it with trembling hands.

Jiao Zhenhai wasted no time opening the box the moment it was in his grasp, and sure enough, there were six vials resting inside, just enough for him and his children.

“The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan will arrive in two weeks. We need to absorb this to strengthen ourselves before then.” Jiao Zhenhai handed a drop of the Immortal Monarch’s blood to each of his children.

“T-Thank you, Senior Envoy!” Jiao Xuanbing bowed to him.

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, you don’t need to thank me. I didn’t give it to you for free. It was an exchange.”

Jiao Zhenhai and the others wasted no time and went to absorb the Immortal Monarch’s blood.

Meanwhile, Yuan asked around about the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

“Who are the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan?” Yuan asked the first person he came across.

“They’ve been long-time rivals of the Azure Dragon Clan since ancient times, as both possess the power to command the waters. However, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan has been adapting more ice-attribute techniques lately.”

“The Azure Dragon Clan and the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan have clashed many times before, but never with stakes this high. Until now, their conflicts were little more than skirmishes.”

“I see… What about the Clan Leader’s wife? What about the rest of his family?” Yuan then asked about the Azure Dragon Clan’s situation.

“The Mistress died trying to defeat the intruder who tried to take away our family heirloom many thousands of years ago. As for the others… while they’re not dead, they all left the Azure Dragon Clan long ago to look for a cure for the curse. However, the Clan Leader should’ve already notified them of the situation, so they should be heading back here as we speak.”

“I see…” Yuan mumbled.

Hearing that the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan has been learning ice-attribute techniques made it very clear why they wanted the Tear of the Azure Heavens—the Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix’s heart.

Unfortunately for them, Yuan had already absorbed the treasure for himself, so they were waging a pointless war against the Azure Dragon Clan.

This made Yuan wonder if the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan would back out once they learn about it.

A week later, Jiao Zhenhai and the others emerged from seclusion, having fully absorbed the Immortal Monarch’s blood.

The first thing they did was test their bloodlines, and as expected, not only had their bloodline’s purity been restored, it had even surpassed its former peak, and everyone except Xuanbing still had poison in their body. As for Xuanbing, who wasn’t affected by the poison, his bloodline had become the purest and most powerful in the clan’s history, by a wide margin.

Seeing Xuanbing’s results gave immense hope and joy to the others, who were eager to have their poison cleansed by Yuan.

“Senior Envoy! Please take care of us after we deal with the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan!” A young man approached Yuan and bowed to him.

Yuan silently nodded.

“To be honest, I was skeptical about the Immortal Monarch’s blood being real until I absorbed it,” Jiao Zhenhai admitted with a stiff smile on his face. “But now, I am really glad I accepted your offer.”

“Pleasure doing business with you,” Yuan smiled.

The following day, something else happened that greatly boosted the Azure Dragon Clan’s spirit and morale—reinforcement had arrived.

“Father! Grandfather and Grandmother have returned!” Jiao Binglan exclaimed as she hurried to his side. Sёarᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Truly?!” Jiao Zhenhai gasped. Before he could take a single step, two figures appeared before him—a middle-aged man and woman, both bearing the unmistakable traits of the Azure Dragon Clan.

“Father! Mother! You made it back in time!” Jiao Zhenhai hurried to greet them.

Although their reinforcement was only two individuals, they were both powerhouses at the God Ascension realm, and Jiao Zhenhai’s parents.

“We rushed back immediately after receiving your emergency contact. What is the situation right now?” asked the middle-aged man.

“The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan will arrive in less than a week,” said Jiao Zhenhai. “Allow me to keep you up to date for now.”

Over the next several hours, Jiao Zhenhai recounted everything to his parents—from their fateful encounter with Yuan to how he had resolved their crisis. He made no attempt to conceal the fact that he had traded away their family heirloom in exchange for Yuan’s help and the Immortal Monarch’s blood.

By the time he finished, both of his parents were trembling—not from rage at the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, nor from the loss of the heirloom, but for an entirely different reason.

“You little brat! How dare you only ask for enough Immortal Monarch’s blood for your children and yourself?! What about us, your own parents?! Have you already forgotten about us in such a short time?!” His father stood up and roared angrily.

His mother expressed similar complaints.

In other words, they were angry about being left out of a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

chapters.title: Chapter 1970 - 1970: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1970 - 1970: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(2)

When Jiao Zhenhai felt his parents’ pressure bearing down on him, he quickly said, “W-Wait! He was the one who initiated the offer! Six drops of the Immortal Monarch’s blood are already a lot! If I asked for more, he would’ve likely refused!”

His parents sat back down and withdrew their pressure after hearing this.

“Do you think he’ll be willing to give two more drops of the Immortal Monarch’s blood—if he even has them, that is.”

“Why are you asking me? You should ask him instead,” Jiao Zhenhai shrugged.

His parents immediately stood back up and began beating him.

Sometime later, Jiao Zhenhai brought his parents to Yuan.

“Senior Envoy, do you have some time? My parents would like to speak with you.”

“Your parents?” Yuan raised an eyebrow as his gaze met the two individuals standing behind Jiao Zhenhai.

“My name is Jiao Longfei, his father.” Jiao Longfei said as he grabbed Jiao Zhenhai’s clothes and yanked him far away.

“I’m Jiao Xuanji, his mother. If you don’t mind, we’d like to have a brief conversation with you.”

Yuan had already guessed their intentions and greeted them with a calm smile. “You’re here to ask about the Immortal Monarch’s blood, aren’t you?”

The couple was stunned by his directness, caught off guard, and left momentarily speechless.

“W-While that is part of the conversation, we mainly wanted to thank you for helping our family…” Jiao Longfei responded in a stiff tone.

Yuan then said, “Unfortunately, my transaction with the Clan Leader has already ended, and I am only helping the Azure Dragon Clan because you have something that I need.”

“That’s…”

Just as Jiao Xuanji opened her mouth, Yuan interrupted, “However, that doesn’t mean I cannot make a new transaction with you two. If you want the Immortal Monarch’s blood, I still have some left.”

“Really?! What do you want for them?!” Jiao Xuanji exclaimed, her voice full of excitement.

“One—No, we’ll give you two treasures from our treasury for each drop!” Jiao Longfei said as he raised two fingers before him.

Yuan responded by showing three fingers.

“Make it three and you’ll have a deal, so six treasures in total.”

“D-Deal!” Although Jiao Xuanji hesitated for a split second, he quickly made up his mind and agreed to Yuan’s offer.

“Thank you. Here you go.”

Yuan immediately retrieved two vials and handed each of them one. S~eaʀᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Since the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan will be invading soon, I’ll give these to you ahead of time.”

“You won’t regret it!”

The couple immediately left to absorb the blood.

Three days later, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan arrived earlier than expected, before Jiao Zhenhai’s parents could finish absorbing the blood.

However, since they have already started absorbing the blood, they couldn’t be disturbed, so the Azure Dragon Clan had to deal with the invasion without them for now.

Meanwhile, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan came to a halt just outside the Azure Dragon Clan’s underwater city, their 50,000-strong army standing tall, exuding unwavering confidence.

Amidst the sea of soldiers, a lone figure with a familiar face stepped forward and spoke in a voice so powerful it echoed through the city, reaching even the depths of the Azure Dragon Clan’s living quarters.

“Azure Dragon Clan, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan will grant you twenty-four hours to hand over the Tear of the Azure Heavens! If you do not meet our demand within the time limit, don’t blame us for whatever happens afterward!”

“T-That’s the Third Elder! So the rumors calling him a traitor were true!”

The people of the Azure Dragon Clan were greatly shocked to see the Third Elder standing with the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

Jiao Zhenhai appeared before them and sneered coldly, “So you’ll turn around and leave if we give you the Tear of the Azure Heavens?”

The Third Elder laughed out loud before responding, “Of course not. If you hand over the treasure, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan will spare your lives. However, you’ll have to come under our clan’s name as servants!”

“The Azure Dragon Clan as servants? In your damn dreams!”

Suddenly, Yuan appeared beside Jiao Zhenhai and scanned the place with a composed expression.

“Where’s the First Elder? Did he still end up dying even after you saved him?” Yuan asked calmly.

In the very next instant, a surge of overwhelming bloodlust swept through the area as a new figure suddenly appeared.

Yuan shifted his attention to this newcomer—a handsome young man he didn’t recognize.

The handsome young man then roared, “I only lost that time because I had underestimated you and didn’t use my full strength to avoid being exposed! It will not happen again! This time, I swear I will shred you to pieces!”

“Oh? You managed to get a new body in such short time? I’m impressed,” Yuan said with a faint smile.

Sure enough, the striking young man before him was none other than the First Elder, who had transferred his soul into this new vessel after Yuan’s poison had destroyed his original body.

However, having transferred his soul to a new body in haste, the First Elder suffered a severe backlash, and his cultivation plummeted by two full levels, diminishing him to a mere True Immortal.

“Whether the Azure Dragon Clan hands over the treasure or not, you’re dead meat, human!”

Jiao Zhenhai then stepped forward and asked, “Where are the rest of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan? I highly doubt they’d entrust such a massive army to you and let you come here alone.”

The Third Elder smiled and said, “Wouldn’t you like to know? Unfortunately, I cannot tell you.”

Jiao Zhenhai and the others frowned, feeling uneasy about the whole situation.

‘What if there are more of them than the 50,000 in front of us right now?’ They all considered this possibility.

After a moment of silence, Jiao Zhenhai spoke, “Is that so? Well, I have some unfortunate news for you, too. The Tear of the Azure Heavens you bastards so desperately want is no longer in our hands. We gave it away.”

“YOU WHAT?!” The First Elder and the Third Elder shouted simultaneously, their eyes growing as wide as saucers.

chapters.title: Chapter 1971 - 1971: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1971 - 1971: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(3)

“Y-You’re lying! There’s no way the Azure Dragon Clan gave away its family heirloom!” The Third Elder pointed his trembling hand at them and shouted, his voice filled with doubt and disbelief.

“Who do you think you’re fooling, Jiao Zhenhai?! We know you better than anyone here! You wouldn’t dare to part with that treasure even at your deathbed!” The First Elder laughed out loud, not believing it for a second.

Jiao Zhenhai sneered and said, “You don’t have to believe me, but you’ll end up fighting a pointless war at this rate.”

“Pointless? Do you really think the Tear of the Azure Heavens is the only reason we’re here?” The Third Elder shook his head.

“The Azure Dragon Clan and the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan have been at odds with each other since ancient times. Don’t you think it’s finally time to end it once and for all?” The First Elder said.

“Don’t you both possess royal bloodlines? Wouldn’t it cause serious complications if the two of you were to clash?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“What would you know, human? Just because we have royal bloodlines doesn’t mean we’re allies. If anything, it only fuels our rivalry!” the First Elder retorted coldly.

‘What should we do?’ Jiao Zhenhai suddenly spoke to Yuan using voice transmission.

‘It’s obvious they won’t back down even though we no longer have the Tear of the Azure Heavens. Should we just wait out the 24-hour limit and see what happens? This also gives my parents time to absorb the Immortal Monarch’s blood.’

Yuan responded, ‘While waiting may seem like the right thing to do, I have a feeling there are more of them than it appears. It’s also suspicious that nobody from the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan is present. They might be planning an ambush separately. If that were the case, we should deal with those present before they can arrive.’

Jiao Zhenhai fell silent, reflecting on Yuan’s words. It was definitely suspicious that the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan had only committed a fraction of their forces to what was supposed to be all-out war, and not a single true member of their clan was present. The more he thought about it, the stronger his unease grew.

‘Since war is unavoidable, we need to make the first move and cripple their current forces before their reinforcements arrive!’

Having made his decision, Jiao Zhenhai summoned his weapon—a majestic blue and gold trident—leveled it at the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s army, and roared with thunderous fury, “KILL THEM ALL!”

“What?!”

The First Elder and Third Elder were stunned by Jiao Zhenhai’s sudden declaration, their expressions frozen in disbelief. Behind them, the army stirred with visible confusion and hesitation. None had expected the Azure Dragon Clan to launch the first strike.

In the very next moment, nearly 80,000 experts surged out from the city like a tidal wave, swiftly encircling the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

“The negotiation has ended! Kill everyone!” The Third Elder roared.

And so, the Azure Dragon Clan and the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan finally clashed.

The battlefield roared with chaos as countless Divine Realm cultivators exchanged blows, shaking the depths of the sea. Among them, the Azure Dragon Clan fielded nearly 2,000 Immortals, outnumbering the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, who had barely half that number at their command.

Jiao Zhenhai lunged toward the Third Elder with unwavering resolve, while the First Elder, eyes burning with fury, pursued Yuan. Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“If you couldn’t defeat me while you’re at the God Ascension realm, what makes you think you’ll be able to defeat me as a True Immortal?!” Yuan remarked as he deflected the First Elder’s first strike.

“Don’t you dare underestimate me! My cultivation may have lowered, but I can still use Celestial Qi!” The First Elder roared as he unleashed his Celestial Qi.

Although it wasn’t as strong as before, it was still enough to pressure Yuan, who’d increased his cultivation to the peak of Immortal Ascension.

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi has increased slightly>

With Number One Under Heaven in his grasp, Yuan fought the First Elder while constantly being suppressed by Celestial Qi. Despite the vast disparity in their cultivation, Yuan wasn’t being pushed back.

‘Wait a second! His cultivation is at the peak of Immortal Ascension! But it was only at the peak of Divine Emperor when I last saw him a few months ago!’ The First Elder realized this amidst their fight, which shook him to the core.

‘No! It’s impossible that his cultivation increased that much in such a short time! This must’ve been his real cultivation since the very beginning!’

Even if Yuan had told him the truth, the First Elder still wouldn’t believe it.

“Hey, I’ve been wondering… Aren’t you curious about the Second Elder’s situation?”

“What?” The First Elder frowned.

“The two of you left him behind, after all.”

“I have no idea what you’re talking about. The Second Elder has nothing to do with the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan!”

Yuan chuckled at the First Elder’s attempt to mislead him.

“There’s no point trying to play dumb. Didn’t you find it weird that the Second Elder isn’t present at such an important event? He’s already exposed himself. As a matter of fact, he’s already dead.”

“What?!” The First Elder suddenly stopped fighting and backed away.

“Impossible! The Second Elder’s strength rivals even Jiao Zhenhai!”

“It’s true. Do you want to know who killed him?”

Yuan pointed at himself and revealed with a cold grin on his face, “The Second Elder died to my sword.”

“Hahaha! You killed the Second Elder?! That’s your biggest bullshit yet!”

Yuan shook his head and elaborated, “No, I didn’t say that I killed him. I said my sword killed him.”

“What’s the damn difference?!”

“I’d love to show you, but unfortunately, my sword is still asleep. Instead, why don’t I show you something else?”

Yuan stowed away Number One Under Heaven and faced the First Elder without any weapons.

“What trick do you have up your sleeves now?”

The First Elder immediately increased his guard, as he had already experienced Yuan’s trickery several times.

chapters.title: Chapter 1972 - 1972: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1972 - 1972: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(4)

“A trick, huh? Well, I guess it may seem like that to you.”

After putting away his weapon, Yuan slowly approached the First Elder.

Seeing this, the First Elder hastily distanced himself from Yuan.

A profound light flickered within Yuan’s eyes, and he spoke in a low but commanding tone, “Halt.”

“?!?!”

The First Elder was greatly shocked when his body suddenly stopped listening to his commands and halted all movements.

“Get on your knees.”

As if Yuan’s words held dominion over his very soul, the First Elder dropped to his knees and knelt before him.

“W-What is going on?! What did you—”

“Shut up. You will only speak when given permission.”

“??????”

The First Elder tried to speak, but not a single word escaped. His lips felt as heavy as a mountain, and even the act of opening his mouth was beyond him.

He frantically darted his gaze across the battlefield, desperate to see if anyone was witnessing this humiliation. But in the midst of the blood-soaked chaos, where every cultivator fought for their life, not a single soul noticed the silent, one-sided submission playing out between Yuan and the First Elder.

‘What the hell is going on?! Why isn’t my body listening to me?! Did he poison me again?! But when did he do it and how?!’ The First Elder believed he was poisoned again, but he’d never experienced or heard of such a powerful poison.

“What’s on your mind? Let me guess… Are you thinking that I’d poisoned you again?” Yuan suddenly spoke.

The First Elder’s eyes widened with surprise at Yuan’s words.

“So I’m right, huh? Well, fortunately for you, I didn’t poison you.”

The surprise on the First Elder’s face turned to puzzlement after hearing this. If he wasn’t poisoned, what could be the cause of this phenomenon?

“Since you’re going to die soon, I’ll let you in on a secret.”

Yuan moved his lips near the First Elder’s ears but spoke through voice transmission just in case, “I was lying when I said that I was Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy. In reality, I am the Immortal Monarch.” Sёarᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“?!?!?!”

The First Elder’s eyes nearly popped out of their sockets from shock.

‘The Immortal Monarch?! What kind of nonsense is he talking about?!’

However, as much as he wanted to deny it, it would explain why he was obeying Yuan’s every command.

“Now… die for me.”

Yuan wasted no time and commanded the First Elder to die.

The First Elder coughed up a mouthful of blood as he subconsciously destroyed his own heart.

However, although the First Elder’s physical body died, his soul was still alive, and while his cultivation had dropped to the True Immortal realm, his soul was still at the God Ascension level.

Yuan knew he couldn’t kill the First Elder so easily—nor did he care to try. Instead, he calmly retrieved Dragon God’s Vengeance and, with a flick of his sleeves, forcefully cast the First Elder’s soul into the weapon, leaving his fate to Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

The moment the soul was sealed within, Dragon God’s Vengeance trembled violently, as though roaring in fury, echoing the wrath of the goddess within.

‘I’ll probably have an earful later…’ Yuan swallowed nervously.

The trembling stopped a few moments later, and since the First Elder’s soul hadn’t been ejected from the weapon, it was clear what Dragon Goddess Yeyou did to him.

This made Yuan wonder if feeding the souls of powerhouses to Dragon Goddess Yeyou would help speed up her recovery.

‘Let’s think about this later.’

Yuan shifted his gaze to the rest of the battlefield, where thousands of mutilated corpses lay scattered amidst bloodstained waters, most of them belonging to the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

He thought about using the Immortal Monarch’s powers to assist the Azure Dragon Clan, but it’d require a tremendous amount of energy to command so many powerful beings, and there was still the possibility of enemy reinforcements.

In the end, Yuan decided to just use his own strength for the time being.

With the Dragon God’s Vengeance in his grasp, Yuan began attacking every single enemy fighter in sight, targeting mostly those within the Divine Realm since they were the easiest targets and were the most abundant.

After being targeted by Yuan, the poor Divine Realm experts stood no chance as all of them were killed within a single strike.

<Dragon God’s Vengeance has increased in strength after consuming the lifeforce of a dragon>

As the number of Yuan’s victims increased, so did the strength of his weapon through its second unique ability.

Not only that, but he was slowly progressing his unique quest that required 100,000,000 kills. Unfortunately, he was still very far away from completing it.

After slaughtering thousands of Divine Realm experts from the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s side, Yuan’s presence could no longer be ignored, and he had finally drawn the gaze of the Immortal Realm cultivators.

However, the Immortals were far too prideful to lower themselves by engaging a mere Immortal Ascension realm human, one who had yet to even set foot into true immortality.

Thus, those who were also in the Immortal Ascension realm began attacking Yuan.

“Why don’t you pick on someone that’s on the same level as you instead of picking on the weak?!”

A peak Immortal Ascension shouted as he pounced at Yuan.

Yuan gave the Immortal Ascension expert a glance before using Shadow Veil, instantly disappearing from his opponent’s view.

“What?! Where did he—”

Before the man could even finish his sentence, Yuan had already arrived behind him like a shadow to land a critical strike.

“Impossible…”

The man muttered his last words as Yuan brutally yanked the Dragon God’s Vengeance from his corpse and watched as it slowly sank to the ocean floor.

“If you want to stop me, you’d better come at me with everything you’ve got!” Yuan laughed out loud as he continued slaughtering the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s army.

“S-Somebody stop him!!!”

“Ahhh!”

The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s army quickly descended into chaos, their formation crumbling under the weight of fear and confusion.
