chapters.title: Chapter 1973 - 1973: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(5)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1973 - 1973: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(5)

Noticing how fast their army was falling apart, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s Immortals could no longer sit still and went after Yuan.

“About time I have some worthy opponents!” Yuan laughed as he confronted a Bronze Immortal, and their Immortal Qi clashed.

‘What?! I’m being pushed back?!’

The Bronze Immortal was utterly stunned that he was being forced back by someone still in the Immortal Ascension Realm, a human cultivator who hadn’t even truly stepped into immortality.

“How is his Immortal Qi so powerful?! He’s not even an Immortal yet!”

The other Immortals were equally shaken by the sheer intensity of Yuan’s Immortal Qi, and the True Immortals there wore grim expressions, unable to hide their disbelief. Yuan’s Immortal Qi rivaled their own, despite still being in Immortal Ascension, while they stood at the very peak of the Immortal Realm, on the verge of stepping into God Ascension.

In the cultivation world, one only attains true immortality after transcending the Immortal Ascension Realm and stepping into the ranks of Bronze Immortals. Searᴄh the nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Though the Immortal Realm consists of only four distinct stages—Bronze, Silver, Golden, and True Immortal—the gap between each is vast, often requiring as much time and cultivation as ascending through all nine levels of Immortal Ascension. Yet Yuan, a mere cultivator still within Immortal Ascension, had already surpassed even the True Immortals.

What would happen if Yuan were to become a Bronze Immortal, let alone a True Immortal? How terrifying would his power become then? No one present could begin to fathom it—not even the God Ascension cultivators, who already stood beyond immortality.

‘We must kill that human now or he’ll become a huge threat to the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan and our plans!’

The Immortals quickly exchanged voice transmissions in secret, and after a brief but heavy silence, reached a unanimous decision—to strike at Yuan together, even if it meant staining their pride and shattering their reputation.

Suddenly, the battlefield shifted dramatically. Without warning, hundreds of Immortals, even those already locked in fierce combat, abandoned their fights and turned their focus toward a single target: Yuan.

“Senior Envoy! Watch out! They’re all targeting you!”

Members of the Azure Dragon Clan hurriedly shouted warnings to Yuan as they pursued the forces of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan going after him.

“Oh?”

Despite the looming threat, Yuan showed not the slightest trace of panic. In fact, a faint smile tugged at his lips.

“To think so many mighty Immortals would band together against a mere mortal like me… I’m truly honored.”

“To show my gratitude,” Yuan said calmly, his smile widening, “allow me to face you all with my full strength.”

“What?!” The Bronze Immortal fighting Yuan was shocked to hear he wasn’t even fighting at his full potential.

As the Immortals closed in, a profound, chaotic, and overwhelming aura erupted from Yuan’s body like a volcanic explosion, shaking the battlefield and halting even the boldest in their tracks.

“What is that ominous energy?! It’s making my soul tremble!”

The Immortals cried out in alarm, their faces twisted with confusion and unease as they encountered Chaos Energy for the first time—its presence utterly foreign, yet instinctively terrifying.

Although Yuan’s main cultivation had already surpassed that of his Chaotic Heart, he intended to use it solely to enhance his own physique. And with the unparalleled destructive force of Chaos Energy, his combat prowess rose to an unfathomable degree while his cultivation remained the same.

“Why did you all stop?! If you won’t come to me, then I’ll come to you!” Yuan laughed, his voice echoing with battlelust as he lunged toward the Bronze Immortal before him, his speed and power now magnified many times over.

Overwhelmed by Yuan’s chaotic aura, the Bronze Immortal instinctively took a step back. But the moment he turned to retreat, Yuan’s eyes blazed with radiant gold.

“Where do you think you’re going?!” Yuan roared, unleashing Dragon’s Gaze at full power, his voice thundering like the heavens themselves.

The Bronze Immortal instantly froze in place, his soul gripped by an overwhelming pressure that crashed down like a divine hammer.

In the very next moment, without Yuan lifting a finger, the Bronze Immortal coughed up a mouthful of blood and collapsed—his soul shattered under the weight of Dragon’s Gaze.

After all the power he had gained within the Primal Expanse, especially with his dragon powers, Yuan’s Dragon’s Gaze had evolved to a terrifying degree. It was potent enough to shatter souls outright, especially when unleashed at full intensity.

“D-Did he just use Dragon’s Gaze?! Why does he know that technique?! I thought he was human!”

Even though they weren’t directly targeted, Yuan’s Dragon’s Gaze was so powerful that it sent tremors through the souls of everyone nearby, leaving them shaken to their core.

“Not just Dragon’s Gaze—he can use Dragon Awakening too! He’s definitely a dragon!”

“We were fooled into underestimating him!”

Snapping out of their daze, the Immortals steeled themselves and renewed their assault, launching a coordinated attack against Yuan with grim determination.

Not daring to get too close to Yuan because of his Dragon’s Gaze, the Immortals attacked him from afar.

Hundreds of martial techniques rained down on Yuan all at once, yet he made no effort to dodge or defend. He simply continued to charge forward, letting the attacks crash against his body without the slightest concern.

The combined assaults from the Bronze and Silver Immortals couldn’t even leave a scratch on Yuan’s body, while strikes from the Golden Immortals merely allowed him to feel the sting of real pain. As for the True Immortals, their attacks were powerful enough to tear through his defense and draw blood, yet he regenerated any damage received instantly, recovering in full before the Immortals could even blink their eyes in astonishment.

“What the fuck kind of physique does he have?!”

The Immortals were in utter disbelief, their eyes wide with shock as they witnessed the futility of their combined assault.

Meanwhile, every time Yuan struck with Dragon God’s Vengeance, it would claim a life without fail.

“I-It must be a trick! Don’t let him deceive you! Keep attacking!”

The Immortals resumed their assault, their faces with deep, growing despair.

chapters.title: Chapter 1974 - 1974: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(6)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1974 - 1974: The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan'S Invasion(6)

As the Immortals relentlessly unleashed their martial techniques on Yuan, he continued to ignore their attacks and struck whoever was the closest to him, reducing their numbers with terrifying speed and indifference, almost as if the difference in their cultivation meant nothing in his eyes.

As time passed, Yuan’s prowess only continued to increase as the Dragon God’s Vengeance claimed more victims with a dragon bloodline.

“Fuck! Why aren’t our attacks working on him?!”

“Where did this monster come from?!”

“S-Scatter! Get away from him!”

“I can’t! He’s too fast—Ahh!”

“Damn it! How is he disappearing before our eyes, even escaping detection from our divine sense?! This doesn’t make any sense!”

Though it appeared that Yuan was merely chasing them down, he was, in truth, weaving Shadow Veil into every movement, distorting their perception and turning the battlefield into a nightmare of illusion and dread.

In the enemies’ eyes, Yuan was teleporting all over the place while leaving behind clones of himself. Of course, these clones were mere illusions created using shadows with his Shadow Puppet technique. Searᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Moreover, the Azure Dragon Clan arrived in force to support Yuan, launching a coordinated assault that both distracted the enemy and sealed off their escape route.

Witnessing their forces being decimated and thrown into disarray by a single individual, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s God Ascension realm powerhouses could no longer stand by. They finally moved, intent on stopping Yuan themselves.

The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan initially showed up with four God Ascension realm powerhouses, but with the First Elder gone and the Third Elder occupied with Jiao Zhenhai, only two of them were available to deal with Yuan.

Meanwhile, the Azure Dragon Clan originally had seven God Ascension realm powerhouses, but with the Three Elders’ betrayal, they were down to four: Jiao Zhenhai, his parents, and the commander of the Azure Dragon Clan’s army.

The moment the commander of the Azure Dragon Clan’s army sensed the movements of the two God Ascension realm powerhouses, he rushed to intercept, but he was only able to hold off one, leaving the other free to advance toward Yuan.

Because of their immense power and overwhelming influence, an unspoken rule existed in the cultivation world: God Ascension realm cultivators should not interfere in large-scale battles like the ongoing war between the Azure Dragon Clan and the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan unless they were the only ones standing or the situation had reached a critical point and left them no other choice.

Of course, since this was merely an unspoken rule and not a real law, it couldn’t be enforced, and there were always those who disregarded it entirely, acting as they pleased even if it damaged their reputation, just like the First Elder and the Third Elder.

Once the God Ascension realm cultivator entered the fray, Yuan was forced to halt his slaughter and turn his full attention to the approaching threat.

His opponent stood at the second level of God Ascension, the same as the First Elder. Yet, he radiated a pressure and presence that made him seem incomparably more formidable, as though they existed on entirely different tiers.

Once a cultivator stepped into the God Ascension Realm, advancing even a single level could take thousands—if not millions—of years. Because of this, it was common for cultivators in their realm to be at the same level yet differ vastly in strength, comprehension, and abilities.

The First Elder had only recently stepped into the second level of the God Ascension Realm, and as a result, his strength fell far short of its true potential. In contrast, Yuan’s current opponent had long since stabilized at that level, and from the aura he exuded, he seemed to be on the verge of breaking through to the third.

“I don’t know how you killed the First Elder, but you won’t get to experience such a miracle for a second time,” said the man.

“We’ll see about that.”

Yuan suddenly used Dragon’s Gaze without warning.

“…”

Despite being hit by Yuan’s Dragon’s Gaze at full force, his opponent didn’t so much as flinch, as if the technique had no effect on him whatsoever.

“It’s very impressive that you’ve reached that level with your cultivation. However, before a real powerhouse, it’s nothing but cheap tricks,” said the man.

“I can see that you’re a little stronger than that traitor, but so what?” Yuan spoke with a calm smile. “In the end, you’ll still end up like him.”

“When threatened, dogs bark in fear, which is exactly what you’re doing. You can’t even break through my Celestial Qi, much less touch a single hair on my body.”

Yuan didn’t need to verify the man’s words to know they were true. At his current level, he stood no chance against him in a fair fight. The only reason he had managed to defeat the Second Elder, who was at the fifth level of God Ascension, was because he had caught him off guard and poisoned him. Even then, he would not have been able to kill the Second Elder without Zi Xuan’s assistance.

As for his current opponent, he was very wary of Yuan and also had no openings with his entire body covered in Celestial Qi.

“You’re right.” Yuan suddenly spoke. “I do not have the capability to defeat you right now. However, I can definitely humiliate you.”

“What?” The man frowned.

“Put away your Celestial Qi.”

Despite having his guard up, the man could not have anticipated Yuan using the Immortal Monarch’s ability.

“What is this?!”

The man was bewildered when his body listened to Yuan and began withdrawing his Celestial Qi. He hastily tried to resist the command, succeeded, and managed to stop himself from removing all of his Celestial Qi. However, he’d already removed some of his Celestial Qi by then, giving Yuan enough time to strike once.

“Don’t move.”

Yuan struck using the Dragon God’s Vengeance with all of his might, driving it directly into the man’s chest.

The man hastily retreated, but it was too late. By the time he managed to pull away, a gaping hole had already been torn through the center of his chest.

“Hm? What’s this? Not only did I manage to penetrate your Celestial Qi, but I even managed to put a hole in your chest!” Yuan acted surprised, even letting out a cheeky chuckle as the man stared at the hole in silence.

chapters.title: Chapter 1975: Wielding Celestial Qi
chapters.body:
Chapter 1975: Wielding Celestial Qi

Chapter 1975: Wielding Celestial Qi

“H-Ho-How dare you… a mere mortal… MOCK ME?!”

The God Ascension expert trembled in anger as he felt a sense of humiliation he hasn’t experienced for a long, long time.

In his eyes, it was akin to an elephant being crippled by a single ant whom he could easily crush with a finger.

“I’ll fucking skin you alive!”

The man suddenly released a beastly roar as he abandoned his human form and grew into a massive whale that could cover the entire underwater city.

When a beast reverts to its original form, its overall strength would rise exponentially but at the cost of greatly reduced mobility, a trait many regarded as the most critical advantage in battle. For this reason, most chose to remain in their human form, even in the face of death.

However, the God Ascension expert wasn’t worried about losing his mobility since his opponent couldn’t penetrate his defenses without trickery.

“I don’t know what trickery you used just now, but it will not work a second time!”

Even as he stood before a colossal entity capable of swallowing an entire city with a single breath, Yuan’s expression remained unchanged with a calm smile on his face.

In the very next moment, the whale unleashed a barrage of Celestial Qi, shaped into countless thin needles, but in reality, each “needle” was massive, towering like skyscrapers as they rained down toward Yuan with devastating force.

Though consumed by rage, the whale had not lost his reason and remained aware of his comrades battling nearby. As such, he restrained his power, opting for these compressed, controlled attacks to avoid causing collateral damage amidst the chaos.

As for Yuan, since there was nothing he could realistically do to the Celestial Qi, he was forced to dodge the incoming strikes.

However, he purposefully didn’t completely avoid them and would allow the Celestial Qi to land on some parts of his body so he could train his comprehension.

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi has improved slightly>

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi has improved slightly>

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi has improved slightly>

“Looks like you’re having trouble. Need some assistance?” Yu Ning suddenly asked.

“What do you have in mind? Unless I can get through his Celestial Qi, it won’t matter.”

“Only Celestial Qi can cut through Celestial Qi. Although my cultivation has dropped to Golden Immortal, I can still sense and manipulate Celestial Qi to a certain extent,” she said.

“Though I have never tried this before, I can theoretically help you gather Celestial Qi through your Dantian, allowing you limited use. It’s a very reckless thing to do, and I wouldn’t have even brought this up if I weren’t in your Dantian, or I think your body couldn’t handle Celestial Qi. However, it’s clear that your body is beyond common sense.”

An excited smile appeared on Yuan’s face after hearing Yu Ning’s suggestion.

“Let’s try it!” He quickly said without an ounce of hesitation in his voice.

“Okay. Give me a moment.”

A few seconds later, Yu Ning said, “I’m gathering Celestial Qi now.”

Yu Ning began collecting Celestial Qi while using Yuan’s Dantian as its storage.

The very next moment, Yuan coughed up a mouthful of blood as searing pain tore through his body, feeling as if every inch of him was swelling beyond control, on the verge of bursting apart from within.

Upon witnessing this, Yu Ning was overwhelmed with fear and immediately ceased her actions, unable to bring herself to continue.

“Keep going!”

Yuan suddenly said.

“B-But…”

“It’s only a little painful. I can handle it.”

Despite her reluctance, Yu Ning began gathering Celestial Qi again, albeit slowly.

“What is he…?” The whale stopped attacking Yuan when he noticed Celestial Qi was being gathered around him.

“No! It can’t be!”

His voice shook when he realized what was happening.

“He’s a mortal! Mortals cannot wield Celestial Qi! It shouldn’t be possible!”

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi increased drastically>

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi increased drastically>

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi increased drastically>

It took some time, but Yuan eventually adapted to the Celestial Qi coursing through his body and managed to move again. Of course, the pain remained intense, but it was now bearable.

“Ah… It’s been a while since I last felt like this…”

In this moment, the Celestial Qi surging through Yuan made him feel nearly invincible—a sensation reminiscent of when he wielded Heaven’s Supremacy, as though nothing in the world could stand in his way.

“You… you… WHAT THE FUCK ARE YOU?!?!?!” The whale roared in confusion and fear as he continued bombarding Yuan with Celestial Qi.

Now that he could wield Celestial Qi, Yuan no longer avoided it. Instead, he met it head-on, clashing with it directly and without hesitation.

With the Dragon God’s Vengeance cloaked in Celestial Qi, Yuan bore through his opponent’s Celestial Qi with almost no effort.

Yu Ning swallowed nervously as she watched how skillfully Yuan wielded the Celestial Qi like it was part of his own body.

’Even though he may have wielded Celestial Qi in his previous lives, to do so in a mortal’s body is really something else…’

Yu Ning had already regarded Yuan as a monster of boundless potential, but what he was doing now defied even her wildest expectations, leaving her utterly stunned.

“What’s wrong?! Not so confident in your Celestial Qi now, are you?!” Yuan laughed maniacally as he shot toward the whale that loomed above like a mountain.

The sight of Yuan wielding Celestial Qi was so utterly shocking that even those locked in fierce combat froze in place, their eyes fixed on him in disbelief, needing a moment to confirm that what they were witnessing wasn’t some illusion.

“H-He’s wielding Celestial Qi with the body of a mortal?!” Jiao Binglan and the others from the Azure Dragon Clan were utterly speechless.

“S-Stay away from me, you monster!” Sёarch* The nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The whale suddenly returned to his human form and began running away in a panic.

“How dare you run after starting this fight yourself?! Get back here, you damn coward!” Yuan shouted as he chased after him.

chapters.title: Chapter 1976: Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’S Reinforcement
chapters.body:
Chapter 1976: Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’S Reinforcement

Chapter 1976: Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s Reinforcement

As the God Ascension expert turned to flee, Yuan immediately gave chase. However, being underwater—and with his opponent a native sea beast—Yuan quickly found himself outpaced, the distance between them widening with every passing second.

Unable to close the distance, Yuan resorted to mockery, hurling taunts laced with scorn in hopes of provoking his opponent into a reckless, rage-fueled mistake.

“You dare call yourself a God when you’re running away from a mortal like a frightened cat?!”

“Your comrades are still fighting for their lives while you abandoned the battlefield! Are you not ashamed of yourself?! Even if you survive, you’ll become an object of derision!”

“Damn, look how fast you’re going! I can’t wait to tell my friends and the rest of the world about this!”

The God Ascension expert trembled with a mix of rage and humiliation as Yuan’s taunts echoed from behind. He even coughed up a mouthful of blood in an effort to suppress his fury. But in the end, his fear ran deeper than his pride, leaving him powerless to turn back.

However, just as Yuan was about to give up the chase, the man abruptly came to a halt.

Yuan stopped as well, but instead of excitement, a deep frown formed on his face, sensing that something wasn’t right.

Sure enough, moments later, Yuan sensed numerous powerful auras rapidly closing in from the distance.

“Hahahaha! They’re finally here! The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s main army has finally arrived!” The man laughed out loud after realizing what was going on.

“…”

Yuan had anticipated the possibility of reinforcements arriving, but when he saw just how many powerhouses were converging upon him, he couldn’t help but let out a weary smile.

With a single glance, Yuan could tell their reinforcements numbered over 300,000. It was a massive army, far larger than the entire reported population of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan. This could only mean one thing: the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan hadn’t come alone and had arrived backed by other forces.

It didn’t take long for the others to notice the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s reinforcements. And when the Azure Dragon Clan realized the situation, their expressions turned grim, darkened by a growing sense of dread.

Upon seeing their reinforcements arrive, those who had been clinging to life on the battlefield felt a surge of hope, and their morale soared to its peak.

The Third Elder, who had been losing to Jiao Zhenhai, let out a booming laugh. “Hahaha! I hope you didn’t think we were all you had to deal with because the real fun is only just beginning!”

Jiao Zhenhai clenched his fists. He remained silent, but his eyes were filled with despair. Even if his parents showed up right now, they’d still be completely outnumbered by the reinforcements.

At the forefront of the massive army stood several figures exuding overwhelming presence—each one a God Ascension realm cultivator ranging from the first to the fourth level, their very aura weighing down the atmosphere like a crushing tide. Sёarᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Leading them was a strikingly beautiful woman with silver horns curling from her head and a tail swaying behind her. Her cultivation dwarfed the rest, standing at the sixth level of God Ascension.

Some time later, the army halted before Yuan and the God Ascension realm cultivator.

“General Zhi, where do you think you’re going?” the woman suddenly spoke, her voice calm yet oppressive. “Why are you heading away from the battlefield?”

“T-That’s…”

General Zhi hesitated, shame flickering in his eyes. He couldn’t possibly admit to fleeing from a mere mortal—not in front of so many watchful eyes. Even if he survived today, he would be reduced to nothing but a laughing stock.

The woman spoke again, “Is something stuck in your throat, preventing you from speaking? Or are you just too ashamed to admit that you’d run away from the battlefield—from a mortal, no less.”

General Zhi’s entire body trembled the moment he realized the woman had witnessed everything.

“M-My actions are inexcusable, Mystic Empress!” he stammered, dropping to his knees. “I am willing to accept whatever punishment you see fit!”

“Hmph. I will deal with you once we return. Until then, stay there and watch how it’s supposed to be done.”

“As you wish!”

The woman then turned her attention to Yuan with an amused smile tugging at her lips.

After a moment of silence, she spoke, “I have lived a long time, but this is my first time seeing a mortal wield Celestial Qi. You have my admiration and respect.”

“Thank you.”

“My name is Cang Xuanji, the head of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, and I have a proposition for you. Instead of fighting for the Azure Dragon Clan, who are destined to be destroyed soon, why don’t you join my Mystic Sea Dragon Clan?”

“?!?!?!”

General Zhi expressed shock when the Mystic Empress offered Yuan to join their ranks instead of killing him, but he didn’t dare utter a sound.

“If you serve my Mystic Sea Dragon Clan well,” the Mystic Empress said with a faint, amused smile, “I might even consider accepting you into my family—as a concubine.”

Yuan couldn’t help but chuckle at her random offer.

“A concubine, huh? Did you offer the Three Elders the same thing?” he asked.

“What is that bastard saying?!” The Third Elder couldn’t believe what he was hearing.

“The Three Elders? They’re useful, but ultimately nothing more than tools. You’re different. If you desire, I can even dispose of them for you after you join our side.”

“M-Mystic Empress! That wasn’t part of our agreement!” the Third Elder blurted out in panic, his face draining of color.

The Mystic Empress’s expression darkened, her gaze turning icy as it locked onto the Third Elder.

“To think you still have the nerve to speak of our agreement,” she said coldly, her voice rising with each word. “Not only have you failed to obtain the Tear of the Azure Heavens, but you’ve also forced us to abandon a plan that took many millennia to perfect—all because of your incompetence! Trash!”

With that final word, the Mystic Empress’s voice erupted like thunder, unleashing a powerful ripple that swept across the battlefield, shaking the hearts of all who heard it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1977: The Mystic Empress’ Rampage
chapters.body:
Chapter 1977: The Mystic Empress’ Rampage

After hearing the Mystic Empress’ words, the Third Elder fell silent, no longer daring to utter another word.

Instead, he glared at Yuan with a hateful gaze.

’It was all because of that bastard! If he hadn’t shown up, we wouldn’t be in this situation!’

The Third Elder blamed everything on Yuan, who showed up out of the blue and purified their poison. Unfortunately, the only thing he could do was complain about it.

After scolding the Third Elder for his incompetence, the Mystic Empress returned her attention to Yuan and spoke calmly, “So, what do you think about my offer?”

“I’m flattered by it,” he said. “Unfortunately, I cannot join the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.”

“What is the reasoning behind your decision?” The Mysic Empress asked with narrowed eyes.

“First of all, I don’t belong to the Azure Dragon Clan.”

“What? Then why are you fighting for them?”

“They have something I want, and I am helping them to acquire it.”

“In that case, wouldn’t it be better to fight for us? Besides the Tear of the Azure Heavens, I can help you get whatever you want after we defeat them.”

“While that does make logical sense, I do not like to take things by force if I don’t have to. And while I enjoy fighting, I dislike needless bloodshed. Now allow me to tell you the second reason why I cannot join you.”

“The Tear of the Azure Heavens that you’re looking for… it no longer exists.”

“What are you talking about?” The Mystic Empress instantly frowned.

“It no longer exists because I took it and already absorbed it, so you wasted your time by coming here,” Yuan said.

“…”

Everyone standing behind the Mystic Empress froze, dumbfounded by Yuan’s claim. A heavy silence followed as they exchanged uneasy glances with each other. If what Yuan said was true, they wasted an immense amount of time and resources.

The Mystic Empress trembled slightly before speaking in a low voice, “Tell me that you’re joking…”

Yuan shrugged and said, “We’ve already tried telling the Third Elder, but he didn’t want to believe us either. I’m sorry, but it’s true.”

He extended his arm, and with a flicker of will, conjured a beautiful blue flame upon his palm.

“This is the Heaven-Freezing Flame that I acquired from consuming the treasure. You wanted it because you knew what it really was—the Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix’s heart, right?”

When the Mystic Empress saw the flames flickering above Yuan’s palms and heard his words, she trembled again and coughed up a mouthful of blood as numerous emotions surged through her being.

“M-Mystic Empress—!”

“YOU… HOW DARE YOU?!?!?” Sёarᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The Mystic Empress suddenly let out a thunderous roar as her cultivation erupted uncontrollably, surging outward like a tidal wave.

The immense force of her outburst instantly swept through the ranks behind her, sending her own army reeling, even damaging many who were unable to withstand the immense power of her uncontrolled pressure.

“DO YOU HAVE ANY IDEA WHAT YOU HAVE DONE?! WHAT I HAD TO SACRIFICE JUST TO GET HERE?!?!”

The Mystic Empress continued to scream, her voice laced with fury and anguish as her mind began to unravel, her composure quickly rumbling under the weight of rage and frustration.

“M-Mystic Empress! Please, you must calm yourself!” the God Ascension cultivators with her pleaded, their voices tinged with urgency and fear.

“CALM DOWN?!?!? CALM DOWN?!?!?!?!??!”

However, it only enraged her even further.

Yuan swallowed nervously. He knew it would anger her, but he never anticipated such a drastic reaction from the Mystic Empress.

“I’M GOING TO KILL YOU! I’LL KILL YOU ALL!”

Suddenly, the Mystic Empress shot toward Yuan with blinding speed—so fast that, though his eyes followed her movement, his body couldn’t react in time.

Her hand clamped around his neck like an iron vice, and in the next instant, she dragged him down in a streak of fury, slamming him violently into the ocean floor with a thunderous crash.

“I’ll get the Tear of the Azure Heavens even if I have to forcefully remove it from your body!”

Even as he was pinned to the ocean floor, Yuan tried to get the Mystic Empress off him by striking her with the Dragon God’s Vengeance. However, the difference in their Celestial Qi was simply too overwhelming for Yuan’s attacks to have any effect. It was akin to trying to break down a metal door with a toothpick.

Suddenly, two figures shot toward them before working together to remove the Mystic Empress from Yuan.

“Father! Mother!” Jiao Zhenhai exclaimed after seeing his parents’ arrival.

“Are you okay?” Jiao Longfei asked Yuan.

“Yes. Do you think you can hold her off for a minute?”

“A minute…? It’ll be difficult, especially when she’s like that, but we’ll try,” said Jiao Xuanji.

The two of them were both at the fifth level of God Ascension realm, but even if they fought together, they wouldn’t be able to defeat the Mystic Empress.

“How dare you intervene! I’ll swallow all of you!”

In a flash of divine light, the Mystic Empress transformed into her true form—a majestic silver dragon, radiating an overbearing presence that shook the sea itself.

Not daring to underestimate her, Jiao Xuanji and Jiao Longfei responded in kind, assuming their dragon forms without hesitation.

Moments later, the three titanic beasts collided, and the sheer force of their clash unleashed violent currents in every direction, hurling nearby cultivators like leaves in a storm.

While that happened, Yuan ignored everything around him and focused his attention solely on the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

“Yeyou, I need your help. You’re the only one who can stop her rampaging,” he sent his voice into the Soul Weapon, speaking directly to Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“…”

However, there was no response.

“I know you’re upset, and I’m sorry, but I could really use your assistance here.”

“…”

Yuan sighed and continued, “The Nine Dragon Symbols… I remembered our promise. Although we couldn’t fulfil it last time, let’s do it properly this time. I promise.”

Though Dragon Goddess Yeyou didn’t speak, the Dragon God’s Vengeance began to tremble in response.

chapters.title: Chapter 1978: Absolute Obedience
chapters.body:
Chapter 1978: Absolute Obedience

“GET OUT OF MY WAY, YOU TRASH!” Mystic Empress’ aura exploded further.

“At this rate, we won’t be able to block her for a single minute!” Jiao Longfei poured all of his Celestial Qi forward, but it quickly crumbled when Mystic Empress struck back.

“Damn! When the hell did she become so powerful?!” Jiao Xuanji exclaimed as she struggled to keep up.

“My treasure! My plans! My future! Ahhhhh!”

Mystic Empress’s rampage grew so uncontrollably violent that both the Azure Dragon Clan and the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan were forced to cease fighting and retreat in a panic.

Even the stray wisps of Celestial Qi erupting from her body were potent enough to kill anyone there instantly, leaving no one willing to remain near her wrath.

Suddenly, amidst the chaos and carnage, a profound aura descended upon the ocean like a silent decree from the heavens—vast, unfathomable, and absolute. The raging tides stilled, and even Mystic Empress’ rampaging Celestial Qi were forced to disperse.

“What is this pressure?!”

“This presence… A Cultivation God?!” Jiao Longfei gasped, his voice trembling with disbelief as he instinctively turned toward the source of the overwhelming aura, eyes wide with awe and fear.

Everyone present turned to face the source of the aura, including the Mystic Empress. Though still burning with rage, she found herself unable to express it, as if her very body and instincts had seized control, suppressing her fury out of sheer, primal desperation in the presence of something far beyond her comprehension.

Their gazes landed on Yuan, who was standing on the ocean floor with the Dragon God’s Vengeance in his grasp. No… they were all staring at the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

In the very next moment, a faint light emerged from within Dragon God’s Vengeance, swiftly expanding until it took the shape of a human figure, divine and ethereal, with a beauty so transcendent it instinctively commanded reverence from all who beheld it.

Yet, despite her breathtaking beauty, it was her unfathomable presence that held everyone’s attention. Merely standing in her presence compelled all those around her to instinctively seal their cultivation and loosen their stance, as though their bodies moved on their own to show submission and that they meant no harm.

Mystic Empress was no exception. Though her heart seethed with reluctance and resistance, her body betrayed her will. Unable to defy the overwhelming pressure of the presence before her, she returned to her human form and lowered herself in submission to Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

After making an appearance, Dragon Goddess Yeyou ignored everyone and calmly approached Mystic Empress.

“Hey,” she called out in a nonchalant tone.

“Y-Yes!”

Mystic Empress trembled uncontrollably as she responded in a cracked, uneven voice strained by overwhelming fear, feeling like she had suddenly turned into a mortal.

“I’m in a good mood right now, so I’ll spare you for your insolence. However, my patience is limited, so I’ll give you and your minions ten seconds to disappear from my sight. And don’t even think about coming back in the future.”

“Thank you for your mercy!”

Without daring to utter a word in defiance, Mystic Empress turned and swiftly swam away like her life depended on it, her once-dominant aura now subdued and broken. As for the rest of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, they didn’t need any instructions to fall back.

The Azure Dragon Clan stood in stunned silence, watching their enemies retreat in haste. The scene felt surreal, like a dream too impossible to be real, leaving them to question whether their eyes were deceiving them.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou returned to Yuan’s side and asked, “Are you satisfied? Or did you want me to kill everyone?” Sёarch* The novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, this is fine.”

He turned to look at Jiao Zhenhai and continued, “Though they may have plotted against the Azure Dragon Clan for the Tear of the Azure Heavens, this is just the nature of the cultivation world. They were undoubtedly forceful, but so was your aggression toward the Holy Dragon Clan, regardless of your reasons. That said, it’s not like they received no punishment. Because of their greed, they lost many powerful cultivators and Immortals, greatly reducing their influence, and it’s not likely they will return since you no longer have what they want.”

Yuan agreed to protect the Azure Dragon Clan from destruction, which he’d achieved. As for killing everyone from the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, he never considered doing such a thing.

If he, a mere human, were to erase one of the Ten Royal Dragon Bloodlines from existence, it would undoubtedly draw even greater trouble upon him in the future than just offending the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

After a moment of silence, Jiao Zhenhai let out a heavy sigh and said, “No, I completely understand. In the end, you’re just an outsider who was dragged into our conflict. We have no right to expect you to shoulder such a tremendous burden.”

“You helped us chase away the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan just like you promised, and that’s more than enough. Even if they return in the future, it’s a problem we must deal with ourselves.”

Jiao Zhenhai turned to look at the Third Elder, who had tried to escape with the others but was forced to stay behind and continued, “As for you… You’ll pay for betraying our family for the rest of your miserable life!”

“Please! I was wrong! Have mercy!”

Yuan stopped paying attention to them and looked at Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“I’d love to fulfill that promise right now, but with your condition like that…”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou interrupted, “I know, you don’t have to say anything. Until I recover my body, we’ll put that promise on hold. However, this doesn’t mean you can dilly-dally around it.”

Yuan nodded and said, “I’ll restore your body as soon as possible.”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou returned to the Dragon God’s Vengeance while the rest of the Azure Dragon Clan began tending to the aftermath.

chapters.title: Chapter 1979: Mystic Empress’ Obsession
chapters.body:
Chapter 1979: Mystic Empress’ Obsession

While the Azure Dragon Clan worked hard to rebuild their destroyed underwater city, much of which was left in ruins by the invasion, Yuan focused on cleansing the lingering poison from the clan’s remaining children.

Meanwhile, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan did not stop moving at their peak speed until they escaped the water and returned to the surface.

Once outside the water, Mystic Empress halted her movements and stared at the empty space before her in silence. Searᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The army behind didn’t dare to disturb her and patiently waited for her to snap out of it.

After a few days of complete stillness, Mystic Empress finally moved again. She took a deep breath before releasing a profound sigh.

“The cultivation world is quite brutal…”

As the leader of one of the Ten Dragon Clans bearing a royal bloodline, the Mystic Empress had always believed herself to stand at the very pinnacle of the world. Never had she imagined a day would come when she’d be forced to bow her head and retreat so helplessly.

Even now, she would tremble when recalling the presence she felt from Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

’Just who was that woman? I have never felt such fear before—not even when I stood before the Great One!’

With her vast influence, the Mystic Empress had crossed paths with most—if not all—of the most powerful beings in the Nine Heavens. Yet, not a single one had ever made her feel as utterly powerless as Dragon Goddess Yeyou did, and she had done so effortlessly, merely by releasing a sliver of her aura.

’There’s no way such a powerful entity isn’t known across the Nine Heavens! Somebody must know something about her!’

“Mystic Empress… what should we do now?”

Someone eventually asked, pulling her out of her thoughts.

The Mystic Empress turned to look at them and said, “The Tear of the Azure Heavens is no more, so the rest of our plan no longer matters. It’s unfortunate, but this is the end of our cooperation.”

“If you want to blame the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan—me for our incompetence, you’re free to do so, but I will not tolerate anyone thinking of coming after my family for this incident.”

“We won’t blame you, Mystic Empress. That entity’s appearance was completely out of our control. Hell, even if we knew about her existence beforehand, we still wouldn’t be able to do anything about it.”

Some time later, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan and the others went their own way.

Time flowed like a fleeting stream, and in the blink of an eye, a year had passed since the failed invasion of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan.

While everyone tried to move on and forget the incident, one individual had been living a waking nightmare ever since.

No matter how desperately she tried to bury the memory, the Mystic Empress could not escape the looming shadow of Dragon Goddess Yeyou. The goddess’s presence from that day clung to her mind like a curse, and with each passing day, the weight of that humiliation gnawed at her sanity, slowly driving her toward madness.

Boom!

A thunderous explosion echoed from the Mystic Empress’s quarters, followed by a tremor that rippled through the surrounding area. Yet, none of those nearby so much as flinched, as such disturbances had become routine ever since her return.

The Mystic Sea Dragon Clan tried seeking doctors to help her, but since this was a mental battle, there was nothing the doctors could do but suggest pills that strengthened the mind

“I can’t believe a sixth-level God Ascension powerhouse like the Mystic Empress still has to deal with heart demons.”

“Heart demons are cruel and merciless. They can bring down even a Cultivation God if it gets bad enough.”

“What should we do? We cannot let her get to such a point. No matter how many pills we feed her, her condition hasn’t improved. If anything, it has only gotten worse.”

“I’ll contact the other clans to see if they have any ideas…”

Time continued to pass.

Half a year later, several individuals from the other Ten Royal Dragon Clans arrived at the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan after hearing about the Mystic Empress’ unique situation.

“How is the Mystic Empress?” asked a handsome man who had the cultivation at the 7th level of God Ascension.

The members of the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan explained the situation and their incident with the Azure Dragon Clan.

“The Mystic Empress with a heart demon…?”

This news left them stunned, as they were all aware of how strong she was mentally.

When they went to speak with the Mystic Empress, they could hear her yelling inside her room from the halls.

“Who is she?! I need to know!!!”

“…”

The guests exchanged glances with each other.

Someone from the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan explained, “Her fear has turned into some sort of obsession with that entity.”

Some time later, they knocked on the door.

“Mystic Empress, can we speak to you for a second?”

Bang!

The doors burst open with a deafening crash, and the Mystic Empress emerged—her once-elegant appearance now disheveled, her eyes wild and unfocused, resembling more a madwoman than the regal figure she once was.

Before any of the guests could open their mouths, the Mystic Empress suddenly grabbed one of their collars and shouted, “Do you know her?! Do you know who she is?!?!”

“M-Mystic Empress! Snap out of it!”

“It’s fine,” said the handsome man she grabbed.

“That woman… You want to know her identity, right? Well, I think I have an idea.”

“WHO?! WHO IS SHE?!”

“This is merely a guess,” he said slowly, his tone laced with reverence and awe, “but judging by the details of her appearance, I believe she was none other than Dragon Goddess Yeyou—one of the Three Dragons of Origin.”

“What?! Dragon Goddess Yeyou?! But I thought she died long ago!” The others expressed shock after hearing this.

He shook his head and said, “Her death was never confirmed. She’d only gone missing.”

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou…” Mystic Empress mumbled with a dazed look on her face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1980: Two Years Later
chapters.body:
Chapter 1980: Two Years Later

The Mystic Empress became silent after learning of the identity of the entity who humiliated her.

Everything finally made sense to her. If it was one of the Dragons of Origin, it was only natural that she was hopeless before such an entity.

“If Dragon Goddess Yeyou has truly returned, this is huge news,” said one of the guests.

The others nodded in agreement, “Who knows how everyone will react, especially the Great One.”

“The Great One will probably be shaking in excitement once he hears of her return. He’s always fancied Dragon Goddess Yeyou but never had any success because of the Immortal Monarch. Now with the Immortal Monarch gone, he’ll definitely try again.”

“The Nine Heavens will probably get rowdy for some time.”

“Mystic Empress, are you okay?” The handsome man asked her a moment later.

“Yes… Thank you for telling me her identity, or I would’ve been consumed by it,” she said.

“Please, you don’t need to thank me. It’s not guaranteed that she’s really Dragon Goddess Yeyou, either.”

“No, there’s no doubt that she’s definitely Dragon Goddess Yeyou,” she said firmly. “Aside from the Great One, I’ve never experienced such instinctive fear… not even close.”

“If the legends are true—that Dragon Goddess Yeyou is stronger than even the Great One, then it would make sense.”

She turned to look at the others and said, “I need to rest. Sorry for worrying you guys, even making you waste your time by coming here.”

“Since we got to see your face, it wasn’t a waste,” laughed the handsome man.

The Mystic Empress smiled and said, “Once I recover, I’ll invite you guys again.”

Her guests left shortly after, and the Mystic Empress entered seclusion.

As time passed, rumors of Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s return began making their way across the Nine Heavens. However, this rumor was so far-fetched that almost nobody believed it.

After all, Dragon Goddess Yeyou has been missing since the Immortal Monarch’s Era, which ended hundreds of millions of years ago.

Eventually, this rumor reached a certain pair of ears.

“What did you just say? I demand you repeat that.”

The individual’s voice trembled with disbelief, and his sharp golden eyes flickered with a barely contained excitement.

“There’s been a rumor going around claiming that Dragon Goddess Yeyou has return—”

The man suddenly extended his arm to grasp the messenger around the neck and threatened, “If you dare mess with me, I’ll shatter your soul.”

“I-It’s true! I wouldn’t dare to lie to you, Great One!”

“Tell me everything you know!” The Great One roared, his body trembling in excitement.

Meanwhile, two years had quietly passed since Yuan took residence within the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory, dedicating himself to purging the remnants of the poison from their bloodline.

“I’m done.”

Yuan said as he lowered his arms.

“T-Thank you…” Jiao Binglan, who was the last to be treated, quietly thanked him.

Yuan met with Jiao Zhenhai and the others shortly afterward. Sёarᴄh the ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I have fulfilled our agreement.”

“I don’t even know how to thank you…” Jiao Zhenhai sighed.

“No need.” Yuan shook his head before continuing, “Now then, it’s your turn.”

“Of course.”

Jiao Zhenhai retrieved a silver key from his spatial ring and offered it to Yuan.

“This is the key we received from the Holy Dragon Clan.”

Yuan accepted the key and tossed it into his spatial ring.

“Then I’ll take my leave now.”

“Are you sure you don’t want to stay here a little longer?” Jiao Binglan asked him. “You should at least rest before you go…”

Jiao Binglan’s attitude toward Yuan had undergone a profound transformation over the past two years. Once, she had wished for nothing more than his departure, but now, the mere thought of him leaving was unbearable, stirring a quiet ache that was hard to ignore.

Unfortunately, she knew they were not fated to be together.

“Sorry, but I need to return to the Holy Dragon Clan as soon as possible.”

“Th-Then, at least let me escort you back!” she said.

“If that’s what you want.”

Some time later, after bidding farewell to Jiao Zhenhai and the rest of the Azure Dragon Clan, Yuan began his journey back to the Holy Dragon Clan escorted by Jiao Binglan and Jiao Xuanbing.

As they made their way back, they passed through the underwater city. Two years ago, it had lain in ruins, destroyed by the invasion. Yet, in just two short years, not only had the Azure Dragon Clan rebuilt it, they had refined and improved it, crafting a city even grander than before.

Yuan gazed at the underwater city and smiled inwardly, ’Although this journey took over two years, I’d gained a lot.’

Not only did he acquire the Heaven-Chilling Ice Phoenix’s heart, which helped him complete his physique and obtain new abilities, but he’d even acquired six powerful treasures from the Azure Dragon Clan.

A month later, Yuan returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“Do you think we’ll see each other again?” Jiao Binglan asked him as they arrived at their destination.

“I’m sure we’ll cross paths again in the future,” he said.

She silently nodded.

“Welcome back, Senior Envoy.”

The Holy Dragon Emperor greeted him at the entrance a moment later.

“I’m back.”

Jiao Bingland and Jiao Xuanbing returned to the Azure Dragon Clan after confirming that Yuan had safely entered the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“I’m sorry.”

The Dragon Emperor suddenly apologized.

“What for?”

“For not being there with you when the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan invaded,” he sighed.

The Azure Dragon Clan had notified the Holy Dragon Clan of the incident after the invasion had ended, so they were aware of the situation.

Yuan smiled and said, “Look at me. Do I look dead to you?”

“No…”

“Then there’s nothing else to talk about, especially when it happened two years ago.”

“I understand.” The Dragon Emperor nodded and changed the topic.

Some time later, the Dragon Emperor brought Yuan to the training grounds to meet with Xi Meili.

chapters.title: Chapter 1981: Activating Dragon Spiral Mountain’S Teleportation Device
chapters.body:
Chapter 1981: Activating Dragon Spiral Mountain’S Teleportation Device

When Yuan arrived at the training ground, Xi Meili was engaged in a fierce spar with one of the young dragons.

“What happened to her? She’s improved a lot since I last saw her. Even her cultivation has soared,” Yuan remarked as they watched from a distance.

Xi Meili’s cultivation had somehow soared to the peak of Divine Ancestor in two short years, and her aura had grown so exponentially that Yuan almost didn’t recognize her at first.

“She’s trained with our most experienced instructors nearly every single day for the last two years. It was already apparent from the very beginning that she had tremendous potential and talent, but we’ve underestimated how much talent was dwelling within her.”

“After training for a year, her training progress suddenly skyrocketed toward the heavens, as if something within her bloodline awakened,” said the Dragon Emperor.

“Honestly, I was quite surprised when it happened,” he added. “Her bloodline is more powerful than I’d initially anticipated. I’m really intrigued by its origins.”

“She’s closely related to the Dragon Ancestor,” Yuan said.

“Dragon Ancestor… I wonder where he is right now.”

Eventually, Xi Meili defeated her opponent.

“Damn it! I lost again!” The young dragon complained out loud.

“It was a good fight.” Xi Meili extended her arm toward her opponent.

The young dragon grabbed it and spoke with a defeated smile, “You’re a monster, Senior Sister.”

“You only say that because you’ve never seen a real monster before,” Xi Meili chuckled.

It was at this moment that Xi Meili noticed Yuan.

“Speaking of the devil…”

She leaped from the stage and landed before Yuan.

“I didn’t realize you’d be back today! Welcome back, Yuan!”

“I’m back,” he smiled.

“I’ve been so focused on training that it doesn’t even feel that long since you left,” she said.

“I can tell. You’ve worked hard.”

“I know you just came back, but how about a spar?”

“Why not? I might as well stretch my body before I activate the teleportation device.”

Yuan jumped onto the stage with Xi Meili, and the two of them began sparring immediately. However, neither was going all out in the beginning, and they chatted as they fought.

“I heard you had to protect the Azure Dragon Clan from an invasion. How was it?”

“It was a good experience since I got to fight with Immortals and even a few God Ascension experts.”

“Hm? Wait! You’re already at the peak of Immortal Ascension?! I was going to brag about my cultivation progress, too!” Xi Meili exclaimed after noticing his cultivation.

Though she was happy for him, Xi Meili couldn’t help but feel a little dejected since she thought she’d closed the distance between them, even if it was just slightly.

However, she quickly shook off those negative thoughts and declared, “I’m going to train even harder!”

“That’s the spirit.” Yuan laughed.

Their spar quickly increased in intensity, with both of them using Dragon Awakening at the end.

The spar lasted for thirty minutes until Xi Meili ran out of energy and forfeited.

“I’ve defeated you before, and I won’t stop training until I can do it again!” she said afterward.

“I like your enthusiasm. Here, let me give you a little push.”

Yuan suddenly retrieved a spatial ring and offered it to her.

“What’s this?” Xi Meili asked as she accepted it.

“A little something I acquired from the Azure Dragon Clan. Take a look inside.”

When Xi Meili peeked inside the spatial ring, she nearly dropped it from shock.

“Ar-Are you sure I can have this?!” she stuttered.

“Of course. I got it specifically for you.”

“Thank you…” Xi Meili was at a loss for words.

There were several Empyrean-grade treasures inside the spatial ring.

When Xi Meili looked inside the spatial ring again, she realized there was actually a Celestial-grade treasure in there.

“You didn’t rob the Azure Dragon Clan, did you?” she asked in a joking tone.

Yuan chuckled and said, “It could be considered robbery.”

In his eyes, he had obtained these treasures in exchange for merely two drops of his blood, making it feel less like a fair trade and more like outright robbery.

Moreover, although these treasures were powerful, they were meant for dragons. Though Yuan could wield them since he had the body of a dragon, these treasures would not replace his current treasures.

“I’m going to activate the teleportation device now.”

“You’re going to return to your original world, right? Do you think I can come with you to see it?” Xi Meili asked.

Yuan pondered for a moment before saying, “You can visit, but since we don’t know how things will end up, let’s wait until after I regain my body.”

“Okay!”

With the silver key in his possession, Yuan traveled to the peak of the teleportation device at the top of the Dragon Spiral Mountain. S~eaʀᴄh the nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Meanwhile, Xi Meili, the Dragon Emperor, as well as several high-ranking members of the Holy Dragon Clan, followed as spectators.

Some time later, Yuan stood before the stone tablet with the keyhole.

Taking a deep breath, Yuan firmly pressed the silver key into the keyhole.

In an instant, the ancient symbols etched across the platform before him ignited with an ethereal glow, pulsing with a mysterious energy that seemed to awaken the very air around him.

“Won’t this require a lot of spiritual energy to activate?” Yuan asked.

The Dragon Emperor then spoke, “Yes, but this device automatically collects spiritual energy, and since it hasn’t been used since the Immortal Monarch’s Era, it should have gathered enough spiritual energy for a few uses.”

Moments later, a brilliant pillar of light erupted from the platform, shooting skyward with blinding intensity before expanding outward, tearing open the fabric of space and forming a massive, swirling portal suspended in the air.

At the same time on Earth, the Lord, who had been cultivating in silence, suddenly opened his eyes and rose to his feet, his gaze lifting sharply toward the sky, as if sensing a distant ripple that transcended worlds.

“H-He did it! He’s finally activated the teleportation device from the other side!” he exclaimed in excitement.

chapters.title: Chapter 1982: The Celestial Emperor’S Interference
chapters.body:
Chapter 1982: The Celestial Emperor’S Interference

Though it was faint, the Lord could sense something trying to connect with the Dragon Spiral Mountain on Earth.

“He did it! He activated the device from the other side!”

Not daring to waste another moment, the Lord swiftly turned and rushed into the depths of his cave. There, waiting in solemn silence, stood a circular platform and an ancient stone tablet. Without hesitation, he drew forth a golden key and inserted it into the keyhole, its surface trembling faintly in response.

However, unlike the teleportation device in the Seventh Heaven, the one on Earth barely emitted any glow due to a lack of spiritual energy.

Seeing this, the Lord gritted his teeth and roared, “Not enough spiritual energy? In that case, take my cultivation!”

Without a hint of hesitation, the Lord channeled all of his spiritual energy into the teleportation device, even drawing directly from his Dantian to fuel its activation.

Empowered by the immense surge of energy, the device responded instantly—its symbols blazing to life as a radiant glow engulfed the chamber, humming with ancient power.

The Lord’s cultivation plummeted at a rapid rate, but he didn’t care and continued as if his life depended on it.

Eventually, the teleportation device erupted with a golden pillar of light, its brilliance so intense it pierced through the stone ceiling of the cave and shot into the heavens, tearing through clouds as if announcing its activation to the entire world.

This phenomenon shocked everyone on Earth, with some wondering if it was the beginning of an apocalypse.

“Look at the sky! It got split apart!”

“What’s happening?! Is this the end of the world?!”

The Lord looked at the sky with a relieved smile.

“It’s done…” he muttered as his body collapsed onto the ground.

“Lord!!!” Liya rushed over after seeing this.

Meanwhile, at the Seventh Heaven’s Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“I’m going to enter the portal now,” Yuan said to them.

“Before you go into the portal… You know that the other device must also be activated for the connection to work, right?” Dragon Emperor said.

“Yes, I am aware. I have someone on the other side.”

“If you say so… Good luck.”

Yuan nodded and flew toward the sky, disappearing into the portal.

At the same time, somewhere in the Ninth Heaven—

“Your Majesty, somebody activated the Dragon Spiral Mountain’s teleportation device!”

A man draped in a majestic golden robe stood at the edge of a floating island, upon which a vast and imposing palace loomed behind him. His gaze was fixed downward, piercing through clouds and distance, as if observing the world far below with eyes that saw more than mere mortals ever could.

“I am aware. Send the Heaven’s Mandate to investigate and shut it down,” said the Celestial Emperor.

“As Your Majesty commands!”

“Dragon Spiral Mountain… Is it just a coincidence? No, there’s no such thing as a coincidence when it comes to him…” the Celestial Emperor muttered. Sёarch* The NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

After stepping into the portal, Yuan was instantly consumed by a rushing torrent of distorted space. He hurtled through the rift at incredible speed, his body suspended in a void where time and direction lost all meaning—and he had no control over his movements.

Normally, teleportation occurred in the blink of an eye. However, the distance between the Nine Heavens and Earth was so unfathomably vast that even space-bending travel required time to traverse.

A few minutes after Yuan vanished into the portal, the skies above Dragon Spiral Mountain trembled, and a second, even larger portal tore open the heavens. From its swirling depths, an overwhelming army descended—Immortals and God Ascension experts alike, their presence shaking the land with divine pressure.

Noticing this, everyone at the Dragon Spiral Mountain halted their movements and looked toward the sky.

“I am General Wang of Heaven’s Mandate! Under His Majesty, the Celestial Emperor’s command, we have come here to investigate your reason for activating the Dragon Spiral Mountain’s teleportation device!”

The man leading the army, a ninth-level God Ascension powerhouse, demanded an answer.

The Holy Dragon Emperor fearlessly approached the army and spoke, “Since when did the Celestial Emperor take interest in the affairs of our Royal Dragon Clan?”

“Since now.”

“Is that so? In that case, our reason is simple… we activated the teleportation device simply because we can. As far as I’m aware, it’s not against the law to do so.”

“It’s not, but the Celestial Emperor wants you to shut it down, and his words are the law in the Nine Heavens.”

“Perhaps that may be the case for you humans, but we’re the Holy Dragon Clan. We only follow the command of Dragon Goddess Yeyou. The Celestial Emperor should know better than to send a bunch of dogs to bark at us. Leave if you don’t want any trouble.”

“Insolence!” the General roared, laced with fury and disdain. “Even as the Dragon Emperor, you do not have the right to speak so brazenly! Shut down the teleportation device immediately!”

In the next instant, the Dragon Emperor released his aura without restraint. A wave of immense pressure swept across the land, shaking the air itself, so heavy and oppressive that even the clouds above trembled, as if bowing to his might.

“You dare encroach on my territory and issue commands as though you rule it?!” the Dragon Emperor thundered, his voice sending ripples in every direction. “The teleportation device will remain active, and that is final! If you wish to shut it down, you must do it yourself! But know this—the moment you set foot on Dragon Spiral Mountain, we will treat it as an act of invasion and we will retaliate!”

Upon hearing the Holy Dragon Emperor’s declaration, the members of the Holy Dragon Clan unleashed their auras in unison, each one erupting like a dragon’s roar through the heavens, a clear and unified signal to Heaven’s Mandate that they were not bluffing.

“How fucking troublesome…” General Wang clenched his fists in frustration.

He retrieved a medallion and activated it with his spiritual energy.

“Your Majesty, we have a situation here.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1983: Heaven’S Will
chapters.body:
Chapter 1983: Heaven’S Will

Following the Holy Dragon Emperor’s firm refusal to comply, General Wang was left with no choice but to contact the Celestial Emperor for further instructions. The situation had grown too delicate, and any misstep could ignite a conflict that would affect not just the Celestial Emperor but the entire Nine Heavens.

The Heaven’s Mandate certainly possessed the manpower to annihilate the Holy Dragon Clan if they so wished, but doing so would be no different than striking down a foreign diplomat protected by immunity, and they’d have to answer to the other Royal Dragon Clans.

While the Celestial Emperor stood as the single most powerful and authoritative figure in the Nine Heavens, his reach was not absolute. There existed powers beyond even his command. Most notably, the Ten Royal Dragon Clans. As dragons, they stood at the very pinnacle of the beast world, alongside the Phoenix bloodline and a handful of other ancient bloodlines. And as royals among dragons, they possessed the ability to command all lesser dragons beneath them.

If the Ten Royal Dragon Clans were ever to clash against the Celestial Emperor, it wouldn’t be a battle against ten clans alone—it would mean facing the united might of nearly all the dragons in existence, along with countless powerful beings loyal to their cause. Even for the Celestial Emperor, such a conflict would be a calamity of unimaginable scale.

Even if the Ten Royal Dragon Clans were not allies of each other and often clashed among themselves, they would never allow an outsider, especially a human, to trample upon one of their own bloodlines. In other words, internal strife was acceptable, even natural, but any external aggression was seen as an unforgivable affront to the dragon race itself.

“What should we do, Your Majesty? The Holy Dragon Emperor refuses to cooperate and declared war if we tried to stop the teleportation device.” General Wang explained the situation to the Celestial Emperor.

“…”

After a brief moment of silence, the Celestial Emperor spoke, “Let me speak with the Dragon Emperor.”

General Wang turned his gaze to the Dragon Emperor and spoke, “His Majesty, the Celestial Emperor, wishes to speak with you!”

He tossed the medallion to the Holy Dragon Emperor.

“This is the Holy Dragon Emperor speaking.”

“Holy Dragon Emperor, let’s not make things difficult.”

“I don’t want to, but if you want me to deactivate the teleportation device, I will have to.”

“Is it because you’re trying to protect someone?”

“…That is none of your business.”

“No, it is my business,” the Celestial Emperor said coldly. “The one you’re so eager to protect… if left unchecked, he will become the very catalyst for the Nine Heavens’ collapse, and as the Celestial Emperor, it is my duty to protect this world.”

The Holy Dragon Emperor smiled at the Celestial Emperor’s words and calmly responded, “You have your duties just as I have my own. I’m sorry, Celestial Emperor, but you will not change my mind.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, the Celestial Emperor released a faint sigh before speaking again, “I always thought you were one of the wiser Dragon Emperors, but it appears I have been mistaken this entire time. I really don’t want to do this, but you’ve forced my hands.”

“The other Royal Dragon Clans will be upset for a while, but they won’t do anything hasty while I have the Royal Phoenix Clans on my side.”

In the next moment, an invisible ripple spread across the skies above Dragon Spiral Mountain, distorting space itself. It exuded a profound and unfathomable pressure—one so deep and divine that it felt as though Heaven itself were trying to make an appearance.

Then, the word ’execute’ blazed into existence across the heavens, etched in radiant light, vast and commanding, as if carved directly into the fabric of the sky itself, and it hung there like a divine decree.

Seeing this, General Wang smiled and declared loudly, “Heaven has spoken! Follow the Celestial Emperor’s command and destroy the teleportation device! Execute anyone who dares to try and stop us!”

He pointed his sword toward the Dragon Spiral Mountain and roared, “Execute Heaven’s will!”

“RAAAAAH!”

Behind him, the army erupted with a deafening war cry that shook the very heavens. With weapons drawn and killing intent surging, they descended upon Dragon Spiral Mountain like a merciless storm.

In response, the Holy Dragon Emperor unleashed a thunderous roar, his voice reverberating through every corner of Dragon Spiral Mountain like a divine command. Sёarch* The ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Teach these intruders the price of defiling our sacred land! Slay all who dare to stain our home with their presence!”

“For Dragon Goddess Yeyou!”

Thousands of dragon roars erupted at once, shaking the heavens and the sea below. In a breathtaking surge, every member of the Holy Dragon Clan—young dragons, elders, even the humble servants—took to the skies with unwavering resolve.

“W-What should I do?!” Xi Meili cried out, her voice trembling with panic as the skies erupted into chaos around her. The sudden descent into all-out war left her completely overwhelmed and frozen in place.

“This is not your fight. Find a safe place to hide until Yuan returns.”

The Holy Dragon Emperor’s voice suddenly resounded in her head through voice transmission.

“B-But…”

Xi Meili hesitated following this instruction. How could she run and hide while everyone else fought? Moreover, they were fighting for Yuan.

“No! There’s no way I can cower in a corner while everyone else fights for him!”

Xi Meili wore all of the treasures Yuan had recently given her and joined the battle shortly later.

As for the Dragon Emperor, he also entered the battle after sending his message to Xi Meili, clashing with General Wang.

Although the Holy Dragon Clan vastly outnumbered the soldiers of Heaven’s Mandate, sheer numbers meant little in the face of overwhelming cultivation. Every member of Heaven’s Mandate was at least a Bronze Immortal, and with that power, they effortlessly suppressed the Holy Dragon Clan, pushing them back with ease despite being outnumbered.

Yet, even while being overwhelmed, the Holy Dragon Clan showed no signs of yielding and continued their relentless retaliation until their last breath.

chapters.title: Chapter 1984: Defend The Dragon Spiral Mountain!
chapters.body:
Chapter 1984: Defend The Dragon Spiral Mountain!

“How long have I been in here?” Yuan mumbled to himself as his body continued to travel through the space rift, feeling like it’d been ages since he entered the portal.

However, in reality, it’d only been a few minutes since he entered.

“Wait a second… this might be an opportunity to train my Power of the Void…”

Realizing this, Yuan stopped thinking about the passage of time and focused solely on the void around him.

<Your understanding of Power of the Void has increased slightly>

With nothing to do but train his Power of the Void, Yuan focused entirely on it, eventually forgetting that he was in a space rift and traveling back to Earth.

Only an hour had passed since Heaven’s Mandate began their invasion, yet more than half of the Holy Dragon Clan had already fallen. The majority were young dragons and servants—those without the cultivation to survive such a brutal onslaught.

“Look around you! This is the result of your foolish defiance, Holy Dragon Emperor!” General Wang exclaimed with a vicious smile on his face. “Did you really think the Holy Dragon Clan could stop Heaven’s Mandate, the strongest army in the Nine Heavens?!”

“Of course not,” the Dragon Emperor calmly replied.

“Then you must’ve believed your royal bloodline would make the Celestial Emperor hesitate! Unfortunately for you, that didn’t work out either!”

“You won’t understand it even if I were to break it down for you.”

“I don’t need to understand it, nor do I want to!”

The clash between the Dragon Emperor and General Wang—two peak God Ascension powerhouses—was so catastrophic that even the ripples from their blows carried enough force to instantly obliterate an Immortal upon contact.

To prevent their battle from decimating allies and enemies alike, they erected a barrier of Celestial Qi around themselves.

As for Xi Meili, she had managed to survive, though just barely, thanks to the protective treasures Yuan had given her. But mere survival was all she could manage. Against Immortals, her attacks were like droplets against stone, ineffective and easily brushed aside.

“Damn it all… they’re too powerful…” she muttered through clenched teeth, frustration burning in her chest like fire. Her hands trembled—not from fear, but from the unbearable weight of her own powerlessness.

“If only I were a little stronger!” Xi Meili cried out inwardly, frustration and helplessness twisting in her chest.

But before the thought could fade, a wave of killing intent crashed over her like a cold tide.

“DIE!”

An Immortal soldier charged straight at her, blade gleaming with killing intent. Xi Meili turned weakly toward the threat, but her body refused to move—too drained and battered. She couldn’t even lift a finger to defend herself, much less avoid it.

’Ah… Is this the end for me? I’m sorry, Yuan, but this will be the end of my journey with you… It was fun…’’

Xi Meili closed her eyes, having fully accepted her fate.

However, moments later, the death she’d anticipated did not arrive, prompting her to open her eyes again.

“Huh? You are…”

Just as the fatal blow was about to land, two figures appeared before Xi Meili like flashes of divine light. One stood firmly in front of her, effortlessly blocking the Immortal’s attack, while the other moved like a shadow, reappearing behind the soldier and striking him down in a single, precise motion.

These two were none other than Yin and Yang, elite members of the Evil God’s Shadow Army.

“Sorry it took so long. We had to alert the others. Everything will be okay now. Reinforcements have arrived,” said Yang.

In the very next moment, countless rifts tore open across the sky above Dragon Spiral Mountain, and from within them emerged several hundred figures, each cloaked in deep black robes that obscured their features. However, their aura was unmistakable and ominous, and their sudden appearance made the Heaven’s Mandate halt their advancement.

“T-The Shadow Army! The Evil God’s Shadow Army has made an appearance!” Someone suddenly shouted.

“What?!” General Wang exclaimed. He halted his fight with the Dragon Emperor to confirm it for himself.

“Evil God’s Shadow Army…?” The Holy Dragon Emperor frowned, unaware whether this new force was friend or foe.

Suddenly, a cold voice thundered across the battlefield, “Shadow Army… slaughter Heaven’s Mandate and protect the Dragon Spiral Mountain!”

Following the command, the members of the Evil God’s Shadow Army descended upon the soldiers of Heaven’s Mandate, instantly turning the tide against them.

Seeing this, General Wang retrieved another medallion and hastily activated it.

“Y-Your Majesty! We have an emergency! The Evil God’s Shadow Army has shown up to defend the Dragon Spiral Mountain!”

The Celestial Emperor, who had just received this news, sighed, “As I figured, this was no coincidence.”

Upon confirming Yuan’s involvement, the Celestial Emperor cast aside what little hesitation remained. With a swift motion, he retrieved a golden medallion etched with the words “Heaven’s Will.”

Raising it high, his voice boomed with divine authority, “The Evil God’s Shadow Army has revealed itself! Heaven’s Mandate—Third Squad, assist the Seventh Squad immediately!”

A few minutes after the Celestial Emperor’s command, the skies above Dragon Spiral Mountain split once more. More portals tore through the heavens, and from them descended the Third Squad of Heaven’s Mandate—each soldier a Golden Immortal at minimum, their auras blazing like falling stars as they rushed to reinforce the embattled Seventh Squad.

Meanwhile, Yuan’s eyes suddenly snapped open. In the vast expanse of the void ahead, a flicker of white light shimmered into view. Within moments, it expanded rapidly, growing brighter and closer as his body was irresistibly drawn toward it.

A few moments later, Yuan’s body was completely engulfed by the radiant light, his vision drowned in pure whiteness. But then, as the brilliance faded, he found himself suspended beneath a vast blue sky, surrounded by drifting clouds, and the silence of the void was gone, replaced by the gentle breath of the wind and the soft glow of sunlight.

With a smile on his face, Yuan announced out loud, “I’m back!” Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.
