chapters.title: Chapter 1985: Returning To Earth
chapters.body:
Chapter 1985: Returning To Earth

When Yuan emerged from the space rift, for a brief moment, he thought the teleportation had failed—that he was still in the Seventh Heaven. The spiritual energy in the air was simply too dense, too rich, to belong to Earth.

But he quickly realized that it was because the spiritual energy had flowed in from the Seventh Heaven.

The moment the teleportation device connected the realms, the boundless spiritual energy from the Seventh Heaven flowed into Earth. Even though it was only a fraction, for a small world like Earth, even the tiniest trickle of spiritual energy from the Seventh Heaven was enough to blanket the entire planet, saturating every corner with spiritual energy it hadn’t experienced since the Immortal Monarch’s Era.

The cultivators on Earth immediately sensed the shift. The moment the spiritual energy thickened around them, many dropped whatever they were doing and entered seclusion on the spot. Fearing it might vanish as suddenly as it appeared, they rushed to absorb as much of it as possible, eager to seize the rare opportunity to advance in cultivation while the heavens allowed it.

Since the rift opened up above the Dragon Spiral Mountain, it didn’t take long before Yuan noticed it and descended upon it.

When Yuan descended onto the peak of Dragon Spiral Mountain, his gaze was immediately drawn to two distant figures.

It was the Lord and Liya.

But something was wrong. The Lord lay motionless on the ground, seemingly unconscious, his aura faint and unstable. Beside him, Liya was transferring her spiritual energy into him.

Seeing this, Yuan rushed over and asked, “What happened?!”

“He sacrificed his cultivation to activate the teleportation device,” she revealed in a grim tone.

“What?! Let me help!”

Upon hearing that the Lord had sacrificed his cultivation, Yuan immediately moved to help. However, he didn’t rush to pour spiritual energy into the Lord.

With his cultivation reduced to the 2nd level of Spirit Apprentice, the Lord could only absorb what his current realm could withstand. Anything beyond that would do more harm than good.

Instead, Yuan retrieved some of his blood and fed it to the Lord.

Almost instantly, the Lord’s complexion improved significantly. Color returned to his face as the chaotic flow of his body began to stabilize. His breathing, once shallow and erratic, grew steady and calm, as if a heavy weight had been lifted from his chest.

Seeing that the Lord’s condition had stabilized, Liya slowly turned her gaze toward Yuan.

The moment her eyes met his, they widened in astonishment.

He looked completely different—taller, sharper, more refined. But it wasn’t just his appearance. His aura… it was vast, boundless, and utterly unfathomable to her.

It was as if she were staring at a stranger wearing the face of someone she once knew—someone who had soared far beyond her reach.

“A-Are you really Yuan…?” she couldn’t help but mutter.

“I know it’s been a few years since we last saw each other, but I can’t have changed that much, right?”

“Your face hasn’t changed much. I was talking about everything else. Your scent, aura—everything.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Where are the others?”

“Probably still training in the Nine Heavens. They rarely return to Earth these days,” she said. S~eaʀᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Then I’ll leave for now. I’ll come back once I get back into my real body.”

Then, he soared through the sky and returned straight to his home, landing softly on the familiar balcony just moments later.

Stepping inside, a wave of warmth washed over him. There, in the quiet of the room, lay his physical body, resting peacefully beside Chu Liuxiang.

But to his surprise, Meixiu was there as well, resting on the same bed, her expression gentle and serene.

“So, how does this work? Do I jump inside my own body or something?” Yuan questioned out loud.

Yu Ning then said, “Since your soul was separated using the Soul Transference Array, we’ll likely need to undo the formation first.”

“Do you think you can do it?”

“I can try, but I’ll need some time to analyze the formation first.”

“Go ahead. I’ll even lend you my body,” said Yuan.

The next moment, Yu Ning took over Yuan’s soul avatar and began examining Yuan’s real body.

“This formation is much more complicated than I’d anticipated,” she muttered after a single glance at the formation.

Half an hour later, Yu Ning spoke, “I think it’s doable. Fortunately, the Soul Transference Array wasn’t created to be unbreakable and is actually quite fragile. Once I remove the formation from your real body, your soul will likely automatically return to its body.”

“I’ll begin on your command.”

“I’m ready. You can begin whenever you’re ready.”

“Okay, then I will begin now.”

Yu Ning, using Yuan’s avatar, began making array symbols in the air.

About ten minutes later, she combined the array symbols into a single array and sent it directly inside Yuan’s body.

“It’s broken,” she said.

The very next moment, Yuan’s vision went completely blank. Yet, despite the absence of sight, he could still feel. A strange, irresistible force had gripped him, pulling at his very essence. He felt his body being pulled, as though something unseen was trying to consume his very existence.

This sensation lasted for a few seconds before everything went still.

Eventually, Yuan opened his eyes and could see the ceiling right above him.

He sat up on the bed and looked down at his body.

It worked. He’d finally returned to his true body.

“How do you feel?” Yu Ning asked.

“Weak,” he replied.

After examining his body more closely, he realized that his cultivation was no longer at the peak of Immortal Ascension but his true body’s cultivation, which was still in the Spirit Realm. However, he could sense his cultivation slowly improving.

“I’ll probably need some time before my body and soul are fully integrated again.”

“That’s normal, since your soul has been split into two parts for many years.”

Suddenly—

[Celestial Emperor’s Curse has activated]

When the Celestial Emperor’s Curse activated, an excruciating pain tore through Yuan, not in flesh or bone, but from the depths of his soul.

It was a searing agony unlike anything he’d ever known, as if his very existence was being unraveled thread by thread.

[Your soul has been damaged]

Yuan realized what was happening even without looking at the notification.

The Celestial Emperor’s Curse was trying to destroy his soul.

chapters.title: Chapter 1986: Unbreakable Will
chapters.body:
Chapter 1986: Unbreakable Will

“Young Master! Are you okay?!”

Yu Ning and the others exclaimed, their voices filled with horror.

“The Celestial Emperor’s Curse is trying to destroy my soul…”

“What?! Then you should hurry up and destroy the curse using the Sacred Chalice of Purification!” Feng Yuxiang urged.

However, Yuan shocked them by shaking his head and saying, “No, this is exactly what I have been waiting for.”

“That doesn’t make any sense!”

But Yuan didn’t respond and fell silent after entering cultivation.

This left the others flabbergasted. None of them could understand why Yuan was waiting for his soul to be attacked.

If Yuan’s soul hadn’t been fortified through his many reincarnations, the Celestial Emperor’s Curse would’ve instantly shattered his soul, killing him completely.

However, because his soul has existed since the Primordial Era and has been tempered for billions of years, not even the Celestial Emperor could easily destroy it.

Moreover, Yuan has been training Heaven’s Indomitable Soul for years now, which has strengthened his soul even more.

Yet, despite this, the Celestial Emperor’s Curse was relentless and extremely powerful—one of the most powerful curses in the Nine Heavens. The curse continued to gnaw at Yuan’s soul, causing him incredible pain, and no matter how powerful his soul was, it wasn’t indestructible, especially since he hadn’t even reached the God Ascension Realm.

Slow but surely, the Celestial Emperor’s Curse was destroying his soul.

Despite his soul on the verge of being destroyed, Yuan didn’t do anything to stop it, allowing it to happen.

His puzzling actions greatly worried his companions. A destroyed soul wasn’t the same as a destroyed body, and his regeneration ability had no effect on his soul, so if it were truly to be destroyed, he’d die for sure.

Meanwhile, in the Celestial Emperor’s Palace, the Celestial Emperor could sense the activation of his curse.

’So he managed to reunite his soul with his body, huh? However, as long as my curse is on you, you will not live long enough to return to the Nine Heavens!’

Almost half an hour has passed since Yuan began resisting the Celestial Emperor’s Curse.

“It’s time,” he muttered in a low voice.

The very next moment, Yuan stopped resisting the curse, allowing it to complete its assault on his soul.

His soul, which was already on the verge of destruction, began to crack, causing him to cough up several mouthfuls of blood.

“Young Master! What are you doing?!” Feng Yuxiang cried out loud.

However, Yuan was too focused to even hear her voice.

Then—

<Your soul has been destroyed>

Yuan’s body collapsed onto the bed, and his aura completely vanished.

“YOUNG MASTER?!”

Feng Yuxiang and the other emerged from his Dantian.

“H-He’s dead!” Feng Yuxiang couldn’t believe it.

Lan Yingying clenched her fists, unsure of what to say.

“Calm down, you dumb pigeon.”

A calm voice suddenly resounded.

“This voice… Dragon Goddess Yeyou?”

“You should have been with him for long enough to know that he wouldn’t do something like this without a reason.”

“That’s…” Feng Yuxiang fell silent.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou was right. Yuan wouldn’t do something as foolish as killing himself.

Just a they began to calm down, Yuan’s body suddenly became enveloped by a golden shimmer. It was faint at first, but it eventually became so intense that it illuminated the entire room.

<You have fulfilled the hidden requirement for Heaven’s Indomitable Soul>

<Heaven’s Indomitable Soul has evolved into Heaven’s Undying Soul>

<Heaven’s Undying Soul has begun to restore your destroyed soul>

<Your will has remained firm even in the face of the destruction of your very existence>

<You have attained ’Unbreakable Will’>

A few minutes later, Yuan slowly opened his eyes.

<Your soul has been restored>

“Young Master!” Feng Yuxiang cried tears of happiness after seeing he’d returned.

He showed them an apologetic smile and said, “Sorry for not warning you ahead of time.”

“You really scared me there for a second!”

“What happened, anyway?” Lan Yingying asked.

“I’ll answer your questions later. Right now, I have to deal with the Celestial Emperor’s Curse.” Sёarᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Though he may have restored his soul, the Celestial Emperor’s Curse continued to haunt him and attempted to destroy his soul again.

Yuan retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification and fed his blood to it.

<Sacred Chalice of Purification has begun cleansing the curses that bind you>

Unlike the others, whose curses were almost instantly cleansed by the Sacred Chalice of Purification, the Celestial Emperor’s Curse clung stubbornly to Yuan’s soul.

However, no matter how powerful the Celestial Emperor’s Curse was, it was ultimately eliminated by the chalice.

“Cough!”

In the Celestial Emperor’s Palace, the Celestial Emperor coughed up a mouthful of blood when his curse was destroyed.

“Your Majesty!”

The people around him panicked when they saw this.

“I’m fine!” he roared.

He looked at his blood on the ground and clenched his fists in frustration, ’You’re even harder to kill than a damn cockroach! Why must you continue your pointless struggle? You will never defeat them!’

<Sacred Chalice of Purification has purified ’Celestial Emperor’s Curse’ on you>

<Sacred Chalice of Purification has begun to purify the other causes>

<Progress: 1%>

After purifying the Celestial Emperor’s Curse, the Sacred Chalice of Purification began purifying the other curses that afflicted Yuan. However, these curses were much more powerful than the Celestial Emperor’s Curse.

Yuan wasn’t surprised about these curses since he had been warned about them before. In essence, most of them were natural curses that had followed him from his previous lives.

Shortly after Yuan purified the Celestial Emperor’s Curse, the surrounding spiritual energy surged toward him like a flood, consumed by his body at a terrifying rate, even though he wasn’t actively cultivating.

With the curse lifted, the seal that had silently suppressed his potential was gone. His cultivation speed, once shackled, had returned to its natural state.

“The Celestial Emperor is definitely aware of the situation. We need to return to the Nine Heavens as soon as possible,” he said as he jumped off the bed.

Before leaving, he looked at Chu Liuxiang and Meixiu and mumbled, “Let’s meet again in the Seventh Heaven.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1987: Leaving Earth
chapters.body:
Chapter 1987: Leaving Earth

After leaving his home, Yuan immediately made his way back to the peak of the mountain.

“Welcome back, Young Master.” The Lord, who was unconscious when he left, had woken up.

“How are you feeling?” Yuan asked.

“Thanks to your blood, my condition was able to stabilize. However, with my cultivation like this, I am no longer able to control the formation that protects this world. Fortunately, the spiritual energy from the Nine Heavens has temporarily solved this situation.”

“Temporarily… so we’re not completely out of trouble yet.”

“No, not yet. Since the gateway between our worlds won’t remain open forever, we’ll stop receiving more spiritual energy once it closes. The spiritual energy won’t last forever, either. I don’t know how long the spiritual energy we received will last, but it should last a good while,” said the Lord.

“That said, as long as both Dragon Spiral Mountains remain standing, we’ll be able to activate the teleportation device again in the future.”

“Who will be able to activate the teleportation device now that your cultivation is gone?” Yuan asked.

Even if the Lord were to raise his cultivation again, it’d take far too long for him to return to his original cultivation.

Yuan glanced at Liya, who quickly said, “Don’t look at me. My cultivation won’t be enough.”

“I’m sure we’ll think of something. Anyway, Young Master, you shouldn’t linger here for too long. The gateway will be closing soon,” the Lord warned him. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

He nodded and said, “Before I leave…”

He retrieved a bunch of vials, each filled to the brim with a crimson liquid.

“This is my blood. I don’t know when I’ll be able to return to Earth, so I’ll leave for you.”

“There’s so much!” The Lord almost couldn’t believe his eyes.

“Then, until next—”

“Wait.”

The Lord suddenly stopped him.

“What is it?”

“Take Liya with you.”

“Huh?” Yuan’s eyes widened.

“I’d like you to take her to the Nine Heavens.”

“I heard you, but…”

He turned to look at Liya, who wore a perplexed expression on her face, which surprised him, as he thought she would immediately reject such an idea.

“Don’t give me that look. You know we’ve already talked about this.”

“That was before you lost your cultivation! You still want me to leave you when you’re in this condition? I cannot—”

“My cultivation may have declined, but I will be fine.”

“Who will manage the Dragon Spiral Mountain?”

“It can manage itself.”

“Then who will protect you?”

“I can’t even remember the last time I was attacked. And even if someone is foolish enough to invade the mountain, the Young Master’s friends will still be here.”

“…”

Liya fell silent when she couldn’t think of anything else to convince the Lord.

“Go. You’re not fated to spend the rest of your life in this small world, wasting your potential. Young Master, please drag her to the Nine Heavens if you have to.”

“I’ll go…” Liya suddenly spoke in a low voice.

“There’s nothing I can do if you don’t want me here.”

Without a word, Liya turned skyward and soared toward the rift, which was now visibly shrinking.

“Are you certain about this?” Yuan asked.

The Lord nodded and replied, “Yes. She and I have spoken of this ever since we first discussed linking the Nine Heavens with Earth.”

“One last thing before you go… If the Red Heaven Dragon Clan still exists in the Nine Heavens, please bring her there. However, only if they are worthy of her.”

Yuan nodded, “The Red Heaven Dragon Clan, right? I’ll do my best.”

“Until next time, Young Master.”

“Until next time.”

Yuan left the Dragon Spiral Mountain and chased after Liya, who was waiting for him in front of the rift.

“Let’s go.”

Liya turned her head to look at the Dragon Spiral Mountain one last time before following Yuan into the rift.

Meanwhile, in the Seventh Heaven, the battle between the Holy Dragon Clan, the Evil God’s Shadow Army, and Heaven’s Mandate neared its end.

Despite the Seventh Squad’s assistance, Heaven’s Mandate found themselves still on the losing end of the battle.

The Evil God’s Shadow Army was simply too overwhelming. Moreover, Dong Ye, who had been away, joined in on the battle.

“Kill them! These damn dogs of the Celestial Emperor—kill them all!” Dong Ye roared as he teleported from immortal to immortal with a powerful movement technique, killing them with a single strike.

“Fucking Evil God’s army! You should’ve remained in the shadows where you’ve lurked for eras. But now that you’ve revealed yourselves, we will come for you with the full might of our wrath!” General Wang roared.

“Hahaha! I’d love to see you try!” Dong Ye laughed.

Suddenly, as if responding to Dong Ye’s provocation, the heavens above Dragon Spiral Mountain quaked, and an unfathomable pressure descended, sweeping across the battlefield like the will of an ancient god, halting everyone’s movements.

“This feeling… don’t tell me!” As though he realized something, Dong Ye immediately warned the others, “The Celestial Emperor is making a move! Withdraw!”

Without hesitation, the Evil God’s Shadow Army stopped fighting and began scattering in every direction.

In the very next moment, a colossal rift tore through the sky, and from its depths emerged the tip of an immense sword, as if the heavens themselves were being pierced.

Realizing the situation, the Holy Dragon Emperor gritted his teeth in anger.

“That technique… Are you really going that far, Celestial Emperor?!”

“Holy Dragon Clan, you should also withdraw if you don’t want to die to it! This isn’t something any of us can handle!” Dong Ye warned them.

Yet, they remained on the battlefield, unwilling to leave.

“The Dragon Spiral Mountain is our home. We will never abandon it, even if it means we die with it,” the Dragon Emperor declared.

“For Dragon Goddess Yeyou!” The others shouted.

“These stubborn fools!”

Dong Ye turned his attention to Xi Meili before teleporting to her.

“We’re leaving!” he said to her.

“What?! We can’t leave the Holy Dragon Clan alone!” Xi Meili exclaimed.

“They have decided to stay even if it means they’ll die, and I have no authority to stop them! However, you’re my liege’s comrade! I cannot let you die! Sorry, but I’ll take you away even if I have to use force!”

Before Xi Meili could contest, Dong Ye grabbed her arm and teleported them away from the battlefield, leaving the Holy Dragon Clan and Heaven’s Mandate alone.

chapters.title: Chapter 1988: The Celestial Emperor’S Unstoppable Attack
chapters.body:
Chapter 1988: The Celestial Emperor’S Unstoppable Attack

The moment the Celestial Emperor sensed his curse destroyed by Yuan, a resolute glint flashed in his eyes. Without hesitation, he raised his hand, drawing forth his Celestial Qi.

“Your Majesty! What are you doing?! You can’t use such a powerful technique without any warning!” A man bearing a unique white and gold robe hastily approached the Celestial Emperor and exclaimed after seeing this. “The results will be catastrophic, and the Celestial Council will be all over this!”

“I am well aware,” he calmly responded. “However, I cannot let him return to the Nine Heavens. Even if I receive backlash for this, I am willing to bear the responsibility.”

The man fell silent upon hearing the unwavering composure and clear finality in the Celestial Emperor’s tone. He was certain that unless someone forcefully stopped him, the Celestial Emperor had no intention of backing out.

However, the technique the Celestial Emperor was about to unleash would not just destroy the entire Dragon Spiral Mountain, it would cause irreversible damage in the Seventh Heaven!

’Once I reduce Dragon Spiral Mountain to nothingness, his path to the Nine Heavens will be severed. And if he is already making his return… then he will be cast adrift in the boundless void of the universe—no different from death itself,’ the Celestial Emperor mused inwardly.

Meanwhile, in the Seventh Heaven, the Heaven’s Mandate began backing away from the Dragon Spiral Mountain when they saw the slit in the sky.

“T-The Celestial Emperor is going to personally intervene! Fall back!” General Wang shouted before hastily creating a portal for them to escape to.

Witnessing this, the Holy Dragon Emperor paid them no further heed and turned back toward Dragon Spiral Mountain, where the lifeless bodies of thousands of his fallen clansmen lay.

The members of the Holy Dragon Clan who were still alive gathered around him.

“I never imagined that our Holy Dragon Clan would perish in such a situation,” sighed the Dragon Emperor.

He turned to look at the others and continued, “I won’t blame you if you wish to leave.”

“There’s no way we will abandon you and the Dragon Spiral Mountain!”

“That’s right! We have spent our entire lives here, and we will spend our last moments here!” Searᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The members of the clan voiced their refusal to leave.

The Holy Dragon Emperor smiled and said, “In that case, we might as well spend our last moments trying to protect our home.”

He turned his gaze to the colossal sword in the sky, now descended more than halfway through the rift, its presence looming like a divine judgment.

“That is the Celestial Emperor’s ’Divine Judgment’, right? Despite being in this world for over a million years, this is actually my first time seeing it,” one of the elders remarked.

“The ultimate execution technique, powerful enough to kill even a 9th-level God Ascension cultivator with ease…”

“This will sound weird, but it feels like an honor to die to such a magnificent technique…”

Once the Holy Dragon Clan had mentally prepared themselves, they charged toward the sky in preparation to fight against the Divine Judgment.

Meanwhile, countless eyes across the Seventh Heaven—and even those gazing down from the Eighth and Ninth Heaven—watched the unfolding scene in stunned silence. The earlier battle had already stirred the realms, but none had foreseen that the Celestial Emperor himself would take action.

“What did the Holy Dragon Clan do to incite the Celestial Emperor’s wrath?”

“Who were those individuals in black? They appeared out of the blue and nearly defeated the invincible Heaven’s Mandate!”

These people were full of questions and speculations.

Meanwhile, the other Royal Dragon Clans had already mobilized, dispatching emissaries to the Celestial Emperor’s Palace with urgent demands to cease his assault on the Holy Dragon Clan.

Unsurprisingly, the Celestial Emperor had sealed the doors to his palace and completely ignored their demands.

This enraged the Royal Dragon Clans, yet they were powerless to prevent the Celestial Emperor from obliterating Dragon Spiral Mountain. All they could do was bide their time and prepare to seek retribution once the deed was done.

At last, the Celestial Emperor completed the technique and, with unwavering authority, swept his arm downward. In that instant, the sword hanging in the heavens surged with power and plunged forth, its descent no longer gradual but swift and absolute.

The sheer pressure radiating from the sword was so overwhelming that all cultivators below the God Ascension Realm from the Holy Dragon Clan plummeted from the sky like helpless flies. Even the Dragon Spiral Mountain began to sink into the waters beneath it, crushed by the weight of its descent alone.

“This is really too much…” The Holy Dragon Emperor muttered as he struggled to stay in the air, much less fight it.

“Those strong enough to withstand the pressure unleashed their most formidable techniques upon the descending sword, shaking the heavens and earth with their onslaught. Yet their efforts were in vain. The blade remained unscathed, as if they were hurling raw eggs against an immovable mountain.

The Holy Dragon Emperor suddenly charged toward it, his form erupting into that of a colossal silver dragon. With a thunderous roar, he lunged at the descending sword and sank his fangs into it, desperate to halt—or at least slow—its relentless fall.

The others followed suit, but alas, their efforts were futile against the Celestial Emperor’s overwhelming prowess.

“Haaa…”

Just as the sword was about to bring ruin upon Dragon Spiral Mountain, a faint sigh echoed through the heavens, soft yet boundless.

Then, the space just above the peak of the Dragon Spiral Mountain warped and twisted, and like a ghost drawn from the void, a solitary figure draped in black robes and a face veil appeared out of thin air.

This figure exuded an unfathomable presence far beyond the Holy Dragon Emperor, and just their presence alone was enough to slow down the descending sword.

“This presence… it can’t be!”

Gasps of shock rippled through the spectators as they beheld the sudden arrival of a figure who had reached the pinnacle of cultivation—the appearance of a Cultivation God.

chapters.title: Chapter 1989: The Appearance Of A Cultivation God
chapters.body:
Chapter 1989: The Appearance Of A Cultivation God

Chapter 1989: The Appearance of a Cultivation God

“A Cultivation God has appeared!”

“What?! Impossible! Why would a Cultivation God intervene, especially when the Celestial Emperor is involved?”

“Are we sure they are a Cultivation God? There are only a handful of them in the entire Nine Heavens, and I recognize everyone but this individual.”

“That presence… that aura… There’s no doubt about it. This individual is a Cultivation God.”

“Are you saying this is not just the appearance of a Cultivation God but the appearance of a new one?”

The spectators’ astonishment deepened as realization struck—this was an unfamiliar Cultivation God, one whose presence had never graced the public eye. Cultivation Gods were figures of renown, their names etched into the annals of history. For such a being to appear unrecognized could mean only two things: they had either remained hidden in the shadows for an unknown amount of time, or they had just recently reached the pinnacle of cultivation.

“Their appearance may be new, but that unfathomable aura… It’s much stronger than that one Cultivation God I saw a few years ago, so they must’ve been a Cultivation God for quite some time now.”

As the spectators racked their brains trying to figure out the Cultivation God’s identity, the Celestial Emperor wore a deep frown on his face when he realized that someone was trying to stop his Divine Judgment. In the entire Nine Heavens, only Cultivation Gods could possibly defy him, a fellow Cultivation God.

“Who are you, and why are you interfering with my work?” the Celestial Emperor spoke, his voice booming through the realms and resounding before the Cultivation God.

A slight smile tugged at the lips behind the veil, but the Cultivation God did not respond to the Celestial Emperor.

The Cultivation God raised their hand and pointed toward the colossal sword in the sky, revealing a pair of fair, jade-like hands, smooth, unblemished, and adorned with slender fingers. The graceful motion, coupled with the subtle rise of her chest beneath the robes, left little doubt that this mysterious figure was a woman.

“This new Cultivation God is definitely a woman! Looks like we will have more than one female Cultivation God now.”

With this new revelation, the spectators began narrowing down the possible identities of the mysterious Cultivation God. Their minds raced through the annals of history, recalling every female cultivator who had ascended to the peak of the God Ascension Realm within the last few million years.

“It’s hard to tell with her robes, but her figure resembles the Fairy Sword Empress.”

“No, the Fairy Sword Empress is a little shorter.”

“Why are we even using appearance details to figure out their identity? If this individual is trying to hide their identity, it’s very likely that they’re also disguised. She could be a he for all we know.”

The very next moment, the sword began being pushed toward the sky, its pressure on the Dragon Spiral Mountain weakening.

“Heavens! This Cultivation God is firm on defying the Celestial Emperor!”

While the other Cultivation Gods aren’t all on friendly terms with the Celestial Emperor, none of them have shown such obvious defiance, either. After all, nothing good comes out of offending the most influential individual in their world.

Seeing the Cultivation God’s defiance, the Celestial Emperor no longer went easy. He was aware that his actions were being spectated by powerhouses all over the Nine Heavens. If he were to lose to some unknown Cultivation God, he’d lose an immense amount of face.

The Divine Judgment began descending again with even more pressure.

“…”

Confirming that the Celestial Emperor had no intention of backing out, the Cultivation God also began pushing back seriously.

The clash between the Celestial Emperor and the mysterious Cultivation God unleashed shockwaves that tore through the Seventh Heaven, their force so great they stirred tsunamis that reached the heavens in the surrounding seas.

As for Dragon Spiral Mountain—positioned directly beneath their titanic clash—it continued to sink beneath the mounting pressure, with more than half of its once-majestic peak now swallowed by the surrounding waters.

Though countless eyes bore witness to the unfolding battle, none dared to intervene—not even those from Heaven’s Mandate. When Cultivation Gods clashed, the heavens themselves held their breath. No one below their realm could hope to endure such a storm; only another Cultivation God could survive, let alone stand a chance of interfering.

Of course, among the spectators were other Cultivation Gods. Yet their attention was not on the battle itself, but on the newcomer—an unknown deity whose sudden emergence stirred more curiosity.

“Your Majesty! The Celestial Council has arrived outside the palace, and they are demanding that you halt your actions!” someone suddenly said.

Yet, rather than relent, the Celestial Emperor surged with even greater power. seaʀᴄh thё NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

BOOM!

Follow new episodes on the “N0vel1st.c0m”.

The Divine Judgment pulsed with such overwhelming force that fractures began to web across its radiant surface. The Cultivation God was instantly driven back, her figure forced to retreat beneath the sheer weight of the blow.

Despite being forced back, the Cultivation God smiled, her voice calm yet laced with an imperious edge as she spoke, “So this is the power of this era’s Celestial Emperor? You’re scarcely an improvement over your predecessors—perhaps even their lesser.”

The spectators gasped in disbelief at the Cultivation God’s brazen mockery—words so audacious that even other Cultivation Gods would not dare think of them.

“…”

Upon hearing those words, the Celestial Emperor, who had never felt such anger since ascending to his throne, began to tremble, his long-held composure finally fracturing.

“This son of…”

Having his most sensitive weakness poked, the Celestial Emperor instinctively unleashed an even greater surge of power, his fury fueling a force beyond restraint.

“Is the Celestial Emperor trying to bring ruin to the Seventh Heaven?!”

The spectators, once watching with idle fascination, now found their amusement replaced by growing unease and fear.

“Hmph. Your temper is as feeble as your strength.”

The Cultivation God sneered coldly, flicking her sleeve with effortless disdain.

In the very next instant, the colossal sword—its surface already veined with fractures—shattered into countless shards, cascading down upon the Sea of Dragons like a merciless rain of divine blades.

chapters.title: Chapter 1990: Returning To The Nine Heavens
chapters.body:
Chapter 1990: Returning To The Nine Heavens

Chapter 1990: Returning to the Nine Heavens

“Th-The Cultivation God did it! She stopped the Celestial Emperor’s attack!”

A collective gasp swept through the spectators as they drew in a sharp breath, stunned by the unforeseen turn of events.

The moment the Divine Judgment shattered, the Celestial Emperor recoiled as if struck across the face, a searing pain radiating from it. He staggered, blood spilling from his lips, marking the second time in a single day that he had been wounded, a humiliation nearly as deep as the injury itself.

However, the Celestial Emperor swiftly regained his footing and began summoning his Celestial Qi once more—this time with even greater ferocity, his aura surging like a rising tempest that swept throughout the palace.

“Your Majesty!”

His subordinates cried out in alarm, their voices trembling with fear. Yet not one of them dared to intervene, paralyzed by the consequences of doing so.

Once again, the skies above Dragon Spiral Mountain quaked, and multiple rifts tore open in unison, splitting the heavens like wounds in the firmament.

The Cultivation God looked toward the heavens and smiled. “At least your stubbornness rivals your predecessors.”

“T-The Celestial Emperor is really going to destroy the Seventh Heaven at this rate!”

The spectators cried out loud, and some of them began preparing to escape to the other realms.

At this moment, several figures appeared around the Celestial Emperor like ghosts, and their voices rang out in unison, sharp and resolute, “Your Majesty, that is enough of your farce! If you refuse to stand down, do not blame us for taking action!”

“Farce?” The Celestial Emperor turned to face them, his gaze icy and sharp. “Do you truly believe I am acting out of amusement?”

Yet, despite the weight of his glare, the members of the Celestial Council stood firm, their expressions calm and unshaken.

“Not only did you unleash overwhelming force without warning, but you even struck at the Holy Dragon Clan! The Royal Dragon Clans have been raising hell at our gates since the moment this began! Are you truly trying to provoke war with one of the most powerful beast bloodlines in all the Nine Heavens?!” they exclaimed.

“War…?” The Celestial Emperor suddenly burst into laughter, his voice echoing with scorn and fury. “Because of your meddling and that Cultivation God’s interference, the Nine Heavens are now on the brink of a war far greater than anything you can possibly imagine!”

The Celestial Council fell into silence, though none among them seemed particularly alarmed.

“If such a war truly comes, we will face it then,” one of them said calmly. “For now, our priority lies with the rising tensions with the Royal Dragon Clans. A conference has already been arranged—you must attend and make every effort to ease the storm before it breaks. Rumor has it the Great One just emerged from his seclusion, too.”

“…”

Now, it was the Celestial Emperor’s turn to fall silent. The Ten Royal Dragon Clans were of little concern to him, but the Great One was another matter entirely. That being alone could unleash more devastation than all ten clans combined.

In time, the Celestial Emperor withdrew his aura, and the rifts torn across the skies above Dragon Spiral Mountain slowly began to mend. As the heavens healed, a hush fell over the land, and the turbulent waters gradually returned to calm.

However, the Celestial Emperor had not forgotten the Cultivation God. Fixing his gaze upon her that pierced through the realms, he spoke with cold menace, “I do not know who you are, but now that you’ve revealed yourself and declared your opposition, I will hunt you down like a rabbit in the wild.”

The Cultivation God offered no reply—only a bold, silent smile that spoke louder than words. Before departing, she cast a final glance toward the closing portal linking the Seventh Heaven and Earth, her gaze unreadable yet profound.

Elsewhere in the Seventh Heaven, Dong Ye stood in silence, his expression grave after witnessing it all.

“So… she’s finally made her move,” he murmured, a flicker of unease in his eyes.

“What happened to the Holy Dragon Clan and the Dragon Spiral Mountain?!” Xi Meili asked him, her face full of worry.

Dong Ye faced her and explained, “They are safe. A Cultivation God intervened and stopped the Celestial Emperor.”

“A Cultivation God?! Why would someone like that defy the Celestial Emperor?! Oh! Could it be the Dragon Ancestor?!”

“Uh… no, this Cultivation God is a human.”

“Is that so… Anyway, does this mean you can take me back to the Holy Dragon Clan?”

Dong Ye nodded and said, “I can take you back, but we should wait a little longer to make sure it has truly ended.”

Follow new episodes on the “N0vel1st.c0m”.

“What about Yuan?”

“I can sense two presences within that portal, so he should be returning soon.”

“Two?” Xi Meili raised an eyebrow and wondered who that other individual could be.

Sure enough, just a few hours after the battle had ended, Yuan and Liya stepped through the portal, arriving moments before it sealed shut behind them.

Liya’s eyes widened in awe as she took in her surroundings. The spiritual energy in the air was so dense, so pure, that even her ordinary breaths caused her cultivation to surge. It was unlike anything she had ever encountered.

“Welcome to the Nine Heavens,” Yuan said with a gentle smile, watching her wide-eyed wonder, reminiscent of a child discovering the existence of sweets for the very first time.

Noticing his looks, Liya frowned, her tone sharp with indignation. “Don’t you dare treat me like some country bumpkin.”

However, Yuan remained silent, his smile fading as his expression hardened into a deep, solemn frown. Sёarch* The NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“What’s wrong?” she asked.

“I knew activating the teleportation device would invite trouble,” Yuan murmured, his gaze heavy as it fell upon Dragon Spiral Mountain. “But never did I imagine it would escalate to this extent.”

Though the mountain still stood, its once-majestic form bore the scars of devastation—broken, battered, yet barely enduring.

“Let’s go.”

Without another word, Yuan descended toward the mountain, his figure cutting silently through the air. Liya followed close behind.

chapters.title: Chapter 1991: Mysterious Cultivation God
chapters.body:
Chapter 1991: Mysterious Cultivation God

As Yuan landed upon Dragon Spiral Mountain, a wave of shock and sorrow washed over him. The mountain was littered with thousands of corpses, many of whom were from the Holy Dragon Clan. The rest belonged to human cultivators wearing full armor.

“Heaven’s Mandate…”

Despite this being his first time encountering them, he instantly recognized them purely from their armor and its unique patterns. After all, he’d had plenty of encounters with them in his previous lives.

“Did a war happen here or something? These corpses look fresh, so it happened only recently.” Liya remarked as she hovered slightly above the ground, not wanting to touch the corpses.

“It was likely the Celestial Emperor’s doing. He attacked this place in an attempt to prevent me from returning to the Nine Heavens. Clearly, it didn’t work out for him.”

“The Celestial Emperor? Isn’t that the guy who sits above everyone in the Nine Heavens? The ruler of the Nine Heavens.”

Though she was born on Earth, the Lord occasionally taught her about the Nine Heavens.

“That’s right,” he confirmed.

“I’m impressed. How did you end up having beef with the most powerful individual in the Nine Heavens? That’s akin to offending the Lord on Earth,” she remarked.

A bittersweet smile appeared on Yuan’s face as he said, “It’s complicated. Although I have never seen or met this Celestial Emperor, he clearly has a problem with me. Or should I say the ’past’ me?”

“Is that so?” Liya muttered.

Yuan used his divine sense to find the Dragon Emperor and went to find him.

Some time later, Yuan found the Dragon Emperor amidst the wreckage, quietly assisting in the search for fallen members of the clan.

“Dragon Emperor!”

The Dragon Emperor, so absorbed in his search that he hadn’t even noticed Yuan’s return, looked at him with relief in his tired gaze.

“You made it back safely, Senior.”

Though he maintained a calm exterior, the Dragon Emperor’s appearance told another story. His once-majestic robes were torn and stained with blood, and his breath was also somewhat weak.

While he had sustained minor injuries during his clash with General Wang, it was his attempt to block the Divine Judgment that had exacted the greatest toll, wounding not just his body, but his very soul.

“Can you tell me what happened while I was gone? I know the Heaven’s Mandate showed up from their corpses.”

The Dragon Emperor gave a solemn nod before recounting everything to Yuan, from the moment of his departure to the unexpected arrival of an unknown Cultivation God who had descended to defy the Celestial Emperor and save the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

While Yuan wasn’t particularly surprised to learn that the Evil God’s army had appeared, he was surprised to hear that the Celestial Emperor had personally intervened and that a mysterious Cultivation God had descended to protect them.

“Who was this Cultivation God?” he asked. S~eaʀᴄh the ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The Holy Dragon Emperor shook his head.

“I don’t know. They were wearing a face veil that blocked their face. However, she had the figure of a woman. Of course, I cannot tell you if that’s their real appearance or just a disguise.”

“A woman wearing a face veil…”

Yuan wondered if he had known any Cultivation Gods in the past, at least among the memories he had recovered so far. Yet, none came to mind. After all, Cultivation Gods were virtually nonexistent in those ancient days, and his memories were still returning piece by piece with each passing day.

The only Cultivation God Yuan knew was Dragon Goddess Yeyou, the Dragon Ancestor, and the Great One. Even Zi Xuan had only reached the 9th level of God Ascension. However, Dragon Goddess Yeyou was with him during the invasion, and the Great One wouldn’t do something like hide his identity.

’Could it be the Dragon Ancestor? No… he wouldn’t show up disguised as a woman, either. Maybe it was one of the Nine Divine Supremes? But they wouldn’t disappear immediately afterward. Maybe Dong Ye knows something. I’ll ask him later.’

In the end, Yuan couldn’t recall anyone who matched the description, leading him to wonder if the mysterious figure was someone from a part of his past he had yet to remember. After all, he’d only recovered most of the Evil God, Divine Paragon, Immortal Monarch, and Exalted Blacksmith’s memories.

“By the way, where’s Xi Meili?” Yuan asked when he couldn’t see her around.

“You see…” The Holy Dragon Emperor began to sweat the moment Xi Meili’s name was brought up. “I told her to stay out of the battle, but… apparently, she ignored that and joined the fight anyway. She disappeared somewhere amidst the chaos. I haven’t seen her body, so… she’s likely still alive.”

Yuan frowned upon hearing this, but if they didn’t find her body, she was most likely alive.

’Since the Evil God’s Shadow Army showed up, they likely took her away to protect her.’

Sure enough, just as he thought that, a familiar voice rang inside his head.

“My liege, please don’t worry about Lady Xi. She’s currently with me. Though she’d suffered some injuries, they’re not life-threatening.”

Dong Ye notified him.

“Dong Ye? Where are you right now?” he asked.

“I am currently in hiding with the rest of the Shadow Army. Since we made an appearance with the Celestial Emperor watching, we’re likely going to be hunted for a while. By the way, you should leave the Dragon Spiral Mountain as soon as possible while the Celestial Emperor is distracted. I just received the information that he’s occupied with the Celestial Council and the Ten Royal Dragon Clans.”

Yuan nodded and said, “I’ll leave soon. Let Xi Meili know that I have returned, and keep her safe for the time being. Let’s meet once things have calmed down.”

“As you command.”

After his brief conversation with Dong Ye, Yuan said to the Holy Dragon Emperor, “I really don’t know how to thank the Holy Dragon Clan for their sacrifice. If there’s anything you need, don’t hesitate to let me know.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1992: Departing From The Dragon Spiral Mountain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1992: Departing From The Dragon Spiral Mountain

“Please, don’t call it a sacrifice. We were merely defending our territory—our home,” the Holy Dragon Emperor said solemnly.

Yuan’s expression turned awkward, guilt flickering in his eyes.

“Even so… it’s a fact that the Celestial Emperor attacked because I activated the teleportation device. You wouldn’t have needed to do such a thing if I didn’t show up.”

The Holy Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “Your appearance here, as well as the teleportation device’s activation, is Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s will, and as the Holy Dragon Clan, it’s our duty to fulfill her will, regardless of its cost. I allowed anyone who didn’t want to die a chance to leave, but nobody took the offer. That’s all I have to say on this matter.”

Yuan fell silent to ponder.

Eventually, he said, “Can you gather every member of the Holy Dragon Clan for me as soon as possible?”

“What are you trying to do?” The Holy Dragon Emperor raised his eyebrows.

“You’ll find out soon.”

The Holy Dragon Emperor no longer questioned it and followed his instructions.

Shortly after, the remaining members of the Holy Dragon Clan—now reduced to a mere quarter of their former number—gathered before Yuan in solemn silence.

“Thank you for gathering here on such short notice, and while you’re busy. However, I want to do something for you before I leave,” said Yuan before retrieving the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

“Yeyou, can you say something to them? If it weren’t for their efforts, I wouldn’t have been able to return to the Nine Heavens peacefully,” he said to it.

“D-Did he just…?”

“Yeah… I heard it…”

Besides the Holy Dragon Emperor and some of their top members, nobody knew about Yuan being the Immortal Monarch or that Dragon Goddess Yeyou was resting inside the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

“Haaa…”

A soft sigh resounded from the spear before it emitted a profound glow.

In the very next moment, Dragon Goddess Yeyou appeared before them in her human form.

“T-That’s—!”

Even without needing her to introduce herself, the members of the Holy Dragon Clan immediately knew her identity.

Realizing that they were before the one and only Dragon Goddess Yeyou, everyone there kowtowed before her.

“Our God—Dragon Goddess Yeyou!” They all chanted in unison.

“Ahem.” Dragon Goddess Yeyou cleared her throat and began to speak in a somewhat stiff tone, “My name is Yeyou, and I would like to express my gratitude to the Holy Dragon Clan for everything you’ve done so far, especially for today. Thank you. Though I can only express my gratitude with words right now, once I regain my body, I’ll return to give you a proper thank you.”

“For Dragon Goddess Yeyou!” they cried, voices trembling as tears streamed down their faces.

Unless one had devoted their entire life to a single deity, they could never truly comprehend the depth of emotion surging through the Holy Dragon Clan as they stood before Dragon Goddess Yeyou—their one and only god, the eternal flame of their faith.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou spoke no further, choosing to keep her presence brief and solemn. With a single nod of approval, she returned to the Dragon God’s Vengeance. Yet, what she said was already more than enough to ensure the Holy Dragon Clan continued to worship her, perhaps even more intensely than before.

“Though it pains me to leave you like this after all that has happened, remaining here any longer will only risk drawing the Celestial Emperor’s attention once more,” Yuan said solemnly. “So, I’ll be taking my leave for now. But if fate permits, I promise I’ll return to this place again. Of course, I’ll bring Dragon Goddess Yeyou with me.”

“We’ll await your return no matter how long it takes, Senior Envoy!”

Yuan smiled, then turned to meet the gaze of the Holy Dragon Emperor, who responded with a silent nod of acknowledgment.

“Until next time,” Yuan said quietly.

And with that, he departed from Dragon Spiral Mountain with Liya quietly following behind.

“Where are you going to go, Young Master?” Feng Yuxiang asked shortly after Yuan left.

After a moment of silence, Yuan responded, “I’m going to meet the others.”

Meanwhile, in a grand hall deep within the Ninth Heaven, the Celestial Emperor and the Celestial Council sat in solemn audience before representatives of eight of the Ten Royal Dragon Clans.

The only ones who weren’t present were the Holy Dragon Clan and the Azure Dragon Clan. Despite the recent incident, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan still made an appearance.

“You’d best have a damn good explanation, Celestial Emperor!” one of the representatives snapped, slamming the table with a force that echoed through the hall.

“You didn’t just trespass into the territory of one of the Ten Royal Dragon Clans—you launched a full assault, one that claimed thousands of lives!” another representative roared, his voice laced with outrage.

“And not only that,” a third representative added sharply, “you attempted to annihilate their entire bloodline with Divine Judgment—a power reserved solely for condemning the most heinous of criminals on death row!”

The Celestial Emperor sat beneath a storm of accusations and complaints, the voices of the Ten Royal Dragon Clans rising in fury around him, while the Celestial Council observed in heavy silence, neither defending nor intervening. S~eaʀᴄh the novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I did what had to be done. I hope for your understanding,” the Celestial Emperor finally said, his voice calm and measured, as if the barrage of accusations had done little more than stir the air around him.

“Don’t give us that nonsense! There were countless ways to handle the situation without endangering the Holy Dragon Clan—yet you chose the most brutal and direct path!” One of them refuted.

“Perhaps so,” he replied calmly, “but I was racing against time and did not have the luxury to do things gently.”

“I gave the Holy Dragon Clan fair warning, yet they refused to yield. I was left with no other choice. It is regrettable, but I acted as I must to protect the Nine Heavens, which is my top priority as the Celestial Emperor. I understand your anger, but if I could rewind time and redo it, I wouldn’t have done it any differently.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1993: Ten Royal Dragon Clans
chapters.body:
Chapter 1993: Ten Royal Dragon Clans

Chapter 1993: Ten Royal Dragon Clans

“The audacity!” one of the representatives shouted, his voice trembling with fury. “Do you truly wish to wage war against the Ten Royal Dragon Clans, Celestial Emperor?!”

The Celestial Council, frowning at the escalating tension, finally stepped in.

“Let us all take a moment to calm ourselves,” one of the council members said firmly. “If the Ten Royal Dragon Clans were to truly wage war against His Majesty, the Celestial Emperor, it would result in countless deaths and destruction across the Nine Heavens.”

“All this… for the Holy Dragon Clan?” another said. “Let’s be honest. While they may hold a seat among the Ten Royal Dragon Clans, they spend most of their existence secluded within Dragon Spiral Mountain, and they’ve done little to justify that title.”

“Moreover, their bloodline only rose to the top three because of the blessing from Dragon Goddess Yeyou, one of the Three Dragons of Origin. Your clans, on the other hand, were born with that level of purity. Can their so-called ‘royal’ bloodline truly be considered authentic?”

“…”

An uneasy silence fell over the Royal Dragon Clans as the Celestial Council cast doubt on the authenticity of the Holy Dragon Clan’s bloodline, a heavily debated subject in the past.

Since the Holy Dragon Clan had not been born with an Imperial-grade bloodline, but rather received it through the blessing of Dragon Goddess Yeyou, the Royal Dragon Clans of the time fiercely debated whether they should be granted a seat among their ranks.

In the end, they chose to accept the Holy Dragon Clan—not out of consensus, but out of caution. To reject the clan would have been tantamount to rejecting Dragon Goddess Yeyou herself, a risk none were willing to take.

However, since their acceptance into the ranks of the Royal Dragon Clans, the Holy Dragon Clan had contributed little, if anything, to the collective. Reclusive and self-focused, they remained within the confines of Dragon Spiral Mountain, showing little interest in the affairs of the other clans.

The Celestial Council smiled inwardly after seeing the Royal Dragon Clan’s reaction.

“And just so you’re aware,” the Celestial Emperor said suddenly, his tone calm but edged with warning, “a war against us would not pit you against my forces alone but against the Royal Phoenix Clans as well.”

The representatives’ expressions darkened at once, frowns forming as the weight of those words sank in.

“You… are you threatening us with the Royal Phoenix Clans?”

“No,” the Celestial Emperor replied, his tone as calm as still water. “I’m merely informing you so that you may choose wisely.”

Before the Royal Dragon Clans could respond, the Celestial Emperor rose to his feet and spoke coldly:

“If all you intend to do here is bicker over this matter, then I’ll take my leave. I’ve already given my explanation—whether you accept it or not is irrelevant to me. I have more important matters to attend to.”

“Celestial Emperor—!”

Angry voices rang out behind him, but the Celestial Emperor paid them no mind, departing without so much as a glance back.

His abrupt exit left the Celestial Council in an awkward position. After all, they had aided him by sowing doubt among the Royal Dragon Clans, yet he had discarded that advantage without hesitation.

Though the Celestial Emperor had departed, the Celestial Council remained behind to continue the negotiations with the Royal Dragon Clans, stretching on for many hours.

After his departure, the Celestial Emperor did not return to the Celestial Palace. Instead, he journeyed to his personal realm. There, a mysterious device displayed a projection of an unknown location within the vast starry void. Within that projection, several indistinct entities could be seen, their forms dormant… yet far from lifeless.

“Unlike my predecessors… I will not fail you,” he murmured, a resolute flame burning in his eyes, unwavering and fierce.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Seventh Heaven, Kelan gathered Meixiu and the others after learning about what happened at the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“Alright, listen up. I don’t know what kind of trouble your friend, Yuan, has gotten himself into, but there was a huge war between Heaven’s Madate and the Holy Dragon Clan that dwells there.”

“Heaven’s Mandate? What’s that?” Wang Ming asked.

“They’re the Celestial Emperor’s private army.”

“The Celestial Emperor?!”

“That’s right. If I recall correctly, Yuan said something about heading to the Dragon Spiral Mountain, right? Though he was not present, there’s no way this is a coincidence.”

“Let me log off real quick and see if Yuan has returned to Earth!” Chu Liuxiang suddenly said.

“I’m going too,” said Meixiu.

They logged off at the same time.

After logging off, Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang removed the device and turned to look beside them. However, Yuan’s body, which should’ve been there, was gone.

“H-He’s gone!” Chu Liuxiang exclaimed.

“I’m going to speak with the Lord. You go back and inform the others,” Meixiu said as she swiftly rose from the bed. Without another word, she stepped onto the balcony and took flight, soaring straight toward the mountain’s peak.

Chu Liuxiang immediately returned to Cultivation Online and explained the situation to the others.

“What? Yuan’s body is gone? So he’s back on Earth?” Shi Lang said.

“Most likely, but I couldn’t sense his presence anywhere,” Chu Liuxiang responded.

“Surely, he didn’t leave Earth without a word, right?” Wang Ming said.

“Maybe he was tight on time and had to leave immediately,” Wang Bingbing commented.

“That’s probably what happened.”

While the group speculated on the situation, Meixiu met with the Lord.

“Yes, he came back.” The Lord spoke before Meixiu even got the chance to open her mouth. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Where is he now?”

“He’s already returned to the Nine Heavens. The gateway would only stay open for so long, so he had to leave as soon as possible.”

“I see… It’s a pity that we missed him, but at least we’ll see him soon in the Seventh Heaven. By the way, your cultivation…”

“I sacrificed it to open the portal.”

“That’s…” Meixiu was speechless.

“You don’t have to worry about me. I’ll be fine. However, things might get rowdy here soon.”

“What do you mean?” Meixiu raised an eyebrow.

chapters.title: Chapter 1994: Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect
chapters.body:
Chapter 1994: Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect

Chapter 1994: Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect

“You weren’t there to see it, but it was quite the spectacle. I’m sure governments worldwide are making their way to the Dragon Spiral Mountain as we speak to investigate,” the Lord explained.

“I see… where’s Miss Liya? I’m sure she’ll handle it.”

The Lord showed a bittersweet smile and revealed, “Liya followed Yuan back to the Nine Heavens. She’s no longer with us.” seaʀᴄh thё novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“What?!” Meixiu’s eyes widened after learning this.

“Then what should we do?” she then asked.

“Nothing,” the Lord calmly responded. “The Dragon Spiral Mountain is a unique place. They cannot enter it without my permission. Even if they do, the mountain has multiple powerful formations that can kill even Immortals. That said, I lack the spiritual energy to activate them, so I’ll be needing your help when that time comes.”

Meixiu nodded.

Some time later, she returned to Cultivation Online and relayed what the Lord told her.

“What? The manager followed Yuan to the Nine Heavens?”

The others were greatly shocked by this news since it was the last thing they expected to hear… no, they didn’t even think Liya was capable of leaving Earth and the Lord.

“Did the Lord say anything about Yuan’s next plans?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

Meixiu shook her head.

“Is that so…”

“I highly doubt he’ll be coming here anytime soon, despite already reaching the Seventh Heaven, especially when he’s got the Celestial Emperor’s attention,” remarked Kelan.

“I’m fine just knowing that he’s reunited with his body and has returned to the Nine Heavens. We’ll meet eventually,” said Li Jinxi.

“That’s right. It’s not like he’s forgotten about us or anything. Let’s continue our training so we can surprise him the next time we meet!” Wang Ming said.

Just under a year later, Yuan and Liya finally left the vast Sea of Dragons and set foot on land once more.

“I can’t believe how massive this world is. How do normal people travel?” Liya asked, feeling slightly overwhelmed by the sheer size of the Nine Heavens.

“Teleportation formations,” said Yuan.

“I’m talking about ordinary people—mortals. I doubt they can afford to use them.”

“Oh, if you’re talking about mortals, the majority of them tend to stay within their birthplace and rarely travel.”

The Nine Heavens was simply too vast for ordinary people, most of whom couldn’t afford the thousands of spirit stones required to use teleportation devices. As a result, they only traveled short distances, while long journeys were a luxury reserved for wealthy families and influential merchants.

After arriving at the teleportation device, Yuan gave his destination to the operator.

“I’d like to travel to the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect or whatever is closest.”

Liya raised her eyebrows at such a terrible name for a sect.

“Since they have a teleportation device right in front of their sect, I can bring you to their entrance. That will cost 50,000 spirit stones per person. If you want a cheaper option, there’s a city—”

Without hesitation, Yuan handed 100,000 spirit stones to the operator before he could even finish his sentence.

“I’ll prepare the teleportation device right now.”

A few moments later, Yuan and Liya stepped through the teleportation device, disappearing from the city and emerging several million miles away.

Upon exiting the portal, they found themselves a few dozen meters from a colossal steel gate, nestled between two towering mountains. Yet, these were no ordinary peaks. Their surfaces were not carved from stone, but forged from solid metal. And if one looked closely, the contours and ridges suggested they had been shaped not by nature, but by the force of bare hands. Moreover, the name of the sect, Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect, was not simply carved, but engraved into the metal through one’s fist.

Yuan calmly ignored the peculiar name and made his way toward the gate, where a tall, muscular man stood guard.

“Welcome to the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect. What business do you have with us today?” the disciple asked.

Yuan showed the disciple his Celestial Overlord symbol and said, “I’m here to speak with Kelan.”

“I understand. Give me a moment to contact the Sect Leader. By the way, can I have your name?”

“Xiao Yang.”

The disciple retrieved a communication jade slip and relayed a message to a sect elder, who in turn reached out to Kelan.

A few minutes later, Yuan could sense a powerful presence flying in their direction with immense speed.

Boom!

This individual landed right in front of the gates.

“This disciple greets the Sect Leader!”

However, Kelan didn’t appear too welcoming.

With a frown on his face, he spoke to Yuan using voice transmission, “Are you insane? Why would you come straight here after what happened at Dragon Spiral Mountain? Do you have any idea what kind of trouble you might bring to my sect?”

Yuan smiled and responded, “Relax, the Celestial Emperor isn’t aware of my identity yet. If he knew my identity, he would’ve come straight to me. Even if he is aware of my identity, he won’t be stupid enough to attack everyone I interact with… I hope.”

Kelan rubbed his eyes and took a deep breath before speaking, “Come with me. I’ll bring you to the others.”

Yuan and Liya followed Kelan into the sect.

As they traveled, Yuan said, “By the way, did you come up with the sect’s name?”

“Yes. It’s very unique, isn’t it?”

“It’s definitely unique in its own way.” Yuan smiled.

“How are the others?”

Kelan touched his nose in a prideful manner and said, “I’ve personally trained them for the last several years. You’ll be surprised by how much they’ve—Wait! You’ve already reached the peak of Immortal Ascension?! How is that even possible?!”

Kelan nearly fell out of the sky from shock when he finally noticed Yuan’s cultivation.

After recovering from his shock, Kelan spoke with an excited smile, “I cannot wait to have a serious spar with you later.”

Yuan chuckled and said, “Don’t be too upset if you lose.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1995: Sparring With Kelan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1995: Sparring With Kelan

Chapter 1995: Sparring With Kelan

“By the way, who’s that pretty lady? Wait! Let me guess—she’s another lover—”

“No, she’s a friend. I brought her here from my world,” Yuan hastily interrupted when he sensed Liya’s cold glare from behind.

“What? She’s from the forgotten world?” Kelan looked at Liya with interest. “Why’d you bring her to the Nine Heavens?”

“It’s a bit complicated, and it wasn’t my decision.”

“Is that so?”

Some time later, they arrived at the heart of the sect, a domain reserved only for Core Disciples and Sect Elders.

Kelan continued a little further until they reached a restricted area accessible only to the Sect Leader, meaning only he could enter. However, when they arrived, Yuan could see several figures already waiting for their arrival.

“I notified them on our way here,” Kelan said as he descended to the ground.

“Yuan! Manager!”

Chu Liuxiang and the others greeted them with bright expressions.

“Hey, everyone—”

Yuan tried to greet back, but he was interrupted when Chu Liuxiang pounced at him, giving him a tight embrace.

“I’ve missed you!”

“I missed you, too.”

Chu Liuxiang didn’t hog Yuan all to herself and released him a few seconds later so Meixiu could do the same.

“Yuan.” Meixiu wasted no time and entered his embrace.

“Sorry, I couldn’t see any of you when I returned to Earth,” Yuan said to the others a few moments later. “I had to rush back as soon as possible.”

“We figured that was the case,” Wang Ming said.

“How long has it been since then? Five years?”

“Around that, I guess. I haven’t kept track.” Yuan admitted.

“I doubt any of us kept track, either. We’re always so busy.”

Yuan looked at their cultivation and smiled, “I see you’ve all reached the Divine Realm. Congratulations.”

In just a few years, everyone increased their cultivation to Divine King, with Li Jinxi even reaching Divine Ancestor.

“What cultivation have you reached? I cannot sense it.” Shi Lang asked.

“Ninth level Immortal Ascension,” he calmly replied.

“So you’re already half a step into becoming an Immortal?!”

“At this rate, you’ll be a True Immortal by the next time we see you,” Wang Bingbing remarked.

“Let’s chat inside,” Kelan said as he opened the doors to his pavilion.

Once everyone was seated inside, Yuan recalled his experience over the last several years, from meeting the Holy Dragon Clan to visiting the Azure Dragon Clan.

“To think you’d met with not one, two, but three of the Ten Royal Dragon Clans in such a short time. You know most people go their entire lives without even meeting one of them, right?” Kelan remarked.

“I guess my fate is quite strong when it comes to dragons,” he shrugged.

“What are your plans now?” Chu Liuixang asked.

Yuan didn’t immediately respond.

After some pondering, he spoke, “There are so many things to do that I don’t know where to begin. Since it’s been a while, I’m thinking about staying here to spend some time with the rest of you while I figure out my next plan.”

“That sounds like a great plan!” Chu Liuxiang immediately said.

“You don’t mind, right?” Yuan looked at Kelan.

“I don’t mind… as long as you don’t cause any trouble.”

Yuan smiled and said, “The others won’t even know I’m here.”

Wang Xiuying suddenly asked, “Hey, where’s Xi Meili? I thought she was with you.”

“Oh, we had to split temporarily. She’ll come here soon.”

Some time later, after finishing their conversation, Yuan and the others followed Kelan to a nearby building so they could finally spar.

At a glance, the building looked unremarkable, barely larger than a standard warehouse. Yet, the moment they stepped inside, the world around them shifted, and they found themselves in a boundless white space that seemed to stretch into eternity.

“We can fight to our heart’s content on here without needing to worry about damages,” Kelan said.

“Do you need some rest before we start?”

“No, I can spar right now.”

“Then to keep things fair, I’ll suppress my cultivation to match yours,” Kelan continued.

Yuan offered no response, simply nodding in silence.

Kelan blinked in mild surprise before chuckling. “Huh. I was certain you’d say something like, ‘No need to hold back,’ or challenge me to go all out.”

Yuan smiled faintly.

“It’s easier to just show you.” Searᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

A gleam of excitement flashed across Kelan’s face. Without hesitation, he shrugged off his outer robes, revealing the training garb beneath—minimal and fitted, covering only his lower body.

“Whenever you’re ready,” he said.

“Then…”

Yuan gave a quick stretch of his arms before launching himself toward Kelan, not a trace of aura leaking from his body, relying solely on his physical strength.

“A clash of physiques first, huh? Just what I was hoping for!” Kelan laughed heartily as he charged to meet him.

“Wha—”

The moment their bodies collided, Kelan’s expression twisted in disbelief. It felt as though he had slammed into an immovable mountain, and before he could even process what had happened, his body was hurled through the air, like a man struck head-on by a truck.

Wang Ming and the others could hardly believe what they had just witnessed. After years of training alongside Kelan, they were well aware of how formidable his physique was. In fact, not once had they seen him overpowered in terms of raw strength—until now.

After the unexpected blow, Kelan quickly picked himself up. Yet rather than frustration, his face lit up with unrestrained excitement.

“I couldn’t really tell before because your body was mostly made of your soul,” he said, eyes gleaming. “But that physique… could it be the legendary Golden Immortal Physique?”

Yuan gave a simple nod.

“That’s right.”

“HAHAHA!” Kelan burst into laughter, his voice echoing through the boundless space.

“Unbelievable! I’m actually clashing with the legendary Golden Immortal Physique! This is a dream come true!”

Yuan then said calmly, “If you truly want to experience the Golden Immortal Physique… you’ll have to try a lot harder.”

Because his avatar had been composed mostly of his soul, it had barely allowed the Golden Immortal Physique to reveal even a fraction of its true power. But now that Yuan had reclaimed his physical body, the full might of the legendary physique could finally shine without restraint.

chapters.title: Chapter 1996: Sparring With Kelan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1996: Sparring With Kelan(2)

Chapter 1996: Sparring With Kelan(2)

“This is my first time fighting in my true body, so give me everything you’ve got,” Yuan said as he beckoned Kelan.

“Then… don’t mind if I do!”

Kelan unleashed his cultivation, but he still kept it suppressed to the peak of Immortal Ascension.

“Here I come!”

With a roar of excitement, Kelan charged forward, his fists surging with immense energy.

However, Yuan didn’t so much as lift a finger to defend. He stood firm, like an immovable mountain, taking every one of Kelan’s strikes head-on with nothing but his bare body.

“This is rather relaxing. You might have a future as a masseur,” Yuan said calmly, a faint smile on his lips. He stood unbothered, as though Kelan’s blows were nothing despite each strike sending ripples through the air and making the very space around them tremble.

“I refuse to believe I can’t break through your defenses!” Kelan roared, his aura flaring violently as it shifted. Without hesitation, he began unleashing powerful martial techniques.

“He’s already using his Ninety-Nine Divine Fists technique!” Shi Lang exclaimed, a shiver running down his spine as memories flooded back—vivid flashes of being beaten to a pulp by that very technique.

The technique comprised a total of ninety-nine strikes, each one building in power and ferocity, much like Yuan’s own Nine Supreme Swords.

“Fifty-Fifth Divine Fist!” Kelan bellowed as his fist crashed forward, the air trembling from the force.

Yet, even after unleashing more than half of the technique, he felt no closer to breaching Yuan’s defenses. It was like striking a mountain of metal with a brittle wooden branch.

“Ninty-Ninth Divine Fist!”

Even after the final strike landed, Yuan wasn’t pushed back even slightly.

With a faintly provocative smile, Yuan turned slightly and pointed to his back.

“Why don’t you give the back some attention too?” he said, as if inviting a massage rather than an attack.

Kelan’s eyebrow twitched, a flicker of irritation crossing his face as his pride took a hit.

With a slightly cold edge to his voice, he muttered, “You asked for this…”

Suddenly, his suppressed cultivation surged, breaking through to the Bronze Immortal level.

Now wreathed in swirling Immortal Qi, Kelan launched a renewed assault on Yuan.

“Twenty-Second Divine Fist!”

“…”

“Seventy-Fifth Divine Fist!”

“…”

After going through another 99 strikes, Kelan distanced himself from Yuan before staring at him with wide eyes that flickered with disbelief.

’It’s like he doesn’t even feel my attacks! How is that possible?!’

Knowing full well that Yuan possessed powerful regenerative abilities, Kelan didn’t hold back and poured every ounce of Immortal Qi he could muster as a Bronze Immortal into his attacks.

Yet, despite his full strength, the outcome was the same as before—utterly ineffective. Moreover, Yuan was still relying solely on his bare body to endure the onslaught. This meant no techniques and no defenses—just sheer physique.

At this point, it was no exaggeration to say that he could withstand a full-powered assault from a Bronze Immortal in his sleep… and quite possibly sleep through it.

“That wasn’t bad, but it would’ve been better if you’d used a little more strength. How about increasing your cultivation to True Immortal?” Yuan suddenly said.

“True Immortal…? You know this is just a spar, right?” Kelan said.

“And you know you won’t even be able to give me a ’warm up’ with this level of sparring, right?” Yuan responded. “What happened to wanting to experience the Golden Immortal Physique? You’re barely seeing the tip of its prowess right now.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Kelan took a deep breath and spoke in a serious tone, “Don’t blame me for whatever happens next.”

Kelan increased his cultivation to Silver Immortal, then Golden Immortal, finally stopping at True Immortal.

Yuan smiled and said, “That’s more like it.”

“Here I come.”

After giving Yuan a brief moment to ready himself, Kelan sprang into motion, soaring toward him with fists infused with Immortal Qi and just a trace of Celestial Qi.

Despite this, Yuan remained still. Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Boom!

A split second later, Yuan’s body was sent hurtling into the distance from the force of the blow.

However, he swiftly regained control mid-air and landed gracefully on his feet, completely unharmed. The strike had possessed just enough power to send him flying, but it was still far from enough to break his defenses.

“Seriously…?” Kelan stared at Yuan, utterly dumbfounded.

He had always assumed the legends surrounding the Golden Immortal Physique were exaggerated. But after facing it firsthand, Kelan could say without the slightest doubt—if anything, the legends underplayed its true prowess.

“So? What do you think?” Yuan asked as he calmly walked toward Kelan. “Satisfied yet?”

Kelan offered a nervous smile, sweat beading at his brow.

“I thought it’d be fun… but honestly, it was more terrifying than I expected.”

Yuan let out a light chuckle.

“Then what if I told you that my body still isn’t fully merged with my soul?”

“What?!” Kelan blurted out, his eyes widening in disbelief.

Yuan nodded calmly. “It’s been nearly a year since I reclaimed my true body. I thought that would be enough to fully adapt, but so far, I’ve only managed about fifty percent.”

“You’re telling me that you’ve only shown me about fifty percent of your true prowess?!” Kelan exclaimed.

“Hm? No, I haven’t even begun to show you anything yet. I’m strictly talking about my physique. If we’re talking about my prowess…”

Yuan abruptly stopped speaking, and in the next moment, unleashed his cultivation and Immortal Qi in a sudden surge, its sheer force nearly knocking Kelan off his feet.

“Heavens! What is that amount of Immortal Qi?! I have met many True Immortals, but none of them can come close to having that much Immortal Qi!” Kelan exclaimed.

“While my cultivation has fully merged and my physique is about halfway there, my bloodline and the rest of my talents have only just begun to merge. Honestly, I have no clue how powerful I will become once my true body and soul fully merge.” Yuan calmly shrugged, leaving Kelan and the others speechless.
